surfaceAudits.surface: links (Redirects / Internal Links / Dead Links / Architecture)
surfaceAudits.headline: The rebuilt Links workshop is genuinely richer than legacy (URL-persisted state, sortable evidence tables, GSC click evidence, real error states, an Architecture lens), but it silently dropped every open-in-new-tab affordance a link-repair operator relies on, truncated the architecture orphan list behind a misleading pointer, and shipped a do-nothing "Add page" button.
legacySources: src/components/SiteArchitecture.tsx (mounted via src/components/PageIntelligence.tsx:239, NOT legacy LinksPanel — legacy Links had only 3 tabs, LinksPanel.tsx:18-22)

functionalityLost.capability: Open a URL in a new tab from anywhere on the surface. Legacy rendered clickable external anchors for the dead/redirecting link itself (LinkChecker.tsx:255-263, <a target=_blank> on link.url), the redirect target of a scanned page (RedirectManager.tsx:502-506), and a chain's final destination (RedirectManager.tsx:333-335). The rebuilt surface has zero rendered anchors — grep of links-rebuilt/ finds only the copied-HTML string template (InternalLinksLens.tsx:89). Dead-link drawer offers only Copy URL (DeadLinksLens.tsx:131-134); redirect target column is plain text (RedirectsLens.tsx:488-500); chain final destination is plain text (RedirectsLens.tsx:379-381). Verifying a broken link now takes copy -> new tab -> paste on every row.
functionalityLost.evidence: legacy LinkChecker.tsx:255-263, RedirectManager.tsx:333-335 + 502-506 vs rebuilt DeadLinksLens.tsx:240-248 + 126-145, RedirectsLens.tsx:379-381 + 488-500

functionalityLost.capability: Full architecture orphan-path list. Legacy listed every orphanPath in its own card (SiteArchitecture.tsx:377-392). Rebuilt shows only the first 3 as chips and claims "+N more in the URL tree" (ArchitectureLens.tsx:421-430) — but TreeNodeRow renders no orphan marker (ArchitectureLens.tsx:94-138) and the schema PriorityQueue dropped legacy's per-row "orphan" flag (legacy SiteArchitecture.tsx:470-472 vs rebuilt ArchitectureLens.tsx:273-318 columns: Page/Priority/Inbound/Link score). Orphans 4..N are unidentifiable anywhere in the rebuilt lens, and the banner actively misdirects.
functionalityLost.evidence: legacy SiteArchitecture.tsx:377-392 + 470-472 vs rebuilt ArchitectureLens.tsx:414-436, 94-138, 273-318

functionalityLost.capability: Persistent "Sent" state after send-to-client. Legacy latched sentToClient and disabled the button, relabeling it "Sent" for both redirect proposals (RedirectManager.tsx:83-84, 362-364) and internal links (InternalLinks.tsx:38-39, 178-180). Rebuilt buttons re-enable after success with only a transient toast (RedirectsLens.tsx:317-327, InternalLinksLens.tsx:563-566), so an operator can accidentally create duplicate client actions and has no on-screen record that a batch was already sent.
functionalityLost.evidence: legacy RedirectManager.tsx:362-364, InternalLinks.tsx:178-180 vs rebuilt RedirectsLens.tsx:174-195 + 300-330, InternalLinksLens.tsx:321-343 + 546-569

functionalityLost.capability: Longest-redirect-chain metric ("longest: N hops" sub on the Chains stat, RedirectManager.tsx:284). summary.longestChain is fetched but rendered nowhere in the rebuilt lens.
functionalityLost.evidence: legacy RedirectManager.tsx:284 vs rebuilt RedirectsLens.tsx:549-601 (no longestChain usage; grep confirms)

functionalityLost.capability: Deep-page warning in depth distribution. Legacy showed a conditional amber callout when any depth > 3 ("Consider adding hub pages", SiteArchitecture.tsx:498-505). Rebuilt DepthDistribution has only static meta copy (ArchitectureLens.tsx:202).
functionalityLost.evidence: legacy SiteArchitecture.tsx:498-505 vs rebuilt ArchitectureLens.tsx:195-219

functionalityLost.capability: Per-page schema type names. Legacy tree exposed the actual schema types via tooltip (title={`Schema: ${cov.schemaTypes.join(', ')}`}, SiteArchitecture.tsx:149). Rebuilt shows only a count badge ("N schema", ArchitectureLens.tsx:116-120); the type names are unreachable in the Links architecture lens.
functionalityLost.evidence: legacy SiteArchitecture.tsx:147-158 vs rebuilt ArchitectureLens.tsx:116-120

functionalityLost.capability: Row-level Insert for internal-link suggestions (prototype capability, never in legacy) — rebuilt intentionally offers HTML copy + client-send instead.
functionalityLost.evidence: docs/ui-rebuild/parity/links-contract.md "Explicit exception carried into owner review"; parity-backlog.md item 18
functionalityLost.whereDocumented: docs/ui-rebuild/parity/links-contract.md (owner-approved exception, 2026-07-10) and docs/ui-rebuild/parity/parity-backlog.md item 18

regressions.issue: The Architecture gaps "Add page" button is a misleading control: clicking it performs no action and no navigation — it only toasts "Open Content Pipeline to create {path}; Links does not publish pages directly." Legacy had no button at all (gaps were informational, SiteArchitecture.tsx:354-374), which was more honest than an action-styled button that tells you to go do it yourself somewhere else.
regressions.evidence: rebuilt ArchitectureLens.tsx:249-253 + LinksSurface caller at ArchitectureLens.tsx:448-451 (onAddPage={(path) => toast(...)}) vs legacy SiteArchitecture.tsx:354-374 (no action affordance)

regressions.issue: Misleading success state on insufficient data: rebuilt renders "No internal-link gaps detected / Your site has good internal-link coverage" whenever suggestions.length === 0 — including when pageCount is 0 or 1. Legacy explicitly guarded this with "Content fetch issue" (pageCount < attempted*0.5) and "Not enough pages to analyze. At least 2 published pages required" (pageCount < 2) before ever showing the green banner. Rebuilt's partial-fetch banner only fires when attemptedPageCount !== pageCount, so a 1-page site gets a clean success message.
regressions.evidence: legacy InternalLinks.tsx:438-458 (three-way guard) vs rebuilt InternalLinksLens.tsx:571-577 (unconditional success when suggestions empty) + 538-544 (banner only on count mismatch)

regressions.issue: Redirects lens table rows are keyed by source.path (getRowKey), but pageStatuses can contain the same path from multiple sources (static/cms/gsc); legacy keyed by index. A collision would drop/mis-render rows silently.
regressions.evidence: rebuilt RedirectsLens.tsx:590 (getRowKey={(record) => ...source.path}) vs legacy RedirectManager.tsx:487 (keyed by idx)

confusions.issue: Lens count badges mix semantics: Redirects shows total scanned pages (pageStatuses.length), Dead Links shows broken-link count, Internal shows suggestion count, Architecture shows total pages (LinksSurface.tsx:115-129). An operator scanning the lens bar reads all four as "problems" — a "212" on Redirects looks like 212 redirect issues when it's just the crawl size.
confusions.why: Counts on workshop tabs conventionally mean work items; inconsistent units make triage-by-glance actively wrong.

confusions.issue: The shared search box persists across lens switches — setTab clears only the detail param, never the ?search= param (useLinksSurfaceState.ts:100-105), so searching "blog" in Redirects then switching to Architecture silently pre-filters the URL tree.
confusions.why: The operator's mental model is per-tab search (legacy had independent inputs per tab); a carried-over filter makes a lens look mysteriously empty even though the term is visible in the toolbar.

confusions.issue: Architecture now lives in two rebuilt homes with different capabilities: the Links Architecture lens (ArchitectureLens.tsx) and Page Intelligence's Architecture tab, which still mounts the LEGACY SiteArchitecture component (page-intelligence-rebuilt/PageIntelligenceSurface.tsx:266). The legacy copy shows full orphan lists, depth warnings, and schema-type tooltips that the Links lens dropped — same data, two contradictory presentations.
confusions.why: An operator who knows the Page Intelligence version will think Links' version is broken (or vice versa), and "which is canonical?" has no answer on screen.

confusions.issue: Client-send actions moved from always-visible header buttons (legacy InternalLinks.tsx:176-186, RedirectManager.tsx:359-373) into collapsed-by-default GroupBlocks at the bottom of the lens (InternalLinksLens.tsx:546-569 "Send recommendations to client" defaultOpen=false; RedirectsLens.tsx:300-330 "Accepted redirect batch" defaultOpen=false).
confusions.why: Sending to the client is the payoff action of the whole workshop; burying it behind an expand at the page bottom costs discoverability, especially for redirects where the export button stays in the card header but copy/send do not.

overcomplication.issue: Dead deep-link machinery: useLinksSurfaceState declares a ?detail= param with openDetail/closeDetail and clears it on tab change (useLinksSurfaceState.ts:28, 87, 100-105, 139-145), but no lens consumes it — both drawers use local useState (InternalLinksLens.tsx:301, DeadLinksLens.tsx:195). Drawer state is not actually URL-persisted despite the machinery existing.
overcomplication.evidence: grep for openDetail/closeDetail across links-rebuilt returns zero call sites outside the hook
overcomplication.suggestion: Either wire the drawers to ?detail= (making dead-link/suggestion detail deep-linkable — a real win for handoffs) or delete the param, the setters, and the setTab clear.

overcomplication.issue: The immediate success toast "Architecture refresh started" fires synchronously on Re-analyze (LinksSurface.tsx:152-156) before either refetch resolves, and no completion/failure toast follows (the stale-warning banner in ArchitectureLens.tsx:395-399 only shows if the query errors while data exists).
overcomplication.suggestion: Toast on settle (success/error) like the other three lenses' mutations do, instead of announcing the start.

gains: Architecture is now a peer lens on Links with ?tab=architecture deep-link support (useLinksSurfaceState.ts:5-10); legacy Links had only 3 tabs (LinksPanel.tsx:18-22) and architecture was buried inside Page Intelligence.

All filter/view/search state is URL-persisted and deep-linkable (?search/?status/?priority/?view/?list/?type/?source, useLinksSurfaceState.ts:20-27); legacy state was ephemeral useState lost on any navigation.
Legacy ?tab=dead alias and invalid-tab fallback are handled (useLinksSurfaceState.ts:41-45); legacy LinksPanel silently fell back to Redirects on ?tab=dead (no alias in LinksPanel.tsx:28-33).

Redirect scan table surfaces clicks/impressions/matchScore evidence the server already returned but legacy UI discarded (useAdminLinks.ts:35-37 vs legacy local PageStatus interface RedirectManager.tsx:30-40), plus a new honest "Search clicks at risk" metric with a dash fallback when click evidence is absent (RedirectsLens.tsx:413-417).
Sortable DataTable columns in all four lenses (status/path/source/clicks/priority/etc.); legacy tables had no sorting anywhere.
Dead Links gained search (legacy LinkChecker had none), a detail Drawer, session "Mark reviewed" tracking, and a direct handoff back to Redirects (DeadLinksLens.tsx:95-176, 466-476).

Real error states with retry: legacy LinkChecker swallowed failed runs entirely (catch -> console.error, LinkChecker.tsx:66-69) leaving the operator staring at a spinner's aftermath; rebuilt shows error banners with Retry on every lens (e.g. DeadLinksLens.tsx:356-363).
Internal Links gained a full page-health evidence table with per-page Meter scores (InternalLinksLens.tsx:184-215); legacy only used pageHealth for orphans and the average.
In-surface no-Webflow-site, workspace-error, and workspace-loading states (LinksSurface.tsx:219-264) instead of relying on the App.tsx shell guard.
Toast feedback on copy/export/send actions and consistent mutation error extraction via the canonical extractErrorMessage (linksMutationFeedback.ts).

extensions.opportunity: Restore open-in-new-tab anchors: URL cell + drawer "Open link" for dead links, redirect target and chain final-destination anchors in Redirects.
extensions.value: Removes the copy/paste round-trip from the single most common verification step in a link-repair workflow; directly fixes the biggest undocumented loss.

extensions.opportunity: Make the Architecture "Add page" gap action a real handoff — navigate to content-pipeline?tab=briefs with the suggested path prefilled (the fixContext state-passing pattern already exists in useSiteAuditRebuilt.ts:357-382) — or demote it to plain guidance text.
extensions.value: Converts a trust-eroding do-nothing button into the cross-surface workflow the toast copy already describes.

extensions.opportunity: Surface the full orphan list: mark orphan nodes in the URL tree (data already keyed by path) and restore the orphan flag column in the schema priority queue.
extensions.value: Makes orphans 4..N actionable again and makes the existing "+N more in the URL tree" banner copy true.

extensions.opportunity: Latch sent-state per snapshot (disable + "Sent" label after a successful client-send, keyed on sourceId) or show the existing client action if one was already created for this analyzedAt/scannedAt.
extensions.value: Prevents duplicate client inbox items and gives the operator a durable record that the batch went out.

extensions.opportunity: Wire the already-built ?detail= param to the dead-link and internal-link drawers.
extensions.value: Deep-linkable detail rows enable Site Audit and Slack/handoff links to land on a specific broken link, and the machinery is already written.

extensions.opportunity: Unify lens count badges to problem counts (404s for Redirects, suggestions for Internal, dead links for Dead, gaps for Architecture).
extensions.value: Makes the lens bar a truthful triage strip instead of mixed units.

extensions.opportunity: Consolidate the two Architecture homes: either retire the Page Intelligence tab's legacy SiteArchitecture mount in favor of the Links lens, or extract one shared component.
extensions.value: Eliminates a drifting duplicate (they already disagree on orphans/depth-warning/schema tooltips) per the rebuild's delete-the-predecessor rule.

surfaceAudits.surface: local-presence (Page: local-seo) — rebuilt src/components/local-presence-rebuilt/ vs legacy src/components/local-seo/ + src/components/google-business-profile/
surfaceAudits.headline: The two-mode rebuild is a real structural upgrade (desk-based review workflow, manual drafting, map-pack share-of-voice, URL-persisted state), but it silently dropped the GBP connection remediation links, the competitor review benchmark, and the authenticated-review triage readout, and it mislabels the scraped public GBP aggregate as "Authenticated" — none of which the parity contract records as exceptions.

functionalityLost.capability: GBP connection remediation navigation: legacy 'Open connection' (→ workspace-settings?tab=connections) and 'Open mappings' (→ brand?tab=business-footprint) buttons on the mapping status block. The rebuilt Reviews & replies mode shows 'Disconnected' / 'Reconnect needed' badges and 'Connect Google Business Profile first' empty states but offers NO navigation to actually connect or remap — grep confirms zero 'workspace-settings' references in the rebuilt dir.
functionalityLost.evidence: Legacy: src/components/google-business-profile/GbpMappingStatusBlock.tsx:33-50 (both buttons), mounted at src/components/local-seo/LocalPresencePage.tsx:217-219 (reviews tab) and 181-183 (setup tab). Rebuilt: src/components/local-presence-rebuilt/LocalPresenceReviewsPipeline.tsx:162-192 (status-only GroupBlock), 264-269 and 510-517 (dead-end 'Connect... first' empty states); `grep -rn 'workspace-settings' src/components/local-presence-rebuilt/` returns nothing. Contract line 88 protects 'connection/mapping status' but never mentions the remediation nav.

functionalityLost.capability: GBP competitor review benchmark: legacy showed your listing vs the top competitor's rating/review count and the derived 'Review gap: N behind the leader' line, plus the Overview sentence 'the local leader has M reviews'. The server still returns top-5 competitors by review count, but the rebuilt surface reads only gbp.owned and completenessScore — competitor listing data is fetched and discarded (the map-pack share-of-voice table is a different dataset: SERP local-pack repeat competitors, which carry no rating/review counts).
functionalityLost.evidence: Legacy: src/components/local-seo/GbpReviewsPanel.tsx:140-151 (ListingRow topCompetitor + review gap), src/components/local-seo/LocalPresencePage.tsx:116-121 (leader sentence). Server: server/routes/local-seo.ts:263-267 (top-5 competitors returned). Rebuilt: src/components/local-presence-rebuilt/LocalPresenceOverview.tsx:41 and LocalPresenceVisibility.tsx:81 read only `gbp?.owned`; `grep -rn 'competitors' src/components/local-presence-rebuilt/` matches only copy strings. Contract carry-over (local-seo-contract.md:86) lists 'GBP aggregate card, rating/review count, completeness Meter' — silent on the competitor benchmark.

functionalityLost.capability: Authenticated-review triage readout: legacy showed 'Stored low rating' count (red triage signal), total review count, newest-review date, and a 'Recent review excerpts' list (5 most recent synced reviews with Replied/Unanswered badges). Rebuilt sync block shows only Stored / Unanswered / Average — replied reviews and low-rating triage are invisible; recent review content is only visible if a review is unanswered-and-eligible.
functionalityLost.evidence: Legacy: src/components/google-business-profile/GbpAuthenticatedReviewsPanel.tsx:104-109 (4-stat grid incl. lowRatingCount, totalReviewCount, newestReviewAt) and 136-150 (recent excerpts w/ hasReply badge). Rebuilt: src/components/local-presence-rebuilt/LocalPresenceReviewsPipeline.tsx:245-249 (3 stats only); `grep -rn 'recentReviews|lowRating|totalReviewCount|newestReviewAt' src/components/local-presence-rebuilt/` returns nothing.

functionalityLost.capability: GBP completeness missing-signal detail: legacy rendered the concrete gaps behind the score ('Missing: no photos · no attributes · no category'); rebuilt shows only the MetricRingSvg score with no explanation of what to fix.
functionalityLost.evidence: Legacy: src/components/local-seo/GbpReviewsPanel.tsx:44-68 (CompletenessReadout missing[] derivation). Rebuilt: src/components/local-presence-rebuilt/LocalPresenceOverview.tsx:106-110 (ring only). Contract line 52 lists 'fuller GBP profile checklist fields' as a backend exception, but that refers to prototype fields that don't exist — photos/attributes/category ARE available today (server/routes/local-seo.ts:269-276) and were shown by legacy.
functionalityLost.whereDocumented: Adjacent-but-not-matching: docs/ui-rebuild/parity/local-seo-contract.md:52 (backend exception covers unavailable prototype checklist fields, not the available missing-signals list)

functionalityLost.capability: Track-keyword breadth: legacy offered a Track button for EVERY suggested tracking keyword per repeat competitor (server generates up to 3: '<X> reviews', '<X> vs <workspace>', '<X> <city>'); rebuilt's Track column exposes only the FIRST suggestion — the other suggestions are neither trackable nor visible.
functionalityLost.evidence: Legacy: src/components/local-seo/LocalSeoVisibilityPanel.tsx:114-147 (maps all suggestedTrackingKeywords with per-keyword Track + error). Rebuilt: src/components/local-presence-rebuilt/LocalPresenceVisibility.tsx:181 (`suggestedTrackingKeywords[0]` only). Server: server/domains/local-seo/visibility-read-model.ts:91-97. Contract line 86 protects the 'Track keyword action' generically; the breadth reduction is not recorded.

functionalityLost.capability: Visibility trend delta framing: legacy showed a TrendBadge with the signed visible-count change 'vs start' plus the first→latest date range per market; rebuilt shows a sparkline plus only the latest point ('N/M visible on <date>') — direction/magnitude of change must be eyeballed from the sparkline.
functionalityLost.evidence: Legacy: src/components/local-seo/LocalSeoVisibilityTrend.tsx:74-93 (delta + TrendBadge + date range). Rebuilt: src/components/local-presence-rebuilt/LocalPresenceVisibility.tsx:229-241 (latest point only).

functionalityLost.capability: Full review text while drafting: legacy response cards preferred `commentText ?? commentExcerpt` (full review body when synced); rebuilt shows only `commentExcerpt` with line-clamp in both the eligible-review draft cards and the response table, so operators draft public replies to truncated review text.
functionalityLost.evidence: Legacy: src/components/google-business-profile/GbpReviewResponsesPanel.tsx:123,154. Rebuilt: src/components/local-presence-rebuilt/LocalPresenceReviewsPipeline.tsx:377,568 (commentExcerpt only).

regressions.issue: Data-source mislabel: the Rank & profile 'Reviews' mini card is subtitled 'Authenticated profile aggregate', but its data comes from useGbpReviews → /api/local-seo/:id/gbp-reviews, which is the PUBLIC scraped listings aggregate (DataForSEO business_listings, local-gbp flag) — explicitly not the authenticated GBP connection. Legacy honestly labeled the same data 'GBP aggregate'. An operator (or a client on a screenshare) is told scraped data is authenticated first-party data — a trust landmine by the platform's own attribution-honesty standard.
regressions.evidence: Rebuilt: src/components/local-presence-rebuilt/LocalPresenceVisibility.tsx:258 (subtitle) fed by LocalPresenceSurface.tsx:70,285 (useGbpReviews). Server: server/routes/local-seo.ts:252-256 ('Aggregates ONLY... Never returns per-review/author data'). Legacy label: src/components/local-seo/LocalPresencePage.tsx:86 ('GBP aggregate').

regressions.issue: One shared `?search=` URL param filters BOTH the Rank & profile competitor table and the Reviews & replies pipeline (eligible reviews + response rows). A search typed against competitors persists across the mode switch and silently filters reviews (and vice versa), including hiding draft-eligible reviews.
regressions.evidence: src/components/local-presence-rebuilt/useLocalPresenceSurfaceState.ts:90-98,120-125 (single 'search' param); consumers: LocalPresenceVisibility.tsx:101,274 and LocalPresenceReviewsPipeline.tsx:334,338; the header SearchField only renders in reviews mode (LocalPresenceSurface.tsx:253-260) while presence mode uses the in-card field — same state.

regressions.issue: Disabled 'Re-scan' gives no reason: legacy's Refresh button passed report.setupDetail as a title tooltip when refresh was blocked (no active market / non-local posture); the rebuilt header Re-scan is just disabled with no explanation, so an unconfigured workspace shows a dead button.
regressions.evidence: Rebuilt: src/components/local-presence-rebuilt/LocalPresenceSurface.tsx:228-231 (no title). Legacy: src/components/local-seo/LocalSeoVisibilityPanel.tsx:407-417 (title={!canRefresh ? report.setupDetail : ...}).

regressions.issue: One-click 'Publish' now appears on responses that are awaiting client approval (legacy hid publish for awaiting_client; rebuilt canApproveAndPublishReviewResponse includes it). The server legally allows awaiting_client→approved, so this is an intentional-looking admin override — but there is no ConfirmDialog, so a single misclick publishes a reply to Google while the client is still reviewing it.
regressions.evidence: Rebuilt: src/components/local-presence-rebuilt/localPresenceReviewStatus.ts:101-105 and LocalPresenceReviewsPipeline.tsx:461-474. Legacy: src/components/google-business-profile/GbpReviewResponsesPanel.tsx:147 (canPublish = ['draft','approved']). Server: server/state-machines.ts:184-193 (awaiting_client → approved legal).

confusions.issue: Competitor filter chips 'Configured' and 'No data' read as market-setup filters but actually filter competitor rows ('Configured' = competitor appeared in ≥1 named market; 'No data' = no share-of-voice %). An operator will click 'Configured' expecting their configured markets, not competitors-with-market-attribution.
confusions.why: Labels borrow setup vocabulary for a competitor-attribution concept; nothing on screen explains the semantics (src/components/local-presence-rebuilt/useLocalPresenceSurfaceState.ts:15-20, LocalPresenceVisibility.tsx:105-108).

confusions.issue: Two different 'review counts' with no reconciliation: Rank & profile shows the scraped aggregate ('Reviews: 128'), Reviews & replies shows the authenticated synced subset ('Stored: 43'). Legacy sub-labels ('GBP aggregate' vs 'synced review subset') explained the split; the rebuilt's mislabeled 'Authenticated profile aggregate' subtitle makes the numbers look contradictory.
confusions.why: An operator preparing a client call sees two conflicting totals for the same business and can't tell which is truth (LocalPresenceOverview.tsx:56-58 vs LocalPresenceReviewsPipeline.tsx:245-249).

confusions.issue: Hero 'profile name' falls back to the primary market label when no GBP listing is captured — the page renders 'Austin, TX' styled as the business/profile title with a big avatar initial 'A'.
confusions.why: A market name presented in the profile-identity slot reads as a wrong business name to anyone who knows the client (src/components/local-presence-rebuilt/LocalPresenceOverview.tsx:42-43,64-70).

confusions.issue: The 9 status-census chips in the review pipeline look like filters (they sit directly above the desk-filtered table and carry counts) but are display-only; the actual filter is the Segmented desk control whose buckets ('On your desk' etc.) don't map 1:1 to the chips — declined/cancelled live only under 'All'.
confusions.why: Operators will click the chips to filter and conclude the UI is broken; finding a declined response requires knowing it's hidden in 'All' (LocalPresenceReviewsPipeline.tsx:542-556, localPresenceReviewStatus.ts:47-52,77-82).

overcomplication.issue: Dead code shipped with the surface: LocalPresenceSetup.tsx (78 lines, a full setup panel with MetricTiles and GroupBlock) is imported nowhere — the drawer replaced it but the file was left behind; useLocalPresenceSurfaceState also exports an unused clearFilters and unused lens labels.
overcomplication.evidence: grep -rn 'LocalPresenceSetup' src/ tests/ matches only the file itself; clearFilters is referenced only inside useLocalPresenceSurfaceState.ts:85,142,157; LOCAL_PRESENCE_LENSES labels (useLocalPresenceSurfaceState.ts:6-11) are never rendered.
overcomplication.suggestion: Delete LocalPresenceSetup.tsx and the unused hook surface (clearFilters, lens labels) per the rebuild's own flag-retirement/orphan discipline.

overcomplication.issue: Three overlapping narrowing mechanisms in the review pipeline (desk Segmented + 9-chip status census + shared search) for what is typically a single-digit list of responses, while the chips themselves do nothing.
overcomplication.evidence: LocalPresenceReviewsPipeline.tsx:531-556 renders both the Segmented and the census grid; deskIncludesStatus (localPresenceReviewStatus.ts:85-88) is the only real filter.
overcomplication.suggestion: Make the census chips clickable (set desk / a status filter) or collapse them into the Segmented's labels with counts — one mechanism, not three.

overcomplication.issue: Eligible-review draft cards silently cap at 6 with no overflow indicator, and each card carries two always-visible textareas (draft + optional client note), making the pre-pipeline area tall before any work has started.
overcomplication.evidence: LocalPresenceReviewsPipeline.tsx:560 (slice(0,6), no 'N more' affordance), 572-588 (two stacked FormTextareas per card).
overcomplication.suggestion: Show an 'N more unanswered' count and collapse the note field behind a 'Add client note' toggle.

gains: Map-pack share-of-voice percentage is surfaced for the first time — the server computed mapPackShareOfVoicePct all along but no legacy UI rendered it (grep: shared/types/local-seo.ts:550 was the only non-rebuilt reference); the rebuilt DataTable adds sortable Share/Appearances/Losses columns plus search and filters (LocalPresenceVisibility.tsx:144-198,272-291) where legacy was an unsortable card list.

Manual review-response drafting from the surface: 'Save manual' and 'Draft and send' (with optional client note) against real pre-existing endpoints (server/routes/google-business-profile.ts:336,370) — legacy UI offered only the AI 'Draft reply' (GbpReviewResponsesPanel.tsx:126-134).

Desk-based review workflow model (On your desk / With client / Published / All) with per-status counts and plain-language status descriptions (localPresenceReviewStatus.ts:28-88) — legacy was one undifferentiated response list.

URL-persisted, deep-linkable state: ?lens= (+legacy ?tab= aliases incl. drawer-opening ?tab=setup), ?filter=, ?desk=, debounced ?search= (useLocalPresenceSurfaceState.ts) — legacy persisted only ?tab=.

Plan-locked (402/403) ErrorState, stale-summary InlineBanner with retry on background refetch failure, and toast feedback on every mutation (LocalPresenceSurface.tsx:152-167,263-272; mutations throughout) — legacy had silent or inline-only failure modes.
GBP refresh button reflects the actual background job (findActiveJob on LOCAL_GBP_REFRESH), not just the request in flight — legacy GbpReviewsPanel.tsx:85-92 only used isPending.

Admin can approve-and-publish a response that is awaiting client (server-legal override, state-machines.ts:184-189), closing stuck approvals without asking the client to act — legacy offered no UI path for this (also flagged as a confirm-dialog risk under regressions).

Surface-scoped WS invalidation for all five local/GBP events including workspaceDeliverables invalidation on response mutations (LocalPresenceSurface.tsx:115-126, localPresenceReviewActions.ts:26-30).
'Last scanned <date>' freshness readout in the page eyebrow (LocalPresenceSurface.tsx:58-63,222).

extensions.opportunity: Restore remediation navigation in the reviews pipeline: add 'Open connection' (workspace-settings?tab=connections) to the Disconnected/Reconnect states and 'Open mappings' to the map-a-location empty state.
extensions.value: Turns three current dead-end states back into actionable ones; directly fixes the biggest undocumented loss.

extensions.opportunity: Render the fetched-and-discarded gbp.competitors benchmark (top competitor rating/review count + review gap) — e.g. a second row in the Reviews mini card or a column in the share-of-voice band — and fix the 'Authenticated profile aggregate' subtitle to honest 'Public GBP aggregate' wording.
extensions.value: Restores the Decision Engine P7 competitive readout and removes the trust landmine in one pass; data is already in the response.

extensions.opportunity: Add low-rating count and a recent-reviews excerpt list (with Replied/Unanswered) back to the Authenticated review sync block, and prefer commentText over commentExcerpt in the response workflow.
extensions.value: Restores review triage and lets operators read the full review they are publicly replying to.

extensions.opportunity: Make the status-census chips set the desk/status filter, and expose all suggested tracking keywords per competitor (small popover on the Track cell).
extensions.value: Converts two decorative/degraded affordances into the interactions operators already expect.

extensions.opportunity: Add a ConfirmDialog to 'Publish' when the response status is awaiting_client, framing it as an explicit client-approval override.
extensions.value: Keeps the new server-legal override while protecting against one-click publishes of unapproved replies to Google.

surfaceAudits.headline: The rebuilt Performance surface is a genuine upgrade over legacy on nearly every daily path (URL-persisted lens state, Top-N bulk, snapshot-based strategy switching, real Asset Manager navigation, stale-data recovery), but it silently dropped legacy's empty-bulk-result guard: a rate-limited PageSpeed run now fires a success toast and renders a fabricated red 0 "average score" — the exact fabrication the contract claims cannot happen.

functionalityLost.capability: Empty bulk-result guard with actionable rate-limit guidance. Legacy explicitly detected a 200 bulk response with pages.length === 0 and surfaced 'No pages could be tested. The Google PageSpeed API may be rate-limited. Add a GOOGLE_PSI_KEY env variable for higher limits.' plus a Try Again reset; it also rendered any persisted empty snapshot as an honest 'No results available' empty state. The rebuilt lens has no pages-length check anywhere: a 200-empty result (which runSiteSpeed returns when PSI is rate-limited or no baseUrl resolves, and which the route persists unconditionally) is rendered as a real result.
functionalityLost.evidence: Legacy guard: src/components/PageSpeedPanel.tsx:185 (fresh run) and :432 (persisted empty snapshot -> 'No results available'). Rebuilt: src/components/performance-rebuilt/PageSpeedLens.tsx:692-702 renders ScoreSummary + DataTable for any truthy currentBulkResult with no pages.length check; the PSI-hint helper (performanceFormatters.ts:121-123) only fires on thrown errors, never on 200-empty. Server 200-empty source: server/pagespeed.ts runSiteSpeed (returns pages: [] on missing baseUrl / all-failed PSI calls) + server/routes/webflow-pagespeed.ts:39-42 (saves and 200s it unconditionally).
functionalityLost.whereDocumented: Not documented — docs/ui-rebuild/parity/performance-contract.md:91 claims the opposite ('No PageSpeed data or row-level fixes are fabricated'), and the Carry-Over Homes list (contract lines 64-70) omits the empty-result guard entirely.

functionalityLost.capability: Sticky Page Weight toolbar. Legacy pinned the search/source-filter/Re-scan controls to the top of the viewport (sticky, z-sticky, backdrop-blur) so they stayed reachable while scrolling long page lists; the rebuilt Toolbar scrolls away with the content.
functionalityLost.evidence: Legacy: src/components/PageWeight.tsx:171 (sticky top-0 z-[var(--z-sticky)]). Rebuilt: src/components/performance-rebuilt/PageWeightLens.tsx:241 (plain Toolbar, no sticky positioning; no 'sticky' anywhere in performance-rebuilt/).

functionalityLost.capability: Inline row expansion for Page Weight asset breakdown and bulk PageSpeed per-page detail (legacy expanded in place; rebuilt moves both to Drawers).
functionalityLost.evidence: Legacy inline expansion: src/components/PageWeight.tsx:208-246 (toggleExpand + inline asset rows), src/components/PageSpeedPanel.tsx:511-605 (expandedPage inline vitals/opportunities/diagnostics). Rebuilt Drawers: src/components/performance-rebuilt/PageWeightLens.tsx:344-372, PageSpeedLens.tsx:732-745.
functionalityLost.whereDocumented: docs/ui-rebuild/parity/performance-contract.md:43 (owner choice: Drawer over inline expansion) and parity-backlog.md:103-106 ('Drawer detail ... remain explicit exceptions'); rebuilt-surface-inventory.md row 18.

functionalityLost.capability: Row-level 'Fix' action per PageSpeed opportunity (prototype capability; neither era ships it — recorded here only because the contract tracks it).
functionalityLost.evidence: Legacy has no per-opportunity action (src/components/PageSpeedPanel.tsx:279-293 renders static rows); rebuilt likewise renders read-only opportunity rows (PageSpeedLens.tsx:294-329) with only the surface-level Asset Manager banner (PageSpeedLens.tsx:714-730).
functionalityLost.whereDocumented: docs/ui-rebuild/parity/performance-contract.md:45 — deferred until Media/Asset Manager parity confirms the write target.

regressions.issue: A failed/rate-limited bulk PageSpeed run is reported as SUCCESS and rendered as fabricated data: the mutation's onSuccess fires a '{Mobile|Desktop} PageSpeed bulk test complete' success toast, writes the empty SiteSpeedResult into the strategy snapshot cache, and the lens renders a red MetricRing score of 0 labelled '{Strategy} average' with a 'Lab test' badge and six 'Unavailable' vitals above an empty table. Because the server also persists the empty snapshot, this red-zero average survives reloads until a later run succeeds. Legacy treated the identical payload as an explicit error with the GOOGLE_PSI_KEY remediation hint. This is the FM-2 failure mode (external API error recorded as success) and directly contradicts the surface's own no-fabrication contract.
regressions.evidence: Rebuilt: src/components/performance-rebuilt/PageSpeedLens.tsx:441-447 (unconditional success toast), src/hooks/admin/useAdminPerformance.ts:192-199 (onSuccess caches empty result as the snapshot), PageSpeedLens.tsx:692-702 + 263-291 (ScoreSummary renders averageScore 0 / all-null vitals with no pages.length guard). Legacy comparator: src/components/PageSpeedPanel.tsx:185 and :432. Server: server/routes/webflow-pagespeed.ts:39-42 persists empty results; server/pagespeed.ts runSiteSpeed returns pages: [] when PSI calls all fail.

regressions.issue: During a Page Weight re-scan with existing data on screen there is no persistent progress indicator — only a transient toast and a disabled Re-scan button for a 30-60 second operation. The 'Scanning page weight' banner is gated on !data, so it never shows on re-scans. Legacy replaced the view with an explicit spinner + 'This may take 30–60 seconds' copy (destructive, but unambiguous).
regressions.evidence: Rebuilt: src/components/performance-rebuilt/PageWeightLens.tsx:204 (banner only when scan.isPending && !data), :257 (disabled button is the only persistent signal), :113 (toast fires once at start). Legacy: src/components/PageWeight.tsx:101-108 (full loading state on every run).

confusions.issue: The strategy Segmented (Mobile/Desktop) means two different things depending on mode: in Bulk it switches which dataset/snapshot is shown; in Single it only switches which evidence block (opportunities/diagnostics) renders below the always-visible paired Mobile+Desktop cards. An operator who clicks 'Desktop' in Single mode sees the two result cards unchanged and may conclude the toggle is broken.
confusions.why: Same control, same position, different effect per mode — the single-mode effect is below the fold and can render as just an info banner ('No desktop evidence for X').

confusions.issue: In Single mode the SearchField ('Search Webflow pages') only filters the adjacent FormSelect's option list — it does not filter any visible results. If the search excludes the currently selected page, the select's value is no longer among its options while the 'Selected page results' section keeps showing the old page.
confusions.why: A search box above results conventionally filters the results; legacy avoided this by using one combined SearchableSelect (src/components/PageSpeedPanel.tsx:353-360 vs rebuilt PageSpeedLens.tsx:432-436, 575-591).

confusions.issue: Page Weight rows and the detail Drawer title display the raw storage keys ('page:Home', 'cms:Blog Posts', 'css:styles') even though the Drawer already derives a clean Source tile from the same prefix. (Parity with legacy — both show raw keys — but fresh eyes trip on it, and the rebuilt has the machinery to fix it.)
confusions.why: Internal key prefixes read as jargon/bugs to an operator; pageSource()/pageSourceLabel() (performanceFormatters.ts:23-33) already parse the prefix but the label is never stripped (PageWeightLens.tsx:285, 347).

confusions.issue: Verb drift on the same action: the first-run empty state's CTA says 'Run Audit' (both lenses), the toolbar says 'Re-scan', and toasts say 'scan'/'test'. 'Audit' collides with the separate Site Audit surface's vocabulary.
confusions.why: An operator scanning nav sees a 'Site Audit' page and a 'Run Audit' button on Performance and may expect them to be the same operation (PageWeightLens.tsx:195-198 vs :257-260; PageSpeedLens.tsx:710).

overcomplication.issue: Single-page test selection spans two stacked toolbars and three controls (mode/strategy Toolbar, then SearchField + FormSelect + two Test buttons) where legacy did the same job with one SearchableSelect and two buttons.
overcomplication.evidence: Rebuilt: PageSpeedLens.tsx:557-600 (two Toolbars). Legacy: PageSpeedPanel.tsx:351-378 (one SearchableSelect).
overcomplication.suggestion: Replace the SearchField+FormSelect pair with a single searchable combobox primitive (the legacy SearchableSelect pattern DS-ified), collapsing to one toolbar row.

overcomplication.issue: Bulk mode wraps a two-control Toolbar (page-count select + two buttons) inside a titled SectionCard ('Bulk PageSpeed scan' + subtitle), adding a card layer of chrome around what is functionally one control row.
overcomplication.suggestion: Render the bulk controls as a plain Toolbar like Single mode; move the one-line explanation into the Toolbar as caption text.

gains: Lens state is URL-persisted and shareable: switching lenses writes/clears ?tab= (usePerformanceSurfaceState.ts:24-34) with a live receiver and invalid-value fallback; legacy read ?tab= once at mount and never wrote it (Performance.tsx:12-15).
Bulk test size is selectable Top 3/5/10/25 (PageSpeedLens.tsx:94-99, server-capped at 25 in webflow-pagespeed.ts:19) vs legacy's hard-coded 3 (PageSpeedPanel.tsx:181).

Strategy switching in Bulk loads the saved per-strategy snapshot instead of immediately firing a fresh paid PSI run, as legacy's Mobile/Desktop toggle did (PageSpeedLens.tsx:420-421,566 vs PageSpeedPanel.tsx:462-480) — real cost control.

Single mode shows truthful paired Mobile AND Desktop cards for the selected page and recalls saved per-page results out of the persisted bulk snapshots by title/path match (PageSpeedLens.tsx:144-147,426-427); legacy showed one strategy at a time and lost the result on any navigation.

Asset Manager handoff is now an actual navigation to the canonical ?filter=oversized receiver from BOTH lenses (PageWeightLens.tsx:331-340, PageSpeedLens.tsx:714-730; receiver verified at useAssetManagerSurfaceState.ts:64 + AssetManagerSurface.tsx:137); legacy Page Weight had a text-only tip with no link (PageWeight.tsx:251-257) and legacy PageSpeed had no handoff at all.
'Last scanned' timestamps for page-weight and per-strategy PageSpeed snapshots (PageWeightLens.tsx:256, PageSpeedLens.tsx:568-570); legacy showed no snapshot age for page weight.

Failure recovery keeps data: a failed re-scan/re-test over existing data shows a stale-data warning banner with Retry instead of destroying the view into an error screen (PageWeightLens.tsx:263-270, PageSpeedLens.tsx:634-643); snapshot read failures get real ErrorStates with Retry instead of legacy's silent console.error (PageWeight.tsx:82, PageSpeedPanel.tsx:149,168).
Bulk results table adds per-page provenance (Field data/Lab test), opportunity counts, and sortable Score/LCP/CLS/INP columns (PageSpeedLens.tsx:476-549); legacy rows showed three vitals unsorted.
Page-weight bar scaling computes the true max instead of trusting server sort order (PageWeightLens.tsx:97 vs PageWeight.tsx:129 pages[0]).

React Query migration with keyed cache + invalidation on mutations (useAdminPerformance.ts:100-201) replaces legacy's hand-rolled useState/useEffect fetch pattern with in-flight-guard refs (PageSpeedPanel.tsx:137-170).

extensions.opportunity: Guard 200-empty bulk results: when result.pages.length === 0, treat it as a failed run (error toast, do not cache/persist as snapshot, show the GOOGLE_PSI_KEY rate-limit hint) — restores the legacy guard and fixes the fabricated red-zero average.
extensions.value: Removes the surface's single trust landmine and re-aligns with the contract's no-fabrication guarantee.

extensions.opportunity: Consume the Site Audit perf-issue handoff: useSiteAuditRebuilt.ts:378-380 already navigates to performance with state.fixContext (targetRoute 'performance', pageSlug/pageName) but nothing in performance-rebuilt reads it — dead in legacy too. Auto-select the flagged page in the PageSpeed lens or prefill the Page Weight search.
extensions.value: Turns a dead cross-surface payload into the detect→fix loop the contract describes; operator lands on the exact page the audit flagged instead of the default Page Weight list.

extensions.opportunity: Expose the already-persisted single-page PageSpeed results: server/performance-store.ts:342 getSinglePageSpeed has no GET route and no reader, so single tests vanish on reload despite being saved on every run (webflow-pagespeed.ts:80).
extensions.value: Single-page tests (the default mode) survive reloads and lens switches without re-spending PSI quota.

extensions.opportunity: Strip the page:/cms:/css: key prefixes from row and Drawer titles and show the already-computed source as a badge instead.
extensions.value: Removes internal jargon from the highest-traffic list on the surface; the parsing helpers already exist (performanceFormatters.ts:23-33).

extensions.opportunity: Make the Page Weight Toolbar sticky (restore legacy behavior) and add a persistent scanning banner during re-scans with data on screen.
extensions.value: Long page lists keep controls reachable; 30-60s re-scans stop looking dead.

extensions.opportunity: Persist PageSpeed mode/strategy/selected page in the URL (?tab=speed&mode=bulk&strategy=desktop&page=...) alongside the existing lens param.
extensions.value: Shareable/refresh-safe deep links into a specific test view, matching the deep-link discipline the rest of the rebuild follows.

extensions.opportunity: Broadcast page-weight/PageSpeed scan completion via broadcastToWorkspace + useWorkspaceEvents invalidation (neither era has it; scans mutate workspace-visible data and feed the intelligence cache).
extensions.value: Multi-admin sessions and the client-facing views see fresh scan data without manual refetch, per the mandatory data-flow rules.

surfaceAudits.headline: The rebuilt SEO Editor's unified inline worksheet, URL state, and cross-source selection toolbar are a genuine structural upgrade over the legacy stacked editors, but it silently drops real CMS editing depth (extra SEO fields, slug, per-item approval history, per-field AI rewrite) and ships two trust regressions (mismatched missing-metadata counts vs fix scope, invisible CMS send errors) that the parity contract does not record.

functionalityLost.capability: Editing ALL extra SEO fields on a CMS item. Legacy renders an input (with per-field AI rewrite) for every field in getExtraSeoFields(coll.seoFields) — e.g. OG title, search snippet, any additional SEO text field a collection defines. Rebuilt edits only the first title-like and first desc-like field (titleFieldSlug/descriptionFieldSlug from the resolver); other configured SEO fields are neither visible nor editable anywhere.
functionalityLost.evidence: Legacy: src/components/cms-editor/CmsEditorCollections.tsx:285-346 (extraSeoFields.map rendering input + AI per field); resolver maps only two slugs: src/components/editor/seoWriteTargetResolver.ts:77-102. Rebuilt drawer renders only cmsFields.titleField/descField: src/components/seo-editor-rebuilt/SeoEditorPagePanel.tsx:187-190,470-489; worksheet edits only titleFieldSlug/descriptionFieldSlug: src/components/seo-editor-rebuilt/SeoEditorWorksheet.tsx:116-127,348-424.
functionalityLost.whereDocumented: Contract carry-over line 114 claims 'CMS item field edit' is preserved (docs/ui-rebuild/parity/seo-editor-contract.md:114) — it is only partially preserved; no exception is recorded.

functionalityLost.capability: CMS item slug editing. Legacy exposes a writable Slug input saved through PATCH /api/webflow/collections/:cid/items/:iid fieldData; rebuilt shows slug as a read-only KeyValueRow only.
functionalityLost.evidence: Legacy: src/components/cms-editor/CmsEditorCollections.tsx:275-283 (updateField(item.id,'slug',…)) persisted via src/components/cms-editor/useCmsEditorSaveWorkflow.ts:41-44. Rebuilt: src/components/seo-editor-rebuilt/SeoEditorPagePanel.tsx:511 (KeyValueRow label="Slug", no input).
functionalityLost.whereDocumented: docs/ui-rebuild/parity/seo-editor-contract.md:80 ('H1 and slug stay read-only because no writable production field exists') — but that premise is factually wrong for CMS items: a writable production path exists and legacy used it. The exception is recorded on an inaccurate claim.

functionalityLost.capability: Per-item CMS approval history. Legacy shows, inside each expanded CMS item, the latest approval batch (name, status tone, current→proposed value diff, timestamp) plus an expandable history of all prior approval items for that item (buildItemApprovalMap over cmsData.approvalBatches). Rebuilt never consumes approvalBatches; the drawer has no history, so an operator cannot see what was already sent/approved/rejected for an item before re-sending.
functionalityLost.evidence: Legacy: src/components/CmsEditor.tsx:40,94-96 + src/components/cms-editor/CmsEditorCollections.tsx:412-467; map built in src/components/cms-editor/cmsEditorModel.ts:104-118. Rebuilt: grep for approvalBatches/buildItemApprovalMap in src/components/seo-editor-rebuilt/ and src/hooks/admin/useSeoEditorRebuilt.ts returns nothing; drawer CMS branch (SeoEditorPagePanel.tsx:434-544) has no history section.

functionalityLost.capability: Single-field AI rewrite. Legacy: static rows have 'AI' buttons for title-only and description-only (plus 'AI Generate Both'); CMS items have a per-field Wand2 AI rewrite on name and every extra SEO field. Rebuilt exposes only the paired 'Generate pair' (both) per row — staticWorkflow.aiRewrite(field) and cmsWorkflow.aiRewrite(single field) exist in the workflow state but have zero UI callsites for single fields.
functionalityLost.evidence: Legacy: src/components/editor/PageEditRow.tsx:279-291 (title AI), 328-340 (description AI); src/components/cms-editor/CmsEditorCollections.tsx:236-245,295-305 (per-field Wand2). Rebuilt: only aiRewrite(...,'both') at src/components/seo-editor-rebuilt/SeoEditorPagePanel.tsx:364 and aiRewriteBoth at :447; grep confirms no other aiRewrite callsite.

functionalityLost.capability: Full pattern/bulk-preview diff review. Legacy 'Preview Changes' panel lists EVERY affected page with old value (struck through) → new value + length, scrollable, with Apply All and an explicit Cancel that clears the preview. Rebuilt shows an InlineBanner with only the first 3 changes compressed to 'old -> new' text and no cancel/clear control before 'Apply preview' writes to live Webflow pages.
functionalityLost.evidence: Legacy: src/components/editor/BulkOperations.tsx:163-209. Rebuilt: src/components/seo-editor-rebuilt/SeoEditorSurface.tsx:516-520 (slice(0,3) banner), Apply at :503-513 with no cancel path.

functionalityLost.capability: Static bulk AI rewrite scoped to titles-only or descriptions-only. Legacy selection actions offer 'AI Rewrite Both', 'Titles Only', 'Descriptions Only'; rebuilt selected toolbar offers only 'Rewrite static' (both). (CMS kept its granular Names/Titles/Descriptions/All menu.)
functionalityLost.evidence: Legacy: src/components/editor/BulkOperations.tsx:68-95. Rebuilt: src/components/seo-editor-rebuilt/SeoEditorWorksheet.tsx:489-493 (single bulkAiRewrite('both') button); CMS menu at :501-515.

functionalityLost.capability: Reset ALL page tracking (POST /api/workspaces/:id/page-states/clear). Legacy tracking summary offers 'reset all' / 'Reset page tracking'; rebuilt only has per-page 'Clear tracking' in the drawer footer.
functionalityLost.evidence: Legacy: src/components/SeoEditor.tsx:146-150 + src/components/editor/SeoEditorTrackingSummary.tsx:26-36,53-62. Rebuilt: grep 'page-states/clear|resetAllTracking' in seo-editor-rebuilt/ returns nothing; per-page clear only at SeoEditorPagePanel.tsx:226-234.

functionalityLost.capability: Tracking-status breakdown (live / rejected / issue-detected / fix-proposed counts with status badges). Legacy shows the full page-edit-state summary; rebuilt header summary shows only targets/unsaved/in review/approved/avg score/need work.
functionalityLost.evidence: Legacy: src/components/editor/SeoEditorTrackingSummary.tsx:38-63 and src/components/cms-editor/CmsEditorShellPanels.tsx:158-168. Rebuilt: src/components/seo-editor-rebuilt/SeoEditorSurface.tsx:260-267 (summary string).

functionalityLost.capability: Facebook/social preview for CMS items (legacy shows SerpPreview + SocialPreview per item; rebuilt CMS drawer has SerpPreview only — static kept both).
functionalityLost.evidence: Legacy: src/components/cms-editor/CmsEditorCollections.tsx:483-505. Rebuilt: src/components/seo-editor-rebuilt/SeoEditorPagePanel.tsx:532-541 (SerpPreview only in CMS branch; static branch has both at :426-427).

functionalityLost.capability: Recommendation context fields: 'N clicks at risk' (trafficAtRisk), 'Potential: …' (estimatedGain), and Now/Soon/Later priority chips on metadata recommendations. Rebuilt drawer renders only title + insight in an InlineBanner.
functionalityLost.evidence: Legacy: src/components/editor/PageEditRow.tsx:157-183. Rebuilt: src/components/seo-editor-rebuilt/SeoEditorPagePanel.tsx:586-590.

functionalityLost.capability: Per-collection publish affordance with saved-count ('Publish N') visible on the collection header whenever any items are saved. Rebuilt publish-collection is only reachable inside an item's drawer and is disabled unless THAT item is saved, even when other items in the collection have saved drafts waiting to publish.
functionalityLost.evidence: Legacy: src/components/cms-editor/CmsEditorCollections.tsx:158-166 (publish whenever collSavedIds.length>0). Rebuilt: src/components/seo-editor-rebuilt/SeoEditorPagePanel.tsx:249-258 (disabled={!cmsWorkflow.saved.has(cmsTarget.itemId)}).

functionalityLost.capability: Inline client note at the primary CMS send point. Legacy places an optional note input directly beside 'Send to client (N)'; rebuilt's selected-toolbar 'Send CMS to client' sends with no note — the note exists only inside a single item's drawer.
functionalityLost.evidence: Legacy: src/components/cms-editor/CmsEditorShellPanels.tsx:100-123. Rebuilt: src/components/seo-editor-rebuilt/SeoEditorWorksheet.tsx:497-500 (sendForApproval() with no note); note textarea only at SeoEditorPagePanel.tsx:513-524.

regressions.issue: Missing-metadata banner counts include CMS rows but its 'Fix titles'/'Fix descriptions' actions only draft STATIC pages, so the displayed count and the action's scope disagree (legacy header badges counted writable static pages only). An operator reading '23 missing titles … Draft them in one pass' gets fixes for only the static subset with no explanation.
regressions.evidence: Counts from editableRows (static+CMS): src/components/seo-editor-rebuilt/SeoEditorSurface.tsx:154-160,268-269,354-391. Action scope: workflows built with staticPages only — useSeoEditorRebuilt.ts:288-302 (useSeoEditorBulkWorkflow({pages: staticPages})) → handleBulkFix filters pages (static) at src/components/editor/useSeoEditorBulkWorkflow.ts:208-247. Legacy static-only counts: src/components/SeoEditor.tsx:78 + useSeoEditorBulkWorkflow.ts:416-417 fed to SeoEditorHeaderActions.tsx:50-59.

regressions.issue: CMS 'Send to client' from the selected-rows toolbar fails silently. useCmsEditorApprovalWorkflow reports failures via approvalError (validation 'No changes detected…' or network), but the rebuilt surface renders approvalError ONLY inside the CMS drawer's Collection target box — when sending from the toolbar with no drawer open, the button just stops spinning, the selection stays, and nothing tells the operator the batch was not sent. Legacy rendered an ErrorState next to the send button.
regressions.evidence: Error set: src/components/cms-editor/useCmsEditorApprovalWorkflow.ts:62-91. Rebuilt render site (drawer only): src/components/seo-editor-rebuilt/SeoEditorPagePanel.tsx:525-529; toolbar send with no feedback: SeoEditorWorksheet.tsx:495-500. Legacy visible ErrorState: src/components/cms-editor/CmsEditorShellPanels.tsx:134-140.

regressions.issue: Static pattern-apply controls silently no-op without a selection. previewPattern operates on approvalSelected, but the rebuilt 'Static bulk actions' pattern toolbar is far from the selection toolbar and gives no hint selection is required — 'Preview pattern' with nothing selected produces an empty preview and no message. Legacy only revealed Pattern Apply after selection ('Pattern Apply — N pages').
regressions.evidence: Rebuilt: src/components/seo-editor-rebuilt/SeoEditorSurface.tsx:476-514 (always-rendered pattern controls); previewPattern reads approvalSelected: src/components/editor/useSeoEditorBulkWorkflow.ts:249-260. Legacy gating: src/components/editor/BulkOperations.tsx:56-96,100-102.

regressions.issue: Drawer CMS footer 'Add to approval set' can add but never remove: it toggles only when the item is NOT selected, then relabels to 'In approval set' while remaining an enabled button that does nothing on click.
regressions.evidence: src/components/seo-editor-rebuilt/SeoEditorPagePanel.tsx:259-269 (guarded toggle). Legacy checkbox was a true toggle: src/components/cms-editor/CmsEditorCollections.tsx:196-205.

regressions.issue: 'Analyze remaining' shows no remaining count and stays enabled when every page is analyzed — clicking is a silent no-op (analyzeAllPages returns early). Legacy disabled the button and labeled it 'All Pages Analyzed' / 'Analyze Remaining (N)' with an 'N/M pages have analysis on file' indicator.
regressions.evidence: Rebuilt: src/components/seo-editor-rebuilt/SeoEditorSurface.tsx:461-473 (disabled only while job running); early return: src/components/editor/useSeoEditorBulkWorkflow.ts:184-186. Legacy: src/components/editor/SeoEditorTableControls.tsx:50-70 + src/components/SeoEditor.tsx:276.

confusions.issue: The collapsed-by-default 'Production tools' GroupBlock hides three workflow-critical panels: the 'Sent to client' pending-approvals panel (remind/retract), the AI suggestions panel (itself a second collapsed GroupBlock — double-collapse), and the static bulk/pattern tools. After a bulk rewrite, the completion message says 'review in the suggestions panel' but the operator must expand two collapsed groups to find it; pending client batches needing reminders are invisible by default (legacy rendered PendingApprovals at top level).
confusions.why: An operator lands on the worksheet, sees no sign of outstanding client approvals or freshly generated suggestions, and either misses follow-ups or believes the bulk job produced nothing.

confusions.issue: ?tab=edit|research is parsed, validated, and documented ('?tab=research opens the research lens') but drives nothing — state.tab/setTab/tabOptions have zero callsites in SeoEditorSurface; a ?tab=research URL renders the identical worksheet unless ?page= is also set.
confusions.why: Operators sharing or inspecting URLs (and future devs reading the contract) expect a research mode; the contract line 89 'opens the research lens' overstates what the code does (useSeoEditorSurfaceState.ts:102 vs no usage in SeoEditorSurface.tsx).

confusions.issue: Two differently-scoped CMS send paths: the toolbar 'Send CMS to client' (whole selection, no note) and the drawer's 'Send selected CMS to client' (whole selection, with note) — the drawer button sits inside ONE item's detail panel, strongly implying it sends just that item.
confusions.why: An operator reviewing item X with 12 items selected can unknowingly send all 12, or hunt for where to attach a client note.

confusions.issue: Publish semantics are split and inconsistently gated: topbar 'Publish Site' (site-wide, always visible) vs drawer 'Publish collection' (disabled unless the currently open item has a saved draft, even when sibling items are saved).
confusions.why: After saving drafts on several CMS items, the natural next step (publish the collection) appears disabled unless the operator happens to have a saved item's drawer open — reads as a bug.

confusions.issue: The Score column renders '—' for every CMS and Manual row (optimizationScore only exists for static pages via the strategy join), with no explanation of why two-thirds of rows have no score.
confusions.why: A fresh operator reads en-dash columns as broken data rather than 'scores are a static-page concept here' (useSeoEditorRebuilt.ts:157-172 sets no score for CMS/manual).

overcomplication.issue: Dead tab machinery carried in the state hook: SEO_EDITOR_SURFACE_TABS, tab/setTab/tabOptions are exported and computed but unused by the surface; only the URL param validation matters.
overcomplication.evidence: src/components/seo-editor-rebuilt/useSeoEditorSurfaceState.ts:9-14,139-141,171-174 vs zero references in SeoEditorSurface.tsx (grep 'state.tab|setTab|tabOptions' returns nothing).
overcomplication.suggestion: Either implement the research lens the contract describes or reduce the hook to param validation only and update the contract's '?tab=research opens the research lens' wording.

overcomplication.issue: Duplicate data plumbing: usePageJoin and usePageEditStates are each instantiated twice (once in useSeoEditorSurfaceData, again in useSeoEditorSurfaceWorkflows), doubling fetch/refresh work per render tree.
overcomplication.evidence: src/hooks/admin/useSeoEditorRebuilt.ts:88-89 and :230-231; already flagged as 'SEO Editor projection duplication' in docs/ui-rebuild/parity/parity-backlog.md:134.
overcomplication.suggestion: Pass the data hook's pageJoin/pageStates instances into the workflows hook instead of re-instantiating.

overcomplication.issue: Source group bands are faked inside DataTable via an absolute-positioned 28px band rendered in the first cell of each group-start row plus manual pt-7 padding on every cell of that row — repeated in all six column renderers.
overcomplication.suggestion: Add grouped-row/section-header support to the DataTable primitive (other rebuilt surfaces will want it) instead of per-cell padding choreography.

overcomplication.issue: A second React Query cache key for the same suggestions endpoint: SEO_EDITOR_REBUILT_QUERY_KEYS.suggestions duplicates queryKeys.admin.seoSuggestions used by the legacy surface, so the same data lives under two keys with separate invalidation paths.
overcomplication.suggestion: Reuse queryKeys.admin.seoSuggestions so cross-surface invalidations (and future WS handlers) hit one cache.

gains: Full URL/deep-link state: ?source, ?filter, ?collection, ?page (drawer open), ?search, plus validated fallbacks — legacy SEO Editor had zero URL state (all useState) (useSeoEditorSurfaceState.ts:93-195 vs SeoEditorWrapper.tsx:29-32).

True inline spreadsheet editing of title/meta for static AND CMS rows directly in the table with length-tone borders and live counters — legacy required expanding each row/item one at a time (SeoEditorWorksheet.tsx:325-424 vs PageEditRow.tsx:154+ / CmsEditorCollections.tsx:229+).

One unified worksheet across Static/CMS/Manual with source bands, truthful group select-all checkboxes, and a single sticky selected-actions toolbar spanning both static and CMS selections at once (SeoEditorWorksheet.tsx:474-519) — legacy had three stacked, separately-selected editors.

Keyword intelligence in the row: target-keyword chip with title/meta containment checks, rank, and optimization score columns, plus avg score and in-review/approved/need-work counts in the header summary (SeoEditorWorksheet.tsx:299-323, SeoEditorSurface.tsx:162-178,260-267) — legacy showed keyword chips only, no match/rank/score at row level.

Page title rename with save (savePageTitle → PUT /api/webflow/pages/:id/seo) in the drawer — no rename UI existed on the legacy surface (useSeoEditorRebuilt.ts:347-361, SeoEditorPagePanel.tsx:318-350).

Manual-target remediation guidance: read-only drawer explains WHY the row is read-only and lists concrete make-writable paths (edit at source / map to Webflow / redirect workflow) — legacy just listed manual URLs with a one-line reason (SeoEditorPagePanel.tsx:546-563 vs SeoEditorWrapper.tsx:165-186).
Explicit workspace WS invalidation (PAGE_STATE_UPDATED, APPROVAL_UPDATE, STRATEGY_UPDATED → refetchAll) so the surface stays fresh outside the legacy shell (SeoEditorSurface.tsx:199-206).

Proper loading skeleton, workspace-read error state with retry, 'connect a Webflow site' empty state, stale-data banner with retry, and a dismissible deep-link-not-found banner (SeoEditorSurface.tsx:208-245,394-407) — legacy had only a LoadingState and nothing for error/unconnected.
Site publish errors now surface as toasts instead of console-only (useSeoEditorRebuilt.ts:371-386 vs SeoEditor.tsx:131-144).

fixContext handoff opens the exact matching row's drawer (including CMS/manual identity matching), and an outbound Page Intelligence handoff carries fixContext state (SeoEditorSurface.tsx:82-95,190-197; SeoEditorPagePanel.tsx:593-610).

extensions.opportunity: Wire per-item approval history into the drawer by reusing buildItemApprovalMap over cmsQuery.data.approvalBatches (the data is already fetched by useCmsEditor) and, for static pages, the same batches filtered by pageId.
extensions.value: Restores the biggest undocumented trust loss — operators can see what was already sent/approved before re-sending — with no new backend work.

extensions.opportunity: Surface cmsWorkflow.approvalError (and approvalSent) as toasts at the toolbar send site, mirroring the static path's toast wiring.
extensions.value: Eliminates the silent-failure regression on the highest-stakes action (client sends).

extensions.opportunity: Scope the missing-metadata banner counts to static pages (or extend handleBulkFix to also draft CMS title/desc via the existing cms bulk rewrite), so displayed counts match the action's denominator.
extensions.value: Restores count/action honesty per the platform's rate-display rule.

extensions.opportunity: Render remaining extra SEO fields and the slug input in the CMS drawer (iterate getExtraSeoFields like legacy did) and add a small per-field AI rewrite menu next to 'Generate pair'.
extensions.value: Closes three undocumented capability losses at once using workflow functions that already exist (cmsWorkflow.aiRewrite, updateField('slug')).

extensions.opportunity: Promote 'Sent to client' out of the collapsed Production tools group (or show a pending-batch count badge on the GroupBlock header) and open the suggestions group automatically when a bulk rewrite completes.
extensions.value: Makes outstanding client work and fresh AI output discoverable without hunting through double-collapsed groups.

extensions.opportunity: Reuse the legacy per-page diff list for pattern/bulk previews inside the Static bulk actions group (full old→new rows, Apply/Cancel), and disable the pattern toolbar with a 'select rows first' hint when nothing is selected.
extensions.value: Restores reviewable bulk writes to live Webflow pages and removes a silent no-op.

extensions.opportunity: Build the deferred keyboard review queue ('Review pending', arrows + A/S/P/R) once Approve/Request-changes callbacks exist — the worksheet's row model and drawer are already shaped for it.
extensions.value: Completes the prototype's third layer, already owner-gated in the contract (line 129-134), turning triage of 50+ pending targets into a keyboard flow.

surfaceAudits.surface: site-audit (Page: seo-audit, incl. ?sub=content-decay / aeo-review / guide / history receivers)
surfaceAudits.headline: The rebuilt Site Audit console is a genuine upgrade in orientation, triage, URL state, and error feedback, but it silently collapses per-page repair depth — the issue drawer's accept/task/send/fix actions only operate on the FIRST affected page of a group and the page-level inventory (per-page scores, full affected-page browsing) has no rebuilt home — despite the parity contract claiming issue-detail capabilities are "unchanged".

functionalityLost.capability: Per-page-instance repair actions on every affected page. Legacy rendered an action row per page-issue: Apply Now (accept AI suggestion), edit suggestion, Fix→ handoff, Send to Client, Add to Tasks — each targeting THAT page. The rebuilt drawer wires accept/task/flag/open-fix only to the FIRST page instance of a group (`firstPageInstance`); the other listed instances get only Hide / Hide pattern. For a group like 'Missing meta description (14 pages)' you can selectively fix page 1 only; pages 2–14 are reachable solely through indiscriminate bulk actions (Accept all / Add all tasks).
functionalityLost.evidence: Legacy: src/components/audit/AuditIssueRow.tsx:96-124 (per-row Apply Now + edit), 186-199 (per-row Fix→), 222-260 (per-row Send to Client / Add to Tasks / Suppress) rendered per page-issue at src/components/SeoAudit.tsx:776-810. Rebuilt: src/components/site-audit-rebuilt/SiteAuditSurface.tsx:415-418 (firstPageInstance only), 432-447 (drawer footer Open fix/Add task bound to firstPage), 480-515 (Accept suggestion / Send to client bound to firstPage), 521-551 (remaining instances: Hide/Hide pattern only).
functionalityLost.whereDocumented: Not recorded; docs/ui-rebuild/parity/site-audit-contract.md:50 explicitly claims 'issue detail … capabilities are unchanged', which is contradicted by the code.

functionalityLost.capability: Page-level inventory and per-page browsing: legacy listed every crawled page with its own score, 28-day traffic badge (clicks/views with impressions tooltip), edit-state status border + badge, noindex explanation banner, and an expandable per-page issue list. The rebuilt surface is issue-group-only; the drawer caps affected pages at 12 with 'N more affected pages are included in batch actions' and shows no per-page score anywhere.
functionalityLost.evidence: Legacy: src/components/SeoAudit.tsx:723-817 (page list; per-page score :761, traffic badge :753-757, StatusBadge :752, noindex banner :767-772). Rebuilt: src/components/site-audit-rebuilt/SiteAuditSurface.tsx:521 (instances.slice(0,12)), 555-558 (overflow message), no page-level view in SiteAuditSurface.tsx or useSiteAuditRebuilt.ts.
functionalityLost.whereDocumented: The issue-first interaction model is owner-approved (site-audit-contract.md:17-27, 2026-07-10), but per-page score/inventory loss is not listed in Carry-Over Homes (site-audit-contract.md:84-97) or parity-backlog.md.

functionalityLost.capability: Page Intelligence handoff: legacy every page-issue row had a 'Page' button navigating to page-intelligence with fixContext preloading that page. No reference to page-intelligence exists anywhere in the rebuilt surface or hook.
functionalityLost.evidence: Legacy: src/components/audit/AuditIssueRow.tsx:201-210. Rebuilt: grep 'page-intelligence' across src/components/site-audit-rebuilt/ and src/hooks/admin/useSiteAuditRebuilt.ts returns zero matches; openQuickFix (useSiteAuditRebuilt.ts:357-383) routes only to seo-schema/links/content-pipeline/performance/seo-editor.

functionalityLost.capability: In-audit dead-link repair tools: legacy DeadLinkPanel offered per-link 'Fix' → SEO Editor with source-page fixContext, inline redirect queueing per URL, dead-links CSV export, and queued-redirect-rules CSV export. The rebuilt panel is read-only — every row and the header button just navigate to links?tab=dead-links. CSV export and redirect authoring survive inside the rebuilt Links surface (extra navigation), but the per-link 'fix source page in SEO Editor' shortcut has no home anywhere (no fixContext senders in links-rebuilt).
functionalityLost.evidence: Legacy: src/components/audit/DeadLinkPanel.tsx:129-143 (Fix→seo-editor fixContext), 144-199 + 46-52 (redirect queueing), 21-37 (dead-links CSV), 39-44 (redirect rules CSV). Rebuilt: src/components/site-audit-rebuilt/SiteAuditSurface.tsx:228-277 (navigate-only rows); partial re-home: src/components/links-rebuilt/DeadLinksLens.tsx:207-214,394-396 (CSV) and src/components/links-rebuilt/RedirectsLens.tsx:156-171 (redirect targets + CSV); grep 'fixContext' in links-rebuilt/ returns nothing.
functionalityLost.whereDocumented: Only the coarse 'Dead-link handoff to Links' is recorded (site-audit-contract.md:92); the lost per-link fix shortcut and in-context redirect queueing are not.

functionalityLost.capability: In-context Cancel for a running bulk fix-apply job: legacy showed a red Cancel button next to 'Applying N/M…' in the toolbar. The rebuilt surface registers the cancel handler in bulkHandlersRef but never renders any cancel control — the only way to stop AI fixes being pushed to the live Webflow site is the NotificationBell job row.
functionalityLost.evidence: Legacy: src/components/audit/AuditFilters.tsx:86-96. Rebuilt: src/components/site-audit-rebuilt/SiteAuditSurface.tsx:680 (ref captures cancel), 776-783 + 898-929 (only acceptAll invoked; no cancel UI). Mitigation exists: src/components/NotificationBell.tsx:39,54,294 with shared/types/background-jobs.ts:262-268 (cancellable: true).

functionalityLost.capability: Site score delta vs previous audit on the default lens: legacy's hero Site Score StatCard showed the delta from the last snapshot; the rebuilt hero has no delta (only the History lens shows per-row deltas).
functionalityLost.evidence: Legacy: src/components/SeoAudit.tsx:514 (delta={history[0].siteScore - history[1].siteScore}). Rebuilt: src/components/site-audit-rebuilt/SiteAuditSurface.tsx:344-381 (AuditHero, no delta); deltas only in CompactAuditHistory.tsx:77-101.

functionalityLost.capability: Per-metric CWV rating signal: legacy color-coded each of LCP/INP/CLS by its own good/needs-improvement/poor rating, spelled out metric names, and showed an explicit 'No real-user data (CrUX)' note. The rebuilt strip renders all metric values in the same bright text — a poor LCP looks identical to a good one except the number — with only a strategy-level Passed/Needs work/Failed badge.
functionalityLost.evidence: Legacy: src/components/audit/CwvSummaryCard.tsx:9-13 (per-rating colors), 31-48 (per-metric rows), 57-61 (CrUX note). Rebuilt: src/components/site-audit-rebuilt/SiteAuditSurface.tsx:204-218 (uncolored t-stat-sm values; single Meter labelled Field data/Lab simulation).
functionalityLost.whereDocumented: Contract only requires 'Core Web Vitals strip with field-data/lab fallback' (site-audit-contract.md:89).

functionalityLost.capability: Edit-state tracking summary degraded: legacy showed a 6-status bar (live, in-review, approved, rejected, issue-detected, fix-proposed) always visible above the page list; rebuilt shows 3 statuses (in review / approved / live) and only inside an open issue drawer.
functionalityLost.evidence: Legacy: src/components/SeoAudit.tsx:711-721. Rebuilt: src/components/site-audit-rebuilt/SiteAuditSurface.tsx:563-571.

functionalityLost.capability: Findings-driven 'Quick fixes →' triage shortcuts row (SEO Editor / Redirects / Schema / Performance surfaced conditionally from audit findings) and the post-run NextStepsCard. Rebuilt keeps per-issue routing via openQuickFix in the drawer but has no surface-level cross-tool triage strip or completion guidance.
functionalityLost.evidence: Legacy: src/components/SeoAudit.tsx:542-570 (quick fixes row), 491-506 (NextStepsCard). Rebuilt: no equivalent in SiteAuditSurface.tsx; routing exists only per-issue at useSiteAuditRebuilt.ts:357-383.

functionalityLost.capability: Visible share URL after Save & Share: legacy showed a persistent banner with the URL, Copy, open-in-new-tab, and dismiss. Rebuilt silently writes to clipboard and toasts; the hook stores shareUrl (useSiteAuditRebuilt.ts:275,517) but the surface never renders it, so there is no way to see, re-copy, or open the link afterward.
functionalityLost.evidence: Legacy: src/components/SeoAudit.tsx:654-675. Rebuilt: src/components/site-audit-rebuilt/SiteAuditSurface.tsx:755-765 (clipboard+toast only; audit.shareUrl unused).

regressions.issue: Misleading success toast on share: `await navigator.clipboard?.writeText(url)` no-ops when the Clipboard API is unavailable (non-secure context, permissions), yet the 'Share link copied' toast still fires — and since the URL is never rendered, the operator has no fallback.
regressions.evidence: src/components/site-audit-rebuilt/SiteAuditSurface.tsx:757-761 vs legacy visible-banner fallback at src/components/SeoAudit.tsx:654-675.

regressions.issue: Traffic sort is always offered even when the GSC/GA traffic map is empty, in which case it silently does nothing (all groups tie at 0 clicks/0 sessions). Legacy gated the Traffic Impact sort on hasTraffic.
regressions.evidence: Rebuilt: src/components/site-audit-rebuilt/SiteAuditSurface.tsx:862-869 (Segmented always rendered) + useSiteAuditRebuilt.ts:236-241 (tie → keeps prior order). Legacy gate: src/components/audit/AuditBatchActions.tsx:55-75 (hasTraffic).

regressions.issue: Drawer 'Add task' button has no in-flight state: the hook exposes creatingTask but the button only reflects createdTasks, so a slow POST gives no feedback and allows double-clicks until the success set updates.
regressions.evidence: src/components/site-audit-rebuilt/SiteAuditSurface.tsx:438-445 (no loading prop) vs src/hooks/admin/useSiteAuditRebuilt.ts:266,389-400 (creatingTask tracked); legacy per-row loading at src/components/audit/AuditIssueRow.tsx:69,312-314.

confusions.issue: Drawer actions read as group-scoped but act on one page: the drawer is titled with the group check and shows '14 pages' badges, then offers 'Accept suggestion' / 'Send to client' / 'Add task' with no page qualifier — an operator will reasonably believe the action covered all affected pages when it touched only the first instance.
confusions.why: The whole rebuilt model is 'one row = one issue group'; nothing in the drawer copy says the action buttons narrow to a single page (SiteAuditSurface.tsx:432-515). This is a trust landmine when reporting fixes to a client.

confusions.issue: The 'Evidence & repair support' group moves position depending on how you arrived: with a ?sub= deep link it renders directly after the CWV strip/toolbar; on a bare visit it sits collapsed at the very bottom below issues and suppressions.
confusions.why: An operator who opened AI Search Ready from a Cockpit link learns it lives 'near the top', then can't find it there on the next bare visit (SiteAuditSurface.tsx:894-896 vs 1013-1015).

confusions.issue: Vocabulary split between 'Hide' / 'Hide pattern' buttons in the drawer and the 'N suppressed' / 'Suppression rules cleared' language in the summary bar and toasts; legacy also distinguished page vs pattern counts ('2 page + 1 pattern suppressed'), which the flat rebuilt count drops.
confusions.why: The operator has to connect 'hidden' items to the 'suppressed' counter to understand why scores changed (SiteAuditSurface.tsx:544-549 vs 1000-1011; legacy breakdown at AuditBatchActions.tsx:37-49).

confusions.issue: The count chip on the 'Site Audit' lens is the issue-GROUP count, which sits next to a hero that reports raw error/warning counts — three different-looking totals (groups, errors+warnings, filtered 'Showing X of Y groups') with no labels distinguishing them.
confusions.why: lensOptions count = issueGroups.length (SiteAuditSurface.tsx:1138-1146) while the hero badges (369-374) and the issues-card counter (955-957) count different things; a fresh operator can't tell which number to quote to a client.

overcomplication.issue: DataTable row plumbing double-casts through `as unknown as Record<string, unknown>[]` and back to IssueTableRow inside every cell renderer, plus getRowKey/onRowClick — pure ceremony that obscures the row type and invites field drift.
overcomplication.suggestion: Type-parameterize DataTable (or add a typed wrapper) so IssueTableRow[] flows through without casts.
