overcomplication.issue: IssueDetailDrawer receives the entire `audit` object AND six fields individually plucked from that same object (flagSending, applyingFix, createdTasks, flaggedIssues, plus seven callbacks that all close over audit).
overcomplication.evidence: src/components/site-audit-rebuilt/SiteAuditSurface.tsx:383-412 (prop list) and 1033-1083 (call site passing audit + audit.* separately).
overcomplication.suggestion: Pass a single narrowed interface (or just `audit` + toast wrapper) — halves the prop surface and removes the duplicate-source-of-truth risk.

overcomplication.issue: AuditEvidence is mounted from three different branches (loading state, empty state, loaded state) and in two positions within the loaded state, with a keyed inner div to force disclosure remounts — clever state-aware placement that costs positional stability and makes the exact-once invariant depend on four conditionals.
overcomplication.suggestion: Render it once in a fixed slot and drive open-state imperatively from evidenceSub; the deep-link 'open the disclosure' behavior doesn't require relocation.

gains: URL-written, reactive lens state: switching lenses writes/clears ?sub= (useSiteAuditSurfaceState.ts:47-57) and the receiver reads useSearchParams reactively — legacy read ?sub once in a useState initializer (SeoAudit.tsx:72-76) and never wrote the URL, so refresh lost the tab and in-place deep links (WorkspaceHome/ContentPipeline senders) were dead if the component was already mounted.

Category score cards: six derived category scores (Indexability/On-page/Performance/Structured data/Links/Mobile) with per-category error/warning/info counts, affected-page counts, and click-to-filter (useSiteAuditRebuilt.ts:102-142, SiteAuditSurface.tsx:279-336) — legacy had only unlabeled filter pills with no scores.
Multi-select filtering: severity chips and category cards are toggle sets (useToggleSet) allowing combined filters; legacy severity and category were single-select ('all' | one).

Issue-group model with aggregated traffic: groups sortable by severity or traffic with clicks/sessions columns visible per group (useSiteAuditRebuilt.ts:144-246), replacing legacy's opaque page-level 'clicks+pageviews' composite sort.

Send-to-client note is no longer silently dropped: legacy embedded the note only in the no-suggestion fallback branch of proposedValue (SeoAudit.tsx:187), so a note typed alongside an AI suggestion never reached the client; rebuilt always sends it as the API's top-level note field (siteAuditApproval.ts:34-39, accepted by server/routes/approvals.ts:38).

Honest mutation feedback: every mutation (run, schedule, suppress, tasks, flag, share, bulk) surfaces success/error toasts via mutationErrorMessage; legacy swallowed most failures to console.error (e.g. SeoAudit.tsx:108,117,127,194,206,231).

In-surface workspace states: loading skeleton, workspace-error with retry, workspace-not-found, and a 'Connect a Webflow site first' empty state (SiteAuditSurface.tsx:1151-1196) instead of relying on the App.tsx non-null assertion (App.tsx:435 siteId={selected.webflowSiteId!}).
ActionItemsPanel now renders on the audit lens whenever a snapshot exists (SiteAuditSurface.tsx:1029-1031), not just on the History tab as in legacy (AuditHistory.tsx:79).
Schedule drawer adds last-run/last-score MetricTiles and explicit enable toggle (ScheduleDrawer.tsx:66-89) over legacy's collapsed configure form.
History keeps the score trend (Sparkline), per-row deltas with copy/view actions, and the persistent report link in a denser single-card layout (CompactAuditHistory.tsx).

extensions.opportunity: Restore per-instance actions inside the issue drawer: give each of the listed affected pages its own Accept / Add task / Send / Open fix (the hook functions already take (page, issue) — only the wiring narrows to firstPageInstance).
extensions.value: Closes the surface's biggest undocumented capability loss and makes the drawer's group framing truthful; all backend paths already exist.

extensions.opportunity: Render the share URL (audit.shareUrl is already stateful in the hook) in a dismissible row or in the toast with an Open action, and only claim 'copied' when clipboard write actually resolved.
extensions.value: Removes a misleading success signal and restores re-copy/open — near-zero new code.

extensions.opportunity: Wire the already-registered bulkHandlersRef.cancel to the Accept-all button while a job is running (button already shows done/total progress).
extensions.value: In-context stop for AI fixes being pushed to a live client site; one conditional on an existing handler.

extensions.opportunity: Add the score delta vs previous snapshot to the hero (history[0]/history[1] are already loaded for the context line), and per-metric rating tones on the CWV values via the existing Badge/Meter tones.
extensions.value: Restores at-a-glance trend and per-vital risk signal without new data fetches.

extensions.opportunity: Gate or annotate the Traffic sort when the traffic map is empty (audit.traffic.data keys) — e.g. disabled Segmented option with a 'connect GSC/GA' tooltip.
extensions.value: Kills a silently inert control and advertises the integration that powers it.

extensions.opportunity: Add a 'View all N pages' escape from the drawer for groups exceeding the 12-instance cap (filtered list, or a fixContext handoff into Page Intelligence, which would also restore the lost per-page deep-dive path).
extensions.value: Reconnects the issue-first model to page-level investigation, the workflow the rebuild currently orphans.

surfaceAudits.headline: The rebuilt Competitors surface is meaningfully richer than legacy in every viewing workflow (alerts feed, head-to-head authority columns, live gaps, reachable Send-to-client), but its primary configuration affordance — "Edit set" and both setup empty-state CTAs — routes to Workspace Settings, a page that contains no competitor-domain or SEO-provider editor, so the first-run/edit path is a dead end the parity contract wrongly certifies as the "edit home".

functionalityLost.capability: Working route from the surface to the competitor-set editor. Rebuilt 'Edit set' button and BOTH setup empty-state CTAs navigate to workspace-settings (src/components/competitors-rebuilt/CompetitorsSurface.tsx:191,212,231,245), but WorkspaceSettings has no competitor-domain editor and no SEO-provider config (src/components/WorkspaceSettings.tsx:74-76 SectionTab union = connections/features/flags/dashboard/publishing/export/llms-txt; zero competitor references anywhere in src/components/settings/). The actual editor (textarea + auto-discover) lives in the Strategy page settings panel (src/components/strategy/StrategySettings.tsx:114). Legacy had no button but its empty-state copy pointed to the correct home: 'Enter competitors in Strategy Settings → Competitor Domains' (src/components/strategy/StrategyCompetitiveTab.tsx:54). A new operator following the rebuilt setup flow lands on a page that cannot do what the CTA promised.
functionalityLost.evidence: rebuilt: src/components/competitors-rebuilt/CompetitorsSurface.tsx:191,212,231,245 → adminPath(workspaceId,'workspace-settings'); destination: src/components/WorkspaceSettings.tsx:74-76 (no competitor section, confirmed by grep of settings/*); real editor: src/components/strategy/StrategySettings.tsx:114; legacy correct guidance: src/components/strategy/StrategyCompetitiveTab.tsx:53-55
functionalityLost.whereDocumented: docs/ui-rebuild/parity/competitors-contract.md:31,74,82 asserts 'Workspace Settings remains the edit home' — the contract documents the ROUTE but its capability claim is factually wrong; the dead-end is not recorded as an exception anywhere in parity-backlog.md or rebuilt-surface-inventory.md

Legacy rendered CompetitorAlertsPanel unconditionally after load (src/components/competitors/CompetitorsPage.tsx:50-64), so historical alerts stayed visible even if provider credentials were removed or the domain list was cleared. Rebuilt mounts CompetitorAlerts only inside the fully-configured branch (seoDataAvailable && competitorList.length>0), so historical alert rows silently disappear in both setup states.
functionalityLost.evidence: legacy: src/components/competitors/CompetitorsPage.tsx:54 (unconditional); rebuilt: src/components/competitors-rebuilt/CompetitorsSurface.tsx:224-260 (CompetitorAlerts at line 260 only in the populated else-branch)

regressions.issue: Alerts error state renders a reassuring 'No competitor movement detected' EmptyState directly beneath the error banner. On fetch failure alerts=[] (useCompetitorAlerts returns query.data?.alerts ?? []), so isError renders the InlineBanner AND the rows.length===0 branch renders the quiet-empty message — the operator reads 'no movement' when the read actually failed. Legacy showed ErrorState instead of the empty state.
regressions.evidence: rebuilt: src/components/competitors-rebuilt/CompetitorAlerts.tsx:129-155 (banner and EmptyState are independent branches); legacy: src/components/competitors/CompetitorAlertsPanel.tsx:95-104 (isError ? ErrorState : empty)

regressions.issue: 'Re-scan' / 'Last scanned' labels overstate freshness semantics. The button only invalidates client React Query caches and refetches; the server serves provider file-cache (168h TTLs) and fetchedAt is response-assembly time, not a provider scan timestamp. Legacy deliberately labelled this 'Refresh' / 'Updated <ts>' with an honesty comment. An operator clicking Re-scan and seeing identical numbers with a new 'Last scanned' time is being told a scan happened when it did not.
regressions.evidence: rebuilt: src/components/competitors-rebuilt/CompetitorsSurface.tsx:73,90-93,175-180; server: server/routes/seo-provider.ts (fetchedAt: new Date().toISOString() at response assembly, ~line 170); legacy honesty comment: src/components/strategy/CompetitiveIntel.tsx:469-472

regressions.issue: Share-of-voice bar widths changed meaning silently: rebuilt scales bars relative to the LEADER (Meter max=maxShare → leader always renders full-width), legacy widths were absolute share-of-total. A 40%-share leader now looks like 100%. The % text labels remain honest, but the visual encoding changed without documentation.
regressions.evidence: rebuilt: src/components/competitors-rebuilt/ShareOfVoice.tsx:27,57-63 (max={maxShare}); legacy: src/components/strategy/ShareBar.tsx:72-76 (width: pct% of total)

regressions.issue: Send-to-client drops the sendability gate and the estimatedGain/impactBand context. Legacy GapRow disabled send unless isSendable(insight/description/estimatedGain/impactBand) held for an existing rec, and rendered WhyHowResult (insight + estimated gain + impact band). Rebuilt only disables on pending/!keyword and shows rec.insight as a bare caption — an existing non-sendable rec can now be sent, and the operator loses the gain/impact evidence before sending.
regressions.evidence: rebuilt: src/components/competitors-rebuilt/KeywordGapsCard.tsx:150,161-163; legacy production path: src/components/strategy/CompetitiveIntel.tsx:158-165,207-215 (note: this path was unreachable on the legacy Competitors page itself — comparison is vs the strategy-surface production behavior the contract claims to carry over)

confusions.issue: 'Edit set' lands the operator on Workspace Settings with no competitor editor in any of its seven tabs
confusions.why: The button is the surface's most prominent configuration affordance and the rebuilt sidebar now exposes Competitors in global nav (RebuiltSidebar.tsx:83,110), so operators hit this dead end far more often than the legacy deep-link-only page; they must independently know the editor lives on the Strategy page's settings panel

confusions.issue: KD unit is inconsistent inside the same surface: gaps badge says 'KD 42' (no %), the drawer keyword table renders '42%', legacy always said 'KD 42%'
confusions.why: An operator comparing the gaps table to the drawer sees two formats for the same metric and may wonder if they are different scales

confusions.issue: Clicking the own-domain (YOU) row in Head-to-head does nothing, while every other row opens the Drawer and the subtitle says 'select a row for detail'
confusions.why: Uniform row affordance with non-uniform behavior; there is no visual cue that the own row is inert

confusions.issue: Contract claims a 'leader marker' as a head-to-head carry-over home (competitors-contract.md:92) but no leader marker exists in rebuilt OR legacy code — rows are merely traffic-sorted
confusions.why: A future parity audit or builder reading the contract will hunt for a capability that never existed; contract overclaims erode trust in the other claims

confusions.issue: Degraded-data banner reports only a count ('3 competitor data read(s) degraded') though the response carries per-failure area/provider/domain detail
confusions.why: An operator can't tell WHICH competitor's numbers are missing, so they can't judge whether the head-to-head ranking is still trustworthy

overcomplication.issue: CompetitorAlerts carries an unreachable 'No competitor set configured' empty-state branch: the component only mounts when competitorList.length > 0, so competitorCount is always positive
overcomplication.suggestion: Either mount the alerts feed in setup states too (which also fixes the historical-alerts loss) so the branch becomes live, or delete the branch

overcomplication.issue: CompetitorAlerts recovers lastSnapshotDate by reaching into the raw React Query cache with queryClient.getQueryData and a locally-widened response type, instead of the hook returning the full response
overcomplication.evidence: src/components/competitors-rebuilt/CompetitorAlerts.tsx:115-118 + widened types in src/components/competitors-rebuilt/types.ts:60-67, duplicating server-side types declared inline in server/routes/competitor-alerts.ts:17-24
overcomplication.suggestion: Move insightId/lastSnapshotDate into shared/types/competitor-alerts.ts (the server already serves them) and have useCompetitorAlerts return the response; deletes the cache-poking and the type shadowing

overcomplication.issue: competitorMutationFeedback.ts is a two-line re-export shim around lib/extractErrorMessage
overcomplication.suggestion: Import extractErrorMessage directly in KeywordGapsCard and delete the shim

gains: Send to client per keyword gap is actually reachable on this surface: on the legacy Competitors page the send button lived only in CompetitiveIntel's gaps section, which the merged variant hides (CompetitiveIntel.tsx:419 '!merged &&'), so the flags were passed but dead; rebuilt renders it (doubly flag-gated) with client-status feedback badges (KeywordGapsCard.tsx:143-163)

Authority rank and Top-3 keyword counts are now displayed (HeadToHeadTable.tsx:99-141, CompetitorDetailDrawer.tsx:117-118) — the server has returned these fields (server/routes/seo-provider.ts:155-165) but legacy discarded them, a direct fix of the 2026-06 SEO-audit 'fetched-and-discarded' theme; columns render only when data exists per the no-fabrication rule

Head-to-head includes the own domain as a row with a YOU badge in a sortable DataTable (HeadToHeadTable.tsx:45-56,91); legacy merged variant hid the own-domain stat grid entirely, leaving own metrics visible only inside expanded bars

Keyword gaps prefer LIVE gaps from the intel response with an honest 'from strategy' fallback pill (CompetitorsSurface.tsx:270-276, KeywordGapsCard.tsx:177-178,264); legacy page showed only the cached strategy-blob gaps (StrategyCompetitiveTab.tsx:70)

Alerts feed adds the this-week count, 'updated <date>' sync line from lastSnapshotDate, Insight-linked badge, created-vs-snapshot dates, and a Retry action (CompetitorAlerts.tsx:90,98-99,120-123,133-140; server wiring server/routes/competitor-alerts.ts:38,59); legacy showed none of these and had no retry
Header Re-scan invalidates intel + backlinks + alerts together (CompetitorsSurface.tsx:175-180) vs legacy Refresh touching only competitorIntelAll (CompetitiveIntel.tsx:336-343)
Distinct provider-not-configured vs add-domains setup states each with an action button (CompetitorsSurface.tsx:224-251); legacy EmptyStates had no CTA at all (StrategyCompetitiveTab.tsx:40-57)

Strategy-read failure gets an explicit ErrorState with Retry, and a stale-competitor-set warning banner when a refetch fails with cached data (CompetitorsSurface.tsx:184-204,254-258); legacy silently fell through to the provider empty state on error

Create brief passes primaryKeyword in fixContext so the brief prefills 'New brief · <keyword>' (KeywordGapsCard.tsx:130-138, consumed at ContentPipelineWorkspaces.tsx:330); legacy sent only pageName (StrategyCompetitiveTab.tsx:76-78)
Workspace identity eyebrow + competitor-set badges + weekly-cadence/freshness context in the header (CompetitorsSurface.tsx:75-128), replacing legacy's static PageHeader

extensions.opportunity: Fix the edit-set destination: either add a competitor-domains editor (and provider selector) section to Workspace Settings so the contract's claim becomes true, or retarget all three CTAs to the Strategy page settings panel; while there, surface the existing auto-discover endpoint (GET /api/seo/discover-competitors, server/routes/seo-provider.ts:186+) directly on this surface — it is the natural home for it
extensions.value: Unbreaks the first-run setup flow and the surface's primary edit affordance; discover-on-surface removes the cross-page hop entirely

extensions.opportunity: Add a Referring-domains column to the head-to-head table (the data is already in every CompetitiveDomain.backlinks) and consider an explicit leader marker to make the contract claim real
extensions.value: Restores the legacy at-a-glance authority signal without a drawer click

extensions.opportunity: Render providerFailures detail (area + domain) in the degraded banner instead of a bare count
extensions.value: Operators can judge which competitor's numbers are missing and whether the ranking is still trustworthy

extensions.opportunity: Make freshness honest: show the provider snapshot/lastSnapshotDate for 'Last scanned' rather than response-assembly fetchedAt, and either wire Re-scan to a true server-side cache-busting refresh or rename it 'Refresh'
extensions.value: Removes a trust landmine where identical cached data gets a fresh 'Last scanned' timestamp

extensions.opportunity: Mount the alerts feed in setup states (provider-unconfigured / no domains) with the existing quiet-empty copy
extensions.value: Historical competitor movement stays visible when credentials lapse, and the currently-dead 'No competitor set configured' branch becomes live code

surfaceAudits.headline: Parity is structurally strong because the rebuilt Generator reuses the legacy generation/publishing/CMS hooks wholesale, but the pre-scan state silently dropped the schema site-plan, CMS-mapping, and business-profile homes, "Add a page" failures are now completely silent, and list-level client-review status visibility was lost — against genuine gains in live WS invalidation, admin-path recommendations, and honest empty/error states.

functionalityLost.capability: Pre-scan homes for the Schema site plan (generate/roles/canonical entities/activate/send/retract), CMS field mapping, and business-profile callout. Legacy rendered all three in the not-started state so a fresh workspace could do plan-first setup before spending on generation; the rebuilt renders them only inside the pages.length > 0 results block, so on a fresh workspace they are unreachable until after a (paid) generation run — inverting the plan-first ordering the rebuilt's own guide instructs ('Generate the site plan first').
functionalityLost.evidence: Legacy: src/components/SchemaSuggester.tsx:293-350 (!started branch renders SchemaPlanPanel :312, SchemaBusinessProfileCallout :313, SchemaCmsFieldMappingPanel :320). Rebuilt: src/components/schema-rebuilt/GeneratorLens.tsx:324-357 (!started renders only SchemaGeneratorEmptySetup + page inventory); SchemaSitePlanBridge/SchemaBusinessProfilePanel/SchemaCmsMappingPanel mount only inside the pages.length>0 block at GeneratorLens.tsx:369, 525-541. SchemaPlanPanel's only rebuilt mount is SchemaSupportPanels.tsx:345. Guide contradiction: WorkflowGuideLens.tsx:11 ('Generate the site plan first').
functionalityLost.whereDocumented: Contract's Carry-Over Homes (schema-contract.md:69-85) requires these 'reachable exactly once' but does not record the pre-scan removal; parity-backlog item 17 covers only the Drawer exception.

The rebuilt table has no edit-state column and the rollup is gone entirely — publishing.summary is never consumed; status is visible only one page at a time inside the drawer.
functionalityLost.evidence: Legacy: src/components/schema/SchemaPageCard.tsx:120 (StatusBadge per row), src/components/SchemaSuggester.tsx:556 + src/components/schema/SchemaResultsSummary.tsx:84-100 (aggregate). Rebuilt: src/components/schema-rebuilt/SchemaPageTable.tsx:67-128 (columns: page/type/validation/publish only); editState reaches only the drawer at GeneratorLens.tsx:604 → SchemaPageDrawer.tsx:190; no reference to publishing.summary anywhere in schema-rebuilt/.
functionalityLost.whereDocumented: Not in schema-contract.md carry-over list nor parity-backlog item 17 (which covers heavy review/edit/publish/history in the Drawer, not list-level status).

functionalityLost.capability: Per-row at-a-glance badges on the page list: @graph type count, existing-schema count, rich-results-eligible count, open schema-recommendation count, per-row stale '(Nd old)' badge, per-row validation error count, and the per-row one-click Regenerate button. In the rebuilt these all require opening each page's drawer.

regressions.issue: 'Add a page' failures are completely silent. The shared generation hook catches the all-pages fetch error into fetchPagesError, but the rebuilt never renders it — no banner, no toast. If the Webflow pages read fails (expired token, API error), the operator clicks 'Add a page', sees a brief spinner, and nothing happens, with zero feedback. Legacy showed an inline error banner next to the button.
regressions.evidence: Hook sets the error: src/components/schema/useSchemaSuggesterGeneration.ts:172-181. Legacy renders it: src/components/SchemaSuggester.tsx:510-517. Rebuilt: grep for fetchPagesError in src/components/schema-rebuilt/ returns zero hits; GeneratorLens.tsx:390-393 renders only the button.

regressions.issue: The stale-schema warning banner says 'Open stale pages before republishing' but the table gives no per-row indication of WHICH pages are stale (legacy showed a per-row 'Nd old' badge), so the instruction is unactionable without opening every drawer.
regressions.evidence: Rebuilt: src/components/schema-rebuilt/SchemaPageTable.tsx:147-151 (aggregate banner), columns at :67-128 carry no stale indicator. Legacy per-row badge: src/components/schema/SchemaPageCard.tsx:130-132.

regressions.issue: Bulk band (Publish all / Send to client) stays visible and enabled during a streaming re-scan with partial data; legacy hid the whole BulkPublishPanel while loading, preventing publishing a half-generated batch.

Rebuilt: GeneratorLens.tsx:444-489 renders the band unconditionally inside pages.length>0; Publish-all disabled only on bulkPublishing/bulkPublishBlocked (:462).

regressions.issue: Completeness panel's success copy over-claims: 'All generated pages include the recommended profile fields' whenever no finding maps to a known field target — even if pages still carry warnings/errors on non-targetable fields. Legacy required zero findings of any kind before claiming complete and had an explicit fallback message for this case.
regressions.evidence: Rebuilt: src/components/schema-rebuilt/SchemaSupportPanels.tsx:217 (meta keyed only on groups.length===0; groups filtered by fieldToTarget at :188-189). Legacy: src/components/schema/SchemaCompletenessWidget.tsx:39,69,89-92 (fullyClean requires pagesWithFindings===0).

regressions.issue: Surface no longer remounts when a workspace's linked Webflow site changes: legacy keyed SchemaSuggester on webflowSiteId, the rebuilt keys on tab:workspaceId. Re-linking a different site mid-session can leave stale generation data/started state from the old site (the snapshot effect only clears data when the new snapshot is null, not when it exists with 0 results).
regressions.evidence: Legacy: src/App.tsx:445 (key={`schema-${selected.webflowSiteId}`}). Rebuilt: src/App.tsx:518 (key={`${tab}:${rebuiltWorkspaceId}`}); state-clear gap in src/components/schema/useSchemaSuggesterGeneration.ts:53-74.

confusions.issue: Fresh-workspace dead end: the rebuilt guide's step 1 says 'Generate the site plan first', but on a workspace with no generation snapshot the Schema site plan panel does not exist anywhere on the surface — the operator cannot find it until after running full generation.
confusions.why: The guide actively directs operators to a control that is unreachable in exactly the state where they'd follow the guide (new client onboarding). They'll either burn a paid generation run out of order or hunt other surfaces for the plan.

confusions.issue: All production support (site plan, approval queue, CMS mapping, coverage, completeness, impact, publishing paths) is a stack of 7-8 collapsed-by-default GroupBlocks below the page table.
confusions.why: Legacy put the plan panel at the top and kept completeness/impact visible; an operator scanning the rebuilt page sees only headers and must expand each block to learn anything — pending client approvals in particular are hidden behind a collapsed 'Client approval queue' block (GeneratorLens.tsx:542-560, defaultOpen={false}).

confusions.issue: Readiness hero permanently says 'Coverage remains pending until the next trusted crawl' (GeneratorLens.tsx:309) and the ring says 'Ready for review' at the same time.
confusions.why: The pending message never resolves on this surface, so operators will keep looking for a coverage number that never arrives; contradicting 'ready' + 'pending' signals in the same hero erode trust in what the surface is telling them.

confusions.issue: The 5-step workflow strip is decorative — completed/current are hardcoded so it never advances past 'Review' no matter what the operator does (SchemaWorkflowStrip.tsx:24-25).
confusions.why: A stepper that never moves after you publish/validate reads as broken; legacy's WorkflowStepper had the same limitation, so this is inherited rather than new, but the rebuilt's more prominent card treatment makes it more noticeable.

overcomplication.issue: Dead code shipped in the rebuilt dir: SchemaQuickStats (SchemaSupportPanels.tsx:402-409) and formatSnapshotDate (schemaFormatters.ts:109-112) are exported but never used; summarizeSchemaPages computes richEligible/cmsPages/stalePublished (schemaFormatters.ts:59-61) that no UI displays.
overcomplication.evidence: grep across src/ shows zero call sites for SchemaQuickStats and formatSnapshotDate outside their definitions.
overcomplication.suggestion: Either wire them (snapshot date in the hero, rich/CMS/stale as tiles or filters) or delete them — half-built read models mislead the next agent about what the surface shows.

overcomplication.issue: Two parallel five-step stepper implementations now coexist: the rebuilt SchemaWorkflowStrip and the shared WorkflowStepper still used by legacy, both rendering the same hardcoded Scan→Validate strip.
overcomplication.suggestion: When the flag retires, keep one stepper; better, drive its state from real workflow signals (published count, validation status) so it stops being decoration.

overcomplication.issue: The bulk 'Add client note' hides one optional textarea behind a toggle button plus a border-top sub-panel (GeneratorLens.tsx:455-459, 475-487), where legacy just showed the small textarea inline.
overcomplication.evidence: Legacy BulkPublishPanel.tsx:90-100 renders the note field directly.
overcomplication.suggestion: Minor ceremony; acceptable, but if kept, at least show the toggle as pressed/filled state when a note is entered so operators know a note is attached before sending.

gains: Live workspace-event invalidation: SchemaSurface registers SCHEMA_PLAN_UPDATED / SCHEMA_PLAN_SENT / SCHEMA_CMS_MAPPING_UPDATED / SCHEMA_SNAPSHOT_UPDATED / APPROVAL_UPDATE handlers plus a manual Refresh button (SchemaSurface.tsx:32-43, 112-121); legacy SchemaSuggester had zero useWorkspaceEvents wiring, so cross-session changes never refreshed.

Schema recommendations now read through the admin endpoint (useAdminRecommendationSet, GeneratorLens.tsx:89) instead of the client-gated /api/public/recommendations route legacy used (useRecommendations.ts:17,42) — documented in the contract's Safe Work Completed.

Per-row validation status is always visible in the table (SchemaPageTable.tsx:108-115); legacy only exposed the validation badge inside an expanded card, with just an error-count pill on the collapsed row.
Structured workspace-level loading/error/not-found/no-site states with retry (SchemaSurface.tsx:50-96); legacy relied on App-level fallbacks and a bare text banner.

Mutation feedback toasts for scan start, single-page generation, regeneration, bulk publish, batch send, and page-type save failure (GeneratorLens.tsx:155-197, 252, 350, 591, 631), on the sanctioned shared useToast.
Stale-published detection got an aggregate warning banner (SchemaPageTable.tsx:147-151) and a drawer-level stale banner (SchemaPageDrawer.tsx:229-233) in addition to legacy's per-card note.

Page picker upgraded from a cramped 288px dropdown (PagePicker.tsx:28) to a 520px Drawer with match count and DS focus management (SchemaPagePickerDrawer.tsx); JSON workspace in the drawer is larger (max-h-96 vs max-h-64) with an explicit diff of the EFFECTIVE (edited) schema rather than the raw template, which is more honest about what will publish (SchemaPageDrawer.tsx:280-284 vs SchemaPageCard.tsx:258-264).
Sortable Page column via DataTable (SchemaPageTable.tsx:72) and a searchable pre-scan inventory in a collapsible GroupBlock (GeneratorLens.tsx:330-356).

Schema impact panel now has a visible home even when empty ('No measured deployment window yet', SchemaSupportPanels.tsx:271-281); legacy hid the panel entirely when there were no deployments, so operators never learned the capability existed.

extensions.opportunity: Restore pre-scan mounts for SchemaSitePlanBridge, SchemaCmsMappingPanel, and SchemaBusinessProfilePanel in the !generation.started branch of GeneratorLens (they are all self-fetching and already imported).
extensions.value: Fixes the plan-first workflow contradiction and avoids wasted paid generation runs on fresh workspaces; trivially safe since the same components already render post-scan.

extensions.opportunity: Render generation.fetchPagesError (toast + InlineBanner near 'Add a page').
extensions.value: Eliminates the silent dead-click on Webflow API failures — a direct trust repair.

extensions.opportunity: Add an edit-status column (StatusBadge) to SchemaPageTable and a compact rollup chip strip from publishing.summary; add a per-row stale indicator.
extensions.value: Restores list-level client-review oversight and makes the stale banner actionable without opening N drawers.

extensions.opportunity: Use the already-computed but undisplayed read models: snapshot date via formatSnapshotDate in the hero, and richEligible/cmsPages/stalePublished as summary tiles or table filters.
extensions.value: Data freshness visibility and rich-results upside are exactly what an operator uses in client reporting; the computation already exists, only rendering is missing.

extensions.opportunity: Drive SchemaWorkflowStrip from real state (has plan → scan done → edits pending → published count → validation status) instead of hardcoded step indices.
extensions.value: Turns the decorative stepper into an actual workflow readout — the structure now exists to do what legacy never did.

extensions.opportunity: Add a search/filter field over the generated-pages DataTable (by title/path/validation/publish state).
extensions.value: Neither legacy nor rebuilt can filter the results list; on 50+ page sites the drawer-per-page model makes triage slow.

surfaceAudits.headline: The rebuilt Cockpit is a genuine upgrade — verdict-first orientation, a server-classified work queue with real hand-offs, and honest freshness/stale handling — with only minor, mostly-documented losses, though a few silent data differences (positive churn signals landing under "Risk", inflated "briefs awaiting review" counts) and dead URL/route scaffolding slipped through undocumented.

confusions.issue: One chip row mixes two filter semantics: 'All' and 'Risk' chips are stream RADIOS (single-select, mutate ?stream=) while the source chips (Requests/Briefs/Decay/…) are multi-select TOGGLES on a separate axis — visually identical FilterChips, completely different behavior
confusions.why: An operator who just toggled 'Briefs' on expects clicking 'Risk' to add another filter; instead it switches the whole stream and deselects the primary cards. Nothing in the UI signals the radio/toggle split.

confusions.issue: Queue-row action buttons say 'Send' (send stream) and 'Propose' (money stream) but only navigate to another surface — no send or proposal happens
confusions.why: A busy operator will click 'Send' expecting the send flow (or at least a confirm) and instead lands on the Content Pipeline briefs tab; the label promises an action the button doesn't perform. (WorkQueueRow.tsx:45 default labels; CockpitSurface.tsx:155-183 pure navigation. Documented as a deferred prototype exception in parity-backlog.md:64, but the shipped labels still overpromise.)

confusions.issue: 'Risk' stream can contain positive signals and the group title is 'Risk and unclassified'
confusions.why: A positive churn signal ('client engagement up') showing under a red-flag-named group makes the operator distrust the triage queue — is Risk actually risk, or just 'everything else'?

confusions.issue: The 'stays here' pill and the amber footnote ('Technical fixes stay in the Cockpit — they move into the Insights Engine only when they become a measured proof point') on the Technicals card explain internal information architecture, not client work
confusions.why: First-time operators read 'stays here' as a status of the work items themselves; it is actually a migration/IA annotation aimed at people who knew the old layout — noise that will age badly.

confusions.issue: Same phrase, different numbers across flag states: 'N content briefs awaiting review' counts one status flag-OFF and four statuses flag-ON
confusions.why: During the dual-ship window an operator comparing the legacy home (or memory of it) against the Cockpit sees the count jump with no explanation, undermining trust in the queue counts.

overcomplication.issue: Unused state/props plumbing shipped with the surface: ?view= evidence-view state (parsed, never consumed), provenanceBasis formatter (cockpitFormatters.ts:34-39, zero call sites in cockpit), and route.analytics/route.outcomes threaded into CockpitEvidenceRail's prop contract but never rendered

overcomplication.suggestion: Either wire the rankings/technicals view switcher and the analytics/outcomes link-outs, or delete the scaffolding — dead deep-link params and phantom props are exactly the drift the rebuild gates exist to prevent

overcomplication.issue: Deep child-selector Tailwind overrides on WorkStreamSelector from the parent ('[&>button>span:last-child>span:nth-child(2)]:mt-1' etc.) couple CockpitSurface to the primitive's internal DOM
overcomplication.suggestion: Add a size/density variant prop to WorkStreamSelector instead of styling its internals by position — any markup change in the primitive silently breaks the Cockpit calibration

overcomplication.issue: cockpitFormatters.ts and engineFormatters.ts carry byte-similar copies of provenanceBasis/formatMoney/formatDate
overcomplication.suggestion: Hoist the shared formatters (they already share ProvenanceBasis and OutcomeProvenance types) to one module per the 'extract shared interaction patterns' rule

gains: Server-built verdict hero (buildCockpitVerdict, server/routes/workspace-home.ts:154-160) gives one orienting state instead of legacy's headerless card wall

Work is now classified into opt/send/money/risk streams with counts, a URL-addressable ?stream= filter (incl. the truthful Risk receiver), and stream-grouped rows — legacy NeedsAttention was a flat, capped list of 5
New monetization stream surfaces pending_payment / high-priority full_post content requests as pitchable plays (server/domains/work-queue.ts:220-233) — no legacy equivalent existed
Churn signals became actionable: legacy attention rows had no href/onClick (WorkspaceHome.tsx:197-205); rebuilt rows route to the Requests surface (CockpitSurface.tsx:157-159)
Source-type filter chips with stream-scoped counts add a second filtering axis legacy never had (CockpitWorkQueue.tsx:134-153, CockpitSurface.tsx:129-133)

Refresh got honest: invalidates all four KPI-feeding query keys (workspaceHome, roi, auditSummary, intelligenceAll) instead of one, with success/error toasts (CockpitSurface.tsx:139-153 vs WorkspaceHome.tsx:294)
Stale-data and stale-while-error inline banners (CockpitSurface.tsx:345-361) — legacy only tinted the freshness label

Bug fixed in 'active request' filtering: legacy excluded a nonexistent 'resolved' status and wrongly showed completed requests as active; rebuilt excludes the real terminal 'completed' (CockpitEvidenceRail.tsx:151-153 vs WorkspaceHome.tsx:144, RequestStatus in shared/types/requests.ts:4)
Content-in-flight funnel (Drafting → Your review → Ready → Published) with a contextual review CTA (CockpitEvidenceRail.tsx:192-198,299-318) is richer than legacy's single percent StatCard
Content decay now shows avg decline % impact; overall health gets an On track / At risk / Establishing label; unavailable metrics render '—/Establishing' instead of fabricated zeroes
Onboarding checklist gating improved: setup queue rows are suppressed only while the checklist is visible AND incomplete, with stream counts recomputed (useCockpitRebuilt.ts:266-279)
Retired (meeting-brief) and unknown ?tab= receivers degrade with explicit banners instead of silently showing the default view (CockpitSurface.tsx:327-343)

A11y: stream selector is a roving-tabindex radiogroup; the component test suite covers a real flag loading→loaded transition, the a11y floor, deep links, overlay single-mount, and typography roles (tests/component/cockpit-rebuilt/CockpitSurface.test.tsx)

surfaceAudits.surface: keywords — seo-keywords (Keyword Hub → rebuilt Keywords surface)
surfaceAudits.headline: The rebuilt Keywords surface is a genuine IA upgrade (five lenses, URL-backed state, honest truncation/selection semantics, client-feedback panel on-page) but it silently orphans the AI-visibility KPI panel (its only production home is the legacy Hub), removes per-row quick actions, and thins the keyword drawer's evidence (CTR/impressions, full rank-history chart, strategy backlink, tracking dates) beyond what the parity contract's documented deferrals cover.

functionalityLost.capability: AI Visibility (LLM-mention) KPI panel — share-of-voice vs co-mentioned competitors, mention trend, source domains, and the 'Refresh AI visibility' trigger. The feature's flag was retired globally-ON (feature-flags.ts:70-75), so this is live production functionality whose ONLY mount is the legacy KeywordHub. With ui-rebuild-shell ON there is no home anywhere in the rebuilt shell (no ai-visibility Page exists; the prototype nav names 'AI Visibility' but nothing renders it).
functionalityLost.evidence: Legacy: src/components/KeywordHub.tsx:33,660 mounts AiVisibilityPanel (src/components/strategy/AiVisibilityPanel.tsx:88). In the rebuilt table, single-keyword actions require opening the drawer first; the only in-table mutations are bulk actions via checkboxes.

regressions.issue: Rebuilt-surface WS invalidation misses the client-feedback panel: client keyword feedback submission broadcasts STRATEGY_UPDATED, but the rebuilt handler only invalidates the keywordCommandCenter query prefix — queryKeys.admin.keywordFeedback is never invalidated, so the new 'Client keyword feedback' GroupBlock goes stale (60s staleTime) while the admin watches. Violates the mandatory both-halves broadcast rule for a panel this surface itself introduced.
regressions.evidence: server/keyword-feedback.ts:519 broadcasts STRATEGY_UPDATED; keywords-rebuilt/KeywordsSurface.tsx:274-281 invalidates only queryKeys.admin.keywordCommandCenter; src/components/strategy/hooks/useKeywordFeedback.ts:13-18 reads queryKeys.admin.keywordFeedback. Legacy comparator (KeywordStrategy) got this via the shared strategyMutationKeys (src/lib/wsInvalidation.ts:52).

regressions.issue: Default sort silently changed from opportunity-desc to position-asc. Legacy deliberately led with 'the most valuable, winnable keywords' on load; the rebuilt Rankings lens now leads with the best-ranked (already-won) keywords. Same data, different first screen — an operator comparing flag-ON/OFF sees a different keyword order with no explanation.
regressions.evidence: Legacy: src/hooks/admin/useKeywordHubState.ts:146-148 ('Default to opportunity value (highest first)…', sort={key:'opportunity',direction:'desc'}). Rebuilt: keywords-rebuilt/useKeywordsSurfaceState.ts:53 DEFAULT_SORT={key:'position',direction:'asc'}. Not mentioned in keywords-contract.md.

regressions.issue: Drawer leaks raw lifecycle enums: detailItems renders 'Tracking: deprecated' / 'replaced' via labelize(), where legacy explicitly mapped these to the user-facing 'Retired'/'Replaced' labels ('Never surface the raw deprecated/replaced enums').
regressions.evidence: Rebuilt: keywords-rebuilt/KeywordDrawer.tsx:87-89 (labelize) + :109. Legacy: keyword-command-center/KeywordDetailDrawer.tsx:151-162.

regressions.issue: Drawer action buttons lose their explanatory tooltips and the safe-actions disclaimer: legacy showed title={action.disabledReason || action.detail} per button plus 'These actions change keyword lifecycle state… They do not publish content'; rebuilt buttons carry neither, so a protected keyword's WHY is only revealed after clicking (in the confirm dialog).
regressions.evidence: Legacy: KeywordDetailDrawer.tsx:716-732. Rebuilt: KeywordDrawer.tsx:239-249 (no title prop, no disclaimer).

regressions.issue: Rank cells lose the positionColor semantic coloring (top-3/10/20 tiering) that legacy applied to the position column and the drawer's current-position stat — ranks now render as plain monochrome text, removing an at-a-glance triage signal.
regressions.evidence: Legacy: shared/RankTable.tsx DataCell uses positionColor (:76, :526+); KeywordDetailDrawer.tsx:463-465. Rebuilt: KeywordsTable.tsx:299-303 plain span; KeywordDrawer.tsx:297 plain MetricTile.

confusions.issue: Four of the five lens tabs show the identical count (counts.total on rankings/opportunities/pages/lifecycle; only Clusters differs). A lens tray reading 'Rankings 512 · Opportunities 512 · Pages 512 · Clusters 14 · Lifecycle 512' looks like a counting bug and tells the operator nothing about what each lens will show.
confusions.why: Operators use tab counts to decide where to click; identical numbers on different-sounding views erode trust ('is opportunities everything too?'). The code comment (KeywordsSurface.tsx:74-84) explains it as a consistency choice pending server subset counts, but the UI can't show the comment.

confusions.issue: A workspace-wide 'Refresh national ranks' button sits inside a single keyword's drawer under 'Controls', directly beside per-keyword 'Pin' and 'Refresh local visibility' buttons — its scope reads as this-keyword-only but it fires the whole-workspace refresh (nationalRefresh.mutate() with no keyword arg, KeywordDrawer.tsx:498-510).
confusions.why: A paid, rate-limited workspace operation disguised as a per-row control invites accidental cost, and an operator wanting the refresh without a drawer open cannot find it at all.

confusions.issue: Pages/Clusters/Lifecycle lenses group only the current 50-row page. The Lifecycle board especially looks like the canonical stage board for the workspace, but a keyword on page 2 simply isn't on it; the warning banner ('Grouped from the first 50 keywords') mitigates but boards invite reading as complete.
confusions.why: An operator asking 'how many keywords are Published?' will trust column counts that are actually page-1 counts. Documented trade-off (DEF-kw-003) but still the surface's biggest comprehension trap.

confusions.issue: Two adjacent rows of visually identical FilterChips serve different axes — status filters above ('All / In strategy / Tracked…') and sort controls below ('Sort Opportunity / Rank / Clicks…'), while the DataTable's own column headers are NOT clickable for sorting.
confusions.why: Every mainstream table sorts by header click; chips-as-sort next to chips-as-filter (same primitive, same styling) forces a learned distinction the leading 'Sort' label only partially carries.

confusions.issue: The Opportunities lens 'Fix' column renders the recommended action as a Badge that is not clickable — the actual action lives in the drawer. Also, when an advanced ('More filters') filter is active, no primary chip (not even 'All') is highlighted and the active filter lives only in the small select.
confusions.why: A colored action-looking chip labelled 'Create brief' that does nothing on click reads as broken; an active-filter state with no highlighted chip makes the working set's scope hard to see at a glance.

confusions.issue: Placeholder vocabulary is inconsistent within the same drawer: money renders '—' (contract-mandated) but Rank/Opportunity/Difficulty tiles render the words 'No data' (numberOrEmpty, KeywordDrawer.tsx:83-85), and the table uses '—' throughout.
confusions.why: The contract's own judgment call (line 118) was to quiet-em-dash empty values; the loud 'No data' word placeholder in bright stat tiles is exactly what the table comment (KeywordsTable.tsx:122-124) warns against.

overcomplication.issue: Triple-instantiated rows query plumbing: KeywordsSurface owns the canonical rows result, KeywordsLenses conditionally creates its own (enabled: initialRowsResult == null), and KeywordsTable conditionally creates a third (enabled: externalRowsResult == null) — three useKeywordCommandCenterRows call sites for one query, threaded through two optional-prop layers.
overcomplication.evidence: KeywordsSurface.tsx:217-235, KeywordsLenses.tsx:260-263, KeywordsTable.tsx:162-165. DEF-kw-005 already records the redundant-work smell.
overcomplication.suggestion: Lift the rows query into a single hook owned by the surface (or the state hook) and pass the result unconditionally; delete both enabled-guard fallbacks.

overcomplication.issue: Heavy !important CSS override strings to bend shared primitives into the prototype geometry: KEYWORDS_HEADER_CLASS rewrites PageHeader's h2/p internals, SUMMARY_TILE_CLASS reorders MetricTile children via nth-child order swaps, and the DataTable row spacing is overridden with a five-selector arbitrary-variant string.
overcomplication.suggestion: Promote the needed variants (compact header, label-under-value tile, dense row) into the primitives as props instead of per-surface [&>div:nth-child(2)]:order-1 selector surgery — the next DS refactor of MetricTile/PageHeader silently breaks these.

overcomplication.issue: The Suspense fallback for the lazy drawer duplicates a full canonical Drawer shell (open/onClose/title/subtitle/eyebrow/width) that must be kept manually in sync with LazyKeywordDrawer's own Drawer props.
overcomplication.suggestion: Extract a shared KeywordDrawerShell (props object or wrapper) consumed by both the fallback and the loaded drawer so the two cannot drift.

gains: Five-lens interaction model (Rankings / Opportunities / Pages / Clusters / Lifecycle) with distinct table shapes — the Opportunities lens is a real triage view (Opportunity meter + 'Est. gain' + recommended Fix, KeywordsTable.tsx:397-478), and Pages/Clusters surface cannibalization-risk flags from summary data (KeywordsLenses.tsx:73-145) — none of which existed in legacy.

Fully URL-backed state: ?lens/?filter/?search/?sort/?direction/?page/?pageSize/?q are all validated, shareable, and survive reloads (useKeywordsSurfaceState.ts:80-141); legacy kept segment/search/sort/page in memory only.

The ?q= drawer deep link now fetches keyword detail directly and works even when the row is not in the loaded page (KeywordDrawer.tsx:135); legacy only opened the drawer if the keyword happened to match a row in the current 50-row page (KeywordHub.tsx:300-312).

Client keyword feedback panel (requested/declined/approved with per-row 'Add to strategy') now lives on the Keywords page itself (KeywordsSurface.tsx:427-460) — in legacy this capability lived only on the Strategy page.

Richer table row: Intent tag, Opportunity meter, and current monthly $ value columns (KeywordsTable.tsx:284-355) that the legacy list never showed; plus a 'Monthly value' summary KPI from trafficValueMonthly (KeywordsSurface.tsx:345-349).

Honesty fixes: bulk-selection count shows the current-page intersection the action will actually touch (KeywordsTable.tsx:534-547), grouped lenses carry explicit truncation banners scoped to the filtered total (KeywordsLenses.tsx:266-279), and lens counts refuse to fabricate subsets.

Tier-locked (402/403) handling with an explicit upgrade-path ErrorState (KeywordsSurface.tsx:283-299) and a stale-summary warning banner with retry (:355-366) — legacy had only a generic summary error band.

Toast feedback on every mutation (add, bulk, pin, delete, refreshes) instead of legacy's single shared error band; drawer close restores focus to the originating row and the drawer bundle is lazy-loaded with a canonical loading shell.
Sticky in-flow bulk-action bar that stays clickable over the sticky table header (KeywordsTable.tsx:534-571) versus legacy's viewport-fixed floating bar requiring page-bottom padding hacks.

extensions.opportunity: Give the AI-visibility panel a rebuilt home — either the anticipated dedicated 'AI Visibility' surface (prototype nav already reserves it, coverage-audit.md:55; GO-008 anticipates the receiver) or a temporary mount on the rebuilt Keywords surface.
extensions.value: Restores a live, unconditional production feature (share-of-voice, mention trend, refresh trigger) that is invisible the moment ui-rebuild-shell turns on; unblocks GO-008's LLMs.txt relocation too.

extensions.opportunity: Add a per-row overflow/quick-action affordance to the DataTable rows (reusing the drawer's runAction dispatcher and the existing protected-force confirm), restoring one-click Track/Retire/Restore from the list.
extensions.value: Removes the +1-drawer-click tax on the highest-frequency operator flow (triaging 50 rows of needs-review keywords).

extensions.opportunity: Wire keywordFeedback (and keywordStrategy) query invalidation into the surface's STRATEGY_UPDATED handler, or reuse the shared strategyMutationKeys list from wsInvalidation.ts.
extensions.value: Makes the new client-feedback panel live-update when clients submit feedback — closes the mandatory broadcast-both-halves gap.

extensions.opportunity: Ship the server-owned read models the ledger already scopes: batched history-by-keyword (DEF-kw-001) for inline sparklines + 7-day deltas, rollups/deltas (DEF-kw-002) for avg-position KPIs, and full-set grouping (DEF-kw-003) so Pages/Clusters/Lifecycle become true whole-universe views with per-lens distinct counts fixing the identical-count lens tray.
extensions.value: Converts the three biggest honesty caveats (page-scoped boards, no trends in rows, repeated lens counts) into the surface the lens IA promises.

extensions.opportunity: Restore the drawer's missing evidence slices cheaply from data already on the row/detail response: CTR + impressions tiles, 'Tracked since'/'Retired on' dates, awaiting-first-signal note, explanation.reasons, and the gap-origin 'View in Strategy' backlink.
extensions.value: Brings the drawer back to legacy evidentiary depth with no backend work — every field is already fetched and typed.

extensions.opportunity: Move 'Refresh national ranks' back to a page-level control (toolbar or KPI-strip action) and keep the drawer button per-keyword-scoped or clearly labelled 'all keywords'.
extensions.value: Fixes the scope-mismatch trap on a paid operation and restores discoverability when the national-serp-tracking flag turns on.

surfaceAudits.surface: content-pipeline (+ seo-briefs/content/calendar folds, subscriptions alias, content-perf receiving home)
surfaceAudits.headline: The rebuilt Content Pipeline is a genuine structural upgrade over the legacy peer-tab shell (item-backed lifecycle board, honest error/stale states, a real Published readback home), but it ships a racy cross-surface "draft brief" handoff, makes approved posts invisible in primary navigation, puts a matrix-cell count on the Published lens chip, and silently dropped the decay→per-page remediation path — none of which the parity contract records.

functionalityLost.capability: Reliable cross-surface 'Draft brief' handoff (fixContext prefill). Legacy App.tsx captures location.state.fixContext into React state (App.tsx:246-256) and passes it as a prop to the lazy legacy ContentPipeline (App.tsx:446), so the prefill survives chunk loading and even arrives while already mounted (ContentPipeline.tsx:132-136 switches to Briefs). The rebuilt surface receives only workspaceId (rebuiltSurfaces.ts:17-19, App.tsx:518) and reads location.state exclusively in useState initializers (ContentPipelineSurface.tsx:126-127); App.tsx's same effect still clears router state on a setTimeout(0) replace-navigation. When the content-pipeline chunk is cold (first visit of a session), the state is wiped before the lazy surface mounts and the handoff silently no-ops — no blank brief opens, no keyword prefill. Senders affected: keywords-rebuilt/KeywordDrawer.tsx:190-199, competitors-rebuilt/KeywordGapsCard.tsx:133, strategy/ContentGaps.tsx:103/126, StrategyCompetitiveTab.tsx:77, ContentDecay.tsx:261-266, PageIntelligenceSurface.tsx:199. The component test (ContentPipelineSurface.test.tsx:1595) mounts the surface synchronously so it cannot catch the lazy-chunk race.
functionalityLost.evidence: legacy src/App.tsx:246-256 + :446, src/components/ContentPipeline.tsx:132-136 vs rebuilt src/components/content-pipeline-rebuilt/ContentPipelineSurface.tsx:81-84,126-127; src/components/layout/rebuiltSurfaces.ts:50-52 (lazy); senders src/components/keywords-rebuilt/KeywordDrawer.tsx:190-199, src/components/ContentDecay.tsx:261-266

functionalityLost.capability: Visibility of approved (not yet delivered/published) posts. Legacy Posts tab was always one click away (ContentPipeline.tsx:46-48 TabBar) with an Approved status tile + filter, search, and sort (ContentManager.tsx:129-183). The rebuilt board excludes approved posts (ContentLifecycleBoard.tsx:130 'if (post.status === approved) continue'), the lens switcher has no Drafts/Posts entry (ContentPipelineSurface.tsx:88-94), and the Published lens shows only content-performance items (delivered/published requests), so an approved-but-unscheduled post appears nowhere in primary navigation. The ContentManager aggregate is reachable only via the ?tab=posts deep link or the Draft column's launcher card, which renders only when there are ZERO drafts (ContentLifecycleBoard.tsx:263-267). Calendar's schedule picker lists unscheduled drafts (ContentCalendar.tsx:113-116) but only inside a per-day popover.
functionalityLost.evidence: legacy src/components/ContentPipeline.tsx:42-48,317-319 + src/components/ContentManager.tsx:129-152 vs rebuilt src/components/content-pipeline-rebuilt/ContentLifecycleBoard.tsx:130,263-267 + ContentPipelineSurface.tsx:88-94
functionalityLost.whereDocumented: Contract line 135 documents ContentManager's demotion to a '?tab=posts compatibility receiver', but not that approved posts consequently lose every visible home.

functionalityLost.capability: Decay signal → per-page detail and prefilled refresh brief. Legacy pipeline's decay alert was a ClickableRow navigating to seo-audit?sub=content-decay (ContentPipeline.tsx:219-245), where ContentDecay offers per-page 'Draft refresh brief' that returns to the pipeline with the decaying pageSlug prefilled (ContentDecay.tsx:261-266). The rebuilt Content Health lens shows decay rows but clicking any row opens the Intake lane (ContentPipelineLenses.tsx:258 onRowClick → onOpenTab('intake')) — unrelated to the clicked page — and its 'Draft refresh brief' button opens a blank brief workspace with no page/keyword prefill (ContentPipelineLenses.tsx:268-271 → openBriefs, ContentPipelineSurface.tsx:323-332).
functionalityLost.evidence: legacy src/components/ContentPipeline.tsx:219-245 + src/components/ContentDecay.tsx:261-266 vs rebuilt src/components/content-pipeline-rebuilt/ContentPipelineLenses.tsx:258,267-272 + ContentPipelineSurface.tsx:355-359
functionalityLost.whereDocumented: Contract line 75/151 documents only the absence of a per-row 'Queue refresh' (no queue endpoint); the lost navigation to per-page decay detail and the lost prefill are unrecorded.

functionalityLost.capability: Cannibalization remediation detail inside the pipeline. Legacy rendered the detailed CannibalizationAlert: per-page paths with position/impressions/clicks/source, the recommended remediation action (canonical tag / 301 redirect / differentiate / noindex), canonical path, and recommendation text (CannibalizationAlert.tsx:99-141). The rebuilt Content Health row shows only keyword, competing-page count, and severity (ContentPipelineLenses.tsx:133-143), and row click goes to Intake. The detailed component still exists at the KeywordStrategy call site, so the capability survives elsewhere.
functionalityLost.evidence: legacy src/components/ContentPipeline.tsx:250-255 + src/components/ui/CannibalizationAlert.tsx:99-141 vs rebuilt src/components/content-pipeline-rebuilt/ContentPipelineLenses.tsx:133-143

functionalityLost.capability: AI Suggested card visibility for empty and snoozed-only states. Legacy always mounted AiSuggested on the main page — loading skeleton, empty state, and snoozed suggestions with 'Snoozed until' badges visible (ContentPipeline.tsx:261, AiSuggested.tsx:85-98,129-134). The rebuilt mounts AiSuggested only when pending suggestions exist (ContentPipelineIntake.tsx:68,123-127), so snoozed suggestions are invisible until they resurface and the intake count badge (pending-only) can disagree with the mounted list (which also shows snoozed).
functionalityLost.evidence: legacy src/components/ContentPipeline.tsx:261 + src/components/pipeline/AiSuggested.tsx:85-98 vs rebuilt src/components/content-pipeline-rebuilt/ContentPipelineIntake.tsx:68,123-127
functionalityLost.whereDocumented: Contract line 133 documents the Intake re-home ('AiSuggested: Intake lane'), not the pending-only gating.

functionalityLost.capability: Interactive trend chart in published readback. Legacy ContentPerformance expanded rows showed a recharts dual-axis line chart with hover tooltip (per-day clicks/impressions values) and date-range labels (ContentPerformance.tsx:36-73). Rebuilt drawer shows two static Sparklines with no hover values or date axis (PublishedContentLens.tsx:101-143).
functionalityLost.evidence: legacy src/components/ContentPerformance.tsx:36-73 vs rebuilt src/components/content-pipeline-rebuilt/PublishedContentLens.tsx:101-143
functionalityLost.whereDocumented: Contract §Owner-Approved 2026-07-11 approves 'one production Drawer for paired clicks/impressions trend' but does not itemize the loss of per-day tooltip values.

functionalityLost.capability: Calendar month-stat tiles (Briefs/Posts/Requests/Matrix Cells/Published for the month) hidden in pipeline Calendar mode via a CSS nth-child hack.
functionalityLost.evidence: legacy src/components/ContentCalendar.tsx:366-380 vs rebuilt src/components/content-pipeline-rebuilt/ContentPipelineLenses.tsx:302 ('[&>div>div:nth-child(2)]:hidden')
functionalityLost.whereDocumented: docs/ui-rebuild/parity/content-pipeline-contract.md line 73 — 'Compact the outer composition while preserving the broader production capability set.'

functionalityLost.capability: Growth-tier gate on cannibalization signals. Legacy wrapped the alert in TierGate(required='growth') and excluded free-tier workspaces from the alert count (ContentPipeline.tsx:176-177,250-255). Rebuilt shows all cannibalization rows regardless of tier with only an informational upgrade banner (ContentPipelineLenses.tsx:238-242).
functionalityLost.evidence: legacy src/components/ContentPipeline.tsx:176-177,250-255 vs rebuilt src/components/content-pipeline-rebuilt/ContentPipelineLenses.tsx:161,238-242

regressions.issue: The Published lens chip count is a different metric than the lens contents: MODE_OPTIONS uses pipelineData.summary.published, which useContentPipeline computes as the number of published MATRIX CELLS (allCells.filter(status==='published').length), while the Published lens lists content-performance items (delivered/published requests + matrix pieces from the shared read model). A workspace with 3 published request-backed posts and no matrices shows 'Published 0' on the switcher while the lens contains 3 result cards; a matrix-heavy workspace shows an inflated chip over a near-empty readback list.
regressions.evidence: src/components/content-pipeline-rebuilt/ContentPipelineSurface.tsx:91 (count: data?.summary?.published) + src/hooks/admin/useContentPipeline.ts:63-71 (published = matrix cells) vs src/components/content-pipeline-rebuilt/PublishedContentLens.tsx:312-315 (items from useAdminContentPerformance)

regressions.issue: Cross-surface fixContext handoff is intermittent (works with a warm chunk, silently drops with a cold one) because the rebuilt surface samples router state only in mount-time useState initializers while App.tsx clears that state on a 0ms timer — the same defect described under functionalityLost, listed here because it is timing-dependent rather than a designed-out capability.
regressions.evidence: src/App.tsx:246-256 + src/components/content-pipeline-rebuilt/ContentPipelineSurface.tsx:126-127 + src/components/layout/rebuiltSurfaces.ts:50-52

regressions.issue: Content Health rows are misleading controls: every decay and cannibalization row's click handler opens the Intake lane (requests/ideas/orders), which has no relationship to the clicked page or keyword. Legacy's equivalent click landed on the decay detail view.
regressions.evidence: src/components/content-pipeline-rebuilt/ContentPipelineLenses.tsx:258 (onRowClick={() => onOpenTab('intake')}) vs src/components/ContentPipeline.tsx:219-222

regressions.issue: Closing the capacity drawer after a ?tab=publish / ?tab=subscriptions deep link always rewrites the lens to 'briefs' (closeCapacity → state.setTab('briefs')), discarding any lens the operator had before following the link; legacy kept the operator on the Publish tab after interacting with subscriptions.
regressions.evidence: src/components/content-pipeline-rebuilt/ContentPipelineSurface.tsx:377-380 vs src/components/ContentPipeline.tsx:320-322

regressions.issue: Opening a ?post= deep link whose id no longer resolves still opens the full-screen Draft workspace drawer with generic 'Continue this draft' framing and rails in permanent 'Loading draft status…' state (postWorkspaceOpen = Boolean(state.postId), focusedPost lookup can be null); there is no 'post not found' state at the drawer level.
regressions.evidence: src/components/content-pipeline-rebuilt/ContentPipelineSurface.tsx:167-170,550 + ContentPipelineWorkspaces.tsx:212-215,366-375

confusions.issue: The 'Queued' board column can never contain anything: deriveLifecycleBoardItems only ever emits stages 'brief', 'draft', 'review' (requests live in Intake instead), yet Queued occupies a quarter of the 920px-min board and is the column highlighted when Intake focus is active.
confusions.why: An operator scanning the board reads a permanently-empty first column ('Nothing is waiting to be triaged') as either a broken feed or proof the tool is idle; the teal focus ring on an empty column when arriving via ?tab=intake reinforces the wrong mental model.

confusions.issue: ?tab=posts renders the legacy Drafts/Posts aggregate while the lens switcher highlights 'Board' (modeForTab maps 'posts' → 'board'), and no lens option can get you back to that aggregate once you leave it.
confusions.why: The visible mode selector claims you are on the Board while an entirely different list UI (status tiles, search, sort) is on screen; the operator cannot re-find this view from the UI, only from a bookmark.

confusions.issue: Workspace rails look interactive but are not: RailNav renders numbered 'Brief sections' / 'On this page' entries styled with active states inside a <nav> landmark, but items are plain divs with no click/scroll behavior, and the Draft rail's 'active' marker is hardcoded to the first section (active: index === 0).
confusions.why: Operators will click rail entries expecting scrollspy navigation of the brief/draft; nothing happens, and the always-first 'active' marker misreports where they are in the document.

confusions.issue: 'Draft refresh brief' on the Content Health lens opens a completely blank brief generator with no connection to any decaying page listed above it.
confusions.why: The button sits directly under a table of specific decaying URLs; an operator reasonably expects the brief to be seeded with the page they were just shown, and must instead re-type keyword and slug from memory.

confusions.issue: Intake badge counts pending items only, but the expanded lane's AI Suggested card renders snoozed items too (and legacy operators are used to seeing it on the main page).
confusions.why: The collapsed summary says '1 idea' while the expanded lane shows two entries, one badged 'Snoozed until …' — small arithmetic mismatches erode trust in every other count on the page.

overcomplication.issue: The permanent four-column board reserves a dead 'Queued' column (no producer code path) purely for prototype-shape parity, forcing min-w-[920px] horizontal scroll on smaller viewports for three columns of real content.
overcomplication.evidence: src/components/content-pipeline-rebuilt/ContentLifecycleBoard.tsx:97-156 (no stage 'queued' ever emitted) and :246-248 (column rendered unconditionally)
overcomplication.suggestion: Either wire requests/work-orders into Queued as true triage items (making Intake the expanded view of that column) or drop the column until a producer exists.

overcomplication.issue: Calendar compaction relies on a brittle CSS structural hack — '[&>div>div:nth-child(2)]:hidden' — to hide the legacy calendar's month-stats grid; any reordering of ContentCalendar's children silently hides the wrong block (and during Suspense it targets the loading component's children instead).
overcomplication.suggestion: Pass a prop (e.g. hideMonthStats) to ContentCalendar — it already takes 'embedded' — instead of addressing its DOM by position.

overcomplication.issue: Capacity drawer open-state is a union of two sources (local capacityDrawerOpen OR state.tab === 'publish') with asymmetric close behavior, plus four derived intelligence booleans (pending/unavailable/stale/available) to render one two-line button.
overcomplication.suggestion: Fold capacity into URL state exclusively (?tab=publish as the only open signal) so open/close is one source of truth and closing restores the previous lens.

overcomplication.issue: Brief/Draft workspace side rails re-derive readbacks (readiness checks, review checklist, section list) that the embedded production editor already displays, adding ~250 lines of parallel presentation whose data can drift from the editor's own state (e.g. rail checklist reads post.reviewChecklist while the editor mutates it live).
overcomplication.suggestion: Keep the rails but derive them from the same query objects the editor mutates (they already share React Query cache — fine today) and remove the static 'active' section marker until real scrollspy exists.

gains: Item-backed lifecycle board replaces the legacy peer-tab shell: every persisted brief/draft/review piece renders as one deduplicated card with source, age, title, keyword, page type, status, progress detail, and a next action (ContentLifecycleBoard.tsx:97-156) — legacy offered only tab launchers plus a count bar.

New Content Health mode: decay + cannibalization synthesized into metric tiles and an actable table with refresh-brief stats — legacy had only two dismissible alert bands behind a Disclosure (ContentPipelineLenses.tsx:147-277).

Published readback folded into the pipeline with verdict-led result cards (Strong win/Win/Loss/Early/Flat from outcome scores), OutcomeReadbackChip, status filters (All/Published/Delivered — new vs legacy), aggregate impressions/sessions band, position-gain arrow with sign-correct coloring, coverage/joinback drawer, and a View-live button legacy never had (PublishedContentLens.tsx).

Honesty upgrades: legacy ContentPerformance rendered a fabricated sparkline from clicks*0.6/0.8/1.0 (ContentPerformance.tsx:306-312) — removed in favor of real on-demand daily trend; legacy silently swallowed decay-fetch failures (.catch(() => null)) while the rebuilt surfaces ErrorState with retry and stale-data banners (ContentPipelineSurface.tsx:478-541).

Surface-level WebSocket invalidation for 8 event types including OUTCOME_SCORED and CONTENT_SUBSCRIPTION_UPDATED (ContentPipelineSurface.tsx:251-268), plus an explicit Refresh action with truthful success/failure toasts (awaitSuccessfulRefetches) — legacy had neither.

Capacity is now visible from every lens: live active-plan/pages-covered meter in the header opening a 440px subscription drawer, with pending/unavailable/stale states (ContentPipelineSurface.tsx:181-199,442-456).
Intake surfaces paid content work orders (pending/in-progress) alongside requests and AI ideas — a population legacy pipeline never showed (ContentPipelineIntake.tsx:69-70,129-158).
URL-persistent state everywhere (?tab, ?post, ?item survive refresh and are shareable) — legacy deliberately stripped ?tab on manual tab changes (ContentPipeline.tsx:77-83).
Draft workspace adds a generation-diagnostics rail for needs_attention posts (per-stage repair messages) and a review-checklist readback rail (ContentPipelineWorkspaces.tsx:212-313).
New 'New piece' topbar CTA and export links hardened with noopener,noreferrer (ContentPipelineSurface.tsx:270-273,399-401).

extensions.opportunity: Make the fixContext handoff robust: read location.state in a useEffect (or have App pass fixContext through RebuiltSurfaceProps like the legacy branch does) so the prefill survives lazy-chunk loading and arrives while the surface is already mounted.
extensions.value: Restores a heavily-used cross-surface workflow (6+ sender surfaces) to legacy reliability and removes an intermittent, hard-to-reproduce operator complaint.

extensions.opportunity: Fix the Published lens chip to use the shared content-performance summary (summary.piecesPublished from useAdminContentPerformance) instead of matrix-cell counts, and consider making the Content Health chip count decay+cannibalization rows rather than decay pages only.
extensions.value: Every count on the primary mode switcher becomes the count of what the lens actually shows — direct trust win.

extensions.opportunity: Give approved posts a home: either include an 'Approved' column/section on the board or add a Drafts lens entry so the ContentManager aggregate (with its approved filter, search, sort, send-to-client) is reachable without a bookmark.
extensions.value: Closes the 'my post vanished after I approved it' gap between review and delivery/scheduling.

extensions.opportunity: Wire Content Health rows to real destinations: decay rows open per-page decay detail (or at minimum prefill 'Draft refresh brief' with the row's pageUrl/keyword via the existing buildSignalPrefill), cannibalization rows open the detailed remediation view that already exists in CannibalizationAlert/Keyword Hub.
extensions.value: Turns the health lens from a read-only report into the maintenance loop the prototype promised, without the queue endpoint the contract defers.

extensions.opportunity: Board ergonomics for scale: search/filter within the board (keyword, source, age) and a real 'Queued' producer (requests awaiting triage), reusing FilterChip/SearchField primitives already used by the Published lens.
extensions.value: The alphabetical four-column board is excellent at 10 items and unusable at 60; agencies with content subscriptions will hit that quickly.

extensions.opportunity: Add hover values/date range to the Sparkline trend in the Published drawer (or a compact tooltip layer on the existing primitive).
extensions.value: Restores the per-day inspection capability legacy operators used to verify decay/spike claims before client calls.

surfaceAudits.headline: The rebuild is a genuine structural upgrade (URL-addressable state, demand mix finally surfaced, prior-period overlay, anomaly home, richer annotations) but ships two undocumented regressions — an uncapped up-to-500-row Detail table that buries the contract-critical lower Search band, and a clicks/users series color collision that defeats the hidden cross-source Overview — plus a scatter of small GA4 engagement-field losses nobody recorded.

functionalityLost.capability: Overview (cross-source GSC+GA4) as a visible, default report
functionalityLost.evidence: Legacy: src/components/AnalyticsHub.tsx:19-27 (Overview is first tab and default). Rebuilt: src/components/search-traffic-rebuilt/useSearchTrafficSurfaceState.ts:6-21 (three visible lenses, DEFAULT_LENS='search', 'overview' accepted only as hidden URL value); OverviewLens.tsx renders only via ?lens=overview.
functionalityLost.whereDocumented: docs/ui-rebuild/parity/analytics-hub-contract.md:49-60 (owner-approved 2026-07-10, hidden compatibility receiver) and rebuilt-surface-inventory.md:19

functionalityLost.evidence: Legacy: src/components/SearchDetail.tsx:352-424 (inline sidebar). Rebuilt: behind a Breakdowns button → Drawer (SearchLens.tsx:329-333, BreakdownsDrawer.tsx:51-84) — one extra click, no share-percentage bars.

Rebuilt: TrafficLens.tsx:233-241 shows only share, organic user count, and organic engagementRate — organicBounceRate and avgEngagementTime (shared/types/analytics.ts:172,174) have no rendered home anywhere in the rebuilt dir.

Rebuilt: TrafficLens.tsx:269-283 renders only users count + engagement rate as a KeyValueRow — percentage and avgEngagementTime (shared/types/analytics.ts:164-165) dropped.
