functionalityLost.evidence: Legacy: src/components/TrafficDetail.tsx:245-250 ('Top page avg. engagement' row). Rebuilt: no home — TrafficLens Engagement analysis omits it and the Drawer's top-pages columns are path/views/users/sessions only (BreakdownsDrawer.tsx:38-43).

functionalityLost.capability: Explicit GA4 failure state with the error message and a Retry button
functionalityLost.evidence: Legacy: src/components/TrafficDetail.tsx:101-111 ('Failed to Load Analytics' + error text + Retry that invalidates ga4All). Rebuilt: TrafficLens.tsx:96-104 collapses error+no-data into a generic 'No GA4 data' EmptyState; the error text only appears in the report-header narrative (SearchTrafficSurface.tsx:86-90) and recovery requires knowing the chrome Re-scan button doubles as retry.

functionalityLost.capability: No-site gate CTA to Settings
functionalityLost.evidence: Legacy: src/App.tsx:420-430 ('Link a Webflow site…' with a 'Go to Settings' button). Rebuilt mounts bypass that gate (App.tsx:489-520) and the surface's own gate (SearchTrafficSurface.tsx:207-219) is an ErrorState with no action button.

regressions.issue: The Detail table renders every returned GSC row (up to 500 queries / 500 pages) directly into page flow with no height cap, pagination, or virtualization — legacy contained the same data in a sidebar-height scroll box. On any workspace with real query volume this makes the Search report tens of thousands of pixels tall and buries the owner-approved lower band (Demand mix, Priority insights, Monitoring & insights) that the parity contract fought to keep 'mounted exactly once'. The DataTable sticky header also only sticks inside its own overflow-x container, so column context is lost while page-scrolling.
regressions.evidence: Legacy: src/components/SearchDetail.tsx:248-250,279 (maxHeight from sidebar ref + overflow-y-auto). Rebuilt: SearchLens.tsx:390-396 passes full rows to DataTable inside an uncapped ChartCard; src/components/ui/DataTable.tsx:122 has only overflow-x-auto; server default is 500 query rows + 500 page rows (server/search-console.ts:226-227). Not recorded in the contract, parity-backlog, or the deferred ledger (only DEF-search-traffic-001 exists, about insight graduation).

regressions.issue: Series color collision in the cross-source Overview receiver: users uses the same token as clicks (both var(--blue)) and sessions the same as impressions (both var(--cyan)). The receiver's default active lines are clicks + users — two indistinguishable blue lines in the one view whose entire purpose is telling GSC and GA4 movement apart. Legacy kept users visually distinct (teal). The contract documents the retint away from teal but not that the replacement collides with the GSC palette.
regressions.evidence: Rebuilt: src/components/search-traffic-rebuilt/searchTrafficUtils.ts:14-24 (SERIES.clicks and SERIES.users both 'var(--blue)'; SERIES.impressions and SERIES.sessions both 'var(--cyan)'); OverviewLens.tsx:61 (default toggles ['clicks','users']). Legacy: src/components/AnalyticsOverview.tsx:20-27 (users=teal, sessions=blue, clicks=blue — never both blues active by default on the same axis).

regressions.issue: Column sort direction flipped: first click on a metric column now sorts ascending (smallest clicks/impressions first); legacy sorted descending first, which is what an operator scanning top performers wants.
regressions.evidence: Rebuilt: src/components/ui/DataTable.tsx cycleSort (first transition sets 'ascending', ~line 90). Legacy: src/components/SearchDetail.tsx:128-131 (new sort key → sortAsc=false, i.e. descending first).

regressions.issue: KPI delta badges lost their units and sign: percent deltas render as bare '12.3 vs prior' (no % or +) because MetricTile passes suffix='' to TrendBadge and TrendBadge shows Math.abs(value). Legacy showed '+12.3%'. Direction survives only via the tiny trend icon; magnitude unit is ambiguous (percent vs absolute).
regressions.evidence: Rebuilt: src/components/ui/MetricTile.tsx:79 (suffix='') + src/components/ui/TrendBadge.tsx:50-52 (abs value, no sign without showSign). Legacy: src/components/SearchDetail.tsx:119-123 and AnalyticsOverview.tsx:94-99 (explicit sign + '%'/'pt' suffix).

regressions.issue: 'Data as of Jul 14, 8:00 AM' fabricates a clock time — the code appends 'T12:00:00.000Z' to the provider's date-only window end and formats it with hour/minute, presenting invented precision as a data freshness timestamp.
regressions.evidence: Rebuilt: src/components/search-traffic-rebuilt/SearchTrafficSurface.tsx:162 (`${dataWindow.end}T12:00:00.000Z`) + searchTrafficUtils.ts:6-11 (DATE_TIME_FORMAT includes hour+minute). Legacy showed only the raw date range (TrafficDetail.tsx:127).

regressions.issue: The domain insight feed (with filter chips) was always visible mid-page on legacy Search/Traffic tabs; rebuilt collapses it (plus AnomalyAlerts) inside a closed-by-default Disclosure at the very bottom of a potentially enormous report — the contract documents the 'monitoring last' ordering but not that it ships collapsed.
regressions.evidence: Legacy: src/components/SearchDetail.tsx:233-241 and TrafficDetail.tsx:194-202 (InsightFeed always rendered, step 3/4 of the page). Rebuilt: SearchLens.tsx:400-415 and TrafficLens.tsx:321-335 inside <Disclosure> with no defaultOpen (src/components/ui/Disclosure.tsx:35 renders closed).

regressions.issue: Growth signals 'Duration change' lost its direction: rebuilt renders formatDuration(Math.abs(...)) with no sign or color, so an operator cannot tell whether session duration went up or down; legacy rendered an explicit +/- prefix.
regressions.evidence: Rebuilt: src/components/search-traffic-rebuilt/TrafficLens.tsx:264. Legacy: src/components/TrafficDetail.tsx:167 (explicit '+'/'-' before formatDuration).

confusions.issue: The unified cross-source trend is retained but has zero visible affordance — no button, link, or menu item anywhere reaches ?lens=overview (repo grep shows no senders append it). A returning operator whose muscle memory is 'Analytics opens the combined Overview' lands on Search performance with no discoverable path back to the combined view.
confusions.why: Knowledge of a hand-typed URL parameter is tribal; the capability effectively exists only for people who read the parity contract. Owner-approved as direction, but the contract itself says 'circle back if operator evidence shows the compatibility-only trend needs a visible home' — a fresh operator cannot generate that evidence because they cannot find the view.

confusions.issue: Metric tiles are inconsistently interactive: Clicks/Impressions/CTR/Position and Users/Sessions are chart-line toggles (cursor + teal active border), while Bounce rate and Avg duration are visually identical tiles that do nothing when clicked.
confusions.why: Same component, same styling, different behavior — an operator clicking Bounce rate to add it to the chart gets silent nothing; the only affordance difference is a subtle border on already-active tiles. Legacy MetricToggleCard had an explicit displayOnly treatment.

confusions.issue: The new prior-period clicks overlay joins the prior window to the current window by array index (priorTrend[index]), not by date offset — if GSC returns a prior window with missing days, prior values silently shift onto the wrong current dates.
confusions.why: An operator comparing 'this Tuesday vs prior Tuesday' on the chart trusts the vertical alignment; an off-by-days misalignment is exactly the kind of silent data lie that erodes trust in the whole report, and nothing in the UI signals the join method.

overcomplication.issue: A whole fourth hidden lens ('overview') is maintained — its own component, lens-conditional GSC/GA4 metric matrices in useSearchTrafficData, URL canonicalization special-cases, and 'exact-once' mount tests — for a compatibility receiver that no code in the app links to (grep shows zero ?lens=overview senders; all cross-surface navigations to analytics-hub are bare).
overcomplication.evidence: src/components/search-traffic-rebuilt/useSearchTrafficSurfaceState.ts:18-21,47-57; useSearchTrafficData.ts:23-33; OverviewLens.tsx (212 lines); repo grep for 'analytics-hub' navigations shows only bare adminPath calls (WorkspaceHome.tsx:443,482; PageIntelligenceSurface.tsx:215; CockpitSurface.tsx:76; DiagnosticsListView.tsx:37,57).
overcomplication.suggestion: Decide its fate: either promote it as a visible 'Combined' option in the LensSwitcher (one array entry) or schedule retirement with a redirect to bare Search — carrying a bookmark-only lens with its own fetch matrix forever is exactly the flag-retirement-style debt CLAUDE.md warns about.

overcomplication.issue: Two parallel annotation mutation stacks now exist and have already drifted: AnnotationsLens hand-rolls its own useMutation create/update/delete trio (with toasts and pageUrl support) while the shared useCreateAnnotation/useUpdateAnnotation/useDeleteAnnotation hooks — still used by SearchLens, TrafficLens, and legacy — have a typed body that omits pageUrl and no toast feedback.
overcomplication.evidence: src/components/search-traffic-rebuilt/AnnotationsLens.tsx:87-125 (local mutations, pageUrl) vs src/hooks/admin/useAnalyticsAnnotations.ts:9-17,31-40 (Annotation interface and create body lack pageUrl); rebuilt duplicates the type locally as RebuiltAnnotation (types.ts:28-37).
overcomplication.suggestion: Fold pageUrl and toast callbacks into the shared hooks and delete the local trio + RebuiltAnnotation type — the CLAUDE.md 'extract shared interaction patterns' rule exists precisely because this drift (chart-created annotations can never carry pageUrl) is already live.

overcomplication.issue: SearchContextBand is mounted from four separate code paths (SearchLens configured/loading-error/no-overview branches plus OverviewLens) with an unavailableMessage prop threaded through to guarantee the 'exactly once' contract, which the component tests then have to police.
overcomplication.suggestion: Mount the band once at the surface level below the lens outlet (it only needs lens + searchData) and drop the per-branch duplication and the exact-once test burden.

extensions.opportunity: Cap the Detail table (e.g. first 25 rows + 'Show all 500' expander, or a max-height scroll region matching legacy) and add an in-table text filter for queries/pages.
extensions.value: Fixes the major burial regression and finally gives operators the 'find my query in 500 rows' tool neither legacy nor rebuilt has — the DataTable structure makes both trivial now.

extensions.opportunity: Give GA4 series their own hues (e.g. keep clicks/impressions on blue/cyan, move users/sessions to emerald/orange from the chart palette) in SERIES.
extensions.value: Restores source distinguishability in the cross-source receiver and makes the per-lens tiles' accents actually encode provenance, which the contract's 'operator can tell which source produced each number' requirement asks for.

extensions.opportunity: Add action CTAs to the gate states: 'Open Workspace Settings' on the no-site / GSC-unconfigured / GA4-unconfigured empty states, matching the legacy Go-to-Settings affordance.
extensions.value: Every unconfigured state currently dead-ends; a one-click path to the fix is standard EmptyState practice in this codebase.

extensions.opportunity: Surface pageUrl-scoped annotations: now that the UI writes pageUrl, filter chart markers by the page rows in view and show a page-link chip on markers.
extensions.value: Turns the new pageUrl field from stored-but-inert metadata into the 'annotations explain traffic movement' story the contract sells.

extensions.opportunity: Date-keyed join for the prior-trend overlay (align by date offset rather than array index) plus a dashed style legend note ('prior period, shifted').
extensions.value: Removes the silent misalignment hazard in the surface's flagship new comparison feature.

gains: URL-addressable report state — ?lens=, ?days=, ?view= deep links with validation and canonicalization (useSearchTrafficSurfaceState.ts); legacy kept all tab/date/table state in ephemeral useState with no shareable URLs

Branded vs non-branded Demand mix band — the server has attached brandedDemand to the GSC overview (server/routes/google.ts:337-351) but legacy never rendered it anywhere; the rebuilt SearchContextBand surfaces it with honest status/error states

Prior-period clicks overlay on the Search trend chart with a dedicated prior-trend fetch (useSearchTrafficData.ts:62-67 hitting performance-trend?previous=true) and a 'prior loading' indicator — legacy had no period-over-period line
Sparklines on every KPI tile (SparkMetricTile) giving in-tile daily shape; legacy MetricToggleCards were value+delta only
Actionable AnomalyAlerts home on both Search and Traffic lenses (SearchLens.tsx:402, TrafficLens.tsx:323) — in legacy, anomalies lived only on WorkspaceHome, never on the analytics surface
Ranking Movement section (Biggest gains / Needs attention) giving ranking_mover insights a scannable report home (SearchLens.tsx:351-364)
Strategy-keyword star markers and read-only Top 20 badges on query rows (SearchLens.tsx:91-94), joining the strategy keyword set client-side — absent from legacy SearchDetail

Annotation upgrades: optional pageUrl on create/edit/display (server supported it; legacy UI never exposed it), success/error toasts, per-category filter counts, colored category rail, and a contextual annotated trend chart inside the Annotations report (legacy tab was a bare CRUD list with no chart)
Explicit Re-scan action that invalidates all provider/insight/anomaly/strategy caches in one click (SearchTrafficSurface.tsx:148-155)
Narrative verdict headers per report ('Search clicks are up 12.3% this period…') replacing the static 'Analytics' title
Landing pages and conversions become sortable DataTables, conversions visible by default (contract-documented), and landing pages no longer truncated to 20 rows

Traffic Breakdowns Drawer adds full country/sources detail with users columns; Search Drawer gives countries the full clicks/impressions/CTR/position metric set (legacy sidebar showed only clicks + position for top 8)
Skeleton loading states and stale-data InlineBanners (data kept visible during refetch errors) replacing legacy's spinner/blank treatments
One unified date-range control across all reports including 14d and 16mo for both sources, with the overflow presets in a compact More menu

surfaceAudits.headline: The rebuilt Engine is a genuinely richer operator command surface than the legacy Strategy page — value frame, client preview, staged projections, recommendation history, and far better error/stale states — but it silently drops the OrientZone visibility-score/delta strip and the admin content-gaps evidence list with its pre-seeded gap-to-brief handoff, neither of which is recorded in the parity contract.
legacySources: src/App.tsx:437 (flag-OFF branch renders KeywordStrategyPanel for tab seo-strategy)
src/components/KeywordStrategy.tsx (KeywordStrategyPanel, 1045 lines, three interior branches: flag-OFF v2 / command-center / the-issue)

functionalityLost.capability: OrientZone strategy-momentum readout: CTR-weighted visibility score ring + deterministic verdict + 4-stat delta strip (clicks / impressions / ranked keywords / avg position vs previous strategy generation). Rebuilt keeps only a static 'Average position' tile with no deltas, no visibility score, no clicks/impressions trend.
functionalityLost.evidence: Legacy: src/components/KeywordStrategy.tsx:509-511 (orientEl) rendered in ALL three branches — :783 (the-issue 'Lenses & surfaces' disclosure), :873 (command-center), :912 (flag-OFF); component src/components/strategy/OrientZone.tsx:41-50. Rebuilt: src/components/engine-rebuilt/EngineSurface.tsx:697-707 renders avg position only; `grep -rln OrientZone|visibilityScore src/components` matches no rebuilt surface (only KeywordStrategy.tsx and client components). Not listed in engine-contract.md Carry-Over Homes (lines 106-121) and absent from parity-backlog.md.

functionalityLost.capability: Admin content-gaps evidence list with pre-seeded execution handoffs: intent chips, SERP-feature/AI-Overview badges, and the 'generate content' / 'create brief' CTAs that carry the full computed gap context into content-pipeline. In the rebuilt shell no admin surface renders strategy.contentGaps as a list; the gap-to-pre-seeded-brief workflow is gone (gaps still flow into recommendations server-side via finalization.ts:72 content_gap→topic_cluster and appear per-keyword in the KCC drawer, so mitigation is partial).
functionalityLost.evidence: Legacy: src/components/KeywordStrategy.tsx:467 (leaf), :798 (the-issue 'Diffs & gaps'), :942/:951 (Content tab); CTAs at src/components/strategy/ContentGaps.tsx:99-143 (navigate to content-pipeline with pre-seeded brief context). Rebuilt: no import of ContentGaps/ContentGapRow outside legacy + client (`grep -rln ContentGapRow src/components`); `grep -rn contentGaps src/components/content-pipeline-rebuilt src/components/keywords-rebuilt` returns nothing. engine-contract.md:125 only covers 'content execution'; no gap-evidence record in parity-backlog.md or content-pipeline-contract.md.

functionalityLost.capability: StrategyHowItWorks explainer (data-mode/provenance education: what the strategy is built from, ranking-evidence caveats). Legacy rendered it inline (flag-OFF) or as a '?' tooltip in the page header (command-center).
functionalityLost.evidence: Legacy: src/components/KeywordStrategy.tsx:286-303 (tooltip), :505/:930 (inline). Rebuilt: no import in src/components/engine-rebuilt/ (grep).

functionalityLost.capability: AIContextIndicator on the pre-strategy empty state (shows what AI context is available for strategy generation).
functionalityLost.evidence: Legacy: src/components/KeywordStrategy.tsx:339-341. Rebuilt empty state renders StrategyEmptyState only: src/components/engine-rebuilt/EngineSurface.tsx:740-743.

functionalityLost.capability: NextStepsCard post-generation guidance ('Strategy ready → Review Quick Wins') and the flag-OFF fallback sections (QuickWins/LowHangingFruit/KeywordGaps) shown when the recommendation queue is empty — a strategy-with-zero-recommendations workspace now shows an empty Backing Moves queue with no strategy-blob fallback.
functionalityLost.evidence: Legacy: src/components/KeywordStrategy.tsx:405-419 (NextStepsCard), :913-918 (actQueueEl ?? quickWins/lhf/keywordGaps fallback, flag-OFF branch only — the richest the-issue branch had no fallback either). Rebuilt: src/components/engine-rebuilt/EngineSurface.tsx:933-951 renders BackingMovesQueue unconditionally with activeRecs.

functionalityLost.capability: Lost visibility recovery band never renders for admins: LostQueryRecoveryCard reads the portal-authenticated public insights feed and 401s under admin auth, so the contract's 'Lost visibility / recovery content' evidence section silently self-nulls (also emits a console 401 on every Engine load). Not a legacy loss — legacy never mounted it — but a contract-required section that is dead in production.
functionalityLost.evidence: Rebuilt: src/components/engine-rebuilt/EngineSurface.tsx:875 mounts it; src/hooks/admin/useLostVisibility.ts:15 fetches /api/public/insights/:workspaceId; card returns null on empty (src/components/strategy/LostQueryRecoveryCard.tsx:20).
functionalityLost.whereDocumented: docs/ui-rebuild/parity/engine-contract.md:169 and :221 — ODP-001-V6 owner-approved missing-band exception with a separate backend-contract task

functionalityLost.capability: Interior Content / Rankings / Competitive tabs dissolved into cross-surface redirects (?tab=content → content-pipeline?tab=content-health, ?tab=rankings → seo-keywords?lens=rankings, ?tab=competitive → competitors). Verified the receivers carry the capabilities: decay + topic clusters in content-pipeline/keywords rebuilt, rankings/opportunities/rank-tracking/client-keyword-feedback in keywords-rebuilt (KeywordsSurface.tsx:221-266, 429), StrategyCompetitiveTab in competitors.

regressions.issue: The Shared operator queue's WorkStreamSelector shows a false active state: when the filter is 'all' (the default) or 'unclassified' (Risk), the value passed to the selector is coerced to 'opt', so the Optimize card renders aria-checked=true with the active teal border while the 'All' or 'Risk' FilterChip is the real active filter — two adjacent controls report contradictory state, and the radio group's accessibility state is wrong on first open.
regressions.evidence: src/components/engine-rebuilt/EngineWorkQueue.tsx:125 (`primaryStream = ... ? stream : 'opt'`) + :172-173 (All/Risk chips with their own active state) vs src/components/ui/co/WorkStreamSelector.tsx:69,77,86 (active = option.id === value → aria-checked + teal border).

regressions.issue: Fetched-and-discarded data on every Engine mount: useEngineRebuilt fires useContentDecay unconditionally but no engine-rebuilt component consumes engine.contentDecay (legacy used it for the deleted Content-tab empty check). Similarly moveQueueItems/moveQueueSourceCounts are memo-computed on every recommendation change but have no production consumer (residue of the removed duplicate move-index queue; only the test fixture references them).
regressions.evidence: src/hooks/admin/useEngineRebuilt.ts:149 (useContentDecay, enabled by default per src/hooks/admin/useContentDecay.ts:16) and :201-205 (moveQueueItems); `grep -rn 'contentDecay\|moveQueueItems' src/components/engine-rebuilt/` returns nothing.

regressions.issue: EngineMoveDrawer stamps a hard-coded ProvenanceChip basis='estimate' on every move's evidence block regardless of the recommendation's actual grounding (recs carry a 0.4-1.0 grounded-vs-LLM confidence, shared/types/recommendations.ts:153); a chip that never varies conveys no information and would mislabel any measured-basis gain.
regressions.evidence: src/components/engine-rebuilt/EngineMoveDrawer.tsx:77 (`<ProvenanceChip basis="estimate" />` unconditional).

confusions.issue: 'Backing moves live' means two different numbers on the same page: the value-frame tile shows activeRecs.length (active-only), while the client trust-spine preview tile with the same label shows `curatedCount / cockpitRecs.length` — a denominator that includes completed and dismissed history rows (pinned deliberately by test 'uses the full recommendation set as the client-preview ratio denominator').
confusions.why: An operator reconciling the two tiles (e.g. '5' up top, '3 / 42' in the preview) has no visible explanation for why 'live' totals differ; per the project's own rate-display rule, same-label metrics that don't share a denominator erode trust.

confusions.issue: Six ?lens= values (spine/changes/signals/pov/moves/operations) all render the identical full page and only scroll/focus a section, plus a separate ?stream= param and source-type chips that filter a queue hidden inside the collapsed 'Setup and operator tools' disclosure.
confusions.why: An operator following a shared ?lens=pov link expects a POV view and instead lands on the full spine scrolled mid-page; the ?stream= URL state controls something invisible until a disclosure is opened. This is the owner-ratified ODP-001-A model, but it remains a first-use stumble.

confusions.issue: Two different 'refresh' semantics sit adjacent in the topbar: the Engine 'Refresh' button (free cache invalidate + refetch, toasts 'Engine refresh started') next to StrategyHeaderActions' refresh/regenerate actions (paid data refresh + strategy regeneration).
confusions.why: A busy operator wanting fresh numbers can trigger a paid full data refresh — or believe the free refetch re-ran the strategy — because both read as 'refresh' in the same control cluster (EngineSurface.tsx:395-421).

overcomplication.issue: Brittle descendant-selector surgery on DS primitives instead of presentation variants: EngineSurface styles SectionCard internals with chains like `[&>div:first-child]:flex-col ... max-sm:[&>div:first-child_.t-body]:whitespace-normal` in three places, and overrides LensSwitcher buttons the same way.
overcomplication.suggestion: Add a compact/stacked-header presentation prop to SectionCard (the codebase already threads `presentation='engine-spine'` through BackingMovesQueue/StrategyDiff/lenses — the same pattern belongs on the card) so a SectionCard DOM refactor cannot silently break Engine layout.

overcomplication.issue: The same POV narrative renders four times on one page: derived verdict headline (CommandCenterVerdict), compact POV card, full Drawer editor, and the trust-spine preview verdict — each with its own loading/unavailable copy variants.
overcomplication.suggestion: Owner-approved (V1/V4), but the loading/error copy for the four renderings could be centralized into one POV-presentation helper so the fallback strings can't drift.

overcomplication.issue: Dead model residue in the hook inflates the surface's data model: an unconsumed content-decay fetch and unused move-queue projections are computed on every mount/rec change.
overcomplication.suggestion: Delete moveQueueItems/moveQueueSourceCounts and the useContentDecay call (or pass enabled:false until a consumer exists); update the test fixture accordingly.

gains: Value frame with measured dollars: pipeline value at stake / recovered so far from the workspace-home money frame, with honest provenance chips ('proof pending' fallback) and freshness metadata — legacy strategy page never showed measured value (EngineSurface.tsx:662-717).
Client trust-spine preview: a light-theme portal frame showing exactly what the client will see (verdict, value, proof row) before send (EngineSurface.tsx:206-320).

Staged-move projection lenses: restored KeywordTargetsLens (legacy the-issue v1 dropped it for a bare deep-link row) plus ContentWorkOrderLens, both filtered to the staged-and-sendable set so Stage/Unstage updates the projection live (EngineSurface.tsx:110-204; ContentWorkOrderLens.tsx:106-114).
Read-only move evidence drawer (EngineMoveDrawer) with cannibalization competing-page/keeper evidence — legacy queue rows had no detail drawer.

AUD-D1 absorbed workspace-home capabilities into Operations: full terminal recommendation history grouped by 8 lifecycle categories with guarded un-dismiss, Weekly Briefing review queue (flag-gated), and SEO Change Impact (EngineOperations.tsx:123-168; EngineRecommendationHistory.tsx).

Shared operator work queue with stream selector, All/Risk chips, and per-source-type filters plus typed cross-surface handoffs to requests/content-pipeline/keywords/audit/settings (EngineWorkQueue.tsx; EngineSurface.tsx:423-449).
Cannibalization keeper overrides mounted exactly once under Operations with optimistic query-authoritative writes (EngineOperations.tsx:237-272).

Dramatically better data-state honesty than legacy's single spinner: skeleton loading, distinct retryable errors per read (strategy vs aggregate vs recommendations vs POV), and cached-data-with-stale-warning banners instead of blanking (EngineSurface.tsx:509-552, 719-727, 786-831, 898-931).
Surface-owned WebSocket invalidation across 10 workspace events so Engine stays live even outside the legacy dashboard invalidator (useEngineRebuilt.ts:222-241).

Honest deep-link handling: invalid ?lens/?tab shows a warning banner instead of legacy's silent fallback; all four legacy ?tab= aliases preserved (useEngineSurfaceState.ts:44-83; EngineSurface.tsx:570-585).

Send action always visible once staged (fixed shell topbar) — better than the legacy scroll-dependent docked bar; server-fingerprint POV refreshAvailable replaces the legacy local staleness heuristic (won't false-positive, pinned by test).

extensions.opportunity: Close the ODP-001-V6 backend task: add an admin-authenticated lost-visibility read (or admit admin auth on the insights feed) so the contract's 'Lost visibility / recovery content' band actually renders and the per-load console 401 disappears.
extensions.value: A contract-required evidence section stops being dead code; removes the only remaining console error on the surface.

extensions.opportunity: Re-home the OrientZone metrics: extend the value frame with the visibility score and clicks/impressions/ranked-keyword deltas vs the previous generation (the data already ships on strategy.strategyUx.orient).
extensions.value: Restores the headline strategy-v2 momentum readout operators used to gauge whether the strategy is working — the largest undocumented parity hole.

extensions.opportunity: Restore a content-gap evidence home with the pre-seeded create-brief handoff — e.g. a 'Content gaps' group in the projection section or content-pipeline intake consuming strategy.contentGaps with its computed context payload.
extensions.value: Re-enables the one-click gap→brief workflow (with SERP/AI-Overview evidence) that currently requires manual re-entry in content-pipeline.

extensions.opportunity: Pass null to WorkStreamSelector when stream is 'all'/'unclassified' (and render no active card) so the selector and chips can never disagree.
extensions.value: Removes the misleading default-selected Optimize card and fixes the wrong aria-checked state.

extensions.opportunity: Add the contract-suggested ?projection=keywords|content URL state to the projection lens, and surface real per-rec grounding (opportunity.confidence) in EngineMoveDrawer instead of the static 'estimate' chip.
extensions.value: Deep-linkable projections for handoffs; move evidence provenance becomes informative instead of decorative.

surfaceAudits.headline: The rebuilt Asset Manager is a genuine upgrade — URL-backed deep links, quota-aware AI actions, a real asset detail drawer, and safer/more consistent bulk operations — but it ships three undocumented regressions: upload is now impossible for workspaces without a linked Webflow site, audit rows keep a stale (deleted) asset id after compression, and the audit health score is a permanently dead metric dressed in 'awaiting server score' copy.

functionalityLost.capability: Upload and processing queue for workspaces WITHOUT a linked Webflow site. Legacy rendered the Upload sub-tab (DropZones + processing queue) unconditionally — only Browse and Audit were siteId-gated — and the server upload route is pure folder-based optimization with no Webflow dependency. The rebuilt surface early-returns a 'Link a Webflow site' EmptyState for the ENTIRE surface, making UI-driven upload/optimization impossible for unlinked workspaces (only the global clipboard-paste path survives).
functionalityLost.evidence: Legacy: src/components/MediaTab.tsx:51-66 (upload sub-tab has no siteId gate; contrast browse/audit gates at :69-92); server/routes/misc.ts:35-51 (upload → triggerOptimize, no site needed). Rebuilt: src/components/asset-manager-rebuilt/AssetManagerSurface.tsx:777-788 (whole-surface !siteId EmptyState: 'needs a connected Webflow site before it can list, audit, or upload media').

functionalityLost.capability: Audit health score as a rendered number. Legacy computed the score client-side (100 - issueCount/totalAssets ratio) and displayed it in the summary card. Rebuilt only renders a server-provided audit.healthScore — and the server audit endpoint never returns that field — so the tile is permanently '—' with 'Awaiting server score'.
functionalityLost.evidence: Legacy: src/components/AssetAudit.tsx:257-258,271-273 (client-computed score displayed). Rebuilt: src/components/asset-manager-rebuilt/AuditLens.tsx:499,519-535 (serverScore only, fallback tile + banner); server/routes/webflow-audit.ts:130-140 (response shape has no healthScore).
functionalityLost.whereDocumented: docs/ui-rebuild/parity/media-contract.md:99 ('Audit score fallback copy no longer exposes rebuild/browser implementation language') and :179 (test floor asserts fallback copy) — the fallback state is acknowledged, but neither doc states the score is permanently unavailable

functionalityLost.capability: Copy URL (and any open-in-new-tab) from the audit issue detail view. The legacy audit lightbox had a Copy URL button; the rebuilt audit issue Drawer offers only generate-alt / compress / delete.
functionalityLost.evidence: Legacy: src/components/AssetAudit.tsx:707-715 (Copy URL in lightbox). Rebuilt: src/components/asset-manager-rebuilt/AuditLens.tsx:626-679 (issue Drawer action toolbar has no Copy URL / Open).
functionalityLost.whereDocumented: media-contract.md:92 lists 'issue drawer' as a carry-over home but only itemizes open/copy URL for the Asset Detail drawer (:91)

functionalityLost.capability: Inline row-level editing on Browse: click-to-edit alt text, inline rename with Enter-to-save, always-visible generate-alt, and per-row open-in-new-tab. Rebuilt grid cards carry only two contextual actions (Compress + Alt-text-or-Rename); alt editing, rename, and open/copy now require opening the detail drawer (table view retains three icon actions + open).
functionalityLost.evidence: Legacy: src/components/assets/AssetCard.tsx:169-215 (inline alt edit), :99-127 (inline rename, Enter saves), :224-265 (generate-alt always, open-in-new-tab link). Rebuilt: src/components/asset-manager-rebuilt/AssetGrid.tsx:161-202 (two contextual actions), AssetDrawer.tsx:203-268 (edit ops moved to drawer).
functionalityLost.whereDocumented: docs/ui-rebuild/parity/media-contract.md:60 ('two labeled contextual actions' / 'Full alt/name/compress/open/copy/delete operations remain in Asset Detail') and rebuilt-surface-inventory.md:21

functionalityLost.capability: Persistent, always-visible Browse controls: search bar, sort select, Organize button, and CMS shortcut were top-level in legacy; rebuilt hides search/sort/view/refresh/Organize/Repair results/CMS filters behind a collapsed 'More' expander.
functionalityLost.evidence: Legacy: src/components/assets/AssetFilters.tsx:63-115 (sticky always-visible search+sort+pills), AssetBrowser.tsx:530-551 (stats-bar CMS toggle + Organize button). Rebuilt: src/components/asset-manager-rebuilt/AssetManagerSurface.tsx:923-998 (all inside browseControlsOpen && block).

regressions.issue: Audit issue rows keep a stale asset id after compression. Compress replaces the Webflow asset and deletes the old one server-side; legacy remapped the row to newAssetId (and updated fileSize) so follow-up actions kept working. Rebuilt only strips the oversized/unoptimized-png issue types and ignores the returned newAssetId, so on a multi-issue asset (e.g. oversized + missing-alt) the surviving row's Generate alt / Delete actions POST/DELETE against a deleted asset id and fail.
regressions.evidence: Rebuilt: src/components/asset-manager-rebuilt/AuditLens.tsx:235-254 (handleCompress → removeIssueType only, newAssetId discarded). Legacy: src/components/AssetAudit.tsx:154-167 (remaps assetId: newAssetId, fileSize: newSize). Server: server/domains/webflow-assets/image-optimization.ts:309 (deleteAsset(oldId)) and :318-320 (returns newAssetId).

regressions.issue: Misleading permanent-pending copy on the dead health score: 'Awaiting server score' / 'Audit health score appears when the audit returns an authoritative score' implies a transient state, but the audit endpoint never returns healthScore, so every operator on every run sees a metric that looks temporarily delayed rather than nonexistent.
regressions.evidence: src/components/asset-manager-rebuilt/AuditLens.tsx:525 ('Awaiting server score'), :532-535 (banner); server/routes/webflow-audit.ts:130-140 (no healthScore field in response).

regressions.issue: Audit bulk compress no longer auto re-scans. Legacy ran runAudit() after bulk compression so the issue list (and asset ids) refreshed; rebuilt leaves the stale rows in place and only appends 'Re-scan for the latest issue list' to the success banner — compounding the stale-assetId defect above.
regressions.evidence: Legacy: src/components/AssetAudit.tsx:124 (runAudit() after bulk compress). Rebuilt: src/components/asset-manager-rebuilt/AuditLens.tsx:309-326 (no re-scan; banner asks the operator to do it).

confusions.issue: The in-flow Repair results (audit) section and the Browse grid are visible simultaneously but share ONE ?filter, ?search, and ?sort URL param. Clicking the audit chip 'Oversized' silently re-filters the asset library below to oversized too (both vocabularies accept 'missing-alt'/'oversized'/'unused'); typing in the audit search box filters the Browse grid at the same time; changing the Browse sort select while Repair is open resets the audit sort to its default (and vice versa).
confusions.why: An operator drilling into audit issues will see the library underneath reshuffle for no visible reason, and two visible sort selects fight over one param — it looks like the UI has a mind of its own.

confusions.issue: Search, sort, view toggle, Refresh, Organize, CMS filters, and the Repair results entry point are all hidden behind a collapsed 'More' button by default.
confusions.why: Search and 'run an audit' are the two most common daily actions on a media library; a first-time operator scanning the default viewport sees only All/Oversized/Missing alt/Unused chips and Upload, and may conclude search/audit don't exist. (Owner-approved exception, but it remains the top discoverability tax on the surface.)

confusions.issue: Opening Upload or Repair results clears the active Browse filter (setLens nulls ?filter) even though Browse stays visible. An operator who filtered to Oversized, then opens Repair results expecting the same context, lands on an unfiltered audit AND returns to an unfiltered library.
confusions.why: State loss with no visible cause — the workshop framing promises continuity ('without leaving the asset library') but the filter resets.

confusions.issue: Two independent sort mechanisms in table view: DataTable header clicks sort client-side (Asset/Size columns) while the FormSelect under More writes ?sort — the header sort silently overrides the select and is not reflected in the URL.
confusions.why: Sorting by header then sharing the URL reproduces a different order for the recipient.

confusions.issue: The 'Oversized images' metric tile counts SVGs over 500KB (computeStats has no SVG exclusion) but the grid card 'Oversized' badge excludes SVGs (AssetGrid isOversized) — the tile count can exceed the number of badged cards under the oversized filter.
confusions.why: Operators reconcile counts against what they see; a 12-count tile with 11 badged cards erodes trust in the numbers.

overcomplication.issue: Two permanent, non-dismissible narrative banners frame every visit: 'Fixes the source, not the symptom.' above the toolbar and 'From media fix to proof' below the grid (AssetManagerSurface.tsx:850-854, 1075-1079). Useful once; on visit fifty they are ~120px of ceremony between the operator and the grid.
overcomplication.suggestion: Make both dismissible (persist per-admin) or show only until the first successful compression on the workspace.

overcomplication.issue: Quota exhaustion produces stacked, overlapping notices: the surface-level warning banner (quotaLocked), a bulkResult 'Quota reached during bulk run' banner, plus the AuditLens's own local result banner after a quota-broken bulk run — up to three banners describing one event.
overcomplication.evidence: src/components/asset-manager-rebuilt/AssetManagerSurface.tsx:325-336 (markQuotaHit sets both banner states), AuditLens.tsx:300-307 (local result banner still set after quota break)
overcomplication.suggestion: Route quota state through a single banner; suppress the generic bulk-finished banner when the run ended on quota.

overcomplication.issue: Audit filter values and browse filter values share the single ?filter param by design (compatibility), forcing the mutual-interference behavior noted in confusions and making the state hook validate one raw string against two vocabularies.
overcomplication.evidence: src/components/asset-manager-rebuilt/useAssetManagerSurfaceState.ts:63-72 (same param parsed twice), types.ts:12-39 (overlapping id sets)
overcomplication.suggestion: Namespace the audit drill-in param (e.g. ?repair=oversized) while keeping ?filter= accepted as a legacy alias for one release.

gains: Full URL/deep-link state — ?tab, ?filter (multi-value), ?search, ?sort, ?view, ?asset all validated and initialized (useAssetManagerSurfaceState.ts:98-124); legacy MediaTab held everything in useState (MediaTab.tsx:25) so nothing was linkable or refresh-safe. Cross-surface repair handoffs from Performance (PageWeightLens.tsx:334, PageSpeedLens.tsx:723) and Site Audit (SiteAuditSurface.tsx:589) now land in the exact filter state.

AI quota (429) lock UX: banner, per-action disabling with explanatory tooltips, and partial-progress reporting when a bulk run hits the limit (AssetManagerSurface.tsx:325-336, 856-862); legacy surfaced quota failures as generic error text at best.

Asset detail drawer — preview, dimensions, created date, asset id, CMS usage badges, alt edit + AI generate, rename + smart name, compress with SVG/RichText-only explanations, open/copy URL, and per-asset delete with ConfirmDialog (AssetDrawer.tsx). Legacy Browse had no detail view and no single-asset delete at all.

Consistent bulk-operation scope: all bulk actions operate on the full selection (AssetManagerSurface.tsx:315-321). Legacy had a silent mismatch — generate/rename/compress processed only selected∩currently-filtered rows while the bar showed the total selected count and bulk delete removed ALL selected (AssetBrowser.tsx:258,365,392 vs :488).

Safety fix: RichText-only CMS images are excluded from bulk compression (AssetManagerSurface.tsx:626); legacy bulk compress had no such guard (AssetBrowser.tsx:392) and could attempt to replace assets with no backing Webflow id.
Table view with sortable columns and per-row actions as a peer to the grid view (AssetTable.tsx); legacy offered a single fixed row layout.
New evidence metrics per the prototype: Total media weight, Potential savings (blue read-only), and a 'Data as of' freshness timestamp (AssetManagerSurface.tsx:809-848, 800-806).

Destructive actions now use ConfirmDialog with explicit counts (single delete, bulk delete, delete-all-unused) instead of window.confirm (AssetManagerSurface.tsx:1126-1143, AuditLens.tsx:681-698 vs AssetBrowser.tsx:486, AssetAudit.tsx:193).
Mutation failures surface as toasts/banners via mutationErrorMessage; legacy audit swallowed most errors to console.error (AssetAudit.tsx:95,120,168).
Filter chips show live counts for all eight browse filters (AssetManagerSurface.tsx:304-313); legacy pills were uncounted.
Name sort falls back displayName → originalFileName → id (AssetManagerSurface.tsx:280); legacy sorted on displayName only, dumping unnamed assets together (AssetBrowser.tsx:201).

Skeleton loading states preserving layout, an action-oriented filtered-empty state with a Clear-filters CTA (AssetGrid.tsx:80-93), and a stale-data warning banner when a refresh fails but cached assets exist (AssetManagerSurface.tsx:864-868).

extensions.opportunity: Return healthScore from GET /api/webflow/audit/:siteId (the same issueCount/totalAssets computation legacy did client-side, now server-authoritative) to light up the permanently-dead Meter tile.
extensions.value: Restores the audit's headline metric and eliminates the misleading 'Awaiting server score' state with a ~5-line server change.

extensions.opportunity: Auto-run the audit when arriving via ?tab=audit deep link (currently the operator from Site Audit/Performance lands on a 'Run Asset Audit' gate with context copy but must click run manually).
extensions.value: The cross-surface repair handoff becomes one click shorter and delivers on the 'lands operators in an asset-repair state' contract requirement more literally.

extensions.opportunity: Apply the compress response's newAssetId/newSize to surviving audit rows (mirror legacy AssetAudit.tsx:155-166), and/or trigger the promised auto re-scan after bulk compress.
extensions.value: Fixes the stale-id regression and keeps the Repair results table truthful without manual re-scans.

extensions.opportunity: Distinct error state when the asset list fails to load and no cache exists (assetsQuery.isError with assets.length === 0 currently falls through to the 'No assets match this view / Clear filters' empty state).
extensions.value: An operator with a Webflow token problem sees a retryable error instead of concluding the library is empty.

extensions.opportunity: Un-gate the Upload drawer (and processing queue) from siteId, restoring the legacy pre-link optimization workflow — the server pipeline already works without a site (server/routes/misc.ts:35-51).
extensions.value: New-client onboarding can start optimizing media before the Webflow link exists, matching legacy capability.

extensions.opportunity: Invalidate the webflowAssets query when processing-queue items complete (queue events already broadcast 'files:uploaded'), so freshly uploaded assets appear in Browse without a manual Refresh.
extensions.value: Closes the upload→browse loop inside the single-workshop framing the surface sells.

surfaceAudits.headline: The rebuilt master/detail workbench genuinely improves navigation (deep links, mobile flow, truthful empty states) and preserves every write/handoff path, but it silently thinned the page rail — the ranked top-5 fix queue collapsed to a single chip, and the score-trend, unvalidated-keyword, and per-row metric signals vanished with no documented exception.
legacySources: src/App.tsx (flag-OFF branch, line 443; needsSite gate lines 400-401)

functionalityLost.capability: Ranked fix queue (top 5 pages by impressions x optimization gap, each showing rank, impressions, score/100, and impact, with the 'ranked by traffic x optimization gap' rationale label; clicking any item opens that page)
functionalityLost.evidence: Legacy: src/components/page-intelligence/PageIntelligencePagesHeader.tsx:142-175 renders all buildFixQueue items (pageIntelligenceData.ts:119-136 returns slice(0,5)). Rebuilt: src/components/page-intelligence-rebuilt/PageIntelligenceSurface.tsx:282-288 renders only fixQueue[0] as a single 'Fix first' chip with a bare '+impact' number — items 2-5, per-item impressions, and per-item score are gone. Not recoverable via Priority sort: that uses a different formula (opportunityScore, pageIntelligenceDisplay.ts:42-51) than fix-queue impact.
functionalityLost.whereDocumented: Not documented as an exception — parity-backlog.md:79 and owner-decision-packet.md:421 both claim 'fix queue' is preserved; the contract (page-intelligence-contract.md:22-27) only says 'fix-first page rail' without recording the 5-to-1 reduction.

functionalityLost.capability: Optimization score trend indicator (delta badge with up/down arrow and 'changed from X to Y' tooltip, driven by optimizationScoreHistory via summarizeScoreTrend)
functionalityLost.evidence: Legacy: src/components/page-intelligence/PageIntelligencePageRow.tsx:86-96,173-181 (summarizeScoreTrend defined at pageIntelligenceData.ts:24-37). Rebuilt: grep for summarizeScoreTrend in src/components/page-intelligence-rebuilt/ returns zero matches; neither the rail row (PageIntelligenceSurface.tsx:296-320) nor the detail pane (PageIntelligenceDetailPane.tsx:89-125) renders any trend. This was the surface's only visualization of score history — 'did our fixes move the score' evidence is now invisible flag-ON.
functionalityLost.whereDocumented: Nowhere — contract, backlog, and inventory never mention trend/history.

functionalityLost.capability: 'Unvalidated' keyword badge (strategy.validated === false, tooltip 'Keyword not validated in DataForSEO')
functionalityLost.evidence: Legacy: src/components/page-intelligence/PageIntelligencePageRow.tsx:158-162. Rebuilt: grep for 'validated' across src/components/page-intelligence-rebuilt/ returns nothing, and the shared detail sections it composes (PageIntelligenceStrategySection.tsx, PageIntelligenceAnalysisSection.tsx) never render it either — so the rebuilt surface presents volume/KD/CPC figures (StrategySection.tsx:112-141) with no cue that they may be provider-unvalidated.
functionalityLost.whereDocumented: Nowhere in docs/ui-rebuild/parity/.

functionalityLost.capability: Search-intent visibility for mapped-but-never-analyzed pages
functionalityLost.evidence: Legacy showed the intent chip on every strategy-mapped row directly from strategy.searchIntent (PageIntelligencePageRow.tsx:135-139). Rebuilt shows intent only inside PageIntelligenceAnalysisSection.tsx:74-81, which returns null without an analysis object (line 42), and effectiveAnalyses only hydrates when sp.analysisGeneratedAt && optimizationScore != null (pageIntelligenceData.ts:46). A page mapped by strategy generation but never page-analyzed shows its intent nowhere on the rebuilt surface.
functionalityLost.whereDocumented: Not recorded.

functionalityLost.capability: Per-row at-a-glance evidence: KeywordMetricCell (volume/KD/position), local-visibility badge, and inline track-keyword button on every row without selecting it
functionalityLost.evidence: Legacy: PageIntelligencePageRow.tsx:140-169 (keyword + track button 140-156, LocalSeoVisibilityBadge 157, KeywordMetricCell 163-169). Rebuilt rail row shows only title, path, primary keyword text, and score/Mapped badge (PageIntelligenceSurface.tsx:307-319); metrics, track, and local badge now require selecting the page (DetailPane via shared StrategySection + localSeoVisibility at DetailPane.tsx:117).
functionalityLost.whereDocumented: page-intelligence-contract.md:22-30 — the owner-approved master/detail adaptation with 'keyword assignment/editing, rank tracking, provider metrics, local visibility... reachable exactly once'; rebuilt-surface-inventory.md line 32.

functionalityLost.capability: 'Analyze Remaining (N)' count and disabling of bulk-analyze buttons while single-page analyses are in flight
functionalityLost.evidence: Legacy: PageIntelligencePagesHeader.tsx:85-105 shows the remaining count '(pageCount - analyzedCount)' and disabled={analyzingCount > 0} on both buttons. Rebuilt: PageIntelligenceSurface.tsx:248-255 has no count and no disable — 'Analyze remaining'/'Re-analyze all' stay clickable while analysis.analyzing is non-empty.
functionalityLost.whereDocumented: Not recorded.

regressions.issue: Empty inventory renders a search-flavored empty state even when no search is active
regressions.evidence: Rebuilt: PageIntelligenceSurface.tsx:323 always shows 'No matching pages / Try a page title, path, or target keyword' when filteredPages is empty — including a workspace whose page inventory is genuinely empty. Legacy distinguished: 'No pages match your search.' vs 'No pages found.' (PageIntelligencePageList.tsx:97-101). An operator on a fresh workspace is told to retype a search that doesn't exist.

regressions.issue: A workspaces-list fetch failure is misreported as 'Connect a Webflow site'
regressions.evidence: Rebuilt derives siteId from useWorkspaces().data?.find(...) (PageIntelligenceSurface.tsx:62-64); if the workspaces query errors (isLoading false, data undefined), siteId is '' and line 218-220 renders the 'Connect a Webflow site' EmptyState — a wrong diagnosis with no retry. Legacy received siteId as a prop from the already-resolved App shell (App.tsx:443), so this state was unreachable.

regressions.issue: Bulk analyze can be triggered while single-page analyses run (lost guard)
regressions.evidence: Legacy disabled Analyze All/Remaining whenever analyzingCount > 0 (PageIntelligencePagesHeader.tsx:91,101). Rebuilt only branches on analysis.bulkProgress (PageIntelligenceSurface.tsx:248), so an operator can start the bulk PAGE_ANALYSIS job mid-single-analysis, double-processing the same page.

confusions.issue: Sorting by Position, Volume, or Score gives no visible feedback — the rail rows don't display the sorted metric
confusions.why: The Segmented sort offers Priority/Position/Volume/Score (PageIntelligenceSurface.tsx:42-47,279) but rows render only title/path/keyword/score (307-319). Sort by Volume and the list reorders with no volumes visible anywhere; legacy rows carried KeywordMetricCell (volume/KD/position) so the ordering was self-evident. To an operator the sort looks broken or arbitrary.

confusions.issue: The 'Fix first' chip's '+142'-style number is unexplained
confusions.why: Legacy labeled the queue 'ranked by traffic x optimization gap' and showed impressions + score/100 per item (PageIntelligencePagesHeader.tsx:147,158-170). Rebuilt shows only '+{impact}' (PageIntelligenceSurface.tsx:286) with no unit, tooltip, or rationale — an operator can't tell if it's impressions, points, or dollars.

confusions.issue: Sort-direction toggle is undiscoverable
confusions.why: Direction flips only by re-clicking the already-active Segmented radio (changeSort, PageIntelligenceSurface.tsx:184-191); the 'High → low' text (line 280) reads as a passive caption, not a control, and role=radio semantics (Segmented.tsx) imply re-selection does nothing. Legacy put the arrow on the sort button itself (PagesHeader.tsx:200).

confusions.issue: 'View traffic' in a page's action footer opens the unscoped Analytics Hub
confusions.why: The button sits in the selected page's sticky footer (PageIntelligenceDetailPane.tsx:132) implying page-level traffic, but openTraffic navigates to adminPath(workspaceId,'analytics-hub') with no page context (PageIntelligenceSurface.tsx:215) — the operator lands on the hub root and must re-find the page.

overcomplication.issue: Selected-page score renders twice within one viewport
overcomplication.evidence: DetailPane header card shows 'Score {n}/100' (PageIntelligenceDetailPane.tsx:97) and the Optimization tile immediately below repeats the identical number with a bar (PageIntelligenceAnalysisSection.tsx:64-71).
overcomplication.suggestion: Drop the card-header 'Score n/100' micro-label (or reserve it for pages without an analysis section) so the Optimization tile is the single score home.

overcomplication.issue: Two stacked 'run analysis' invitations for unanalyzed pages
overcomplication.evidence: For a page with persisted strategy but no fresh analysis, the detail pane renders the 'Run AI analysis' empty block path (DetailPane.tsx:119) and separately PageIntelligencePersistedAnalysisSummary's 'Run fresh analysis' link (DetailPane.tsx:124 → PersistedAnalysisSummary.tsx:23-30) depending on state — the same verb in two visual homes inherited from the legacy inline layout rather than re-fit to the pane.
overcomplication.suggestion: Consolidate the analyze CTA into one location in the pane (e.g. the section header) now that the master/detail structure exists.

extensions.opportunity: Restore the top-5 ranked fix queue — buildFixQueue already returns 5 items (pageIntelligenceData.ts:135); the rebuilt UI just discards 4. A compact expandable strip above the rail (or a 'Fix queue (5)' popover on the existing chip) restores triage without breaking the workbench.
extensions.value: Recovers the legacy prioritized-work loop the docs claim is preserved; operators plan a session, not one fix.

extensions.opportunity: Show the active-sort metric as the row's right-hand value (position when sorted by Position, volume for Volume, keeping score otherwise), reusing KeywordMetricCell formatting.
extensions.value: Makes sorting legible again and restores at-a-glance scanning the master/detail move took away, without re-densifying every row.

extensions.opportunity: Reintroduce the score-trend delta (summarizeScoreTrend already exists) as a chip next to the rail/detail score, and the 'Unvalidated' qualifier next to volume/KD in the shared StrategySection metrics row.
extensions.value: Restores progress evidence and data-honesty markers that currently have no flag-ON home.

extensions.opportunity: Make 'View traffic' page-scoped: pass the page path (state or query param) and teach the analytics-hub/search-traffic receiver to pre-filter to it.
extensions.value: Turns a shallow, mildly misleading handoff into the cross-surface research move the button promises.

extensions.opportunity: Upgrade senders (AuditIssueRow.tsx:203, ContentDecay.tsx:276, DecayingPagesCard.tsx:203, KeywordHub.tsx:440, SeoEditorPagePanel.tsx:597) from ephemeral location.state fixContext to the new durable ?page= deep link.
extensions.value: Selections become refresh- and share-safe end-to-end, retiring the retained-fixContext ref machinery over time.

gains: URL deep-linking: ?tab=pages|architecture|guide and ?page=<id|slug|path> with validated fallback and refreshable/shareable selection (pageIntelligenceRouting.ts:8-33, PageIntelligenceSurface.tsx:127-172) — legacy tab and expansion state were purely local (PageIntelligence.tsx:58,84 and the 'tab-deeplink-ok' comment at line 226).

Truthful no-site state: an explicit 'Connect a Webflow site' EmptyState (PageIntelligenceSurface.tsx:218-220); legacy App.tsx excluded page-intelligence from SEO_TABS (App.tsx:400-401) and mounted it with webflowSiteId! undefined (App.tsx:443), leaving usePageJoin disabled with no explanation.

Mobile list-to-detail flow with focus transfer to Back and focus restoration to the originating row (PageIntelligenceSurface.tsx:141-156, DetailPane.tsx:68-71), plus the local-SEO panel hidden while detail is open at <md (line 273) — legacy inline-expansion had no mobile adaptation.

fixContext with pageId-only now resolves (resolveInitialPage uses pageId ?? pageSlug, pageIntelligenceRouting.ts:32); the legacy effect required pageSlug to be present at all (PageIntelligence.tsx:114).

Persistent detail pane with sticky target-keyword context and a sticky real-actions footer — evidence stays on screen while scrolling, vs legacy's inline expansion pushing actions below long analysis sections.
A dedicated component-test floor (tests/component/page-intelligence-rebuilt/: routing, exact-once actions, flag transition + flag-OFF receiver, responsive flow) that the legacy composition never had.
New 'View traffic' handoff to Analytics Hub from a page's action footer (DetailPane.tsx:132) — absent from legacy PageIntelligencePageActions.tsx entirely (albeit currently unscoped).

surfaceAudits.headline: Every legacy capability keeps a home (the whole legacy BrandHub context tab, incl. Page Strategy/copy pipeline, is carried inside the modal shell) and the rebuild adds real gains — an aggregated approvals hub, URL-written modal state, focused generator deep links — but the new cockpit ships a completeness metric that mathematically can never be satisfied, the modal shell can silently discard unsaved brand-voice/KB edits on Esc/backdrop-click, and the right-rail copy misdirects operators away from the Page Strategy workbench that in fact lives only on this surface.

functionalityLost.capability: Overview-level Local SEO locations rollup: legacy overview queried real location data (useLocalSeoLocations) and surfaced total/confirmed/needs-review counts plus a 'N locations need review' summary card; the rebuilt overview's 'Locations & service areas' row only checks targetGeo presence and never reads the locations store, so the at-a-glance review nudge is gone (data now only visible after opening the Business Footprint modal).
functionalityLost.evidence: Legacy: src/components/brand/BrandOverviewTab.tsx:81-88,106-110,150-162. Rebuilt: src/components/brand-ai-rebuilt/BrandAiSurface.tsx:406-415 (targetGeo-only check); grep confirms zero useLocalSeoLocations/needs_review references in src/components/brand-ai-rebuilt/.
functionalityLost.whereDocumented: docs/ui-rebuild/parity/brand-ai-contract.md:60 ('confirmed-geo rollups remain documented backend exceptions rather than simulated data'). Note: the exception's premise is shaky — the backend/hook exists and the legacy overview rendered this real data, so this is a dropped real feed, not an unavailable one.

functionalityLost.capability: One-click lateral navigation between the nine workflows via the visible top tab strip (TabBar with icons). In the rebuilt, moving e.g. Brandscript→Voice requires closing the modal, re-orienting on the overview, and clicking another row; Intelligence Profile is reachable only through the single 'Industry & goals' row inside the Knowledge group.
functionalityLost.evidence: Legacy: src/components/BrandHub.tsx:521-535 (9-tab TabBar). Rebuilt: src/components/brand-ai-rebuilt/BrandAiSurface.tsx:1005 (modal-per-workflow, no strip), :363-372 (only intelligence-profile entry point).
functionalityLost.whereDocumented: docs/ui-rebuild/parity/brand-ai-contract.md:29-30 ('The overview should not show the legacy top tab strip. The grouped context inventory is the navigation surface') — owner-approved interaction-model change.

regressions.issue: The cockpit's headline completeness metric can never be satisfied: the Voice & Messaging group counts at most 1 configured input (only hasText(brandVoice)) against a total of 3, so a workspace with a fully saved brand voice shows 33% with a red 'Needs setup' badge forever, directly contradicting its own item rows (the Brand voice row shows emerald 'Ready' at the same time). The surface-wide counter consequently caps at 9/11 'inputs configured' — 11/11 is unreachable in any workspace state. The parity contract's V1 claim of a 'truthful 5/11' fixture masked this because the fixture never approached the ceiling.
regressions.evidence: src/components/brand-ai-rebuilt/BrandAiSurface.tsx:255 (voiceConfigured = countComplete([hasText(brandVoice)]), max 1), :291-292 (configured: voiceConfigured, total: 3), :266 + :385 (audience max 1/1 — fine), :441-442 (surface total 11, max reachable 9), :493-497 (33% → 'empty'), :474-478 (statusTone('empty') → red), :300-327 (item rows show 'set'/'part' from the same field). Contrast docs/ui-rebuild/parity/brand-ai-contract.md:69 ('truthful 5/11 inputs configured'). Legacy showed qualitative notes, never a percent (src/components/brand/BrandOverviewTab.tsx:119-123).

regressions.issue: Unsaved brand-voice, knowledge-base, and persona edits are silently discarded when the Context editors modal closes: the DS Modal traps Escape at document level 'regardless of focus target' (so Esc while typing in the RichTextEditor closes the modal) and also closes on backdrop click, and the workflow modal's onClose unconditionally returns to overview with no dirty-state guard. Legacy carried the same local-draft state but on a full page, where accidental dismissal was far less likely than Esc/stray-click on a modal.
regressions.evidence: src/components/ui/overlay/Modal.tsx:96-101,129 (document-level Escape trap), :175-190 (backdrop close); src/components/brand-ai-rebuilt/BrandAiSurface.tsx:1010,1134-1135 (onClose → setTab('overview'), no guard); src/components/BrandHub.tsx:281-283,310-311 (local draft state), :727-729 (existing 'Unsaved changes' indicator proves dirty state is already tracked but unused on close).

regressions.issue: The right-rail 'Also on this client' card tells operators 'Page strategy and content planning stay with the strategy and content pipeline surfaces' — but Page Strategy (site blueprints + the entire copy pipeline: BlueprintDetail, CopyReviewPanel, BatchGenerationPanel, CopyExportPanel, CopyIntelligenceManager) mounts ONLY inside this surface's Context editors modal, in both flag states. An operator following the signpost to the content-pipeline surface finds nothing; meanwhile no overview row names Page Strategy, so the carried capability is both hidden and actively misdirected.
regressions.evidence: Rail copy: src/components/brand-ai-rebuilt/BrandAiSurface.tsx:839-841. Actual mount: BrandAiSurface.tsx:920-923 (BrandHub chromeless activeTab='context') → src/components/BrandHub.tsx:1003-1026 (Page Strategy SectionCard). Repo-wide grep: PageStrategyTab/BlueprintDetail imported only by BrandHub.tsx; zero Blueprint/PageStrategy references in src/components/content-pipeline-rebuilt/ or ContentPipeline.tsx. Contract requires keeping them here: docs/ui-rebuild/parity/brand-ai-contract.md:110,115.

regressions.issue: Working width for heavy editors dropped from full page width (~1140px canvas) to a 680px modal: two 300px-min-height rich-text editors, the persona editor's 3-column textarea grid (~190px per column after padding), and the BlueprintDetail copy-pipeline workbench all now operate inside max-w-[42.5rem] with a nested scroll region. Panels are vertically stacked so nothing breaks, but the persona grid and blueprint workbench are visibly cramped versus legacy.
regressions.evidence: src/components/ui/overlay/Modal.tsx:40 (workflow: max-w-[42.5rem] = 680px); src/components/brand-ai-rebuilt/BrandAiSurface.tsx:1010-1012 (workflow shell + max-h scroll body); legacy full-width mounts: src/components/BrandHub.tsx:587-1040, persona 3-col grid at :862; docs/ui-rebuild/parity/brand-ai-contract.md:55 (1140px legacy-equivalent canvas).

confusions.issue: The empty-audience CTA is labelled 'Generate personas from site' (with a sparkle icon) but clicking it only opens the Context editors modal — where the persona manager is collapsed behind a 'Manage' button, and the real 'Generate from Website' job button only appears after expanding it. The label promises an action and delivers a three-step navigation.
confusions.why: An operator clicks a button that says 'Generate', sees a modal of text editors instead, and reasonably concludes generation failed or the feature is missing. The actual generation job (PERSONA_GENERATION) is two more undiscoverable clicks away.

confusions.issue: Cockpit group-card click targets are arbitrary per group: the 'Voice & Messaging' card opens the Voice *calibration* modal (not the brand-voice editor its own meter is scored on), 'Knowledge' opens the *Discovery* modal (not the knowledge base), 'Audience' opens the Context editors modal.
confusions.why: The card summarizes group completeness, but clicking it lands somewhere that cannot directly fix the number shown — e.g. the Voice card's 33% is driven solely by the brandVoice field, which lives in the Context modal, not the Voice calibration modal it opens (BrandAiSurface.tsx:555-561).

confusions.issue: The 'Brand voice & style' and 'Knowledge base' rows open the same undifferentiated Context editors modal at the same scroll position — item-level focus is never passed even though in-modal anchors (#brand-voice-textarea, #knowledge-base-textarea) already exist in the carried BrandHub markup.
confusions.why: Rows styled as item-level actions ('Edit' / 'Generate' chips per row) imply targeted destinations; landing on an identical stacked mega-modal each time makes the rows feel broken and forces scrolling to find the section that was clicked.

confusions.issue: Generator tier labels render as raw abbreviations 'ess' / 'pro' / 'prem' in uppercase micro type, while the Identity editor the rows open uses the full legacy labels 'Essentials' / 'Professional' / 'Premium'.
confusions.why: Operators (and clients screensharing) must decode three cryptic tokens that have a written-out canonical form one click deeper (BrandAiSurface.tsx:731 vs IdentityTab.tsx:42-46).

confusions.issue: All tab/focus URL writes use replace:true, so the browser Back button never closes a modal — it exits the Brand & AI surface entirely, despite each modal having its own shareable URL.
confusions.why: A modal that owns a URL (?tab=voice) trains operators to treat it as a navigable location; pressing Back from it teleporting them off the whole surface contradicts that mental model (useBrandAiSurfaceState.ts:64-73).

overcomplication.issue: The 'Context editors' modal is a mega-modal wrapping the entire legacy BrandHub context tab — brand voice editor, knowledge base editor, persona manager, Page Strategy blueprint workbench (with its own nested TabBar, review panels, batch generation, export), and an info footer — all inside one 680px shell reached from five different overview rows.
overcomplication.evidence: src/components/brand-ai-rebuilt/BrandAiSurface.tsx:920-923 mounts BrandHub chromeless; src/components/BrandHub.tsx:587-1040 is everything it contains, incl. BlueprintDetail (720 lines) at :1008-1025.
overcomplication.suggestion: Split the receiver: keep ?tab=context as the compatibility route but add a section/focus param (anchors already exist) so each overview row lands on its section, and give Page Strategy either its own workflow tab or a wider Drawer — the state hook already supports atomic tab+focus writes.

overcomplication.issue: buildBrandContextSummary encodes opaque nested-threshold heuristics — e.g. 'intelligence profile ≥2 of 3 fields' counts as one Knowledge input, 'business profile ≥3 of 4 facts' counts as one Facts input — so an operator cannot tell what to fix to move from 5/11 to 6/11, and the thresholds live only in this component.
overcomplication.suggestion: Flatten to an explicit 1:1 checklist of named inputs (each boolean maps to one visible row), or move completeness to a server-backed contract the client renders verbatim — which would also fix the 9/11 ceiling.

overcomplication.issue: Rail SectionCard titles are restyled via brittle arbitrary-variant selector chains targeting SectionCard's internal DOM ('[&>div:first-child>div>span:last-child]:![font-family:...]' etc.) to implement the owner-approved 13.5px/700 rail-title exception.
overcomplication.suggestion: Add a titleRole/titleSize prop to SectionCard (the exception is now owner-approved DS behavior, per contract line 74) so the override survives SectionCard refactors instead of silently breaking.

gains: Aggregated pending-approvals hub (BrandPendingChanges): one topbar button showing a live count of MCP-proposed voice samples + draft brand deliverables + a voice-finalization nudge, with a full untruncated review modal and version-guarded 'Approve all' batch action — legacy had no equivalent anywhere (BrandPendingChanges.tsx:43-118; approve-all at :69-93).

Surface-level data lifecycle states legacy lacked entirely: loading skeleton, ErrorState with retry, stale-data warning banner when a refetch fails, 'Data as of' timestamp, and an explicit Refresh-context action (BrandAiSurface.tsx:458-465,1048-1099,1118-1122 vs BrandHub.tsx:266-270 which renders silently with empty editors on error).

Tab switching now writes validated URL state (tab + focus, with legacy alias resolution), making every workflow modal bookmarkable/shareable; legacy TabBar clicks never touched the URL (useBrandAiSurfaceState.ts:75-84 vs BrandHub.tsx:523).

Focused deep-link receivers legacy never had: ?tab=identity&focus=<released-type> opens exactly one real deliverable editor with safe fallback to the library for reserved/invalid values, and ?tab=brandscript&focus=existing-brandscript focuses the first real record (BrandAiSurface.tsx:230-232,926-951; legacy always mounted the full library, BrandHub.tsx:546).

All 17 released brand-identity generators are exposed exactly once from the overview via group disclosures (7/2/5/3, verified against RELEASED_BRAND_DELIVERABLE_TYPES in shared/types/brand-engine.ts:200-218), giving direct entry points that legacy hid behind Identity-tab navigation.

Denser, persona-first overview: per-input status rows with real content snippets (markdown-normalized, two-line clamped), persona cards with buying-stage badges rendered directly on the overview, and a usage rail explaining what consumes each context group (BrandAiSurface.tsx:591-695,792-845).

extensions.opportunity: Wire the cockpit's Voice & Messaging score to real calibration data — the surface already fetches voice profile + readiness in BrandPendingChanges (queryKeys.admin.voiceProfile/voiceReadiness); feeding those two booleans plus brandscript existence into buildBrandContextSummary makes the 3-input voice total truthful and the 11/11 ceiling reachable.
extensions.value: Fixes the always-red trust landmine at its root and turns the cockpit into an honest to-do list.

extensions.opportunity: Add a dirty-state close guard to the workflow modal: BrandHub already computes unsaved-changes for the KB (and brandVoice draft state exists); lift a simple isDirty callback up to BrandAiWorkflowModal and confirm before Esc/backdrop close.
extensions.value: Eliminates silent loss of long-form brand-voice/KB edits, the highest-stakes data-loss path introduced by the modal shell.

extensions.opportunity: Pass section focus into the Context editors modal (?tab=context&focus=brand-voice|knowledge-base|personas|page-strategy) and scroll/expand accordingly — the anchors and the atomic tab+focus state writer both already exist.
extensions.value: Makes the five overview rows that currently open one undifferentiated stack land on exactly what was clicked, and gives Page Strategy a first-class named destination.

extensions.opportunity: Give Page Strategy an explicit overview row (Knowledge or a fifth utility group) and correct the rail copy that claims it lives on strategy/content-pipeline surfaces.
extensions.value: Restores discoverability of the entire copy-pipeline workbench and removes an actively false signpost.

extensions.opportunity: Make 'Generate personas from site' start the PERSONA_GENERATION background job directly (job infra + sessionStorage recovery already exist in BrandHub), with the modal as the review destination once results arrive.
extensions.value: The empty-state CTA delivers what it promises, cutting a 3-click misleading path to zero clicks.

extensions.opportunity: Surface the locations needs-review count on the Facts group's 'Locations & service areas' row via the existing useLocalSeoLocations hook (the legacy overview's exact pattern).
extensions.value: Restores the one real data feed the rebuild dropped, at the cost of one query the app already makes elsewhere.

surfaceAudits.headline: Global Settings is a faithful DS rebuild that adds real safety (confirm-gated destructive cleanup, error surfacing, archive/restore home) at the cost of three tiny undocumented trims; Workspace Settings achieves parity by construction — it wraps the legacy component wholesale — but the wrapper keeps a parallel URL-tab state that drifts from the real tab, making the rebuilt breadcrumb lie after manual tab clicks.
legacySources: src/components/settings/ConnectionsTab.tsx (+FeaturesTab, ClientDashboardTab, WorkspaceFeatureFlagOverrides — shared by both paths)
src/components/FeatureFlagSettings.tsx / StripeSettings.tsx / McpApiKeysSettings.tsx (shared by both paths)

functionalityLost.capability: Static 'API Keys' info card (OpenAI API Key — 'Configured via .env')
functionalityLost.evidence: Legacy SettingsPanel.tsx:226-239 renders a dedicated API Keys section; rebuilt GlobalSettingsLens.tsx:112-246 has no equivalent card (only the OpenAI connectivity row inside wave-a/PlatformHealthCard.tsx:20 remains)
functionalityLost.whereDocumented: Not in global-ops-contract.md carry-over list (lines 84-102) nor parity-backlog.md; the card is largely redundant with Platform Health's OpenAI row, but the '.env' provenance hint is gone

functionalityLost.capability: Webflow token-generation help link ('Generate tokens at webflow.com' → webflow.com/dashboard/account/integrations)
functionalityLost.evidence: Legacy SettingsPanel.tsx:196-201 links out for token generation; rebuilt wave-a/WebflowConnectionsCard.tsx:12-17 replaces it with plain subtitle 'Link sites from each workspace's connection settings' and no external link
functionalityLost.whereDocumented: Contract line 88 only requires 'Webflow workspace overview, linked/unlinked workspace state'; the onboarding affordance is not recorded as an exception

functionalityLost.capability: Concrete retention values on the four storage cleanup actions ('Keep last 3 days', 'Keep last 20 per site', '>90 days old', '>6 months old')
functionalityLost.evidence: Legacy SettingsPanel.tsx:380,393,406,419 stated the exact windows (matching server defaults at server/routes/health.ts:459,470,481,492 — 90d chat, 20/site reports, 180d activity, DEFAULT_BACKUP_RETENTION_DAYS backups); rebuilt StorageMonitorCard.tsx:21-24 and GlobalSettingsLens.tsx:36-41 use vague copy ('Keeps the configured local retention window', 'Removes sessions outside the chat retention window') on both the buttons and the destructive confirm dialog
functionalityLost.whereDocumented: Not recorded; note legacy's '3 days' was itself only the default (env-overridable), so the fix is showing the live value, not restoring the hard-coded caption

regressions.issue: Storage cleanup confirm can be double-fired and gives no in-flight feedback: legacy disabled all four prune buttons while one ran and spun the active row; the rebuilt ConfirmDialog's confirm button stays enabled during the pending mutation, so a slow prune looks unresponsive and re-clicks fire duplicate POSTs
regressions.evidence: Legacy SettingsPanel.tsx:373,386,399,412 (disabled={!!pruning}) + :382 spinner; rebuilt GlobalSettingsLens.tsx:101-110 (handlePrune, dialog closes only onSuccess) + ui/ConfirmDialog.tsx:6-15,79-86 (no loading/disabled support); note ConfirmDialog also confirms on Enter (ConfirmDialog.tsx:29-55), making accidental double-submit easier

regressions.issue: No loading indicator while GSC properties fetch after Google resolves connected — the card body renders with neither chips nor a spinner during the gap
regressions.evidence: Legacy SettingsPanel.tsx:171-174 showed 'Loading properties...' with spinner; rebuilt GlobalSettingsLens.tsx:147-161 gates only the empty-state message on !gscSites.isLoading and renders nothing while loading

regressions.issue: Rebuilt-shell breadcrumb sub-crumb and the lens's data-active-tab go stale after a manual tab click inside Workspace Settings: RebuiltBreadcrumb derives the sub-crumb from ?tab=, but the wrapped legacy tab bar deliberately does not write the URL, so after deep-linking to ?tab=dashboard and clicking Publishing the breadcrumb still reads 'Client Dashboard'
regressions.evidence: RebuiltBreadcrumb.tsx:134-152 (sub-crumb from searchParams) vs WorkspaceSettings.tsx:277-291 + comment at :105-111 (setTab without URL write, by design since PR #379); WorkspaceSettingsLens.tsx:104-105 stamps the same stale URL value as data-active-tab and never uses state.setTab; legacy shell had no sub-crumb (layout/Breadcrumbs.tsx:20 shows page label only) so this staleness is rebuilt-only

confusions.issue: Destructive cleanup confirm dialogs don't say what will actually be deleted — 'Removes sessions outside the chat retention window' names no window, and the operator must trust an unstated policy at the exact moment of a destructive action
confusions.why: A solo operator confirming 'Run cleanup' wants the number (90 days? 3 days?) before deleting backups/history; the quick-stats row shows 'Backup retention 3d' for one of the four actions only (StorageMonitorCard.tsx:94), the other three windows appear nowhere

confusions.issue: The 'Unknown Workspace Settings tab' warning banner persists after the user recovers by clicking a valid tab, because manual tab clicks never rewrite the ?tab= param that triggered it
confusions.why: WorkspaceSettingsLens.tsx:108-116 shows the banner whenever the URL param is invalid; the user lands on Connections, clicks around happily, and the warning stays pinned at the top of the page contradicting what they see

confusions.issue: Platform Health uses a trophy icon for the Stripe row and a message icon for Email
confusions.why: wave-a/PlatformHealthCard.tsx:23-24 — legacy used CreditCard/Mail (SettingsPanel.tsx:260-261); a trophy next to 'Stripe' reads as an award, not a payments connection, in a card an operator scans for outage triage
