confusions.issue: Two settings homes with near-identical Google sections: global Settings shows Google connect/disconnect + GSC property chips, and Workspace Settings > Connections shows the same Google account controls again (via the shared legacy ConnectionsTab)
confusions.why: Carried over from legacy so not a parity break, but a new operator disconnecting Google from Workspace Settings may not realize it severs the account for every workspace; the rebuilt footer hint (GlobalSettingsLens.tsx:232-234) mitigates only the opposite direction

overcomplication.issue: WorkspaceSettingsLens maintains a parallel URL-derived tab state whose setTab writer is never used, while the wrapped legacy WorkspaceSettings owns the real tab state from the same param — two readers, one dead writer, and a drifting data-active-tab attribute
overcomplication.evidence: useGlobalOpsSurfaceState.ts:55-68 (useWorkspaceSettingsTabState with setTab) consumed at WorkspaceSettingsLens.tsx:53,104-105 for a test attribute and banner only; WorkspaceSettings.tsx:96-118 re-resolves the identical param independently
overcomplication.suggestion: Lift tab ownership into the lens (pass tab/onTabChange props into WorkspaceSettings) or have the inner tab bar call the lens's URL writer; either move makes the breadcrumb truthful and every tab state shareable via URL

overcomplication.issue: The four cleanup actions are defined twice with slightly different copy — button rows in StorageMonitorCard and dialog labels in GlobalSettingsLens
overcomplication.evidence: StorageMonitorCard.tsx:15-25 (PRUNE_ROWS: 'Keep the configured retention window') vs GlobalSettingsLens.tsx:36-41 (PRUNE_LABELS: 'Keeps the configured local retention window.')
overcomplication.suggestion: Single shared PRUNE_ACTIONS constant exported from the hook module (useGlobalOpsSettings.ts already owns PRUNE_ENDPOINTS) consumed by both card and dialog

overcomplication.issue: Workspace data is fetched twice on the workspace-settings page: the lens reads the full workspaces list to derive name/domain/archivedAt while the wrapped component GETs /api/workspaces/:id again itself
overcomplication.suggestion: Acceptable as a transitional wrapper cost (GO-006 defers the true rebuild), but when the tabs are rebuilt, converge on one React Query workspace-detail hook

gains: Destructive storage cleanup is now confirm-gated: legacy fired the prune POST immediately on button click with no confirmation (SettingsPanel.tsx:371-422); rebuilt routes all four actions through a destructive-variant ConfirmDialog (GlobalSettingsLens.tsx:236-244)

Workspace archive/restore gets its first UI home — the server endpoint (server/routes/workspaces.ts:488-506) had no legacy consumer (src/api/workspaces.ts archive wrapper landed with the rebuild, PR #1516); rebuilt adds a confirm-gated Archive/Restore button, an 'Workspace is archived' info banner with date, and correct restore semantics (WorkspaceSettingsLens.tsx:92-100,118-125,155-165)

Run Audit and Run Strategy job actions are now reachable from Workspace Settings via the background-job platform, with a guard toast when no Webflow site is linked (WorkspaceSettingsLens.tsx:71-90) — legacy had no such affordance on this page

Explicit failure surfacing: a warning banner appears when workspaces/google/health queries error while stale data is still shown (GlobalSettingsLens.tsx:124-130), and storage-read failure gets a retry-oriented banner (StorageMonitorCard.tsx:112) — legacy swallowed all of these into console.error

Invalid ?tab= deep links produce a visible fallback banner instead of a silent default (WorkspaceSettingsLens.tsx:108-116); rebuilt-shell breadcrumb also gains sub-tab crumbs for shared deep links (RebuiltBreadcrumb.tsx:75-83)
Booking-link preview only renders for parseable http/https URLs (GlobalSettingsLens.tsx:43-53) — legacy rendered an anchor for any non-empty string (SettingsPanel.tsx:473-476)
Storage report now shows the scan timestamp (StorageMonitorCard.tsx:56) and a labelled Refresh button with fetching state; prune success message reports bytes freed as before

Data layer modernized to React Query with proper cache invalidation (useGlobalOpsSettings.ts:97-145), window-focus health refetch, and Skeleton loading states — legacy was hand-rolled useState+fetch, which violates current project conventions

Workspace Settings header adds identity context (avatar, 'workspace · domain' line) while preserving inline rename with Enter/Escape semantics (WorkspaceSettings.tsx prototypeHeader branch, driven by WorkspaceSettingsLens.tsx:143-152)

extensions.opportunity: Add a loading/disabled prop to ui/ConfirmDialog and thread mutation isPending into the settings cleanup and archive dialogs
extensions.value: Closes the double-fire window and gives feedback during slow prunes; benefits every rebuilt surface that pairs ConfirmDialog with a mutation

extensions.opportunity: Wire Workspace Settings tab clicks to the already-built URL writer (state.setTab in useGlobalOpsSurfaceState.ts) so ?tab= tracks the visible tab
extensions.value: Makes the rebuilt breadcrumb truthful, clears the stale invalid-tab banner on recovery, and makes every sub-tab shareable/bookmarkable — the receiver half already exists

extensions.opportunity: Render the live retention values in cleanup copy — the storage report already carries backupRetentionDays, and the prune endpoints accept/return maxAgeDays/keepPerSite (server/routes/health.ts:458-500)
extensions.value: Restores destructive-action specificity legacy had, without legacy's hard-coded-default lie; operator confirms exactly what will be deleted

extensions.opportunity: Show GSC permissionLevel next to each Search Console property chip — the field is already fetched and typed (useGlobalOpsSettings.ts:8-11) but displayed nowhere in either UI
extensions.value: Operators debugging a workspace's missing GSC data can see at a glance whether the account has full vs restricted permission on the property

extensions.opportunity: Surface archived workspaces somewhere discoverable (workspace switcher filter or an 'Archived' section in the overview) now that archive has a UI home
extensions.value: Restore currently requires knowing/navigating to the archived workspace's settings URL; an archived workspace hidden from default lists is otherwise effectively lost to a busy operator

surfaceAudits.headline: The Roadmap rebuild adds genuine workflow wins (free-text search, URL-persisted sort, surfaced mutation errors, a working prospect-onboarding receiver that was a dead button in legacy) but ships two undocumented data degradations — a Completion % whose denominator no longer matches the adjacent Total tile, and roadmap feature links reduced to unresolvable "Feature #N" labels — while the four Business tabs achieve parity by re-mounting the legacy panels verbatim inside brittle nth-child CSS frames.

functionalityLost.capability: Roadmap feature-name resolution: legacy joined the features API (featureId -> feature title) so filter options and row chips showed human feature names; rebuilt never fetches features and renders literal 'Feature #N' in both the filter dropdown and row badges, which is unresolvable anywhere in the UI (the Feature Library does not display ids).
functionalityLost.evidence: Legacy: src/components/Roadmap.tsx:38-49 (featuresApi.get + featureMap), src/components/RoadmapFilterBar.tsx:73-81 (name-labelled filter), src/components/RoadmapBacklogView.tsx:100+148-150 and src/components/RoadmapSprintView.tsx:74+105 (FeatureChip with title). Rebuilt: src/components/global-ops-rebuilt/wave-a/roadmapModel.ts:81 (feature: `Feature #${item.featureId}`), RoadmapLens.tsx:114-123 (filter values from that label), wave-a/RoadmapItemRow.tsx:92+113 (badge shows the label).
functionalityLost.whereDocumented: Checked docs/ui-rebuild/parity/global-ops-contract.md:95 (carry-over requires 'item detail' but never mentions feature names), parity-backlog.md:90, and docs/ui-rebuild/phase-a/tickets/global-ops.md:96 ('Preserve existing filter bar') — none record this as an exception.

functionalityLost.capability: Legacy Roadmap deep-link/bookmark params silently break: legacy read `?tags=` with comma-separated OR semantics and `?feature=<numericId>`; rebuilt reads `?tag=` (single value) and `?feature=Feature #<id>` (label-valued). Old URLs fall back to 'all' with no warning banner (only view/tab invalids get banners).
functionalityLost.evidence: Legacy: src/lib/roadmapFilters.ts:36-41 (comma-split OR), :123-131 (params tags/feature), src/components/RoadmapFilterBar.tsx:36+78. Rebuilt: RoadmapLens.tsx:105-123 (params 'tag'/'feature' validated against label values), :132 (single-tag match).

functionalityLost.capability: Backlog one-click column-header sorting with real <table> semantics and aria-sort replaced by a headerless CSS grid plus two separate sort dropdowns; the build ticket's own acceptance criterion required DataTable with aria-sort and keyboard row-activation tests, which was not met.
functionalityLost.evidence: Legacy: src/components/RoadmapBacklogView.tsx:51-52 (ariaSortFor), :66-95 (table + sortable th buttons). Rebuilt: wave-a/RoadmapBacklog.tsx:20-28 (div grid, no table role, no aria-sort), RoadmapLens.tsx:309-322 (sort + direction FormSelects). Ticket criterion: docs/ui-rebuild/phase-a/tickets/global-ops.md:97.
functionalityLost.whereDocumented: Contract line 55 documents the six-column backlog layout, but not the DataTable/aria-sort criterion being dropped.

functionalityLost.capability: Backlog columns Sprint / Tags / Added removed from the scan (Sprint and Added moved into the expansion; tags are not rendered anywhere in backlog rows, and sprint-view rows truncate to the first 2 tags vs legacy showing all).
functionalityLost.evidence: Legacy: src/components/RoadmapBacklogView.tsx:82-94 (Sprint/Feature/Tags/Added columns), :151-157 (all tags). Rebuilt: wave-a/RoadmapBacklog.tsx:24-27 (six columns only), RoadmapItemRow.tsx:114 (tags.slice(0,2), sprint variant only), :122-130 (sprint/added in expansion, no tags).
functionalityLost.whereDocumented: docs/ui-rebuild/parity/global-ops-contract.md:55 (owner-approved six-column Backlog scan: ID, status control, Item, Priority, Est, textual Status); the sprint-row 2-tag truncation is not explicitly recorded.

functionalityLost.capability: Closed-item count dropped from the page header stats (legacy subtitle showed '· N closed'; rebuilt hero shows total/done/active/pending/on-hold only, so closed items are invisible in the summary although they still render as rows).
functionalityLost.evidence: Legacy: src/components/Roadmap.tsx:126+134. Rebuilt: wave-a/RoadmapHero.tsx:21-24 (no closed param), RoadmapLens.tsx:97-101 (closed never counted).

regressions.issue: Completion % denominator silently changed and now disagrees with the adjacent 'Total items' tile: legacy computed done/total; rebuilt computes done/(done+in_progress+pending), excluding deferred and closed, while the tile row still displays the full total. An operator inferring raw counts from the percentage gets wrong numbers. This violates the repo's rate-display rule (numerator/denominator must share a displayed source) and the surface's own build-ticket criterion 'Completion rate denominator must be the same item count displayed next to it'.
regressions.evidence: Legacy: src/components/Roadmap.tsx:142 (Math.round((done/total)*100)). Rebuilt: RoadmapLens.tsx:102-103 (executableTotal = done+inProgress+pending), wave-a/RoadmapMetricStrip.tsx:15-18 (Total tile = full total next to Completion tile). Criterion: docs/ui-rebuild/phase-a/tickets/global-ops.md:95.

regressions.issue: 'Shipping velocity' is the same card name but a different metric: legacy plotted cumulative monthly shipped counts derived from item shippedAt dates (time-based trend, with a 'N features shipped' lifetime total); rebuilt shows done-item counts for only the last 4 fully-completed sprints and ignores shippedAt entirely (any sprint with one non-done item is excluded). Not recorded as an approved exception.
regressions.evidence: Legacy: src/components/RoadmapVelocityChart.tsx:14-44 (byMonth from shippedAt, cumulative). Rebuilt: RoadmapLens.tsx:157-171 (fully-shipped sprints, slice(0,4)), wave-a/RoadmapVelocityCard.tsx:20-46. Contract only says 'velocity' must be kept (global-ops-contract.md:95).

regressions.issue: Roadmap loading state renders real-looking zeros instead of skeletons for the hero and metric tiles: while the query loads, the header reads '0 items · 0 done · 0 active · 0 pending' and tiles show 'Total items 0 / Completion 0%'. Legacy replaced the whole page with skeletons during load.
regressions.evidence: Rebuilt: RoadmapLens.tsx:95-103 (counts computed from empty array), :222+240 (hero/strip rendered unconditionally; only velocity/groups get skeletons). Legacy: src/components/Roadmap.tsx:109-119 (full-page skeleton with aria-busy).

regressions.issue: Sprint view rows are now globally re-sorted (default priority asc, or whatever sort is selected) inside each sprint group, discarding the curated roadmap.json item order legacy preserved.
regressions.evidence: Rebuilt: RoadmapLens.tsx:137-141 (filteredRows sorted) feeding :143-155 (groups filter the sorted list). Legacy: src/components/RoadmapSprintView.tsx:37-40 (items in natural order, sorting only existed in Backlog).

confusions.issue: Roadmap items reference 'Feature #23'-style labels that cannot be resolved anywhere in the product — the Feature Library tab shows titles, not ids.
confusions.why: An operator triaging the roadmap loses the ability to see which sales-library feature an item belongs to without opening data/features.json in an editor; the chip looks like a broken placeholder.

confusions.issue: The same five counts are displayed three times in the first screenful: hero subtitle (items/done/active/pending), the four MetricTiles (Total/Completed/In progress/Completion), and the progress-bar legend (Done/Active/On hold/Pending).
confusions.why: Triple redundancy costs vertical space before the actual work list, and the one number that differs (Completion %, computed on a different denominator) is the one users will try to reconcile against the repeated counts.

confusions.issue: Sorting the backlog now takes two dropdown interactions (Sort key + Ascending/Descending selects) where legacy was one click on a column header with a visible direction indicator.
confusions.why: The most common table gesture (click a header to flip sort) is gone; eight controls sit in one toolbar row (search + 5 filters + 2 sort selects), which reads as ceremony for a single-operator tool.

confusions.issue: Sidebar/nav still shows four separate entries — Revenue, AI Usage, Features, Prospect (src/lib/navRegistry.tsx:167-183) — that all land on a page titled 'Business', and the tab tray relabels 'AI Usage' as 'Usage' (BusinessTabTray.tsx:8) while the fallback banner calls it 'AI Usage' (BusinessLens.tsx:29-34).
confusions.why: Clicking 'Revenue' and landing on 'Business' with a Revenue tab is explainable, but the Usage/AI Usage label drift between nav, tray, and banner makes the same destination read as three different things.

confusions.issue: Inside the Business Revenue tab, the legacy 'Revenue Analytics' PageHeader title/subtitle is sr-only'd by the CSS frame but its destructive 'Purge All' button remains, floating right-aligned with no visible context (BusinessPanelFrame.tsx:6-12; RevenueDashboard.tsx:104-131).
confusions.why: A red 'Purge All' control with no adjacent heading or transaction count is a destructive action stripped of the context that legacy gave it ('N transactions recorded' subtitle).

overcomplication.issue: BusinessPanelFrame restyles the four legacy panels via long chains of nth-child/:has() descendant selectors with !important (e.g. '[&>div>div:first-child:has(button)>div:first-child]:sr-only'), coupling the rebuilt frame to the exact DOM order of components it does not own. It already misfires: FEATURES_FRAME's ':first-child:not(:only-child)]:sr-only' hides the search-row skeleton in FeatureLibrary's embedded loading state (FeatureLibrary.tsx:109-121) because the loading DOM differs from the loaded DOM.
overcomplication.suggestion: Extend the legacy components' own presentation props instead (FeatureLibrary already takes `embedded`, AIUsageSection takes `compact`) — add an `embedded` mode to RevenueDashboard/SalesReport that suppresses their PageHeaders and outer padding, then delete the selector frames.

overcomplication.issue: Roadmap stat plumbing is triplicated (hero subtitle, MetricStrip, ProgressCard legend all recount the same five statuses) and each computes independently in RoadmapLens, which is exactly how the Completion-denominator drift slipped in.
overcomplication.suggestion: Compute one shared stats object (including the completion denominator) and render it in at most two places; drop either the hero subtitle counts or the metric tiles.

overcomplication.issue: Separate 'Sort direction' dropdown alongside the sort-key dropdown for a six-key sort.
overcomplication.suggestion: Fold direction into the sort control (toggle on reselect, like legacy header clicks) or adopt the DS DataTable header-sort the build ticket asked for.

gains: Free-text roadmap search across id/title/notes/sprint/source/feature/status/tags, URL-persisted as ?q= with debounce — legacy had no search at all (RoadmapLens.tsx:59+125-141 vs legacy RoadmapFilterBar with filters only).

Sort state (key + direction) is URL-persisted and shareable, and adds title/sprint sort keys legacy lacked (RoadmapLens.tsx:60-63; roadmapModel.ts:14-21 vs legacy local useState in RoadmapBacklogView.tsx:27-28).

Failed status-cycle mutations are surfaced to the operator via an error InlineBanner and the in-flight row is disabled; legacy silently reverted the optimistic update with only a console.error (RoadmapLens.tsx:206+348-354, RoadmapItemRow.tsx:70-72 vs Roadmap.tsx:74-79).
Roadmap load-error state exists ('Roadmap could not be loaded' banner, RoadmapLens.tsx:232-238); legacy rendered an empty page with no error affordance.

Business lens consolidates Revenue/AI Usage/Features/Prospects behind one ?tab= receiver with validated fallback + explicit invalid-tab warning banner, while keeping all four legacy route ids working as aliases (useGlobalOpsSurfaceState.ts:19-53; BusinessLens.tsx:119-128; GlobalOpsSurface.tsx:27-41).

The SalesReport 'Onboard as Client' button finally works: it writes #new-workspace?url=... (SalesReport.tsx:263-274) which NOTHING consumed in the legacy flag-OFF app (repo-wide grep finds no consumer) — BusinessLens.tsx:49-93 adds a create-workspace-from-prospect card with toast feedback, turning a dead legacy control into a real flow.

Velocity and list areas get explicit loading skeletons and an empty-state message ('No shipped items are dated to a sprint yet') instead of legacy's silent null when under two months of data (RoadmapVelocityCard.tsx:24-29 vs RoadmapVelocityChart.tsx:29).

Accessibility upgrades on Business: real tablist/tab/tabpanel wiring with roving tabindex and auto-scroll of the selected tab (BusinessTabTray.tsx:52-86, BusinessPanelFrame.tsx:53-61), and the roadmap progress bar gains progressbar ARIA semantics (RoadmapProgressCard.tsx:26-32).
Backlog is mobile-safe with table-local horizontal scrolling (min-w-[760px] inside overflow-x-auto, RoadmapBacklog.tsx:20-24) versus legacy's unwrapped full-width table.

extensions.opportunity: Restore feature-name resolution in RoadmapLens by joining queryKeys.shared.features() (the exact legacy featureMap pattern in Roadmap.tsx:38-49) into makeRows, fixing both the filter labels and the row badges.
extensions.value: Removes the 'Feature #N' trust landmine and restores legacy ?feature= numeric deep-link compatibility in the same change.

extensions.opportunity: Reconcile the Completion metric: either revert to done/total or display the executable denominator next to the % (e.g. 'Completion 62% of 40 executable') and align the hero counts to it.
extensions.value: Satisfies the repo rate-display rule and the surface's own ticket criterion; prevents operators inferring wrong raw counts.

extensions.opportunity: Give RevenueDashboard and SalesReport first-class `embedded` props (and migrate RevenueDashboard's hand-rolled fetch to React Query per code conventions) so BusinessPanelFrame's nth-child CSS surgery can be deleted.
extensions.value: Eliminates the most fragile coupling on the surface — today any DOM tweak inside a legacy panel silently breaks the Business layout — and clears an acknowledged convention violation (no useState+fetch).

extensions.opportunity: Upgrade the tag filter to multi-select FilterChips (DS primitive already exists) and accept the legacy comma-separated ?tags= param as a compatibility alias.
extensions.value: Restores the OR-semantics legacy supported at the URL level and makes tag triage one-click now that tags are otherwise invisible in the backlog.

extensions.opportunity: Extend the prospect-onboarding receiver to carry the audited URL onto the created workspace (store the live domain / prefill Webflow linking) and navigate to /ws/:id/workspace-settings after creation.
extensions.value: Today the flow creates a bare workspace named after the domain but drops the URL on the floor (BusinessLens.tsx:82-93 only sends { name }); carrying the domain closes the sales-to-setup loop the button promises.

surfaceAudits.surface: shell-nav — RebuiltAppChrome / RebuiltSidebar / RebuiltBreadcrumb / RebuiltConnectionHealth / AppShell vs legacy Sidebar + Breadcrumbs + StatusBar + main
surfaceAudits.headline: The rebuilt shell is a genuine upgrade over the legacy chrome (rail collapse, mobile drawer, a11y, topbar action portal, prototype zones) with every legacy chrome capability accounted for — but it ships one real trust hazard (global tabs silently adopt the FIRST workspace as context, enabling wrong-client navigation and a wrong-workspace AdminChat) plus a three-way naming drift between sidebar, breadcrumb, and ⌘K palette that makes the same surface answer to two different names on one screen.

functionalityLost.capability: Cross-page amber 'N new content requests from client portal → View' banner above page content on every workspace tab (except content-pipeline)
functionalityLost.evidence: Legacy: src/App.tsx:579-594 renders the banner inside the legacy <main> only. Rebuilt branch (src/App.tsx:491-541) has no equivalent; the count survives only as the sidebar Content Pipeline badge (RebuiltSidebar.tsx:403-405,423) and the topbar requests pill (RebuiltBreadcrumb.tsx:222-237).

functionalityLost.capability: Always-visible topbar 'Client Requests' shortcut — legacy shows the MessageSquare button whenever a workspace is selected (badge only when >0); rebuilt renders the button ONLY when pendingContentRequests > 0, so with zero pending there is no topbar route to Requests
functionalityLost.evidence: Legacy: src/components/layout/Breadcrumbs.tsx:121-137 ({selected && <Button …>}). Rebuilt: src/components/layout/RebuiltBreadcrumb.tsx:222 ({selected && pendingContentRequests > 0 && …}). Sidebar CLIENT-FACING > Requests (RebuiltSidebar.tsx:139-147) remains a home.

functionalityLost.capability: Breadcrumb hover workspace-switcher dropdown — legacy lets the operator jump to any workspace from the topbar workspace crumb; rebuilt crumb is a plain link to the current workspace home
functionalityLost.evidence: Legacy: src/components/layout/Breadcrumbs.tsx:64-81 (group-hover dropdown listing all workspaces). Rebuilt: src/components/layout/RebuiltBreadcrumb.tsx:141-151,188-219 (single onClick to adminPath(selected.id); tooltip shows only the workspace COUNT). Switching now requires the sidebar WorkspaceSelector — and in rail mode an extra expand click first (RebuiltSidebar.tsx:342-365).

functionalityLost.capability: Honest 'no workspace selected' state on global tabs — legacy disables workspace-scoped nav items and shows an empty workspace selector on /settings etc.; rebuilt substitutes workspaces[0] as if it were selected
functionalityLost.evidence: Legacy: src/components/layout/Sidebar.tsx:234 (disabled = !selected …) + src/App.tsx:615 (AdminChat only when selected). Rebuilt: src/App.tsx:280-284 (chromeWorkspace = workspaces[0] on GLOBAL_TABS) feeding RebuiltSidebar selected prop (App.tsx:497) so isNavItemDisabled (RebuiltSidebar.tsx:184-187) enables everything against the first workspace.
functionalityLost.whereDocumented: docs/ui-rebuild/parity/global-ops-contract.md ('Rebuilt global routes now use the first available workspace for shell context when one exists') — the mount-fix mechanism is documented, but the wrong-workspace misattribution consequences below are not.

regressions.issue: Global tabs misattribute context to the FIRST workspace: on /settings, /roadmap, /revenue etc. the rebuilt sidebar's WorkspaceSelector displays workspaces[0] as the active workspace, the breadcrumb reads 'Command Center / <first-workspace> / Settings' for a GLOBAL page, clicking any workspace nav item silently navigates INTO workspaces[0] (an operator who was working in workspace B lands in workspace A's Keywords), and AdminChat mounts bound to workspaces[0] — legacy mounted no AdminChat and disabled workspace nav on global tabs. In a multi-client agency tool this is a wrong-client hazard.
regressions.evidence: src/App.tsx:280-284 (chromeWorkspace fallback), 497 (selected={chromeWorkspace}), 526-539 (CommandPalette + AdminChat get chromeWorkspace) vs legacy src/App.tsx:610-623 (palette gets selected=null, AdminChat gated on selected) and Sidebar.tsx:234 (items disabled without selected); RebuiltBreadcrumb.tsx:141-148 pushes the workspace crumb for any selected.

regressions.issue: Connectivity loss is visually de-emphasized: legacy StatusBar shows a red WifiOff icon when disconnected; the rebuilt footer renders the same state amber ('HTTP Reconnecting'), the same tone used for a merely-missing OpenAI key — an operator can miss that live updates are down.
regressions.evidence: src/components/StatusBar.tsx:15-19 (WifiOff text-red-400/80) vs src/components/layout/RebuiltConnectionHealth.tsx:20-31 (healthy ? emerald : amber for all three items).

confusions.issue: Three-way surface-name drift in one shell: the rebuilt sidebar's local overrides call surfaces 'Insights Engine', 'Keywords', 'Asset Manager', 'Content Pipeline' (RebuiltSidebar.tsx:94-161 spec.label), while the breadcrumb and ⌘K palette use registry labels 'Strategy', 'Keyword Hub', 'Assets', 'Pipeline' (RebuiltBreadcrumb.tsx:111-115 resolveNavLabelById; CommandPalette.tsx:140-155). Typing 'Insights Engine' or 'Asset Manager' into ⌘K finds nothing, and the palette still groups results under the OLD IA headers (Monitoring / Site Health / Strategy / Content — CommandPalette.tsx:44-53) that no longer match the sidebar zones.
confusions.why: An operator learns the sidebar name, then the breadcrumb over the very same page shows a different name and the palette can't find the sidebar name at all — two vocabularies for one product in one viewport. The registry already has the exact mechanism to fix this (flagBehavior labelWhenOn, used for home→'Cockpit', navRegistry.tsx:113-116) but the other four renames bypassed it.

confusions.issue: Dual-shell seam under flag ON: '/' (Command Center / WorkspaceOverview, where presence indicators live) and /ws/:id/subscriptions never mount the rebuilt chrome (App.tsx:489 canMountRebuiltSurface is false at '/'; 'subscriptions' has no REBUILT_SURFACES entry), so every logo click flips the operator between two entirely different sidebars — the legacy one even renders flag-OFF labels/items ('Home' instead of 'Cockpit', a visible-but-disabled 'Content Perf') because Sidebar.tsx:143 resolves all flags as OFF.
confusions.why: The IA, group names, colors, and even which nav items exist change between two adjacent clicks; a real operator will wonder which navigation is 'the app'. The route-level decisions are documented (coverage-audit.md Rebuilt Versus Non-Rebuilt) but the chrome-flip experience is not acknowledged anywhere.

confusions.issue: Rail workspace-initial button doesn't switch workspaces — it only expands the sidebar (RebuiltSidebar.tsx:342-365 'parity escape hatch'), making workspace switching a two-step action in rail mode with no topbar alternative (the breadcrumb switcher was removed).
confusions.why: Operators who live in the collapsed rail (the state the shell persists) pay an extra click for the single most frequent agency action — changing client.

overcomplication.issue: Three parallel label sources for one nav identity: NAV_REGISTRY labels, registry flagBehavior overrides, and RebuiltSidebar-local spec.label overrides — the local layer is what created the sidebar/palette/breadcrumb drift.
overcomplication.evidence: RebuiltSidebar.tsx:94-161 (spec.label) vs navRegistry.tsx:113-116 (flagBehavior labelWhenOn already does flag-conditional renaming for 'home') vs CommandPalette.tsx:140-143 and RebuiltBreadcrumb.tsx:111-115 consuming only the registry.
overcomplication.suggestion: Move the four prototype renames (seo-strategy→Insights Engine, seo-keywords→Keywords, media→Asset Manager, content-pipeline→Content Pipeline) into flagBehavior.labelWhenOn on 'ui-rebuild-shell'; delete the local spec.label layer so sidebar, palette, and breadcrumb agree by construction.

overcomplication.issue: SUB_TAB_LABELS_BY_PAGE is a 70-line hand-maintained duplicate of every surface's internal tab vocabulary inside the breadcrumb, with contract coverage for only one page (Keyword Hub).
overcomplication.evidence: RebuiltBreadcrumb.tsx:25-96; tests/component/layout/RebuiltBreadcrumb.test.tsx only asserts 'only renders Keyword Hub sub-segments the receiver actually honors'.
overcomplication.suggestion: Derive sub-tab labels from shared per-surface tab constants (most surfaces already export them) or extend the receiver-honors contract test across all pages so a renamed/removed tab can't leave a stale breadcrumb.

overcomplication.issue: The topbar 'Command' button opens the palette by dispatching a synthetic ⌘K KeyboardEvent at window (inherited from legacy but now duplicated).
overcomplication.evidence: RebuiltAppChrome.tsx:107-113 and Breadcrumbs.tsx:105-113 both fabricate KeyboardEvent('keydown', {key:'k', metaKey:true}); CommandPalette.tsx:106-124 is the only listener.
overcomplication.suggestion: Expose an imperative open (context or tiny store) from CommandPalette and call it from both triggers; the synthetic-event bridge breaks silently if the palette's key handling ever changes.

overcomplication.issue: RebuiltSidebar mounts twice on narrow viewports while the nav drawer is open (rail copy + drawer copy), duplicating NotificationBell and WorkspaceSelector instances.
overcomplication.evidence: RebuiltAppChrome.tsx:185-201 (AppShell sidebar) + 241-264 (Drawer copy); Drawer returns null when closed (ui/overlay/Drawer.tsx:159) so the duplication is transient but real while open.
overcomplication.suggestion: Acceptable as-is; if NotificationBell ever gains per-mount polling/WS work, render the drawer copy without the footer utilities.

gains: Collapsible icon rail with persisted preference (RebuiltAppChrome.tsx:75-144, 'admin-sidebar-rail') — legacy sidebar was a fixed 200px column (Sidebar.tsx:176)
Real mobile support: auto-rail below 720px plus a left navigation Drawer, with the desktop preference preserved (RebuiltAppChrome.tsx:86-105,241-264) — legacy had no narrow-viewport handling at all

Accessibility floor: skip-to-content link (AppShell.tsx:81-114), roving-tabindex arrow-key nav that skips disabled rows (RebuiltSidebar.tsx:280-286, covered by RebuiltSidebar.test.tsx), aria-current breadcrumbs, aria-live clipboard status (App.tsx:80-91)
RebuiltTopbarActions portal gives every surface an exact-once topbar action home with no double-flash (RebuiltAppChrome.tsx:45-65) — legacy had no shell action slot
Breadcrumb now renders ?tab= sub-segment labels across 11 page families (RebuiltBreadcrumb.tsx:25-96) — legacy breadcrumb stopped at the page level
Focus-mode exit via Escape correctly defers to open Modal/Drawer/Popover overlays (AppShell.tsx:33-37,53-65) — the legacy handler only guarded editable targets (App.tsx:232-243)
Competitors gets a first-class nav home in the Strategy & Content zone (RebuiltSidebar.tsx:81-90) — legacy reachable only by deep link (owner-approved, ODP-011/coverage-audit)

Disabled nav items explain themselves ('Select a workspace first' / 'Connect a site first', RebuiltSidebar.tsx:414,425) — legacy showed the ordinary description tooltip on disabled rows (Sidebar.tsx:241)

Footer health strip labels are truthful: 'OpenAI' replaces legacy's stale 'Alt Text' label and adds the ⌘K kbd hint to the visible Command trigger (RebuiltConnectionHealth.tsx:50-52, RebuiltAppChrome.tsx:220-233)

Collapsed-group pending badge parity is kept and item badge survives on Content Pipeline (RebuiltSidebar.tsx:403-405,423), with component-test coverage the legacy sidebar never had (tests/component/layout/RebuiltSidebar.test.tsx)

extensions.opportunity: Preserve the operator's actual workspace on global tabs: remember the last-visited workspace (in-memory or localStorage) and use it — not workspaces[0] — for chromeWorkspace/AdminChat/palette context; render an honest 'no workspace' selector state when none was visited.
extensions.value: Eliminates the wrong-client navigation hazard and the misattributed 'Command Center / <first workspace> / Settings' breadcrumb; directly serves the multi-client agency operator persona.

extensions.opportunity: Unify nav labels through NAV_REGISTRY flagBehavior (labelWhenOn on 'ui-rebuild-shell') and index prototype names as palette search aliases.
extensions.value: One vocabulary across sidebar, breadcrumb, and ⌘K; searching 'Insights Engine' or 'Asset Manager' starts working; deletes the drift-prone local override layer.

extensions.opportunity: Update the ⌘K palette group headers to the rebuilt zones (Strategy & Content / Search & Site Health / Optimization / Client-facing) when the shell flag is ON, and add Competitors to the palette while the flag is ON.
extensions.value: Palette results match the sidebar map the operator is looking at; Competitors becomes keyboard-reachable.

extensions.opportunity: Always render the topbar Requests affordance (zero-state included) and restore a lightweight workspace switcher in the topbar (breadcrumb workspace crumb → dropdown), especially valuable in rail mode.
extensions.value: Restores the two removed topbar shortcuts without touching the sidebar; keeps client communication one click away during onboarding lulls when the pending count is zero.

extensions.opportunity: Rebuild the '/' Command Center (WorkspaceOverview incl. presence) as a rebuilt surface so the flag-ON session never flips chrome, and give 'subscriptions' a rebuilt receiver or a flag-ON redirect to its pipeline alias.
extensions.value: Closes the dual-shell seam — the last place a flag-ON operator sees the legacy sidebar — and lets the legacy shell retire cleanly when the flag graduates.

extensions.opportunity: Promote the disconnected footer state to error severity (red) and consider surfacing WS/live-update health distinctly from HTTP.
extensions.value: An operator immediately notices lost live updates instead of reading amber as 'configuration nag'; aligns with the ds-rebuilt error-severity gate.

surfaceAudits.headline: Diagnostics and Requests are genuine upgrades and the per-workspace Outcome Dashboard is parity-by-reuse, but the rebuilt Outcomes Book buries the attention-triage workflow behind per-row expansion and stamps an untrue "Rolling 90 days" period label on data that is actually all-time value + 28-day GSC clicks + 30-day win rates.
legacySources: src/App.tsx:260-284,412,433,464-484 (legacy flag-OFF render branch + requests ?tab= receiver)
rebuiltSources: src/App.tsx:489-542 (rebuilt mount, per-workspace remount key at :518)

functionalityLost.capability: Outcomes Book at-a-glance attention triage: legacy sorted attention-needed workspaces to the top, showed an inline warning triangle + attentionReason under the workspace name, and had a dedicated Status column (Review/On track). Rebuilt sorts by value/month and only reveals the attention badge and reason after expanding each row.
functionalityLost.evidence: Legacy: src/components/admin/outcomes/OutcomesOverview.tsx:206-212 (attention-first sort), :112-120 (row triangle + reason), :177-183 (Status column). Rebuilt: src/components/global-ops-rebuilt/OutcomesBookLens.tsx:25-28 (value-desc sort only), src/components/global-ops-rebuilt/wave-c/outcomes/OutcomesBookTable.tsx:91 (no attention column in collapsed row), :236 and :254-262 (attention badge/reason only inside expanded evidence). The lens header even promises 'which workspace is due for attention without opening every dashboard' (OutcomesBookLens.tsx:47).

functionalityLost.capability: Client-computed portfolio aggregate tiles on Outcomes Overview: Avg win rate, Actions tracked, Scored (30d), and Need-attention count.
functionalityLost.evidence: Legacy: src/components/admin/outcomes/OutcomesOverview.tsx:45-80 (AggregateStats). Rebuilt: no equivalent; a permanent 'Book totals are not yet available' banner replaces them (src/components/global-ops-rebuilt/OutcomesBookLens.tsx:60-65).
functionalityLost.whereDocumented: docs/ui-rebuild/parity/global-ops-contract.md GO-004 (line 111) and the judgment-call note 'Removed the React-created Outcomes Book portfolio totals' (line 124); owner-approved 2026-07-10 per parity-backlog.md item 16.

functionalityLost.capability: Per-workspace top-win summary visible for EVERY workspace row (keyword/page + delta%). The rebuilt table shows only a server-owned wins count per row; top-win detail moved to the 'Recent wins' card which is capped at the 6 most recently scored workspaces, so books with >6 workspaces lose row-level top-win detail for the rest (the expanded evidence panel does not include it either).
functionalityLost.evidence: Legacy: src/components/admin/outcomes/OutcomesOverview.tsx:159-174 (Top win column on every row). Rebuilt: src/components/global-ops-rebuilt/wave-c/outcomes/OutcomesBookTable.tsx:195 (count only), :216-237 (expansion has no top-win field); src/components/global-ops-rebuilt/wave-c/outcomes/OutcomeRecentWins.tsx:44-48 (.slice(0, 6)).

regressions.issue: The rebuilt Outcomes Book labels the whole table 'Rolling 90 days' (twice), but none of the displayed data is a 90-day window: 'Value delivered / mo' and 'Wins' come from an ALL-TIME last-100-wins rollup with no date filter, 'Clicks' is a 28-day GSC window, and win rate / scored counts in the expansion are 30-day. Legacy made no period claim (and correctly labelled its one windowed column 'Scored (30d)'). An operator or client-facing report built from this screen would misstate the measurement period.
regressions.evidence: Rebuilt labels: src/components/global-ops-rebuilt/OutcomesBookLens.tsx:43 and src/components/global-ops-rebuilt/wave-c/outcomes/OutcomesBookTable.tsx:77. Data reality: server/routes/workspaces.ts:339 → getWorkspaceOutcomeValueRollup → server/outcome-tracking.ts:98-114 (getWinsWithValueByWorkspace has no date predicate, LIMIT 100; a windowed variant getWinsWithValueByWorkspaceInWindow at :115 exists but is not used here); server/routes/workspaces.ts:133 (getSearchOverview(..., 28, ...) for clicks); shared/types/outcome-tracking.ts:525 (scoredLast30d). Legacy comparator: src/components/admin/outcomes/OutcomesOverview.tsx:268 ('Scored (30d)').

regressions.issue: Win rate, active actions, and scored-30d — legacy top-level scan columns — now require expanding each workspace row individually, one at a time, to compare across the book (there is no expand-all).
regressions.evidence: Legacy: src/components/admin/outcomes/OutcomesOverview.tsx:124-144 (Win rate / Trend / Active / Scored columns). Rebuilt: src/components/global-ops-rebuilt/wave-c/outcomes/OutcomesBookTable.tsx:216-237 (all three only inside the expanded evidence DefinitionLists).

regressions.issue: Diagnostic root-cause confidence tone is semantically inverted vs legacy: high confidence now renders a RED badge (legacy: emerald/success), medium amber, low zinc — so the most trustworthy finding looks like the most dangerous one, and red is reserved for errors under the design laws.
regressions.evidence: Rebuilt: src/components/global-ops-rebuilt/wave-b/diagnostics/DiagnosticsFindings.tsx:5-9 (CONFIDENCE_TONE = { high: 'red', medium: 'amber', low: 'zinc' }). Legacy: src/components/admin/DiagnosticReport/RootCauseCard.tsx:4-8 (high: emerald success, medium: amber, low: muted).

confusions.issue: Requests header copy says 'What your clients sent back' with subtitle 'The operator side of the portal thread', but the DEFAULT tab (Deliverables) lists what the OPERATOR sent TO clients that is still awaiting them (ClientDeliverablesPane groups by 'Awaiting client' etc.).
confusions.why: A busy operator landing on /requests reads 'what clients sent back', sees a list of their own outbound deliverables pending client action, and second-guesses whether they are looking at inbound or outbound work. Legacy's plain 'Client Deliverables' tab label carried no such framing conflict.

confusions.issue: Two different coverage fractions appear for the same workspace with near-identical presentation: the collapsed row subtitle shows 'Coverage reconciled/tracked' (src/components/global-ops-rebuilt/wave-c/outcomes/OutcomesBookTable.tsx:50-55,161-166) while the expansion shows 'Measured coverage measured/tracked' (:230-235). Legacy showed only one ('N/M measured', OutcomesOverview.tsx:150-156).
confusions.why: An operator sees e.g. 'Coverage 2/6' on the row and 'Measured coverage 0/6' one click deeper with no explanation of reconciled-vs-measured; the numbers disagree and neither term is defined on the surface.

confusions.issue: The row-level 'Wins' count (all-time, value-eligible wins from workspace.outcomeValue) sits next to expansion fields computed over 30 days ('Scored in 30 days', 'Win rate'), under a single 'Rolling 90 days' banner — three unlabelled windows in one card.
confusions.why: Cross-referencing 'Wins: 12' against 'Scored in 30 days: 2' with a 90-day banner overhead is arithmetic that cannot reconcile; operators will distrust the table (rate/denominator-honesty rule in CLAUDE.md exists precisely for this).

confusions.issue: The 'Book totals are not yet available' info banner renders permanently on every visit to the Outcomes Book (OutcomesBookLens.tsx:60-65), not just when something failed.
confusions.why: Persistent banner-styled copy reads as a degraded/error state to a fresh operator; after the third visit it is pure ceremony explaining an internal server-authority decision (GO-004) they cannot act on.

overcomplication.issue: OutcomeWorkspaceFrame restyles the reused legacy OutcomeDashboard via a stack of positional descendant selectors ('[&>div>div:nth-child(2)>div:last-child>form]:!grid', ':first-child sr-only', etc.), silently coupled to the exact child order of OutcomeDashboard/RecordPublishedWorkCard DOM.
overcomplication.suggestion: Either pass a compact/headerless prop into OutcomeDashboard (it is already shared by both shells) or accept the legacy spacing; any banner or reorder inside OutcomeDashboard will mis-target these selectors with no compile-time or test signal.

overcomplication.issue: OutcomesBookLens fetches the entire command-center aggregate (useWorkspaceOverviewData = 5 parallel endpoints incl. activity, anomalies, presence, and AI time-saved — none rendered here; the /api/workspace-overview endpoint also performs a live GSC call per workspace server-side) just to obtain per-workspace outcomeValue/gscRollup rows.
overcomplication.suggestion: When the GO-004 server-owned book rollup lands, give the book its own read endpoint (or a params-trimmed overview) instead of piggybacking the heaviest admin query.

overcomplication.issue: RequestsModeTray hand-rolls a pill tab strip (roving tabindex wiring, selected-tab scroll-into-view math, per-state '!' class overrides on Button) for four static tabs when the DS already ships Segmented/LensSwitcher and the legacy TabBar for exactly this interaction.
overcomplication.suggestion: Fold the tray into Segmented (or extend it with the overflow-scroll behavior once) so the next surface doesn't clone this 65-line block.

overcomplication.issue: Diagnostics metric tiles override the MetricTile primitive with a one-off constant of calc()-nudged font sizes and forced mono/uppercase to hit prototype pixel values.
overcomplication.suggestion: If this density variant is legitimate, promote it to a MetricTile size/density prop; per-surface '!' override strings defeat the tokens-only discipline the rebuild gates exist to protect.

gains: Outcomes Book adds server-owned evidence legacy never showed: dollar value/month with relative Meter, GSC clicks and avg position, attribution split (platform vs client-executed), site-health issue matrix badges, and reconciled+measured coverage — all from typed server rollups, honoring the GO-004 no-client-math boundary (OutcomesBookTable.tsx:177-251 vs OutcomesOverview.tsx which had none of these).

Outcomes Book lists EVERY workspace (joined from workspace overview) with an honest 'Awaiting data' badge, instead of only workspaces already present in the outcome overview payload (OutcomesBookLens.tsx:22-30, OutcomesBookTable.tsx:58).

New 'Recent wins' feed with attribution honesty built in: excludes not_acted_on and labels externally_executed as 'Client-side · measured result' (OutcomeRecentWins.tsx:44-48, 83-85) — the CLAUDE.md attribution-honesty seam applied where legacy's topWin column applied no filter.

Outcomes Book shows a warning banner when either query errors while still rendering last-known rows (OutcomesBookLens.tsx:51-58); legacy defaulted errors to an empty array and displayed the misleading 'No outcome data yet' empty state (OutcomesOverview.tsx:204, 235-243).

Diagnostics list gains a real error state with retry (DiagnosticsListView.tsx:43-46) — legacy rendered the empty state on fetch failure (DiagnosticReportPage.tsx:146-169 handles only isLoading); report detail gains 'Try again' + 'All reports' recovery actions (DiagnosticsReportView.tsx:161-171).

Diagnostics report detail gains navigation legacy lacked entirely: 'Back to Search & Traffic' and 'All diagnostic reports' links (DiagnosticsReportView.tsx:43-55); legacy ?report= detail had no way back to the list except browser-back.

Diagnostics evidence is materially richer: full period comparison (impressions/position/CTR with previous→current), query breakdown with prev→current deltas, internal-link deficit, recently-lost backlinks, recent activity, and related signals (concurrent anomalies + existing insights) — DiagnosticsEvidence.tsx:74-175 vs EvidenceAccordion.tsx which showed only position history, current-only query stats, redirects, links, backlinks, unavailable sources.

Diagnostics report title is humanized via pathToTitle instead of legacy's raw path in the H1 (diagnosticPresentation.ts:21-23 vs DiagnosticReportPage.tsx:43), with the raw path preserved as mono metadata; the running state explains the five evidence sources being gathered (DiagnosticsReportView.tsx:57-93) instead of a bare empty-state.

Requests tab state is now URL-backed and shareable/refresh-stable: clicking a tab writes ?tab= (useGlobalOpsSurfaceState.ts:70-83), and same-page ?tab= changes are honored continuously — legacy's receiver effect only fired on workspace/tab-entry change (App.tsx:268-273) and tab clicks never touched the URL.

Requests adds an explicit invalid-?tab= fallback warning banner (RequestsLens.tsx:136-145); legacy silently defaulted to Deliverables. The tab tray is a proper ARIA tablist with roving tabindex keyboard navigation (RequestsLens.tsx:59-93).

All four Requests capability homes (Deliverables incl. Remind + WS invalidation, Signals, All Requests incl. bulk status/delete + cross-workspace filter, Client Actions incl. mark-complete) are preserved verbatim by reusing the legacy panes, remounted per workspace via the App-level key (App.tsx:518), matching legacy's per-pane keys.

extensions.opportunity: Restore attention triage on the Outcomes Book collapsed row: show the attention badge (already built, OutcomesBookTable.tsx:57-62) in a visible column and offer an 'attention first' sort or default secondary sort, matching the header's own promise.
extensions.value: Reinstates the one legacy workflow this table was for — spotting at-risk accounts without opening rows — using data already in the payload; zero new server work.

extensions.opportunity: Make the period label truthful: either switch the book rollup to the existing windowed statement getWinsWithValueByWorkspaceInWindow (server/outcome-tracking.ts:115) with a real 90-day window, or replace the two 'Rolling 90 days' strings with per-column window annotations (all-time value, 28d clicks, 30d win rate).
extensions.value: Removes a trust landmine before a client or the owner screenshots this table into a report; the windowed SQL already exists.

extensions.opportunity: Add live counts to the Requests mode tray (new signals, awaiting-implementation actions, stale deliverables, new requests) — the queries all exist in the reused panes' hooks.
extensions.value: The segmented hybrid (GO-001) currently gives no signal about WHICH tab needs the operator; counts turn four blind tabs into a triage strip and would also serve the eventual single-feed model.

extensions.opportunity: Ship the GO-004 server-owned book rollup endpoint (value, clicks, wins, attention counts with the same attribution rules) and replace both the permanent 'Book totals are not yet available' banner and the piggybacked useWorkspaceOverviewData fetch.
extensions.value: Closes the documented deferral's exit condition, restores the portfolio-level summary legacy users had (now attribution-honest), and cuts the book's server cost dramatically.

extensions.opportunity: Diagnostics list rows could surface the traffic-change headline (changePercent.clicks) for completed reports, since the detail already computes it.
extensions.value: Lets an operator rank which completed diagnostic to open first instead of reading titles and dates only.

surfaceAudits.headline: The rebuilt Page Rewriter preserves the full legacy two-pane workflow and genuinely improves it (a working ?pageUrl deep link where legacy's was dead, an evidence band surfacing server data legacy discarded, race-hardened chat/load, honest error/quota states); the real losses are small undocumented papercuts — picker outside-click dismissal, tuned DOCX heading styles, a never-resetting quota lock — and the surface's biggest weakness is inherited from legacy: no surface anywhere links into it.
legacySources: src/App.tsx:220,223-241,288,483 (flag-OFF mount, focus mode, dead rewritePageUrl handoff)
rebuiltSources: server/routes/rewrite-chat.ts:213-256 (shared load-page endpoint incl. keyword projection)

functionalityLost.capability: Page-picker popover dismisses on outside click. Legacy registered a document-level mousedown listener that closed the combobox when clicking anywhere outside it; the rebuilt popover only closes via its explicit Close button, Escape while the input is focused, or a selection — clicking elsewhere on the page leaves it hanging open over the workspace.
functionalityLost.evidence: Legacy: src/components/page-rewrite-chat/usePageRewriteChatShell.ts:98-107 (document mousedown outside-close). Rebuilt: src/components/page-rewriter-rebuilt/PageRewriterPagePicker.tsx:111-185 (hand-rolled absolute dropdown, Close button at :135, no outside handler) and src/components/page-rewriter-rebuilt/usePageRewriterSurfaceState.ts (no document listener anywhere; grep for mousedown in the dir returns only the print afterprint listener).

functionalityLost.capability: Styled DOCX export. Legacy DOCX embedded four tuned heading paragraph styles (Calibri, sized/spaced/outlined H1–H4, dark ink colors); the rebuilt export passes profile 'rebuilt', which omits paragraphStyles entirely, so exported headings fall back to Word's default heading look. Filenames also silently changed from `{slug}-brief.docx` / `{slug}-brief.md` to `{slug}-rewrite.docx` / `{slug}-rewrite.md`. The exported file IS the deliverable on this export-only surface, so this is a silent output-quality change.
functionalityLost.evidence: Legacy: src/components/page-rewrite-chat/usePageRewriteChatShell.ts:287-294,313 (profile: 'legacy', -brief filenames) → src/components/page-rewrite-chat/pageRewriteDocxExport.ts:107-135 (4 styled headings). Rebuilt: src/components/page-rewriter-rebuilt/pageRewriterExport.ts:55-69,93-99 (profile: 'rebuilt', -rewrite filenames) → pageRewriteDocxExport.ts:138-141 (no paragraphStyles). Deliberate in code (tests/unit/page-rewrite-docx-export.test.ts:123-138 'keeps the rebuilt profile compact') but absent from the parity contract, which only claims all five export formats remain reachable (docs/ui-rebuild/parity/page-rewriter-contract.md:60,99).
functionalityLost.whereDocumented: Pinned by unit test tests/unit/page-rewrite-docx-export.test.ts only; NOT recorded in docs/ui-rebuild/parity/page-rewriter-contract.md or parity-backlog.md

functionalityLost.capability: Fully chrome-free focus mode. Legacy focus mode hid the sidebar entirely; the rebuilt shell retains a 62px collapsed rail in focus.
functionalityLost.evidence: Legacy: src/App.tsx:288 ('Focus mode additionally hides the sidebar'). Rebuilt: controlled shell focus bridge via src/components/page-rewriter-rebuilt/PageRewriterSurface.tsx:21,42-52 with retained rail.
functionalityLost.whereDocumented: docs/ui-rebuild/parity/page-rewriter-contract.md:70 (Joshua explicitly approved retaining the 62px rail on 2026-07-10; 'removal is no longer an open circle-back') and parity-backlog.md item 8

regressions.issue: Playbook chips are live before any page is loaded. Legacy showed the six quick prompts only after a page was loaded (they lived inside the loaded-page empty state); the rebuilt renders all six chips permanently and only disables them while sending or quota-locked, so an operator can fire 'Answer-first intro' or 'Weave in keywords' against no page at all — the AI receives a page-rewrite instruction with zero page context and returns a generic non-answer.
regressions.evidence: Legacy: src/components/page-rewrite-chat/PageRewriteChatPane.tsx:58-72 (QUICK_PROMPTS gated on pageData). Rebuilt: src/components/page-rewriter-rebuilt/PageRewriterChatPane.tsx:220-242 (chips render unconditionally; disabled = sending || quota only) and usePageRewriterSurfaceState.ts:280-287 (page fields sent as undefined when no pageData).

regressions.issue: The first-429 quota lock never releases. setQuotaHit(true) is the only mutation — there is no reset on page switch, param clear, or timer, and the banner text promises 'Try again after quota resets' while the composer, send button, and all playbook chips stay disabled until the surface fully remounts. Legacy simply appended the 429 error to the transcript and allowed immediate retry. Since admin /api routes carry no rate limiter (limiters mount only on /api/public and /mcp — server/app.ts:216-241), any 429 reaching this surface is transient upstream/proxy noise, and one such response bricks the assistant for the session.
regressions.evidence: Rebuilt: src/components/page-rewriter-rebuilt/usePageRewriterSurfaceState.ts:88,296-302 (only setQuotaHit(true); reset effect at :197-212 clears messages but not quotaHit), :389 (aiDisabledReason). Legacy: src/components/page-rewrite-chat/usePageRewriteChatShell.ts:182-193 (error appended, retry allowed). The first-429 lock itself is contract-documented (page-rewriter-contract.md:96,194 'AD-020'), but the absence of any unlock path is not.

regressions.issue: The rebuilt fixed formatting toolbar dropped the legacy selection-guard (onMouseDown preventDefault). Verified working in a Chromium pane via minimal repro (selection survives a plain button click; execCommand bold applied to the full selection), so this is not broken in Chrome — but the legacy code carried the guard deliberately, wrapSelectionHeading silently no-ops on a collapsed selection, and Safari/Firefox behavior is unverified by any test or smoke (component tests assert only that the toolbar exists).
regressions.evidence: Legacy: src/components/page-rewrite-chat/PageRewriteDocumentPane.tsx:162 (onMouseDown preventDefault on floating toolbar). Rebuilt: src/components/page-rewriter-rebuilt/PageRewriterDocumentPane.tsx:170-185 (no mousedown guard), src/components/ui/layout/Toolbar.tsx (none; adds roving-tabindex focus), src/components/page-rewrite-chat/pageRewriteChatActions.ts:19-21 (isCollapsed early-return). Chromium repro: scratchpad seltest — 'at click: collapsed=false', bold applied.

confusions.issue: The rewriter has a prominent 'Back to audit' button but nothing anywhere links INTO it with a page. The validated ?pageUrl= deep-link receiver has zero senders (grep across src finds no PAGE_REWRITER_DEEP_LINK_PARAM or adminPath(...,'rewrite') senders), and the legacy handoff prop was already dead (App.tsx:220/483 — setRewritePageUrl is only ever called with null). An operator triaging an audit issue must navigate to Page Rewriter from the nav and manually re-find the page every single time.
confusions.why: The Back button implies a round-trip workflow (audit → rewrite → back) whose forward half does not exist; operators will hunt the audit surface for a 'Rewrite this page' action that isn't there. Parity-neutral (equally absent in legacy) but the most visible workflow hole on the surface.

confusions.issue: Playbook chip labels ('Add evidence', 'Match brand voice') differ from the full prompt that then appears verbatim as the user's own chat message ('Add citation-ready data points and statistics', ...). Only the hover title reveals the full text before sending.
confusions.why: The transcript shows the operator 'saying' a sentence they never saw themselves choose; on first use this reads as the tool putting words in their mouth. (Documented design: contract line 44/112 keeps full prompts as control titles.)

confusions.issue: Audit issue chips render on a single horizontally-scrolling line (overflow-x-auto) in the evidence band; legacy wrapped all chips so up to 20 were simultaneously visible.
confusions.why: On a page with many issues, most chips are hidden behind an unobvious horizontal scroll — an operator can reasonably conclude the page has 2-3 issues when it has 20. Evidence: rebuilt PageRewriterDocumentPane.tsx:92 vs legacy PageRewriteDocumentPane.tsx:129 (flex-wrap).

confusions.issue: 'Copy as HTML' copies the editor's raw innerHTML, which is saturated with app-internal utility classes (e.g. class="t-caption text-slate-500 leading-[1.7] mb-3" baked in by buildDocHtml) and transient inline styles.
confusions.why: An operator copying HTML expects CMS-pasteable markup; instead they get markup carrying hmpsn-internal Tailwind/token classes that mean nothing outside the app. Evidence: pageRewriterExport.ts:72-80 (raw innerHTML) + pageRewriteChatDocument.ts:25-46 (classes baked into doc DOM).

overcomplication.issue: URL-param scaffolding copied from multi-param surfaces manages exactly one real param. SEARCH_PARAM_DEFAULTS declares comboQuery/comboIdx defaults that never touch the URL, and the generic multi-key updateParams(Record<string, ParamValue>) helper is only ever called with the single pageUrl key.
overcomplication.suggestion: Inline a single setPageUrlParam(url | null) helper and delete SEARCH_PARAM_DEFAULTS; the combobox state is plain local state and should not carry URL-param naming.

overcomplication.issue: The quota banner's hardcoded partial-progress line '0 of 1 responses completed before the quota was hit.' is batch-job telemetry grafted onto a single-request chat — it is always 0 of 1 and adds no information.
overcomplication.suggestion: Drop quotaPartialMessage entirely on this surface; the banner title plus disabled controls already communicate the state.

gains: Working ?pageUrl= deep link with validation, invalid-param warning banner, and URL write-back on every successful load (refresh- and share-safe). Legacy's initialPageUrl handoff was permanently undefined — App.tsx:220,483 never set rewritePageUrl to a value — so legacy had no deep link at all.

Evidence band surfaces primaryKeyword / rank / monthly traffic / optimization score that the load-page endpoint has always returned (server/routes/rewrite-chat.ts:241-245) but legacy fetched and discarded; honest '—' for unavailable values, blue read-only data styling.
Seeded page-specific AI greeting including the target keyword, plus AI/user avatars — orients the operator on what is loaded and what to do next.
New 'Copy as HTML' export mode alongside the five legacy modes (pageRewriterExport.ts:72-80).

Failed page load now renders a typed ErrorState with a Retry action and 502-aware network framing (PageRewriterDocumentPane.tsx:195-204, usePageRewriterSurfaceState.ts:360-364); legacy showed a bare error string with no retry.

Race hardening absent from legacy: request-id guards drop stale page-load responses after A→B→A switches, and a context-epoch guard prevents an in-flight chat answer from landing in the next page's transcript (usePageRewriterSurfaceState.ts:96-109,128-155,259-317) — legacy would append a stale answer regardless.
First-429 quota banner with disabled AI actions gives an honest paused state instead of legacy's raw error line (AD-020).
Sitemap-fetch failure now shows an inline banner in the picker (PageRewriterPagePicker.tsx:138-144); legacy silently coerced errors to an empty list.
Issue chips gain a '+N more' overflow badge beyond 20 (legacy silently truncated) and an affirmative 'No current audit issues' state (legacy hid the row).
Playbook prompts remain reachable after the conversation starts (legacy quick prompts existed only in the pre-first-message empty state).
Picker rows show page title alongside slug, proper listbox/option a11y wiring, and HTML-entity decoding of scraped titles (decodePageText); legacy rendered slug-only rows and raw entities.
Mobile: panes stack below lg with pane-local scrolling; legacy hard-coded two 50%-width columns at every viewport.

Mutation feedback via toasts on apply-to-section success and clipboard copies; honest 'Export-only draft / Not live / Not saved or published to the CMS.' footer replacing legacy's unstated persistence semantics.

extensions.opportunity: Wire senders into the now-working deep link: a 'Rewrite this page' action on Site Audit page rows, Page Intelligence, and Keyword Hub page drill-downs targeting /ws/:id/rewrite?pageUrl=<url>. The receiver is validated, exact-once-guarded, and already covered by component tests — only senders are missing.
extensions.value: Closes the surface's biggest workflow hole (major confusion above): audit-to-rewrite becomes one click instead of manual page re-finding; makes the existing 'Back to audit' button part of a real round trip.

extensions.opportunity: Give the picker popover standard light-dismiss (outside click + Escape from anywhere) by adopting the DS Popover machinery or overlayUtils instead of the hand-rolled absolute div.
extensions.value: Restores the legacy dismissal behavior and aligns with every other rebuilt overlay's interaction contract.

extensions.opportunity: Add an unlock path to the quota state: honor Retry-After / reset on page switch, or add a 'Try again' button to the banner.
extensions.value: Prevents a single transient 429 from disabling the assistant for the whole session; makes the banner's own 'try again after quota resets' promise actionable.

extensions.opportunity: Strip internal class/style attributes when exporting (Copy as HTML, and consider ratifying or reverting the compact DOCX profile with tuned heading styles).
extensions.value: Makes exported artifacts — the surface's only deliverable — clean and client-presentable without manual cleanup.

extensions.opportunity: Gate or contextually rewrite the playbook chips when no page is loaded (e.g. clicking a chip opens the page picker first), and show a 'Loading pages…' state in the picker while pagesQuery is in flight instead of 'No sitemap - paste a full URL above'.
extensions.value: Prevents context-free AI prompts and a misleading empty-sitemap message during the first fetch (the latter is a legacy-inherited papercut).

extensions.opportunity: Make the evidence band actionable: link rank/traffic/optimization values to Page Intelligence for the loaded page.
extensions.value: Turns read-only evidence into a drill-down path, leveraging data the platform already joins server-side.

personas.verdictLine: This is the first version of the admin UI that actually understands my morning — the Cockpit's verdict-first spine, work grouped into fix/send/pitch streams, and the client trust-spine preview on the Engine are exactly what I asked for on July 3, and I'd run flag-ON daily starting tomorrow — but I cannot retire legacy yet, because all three of my distrust triggers fired: numbers contradict each other across surfaces (2215 vs 4187 warnings on the same screen, $0 vs "— Unavailable" for the same missing provider, a "Rolling 90 days" label on data that is nothing of the sort), work I depend on is hidden behind unlabeled footer icons (Requests — where a human client is literally waiting), and worst, the send-and-repair spine quietly lies to me: a Cockpit button labeled "Send" that only navigates, a CMS send-to-client that fails silently, and a Site Audit drawer that reads as fixing 14 pages while acting on one — each of those is a path to me telling a client something happened that didn't, which is the one mistake a solo agency cannot survive.

topFindings.issue: Site Audit issue drawer's Accept/Send/Add-task actions act on only the FIRST affected page while the drawer is titled with the group and shows '14 pages' badges — I would believe I repaired 14 pages, report that to the client, and have fixed one. 'Accept all 785' has no preview or undo. (Code audit rated this the one blocker; per-page inventory has no rebuilt home.)
topFindings.whereSeen: Site Audit issue drawer + bulk band, flag-ON walk + code audit

topFindings.issue: CMS 'Send to client' from the SEO Editor selected-rows toolbar fails silently — the error only renders inside the CMS drawer I don't have open. A client-facing send that dies without telling me is the exact failure mode that costs me an account.
topFindings.whereSeen: SEO Editor toolbar (code audit: approvalError rendered only in drawer)

topFindings.issue: The send spine is inconsistent about what 'Send' means: Cockpit queue buttons say 'Send'/'Propose' but only navigate (nothing sends), and the Engine's real send button only exists once stagedCount > 0 — at zero staged there is no send affordance or hint anywhere, where legacy always showed the green 'Send update' + '0 staged · stage moves below' teaching line (verified EngineSurface.tsx:381,407-417).
topFindings.whereSeen: Cockpit work queue + Insights Engine topbar, browser walk + source check

topFindings.issue: Numbers contradict each other in ways I'd have to re-verify before trusting: Site Audit chips say 2215 warnings while its own copy says 4187 three lines down; 'solid shape' verdict over an On-page 0 red score; legacy home says 'No audit yet' while rebuilt shows the Mar 12 audit at 78; Keyword Hub hard '$0 Monthly value' vs Cockpit '— Unavailable' for the same unconfigured provider; Outcomes Book stamps 'Rolling 90 days' on all-time value + 28-day clicks + 30-day win rates.
topFindings.whereSeen: Site Audit, Cockpit vs Keyword Hub, legacy home vs rebuilt, Outcomes Book

topFindings.issue: My morning triage lost its alert feed: legacy home surfaced 21 anomaly alerts (4 red) with investigation narratives; rebuilt Cockpit compresses to '1 risk signal / Risk (0)' and nobody could say where the 21 alerts live flag-ON. Either the new classification is smarter or I'm blind — until proven, I'm blind.
topFindings.whereSeen: Cockpit vs legacy WorkspaceHome, browser walk

topFindings.issue: Requests and Action Results have no labeled sidebar home flag-ON — they live in an icon-only footer strip. Requests is where clients answer me ('a human is waiting'); hiding it behind an unlabeled icon is my literal distrust trigger.
topFindings.whereSeen: Rebuilt sidebar footer strip (a11y-tree verified in walk)

topFindings.issue: A rate-limited bulk PageSpeed run fires a SUCCESS toast and renders a fabricated red 0 'average score' — the platform inventing a number when the API returned nothing is exactly the honesty failure the rebuild was supposed to eliminate.
topFindings.whereSeen: Performance surface (code audit: empty-bulk guard dropped)

topFindings.issue: Competitors 'Edit set' and both setup CTAs dead-end at Workspace Settings, which contains no competitor editor in any of its seven tabs — the first-run/edit path is broken; on a global tab the shell also silently adopts my FIRST workspace as context (wrong-client breadcrumb, wrong-workspace AdminChat).
topFindings.whereSeen: Competitors surface + /settings breadcrumb, code audit + walk

topFindings.issue: Daily-scale editing tools quietly dropped: SEO Editor lost per-field AI rewrite, CMS slug editing, per-item approval history, and the full bulk-preview diff; the Engine lost the visibility-score/delta strip and the content-gaps→brief handoff; the AI-visibility panel's only home is now the legacy Hub.
topFindings.whereSeen: SEO Editor, Insights Engine, Keyword Hub (code audit, all undocumented)

topFindings.issue: Stale-state papercuts that erode confidence on first load: onboarding modal insists 'Run your first SEO audit' on a workspace with a March audit; document.title stuck on 'Insights Engine' while on Cockpit; breadcrumb says 'Strategy' while the sidebar says 'Insights Engine'.
topFindings.whereSeen: Cockpit first load, browser walk

distrustMoments: Site Audit shows '2215 warnings' in the chip row while its own copy says '4187 more warnings and notices' on the same screen — which number do I tell the client?
Keyword Hub asserts a hard '$0 Monthly value' while the Cockpit honestly says 'Organic value — Unavailable' for the same missing provider — one convention is lying.
A Cockpit queue button labeled 'Send' that sends nothing — it just navigates.

The Outcomes Book banner says 'Rolling 90 days' over all-time wins, 28-day clicks, and 30-day win rates — a period label that is simply untrue on the surface I'd use to tell clients what we delivered.
Legacy home says 'No audit yet' with a red 0 badge while the rebuilt shows the Mar 12 audit scoring 78 — same workspace, same day.
A drawer badged '14 pages' offering 'Accept suggestion' that silently applies to one page.
A success toast and a red 0 average after a bulk PageSpeed run that returned zero pages.
On /settings the breadcrumb claims I'm inside 'Expero 2023' for account-global config, and AdminChat would answer as whichever workspace happens to be first.
The onboarding modal told me to run my first audit on a workspace that has one from March.
extensionsIWant: Cross-workspace morning cockpit: the verdict/stream model rolled up across all 19 workspaces ('which clients need me today'), and restore attention-first sorting + attentionReason in the Outcomes Book — my day starts across clients, not inside one.
Give Requests and Action Results labeled sidebar homes (or one labeled 'Inbox' zone) instead of the icon-only footer strip.
One shared unknown-vs-zero formatter used everywhere: no '$0' or red '0' where the honest answer is '— Unavailable'.

A zero-staged send hint on the Insights Engine ('0 staged — stage moves below to send', like legacy) so the staging model is discoverable; make Cockpit 'Send'/'Propose' actually stage, or relabel them 'Review'.
A flag-ON home for anomaly alerts with a pointer from the Cockpit risk stream, so the 21 legacy alerts are provably classified rather than dropped.
Wire Competitors 'Edit set' to a real competitor-set editor destination.
Per-page scope + a preview/undo step on Site Audit accepts (especially 'Accept all 785').
Restore per-field AI rewrite, CMS slug editing, and per-item approval history in the SEO Editor — those are my at-scale meta tools — or record them as deliberate cuts I signed off on.
noiseToCut: 'Record published work' data-entry form occupying the entire top half of Action Results — reading results is the job; demote the form below the tabs.
The ~30 monospace GSC domain chips walling Global Settings — collapse to a count + expandable list.
The permanently-empty 'Queued' board column forcing 920px horizontal scroll on the Content Pipeline.
Two permanent, non-dismissible narrative banners framing every Asset Manager visit ('Fixes the source, not the symptom' / 'From media fix to proof').
The same POV narrative rendered four times on one Engine page.

Dead prototype scaffolding shipped live: do-nothing 'Add page' button on Links, unused ?view=/?detail= param machinery, orphaned LocalPresenceSetup.tsx — ceremony I'll pay to maintain (and the code audit says CLAUDE.md itself warns about this accretion).
The stale setup-checklist modal re-appearing on Cockpit load for a workspace that's already past those steps.
resolvedFromPriorAsks: Decision-first cockpit: DELIVERED — verdict hero, decision band, fix/send/pitch tiles, whole surface fits one 1440x900 viewport; the code audit independently rates Cockpit 'net: better'.
Work grouped by kind: DELIVERED — server-classified opt/send/money/risk streams with counts and URL-addressable ?stream= filters.

Honest money numbers: SUBSTANTIALLY DELIVERED — 'proof pending'/'Measurement pending' provenance chips, truthful unconfigured-provider states, and attribution honesty ('client-published work recorded as theirs, never claimed as ours') — undermined only by the $0-vs-'—' convention split and the Outcomes Book period mislabel.
Beyond my ask, genuinely welcome: the Engine's client trust-spine preview shows me exactly what the client will see before I send — that's the sign-off tool I never had.

personas.seat: Seat 2 — Account manager on a live client call
personas.verdictLine: Put a client on speaker and open the Cockpit and this rebuild mostly earns its keep — verdict first, one screen, honest about what's stale and what's pending, and the Engine's client trust-spine preview is exactly the artifact I needed and never had. But my two make-or-break call moments are still shaky. "What did you do for us?" used to be answered by four wins and three flags sitting right on the Strategy page; those bullets now live only inside an EDIT drawer, so my talking points require opening an editor mid-call. And "just send me that update" runs into a Send button that doesn't exist until something is staged (legacy's green Send update was always there), a Cockpit row button labeled "Send" that only navigates, and an SEO Editor send that can fail without telling me — three different surfaces, three different send behaviors, none of them the one obvious path I asked for. Stack on the number contradictions ($0 vs "— Unavailable" for the same unknown, 2215 vs 4187 warnings on one screen, "On-page 0" under a "solid shape" headline) and I'm back to double-checking before I read anything aloud — which is the legacy workflow with nicer paint. Trust-but-not-compelled: fix the send spine, put my wins/flags back on the page, and this becomes the screen I actually run calls from.

topFindings.issue: Cockpit work-queue buttons labeled 'Send' (send stream) and 'Propose' (money stream) do not send or propose anything — they only navigate to another surface. On a live call I would say 'sending that now' and nothing went. A button that lies about the platform's most client-facing verb is the single worst failure for my job.
topFindings.whereSeen: Code audit, cockpit CONFUSION-major; browser walk Cockpit work queue

topFindings.issue: No send affordance on the Insights Engine at rest: the topbar 'Send N staged' button only renders when stagedCount > 0 (EngineSurface.tsx:381,407). In the walked state (0 staged) the top actions are Update / Regenerate / Refresh — an operator coming from legacy's permanent green 'Send update' CTA hunts for send mid-call and finds nothing that teaches the stage-then-send model.
topFindings.whereSeen: Browser walk Insights Engine; verified in src/components/engine-rebuilt/EngineSurface.tsx

topFindings.issue: My call script got buried: 'Wins worth saying out loud' and 'What I'd flag' (strategy-pov.ts wins/flags — the JSDoc literally describes them as client-safe) render only inside the Edit POV drawer ('Situation, lead move, wins, and flags'), not on the Engine main surface. Legacy showed 4 wins + 3 flags bullets on the page. Answering 'what did you do for us' now means opening an editing tool while the client waits.
topFindings.whereSeen: Browser walk legacy Strategy vs rebuilt Engine; verified EngineSurface.tsx:1045 + shared/types/strategy-pov.ts:22-25

topFindings.issue: Numbers that contradict each other while a client is listening: Site Audit chip row says 2215 warnings while the sentence below says '4187 more warnings and notices'; 'On-page 0' in red sits under the headline 'in solid shape'; Keyword Hub says '$0 Monthly value' where Cockpit says '— Unavailable' for the same unknown; 'Backing moves live 99' next to '0 staged · 0 with client' is a three-way vocabulary I cannot explain in ten seconds.
topFindings.whereSeen: Browser walk Site Audit, Keyword Hub, Insights Engine

topFindings.issue: Wrong-client hazard: on global tabs (/settings, /roadmap, /revenue) the rebuilt shell silently adopts the FIRST workspace as active context — sidebar, breadcrumb, and AdminChat all claim it. Mid-call, one hop through Settings and my next AdminChat question is answered about the wrong client.
topFindings.whereSeen: Code audit shell-nav REGRESSION-major; browser walk breadcrumb on Settings/Roadmap

topFindings.issue: SEO Editor's toolbar 'Send CMS to client' fails silently — validation/network errors render only inside the CMS drawer, not at the toolbar I sent from. I stage an update during the call, tell the client it's on the way, and it never went.
topFindings.whereSeen: Code audit seo-editor REGRESSION-major

topFindings.issue: Client replies — the 'a human is waiting' items I check before every call — have no labeled home flag-ON: Requests moved off the main rail to an icon-only footer strip. Legacy listed 'Requests' as a labeled sidebar item.
topFindings.whereSeen: Browser walk Requests + cross-cutting sidebar footer observation

topFindings.issue: Outcomes Book stamps 'Rolling 90 days' on data that is actually all-time value, 28-day clicks, and 30-day win rates — if I prep call numbers from it I quote an untrue window to the client.
topFindings.whereSeen: Code audit global-ops-work REGRESSION-major (minor from my seat — it's prep, not live)

topFindings.issue: The fast answer to 'did you see our traffic spike?' regressed: legacy home showed 21 dismissible anomaly cards with narratives ('Website users surged 56.3% — 2h ago'); rebuilt Cockpit compresses to '1 risk signal' and the anomaly detail requires a hop to Search & Traffic.
topFindings.whereSeen: Browser walk legacy Home vs rebuilt Cockpit discrepancy note

distrustMoments: Clicked a Cockpit row button labeled 'Send' and nothing was sent — it navigated me somewhere else. If I hadn't checked, I'd have told the client an update was on its way.
