Keyword Hub told me the client's keywords are worth '$0 Monthly value' when the truth is the data provider isn't connected — the Cockpit says '— Unavailable' for the exact same unknown one click away. Reading the $0 aloud would have told the client their SEO is worthless.
Site Audit shows 2215 warnings in the chip row and '4187 more warnings and notices' in the sentence beside it — I don't know which number to say on the call.
'On-page 0' in red directly under 'Expero 2023 is in solid shape, with a few fixes' — if the client is screen-sharing with me, I'm explaining a contradiction instead of a plan.

The breadcrumb on Settings read 'Command Center → Expero 2023 → Settings' for account-global config, and on global tabs the shell silently claims the first workspace — I was one AdminChat question away from quoting the wrong client's numbers.

Legacy home says 'Site Health: No audit yet' while the rebuilt Cockpit and Site Audit show a Mar 12 audit scoring 78 — same workspace, two UIs, one of them is wrong about whether we've even audited the site.
extensionsIWant: A permanent Send affordance on the Insights Engine: a disabled 'Send update — 0 staged' state that teaches the stage-then-send model instead of the button not existing at rest.

Put the wins/flags bullets back on the Engine main surface as a read-only 'Talking points' card — the client preview proves the content and layout already exist; I just need it without opening an editor.
Make the client trust-spine preview reachable in one action from the Cockpit (it is the single best artifact for 'here is exactly what you'll receive') — today it lives only partway down the Engine.
Rename Cockpit queue actions to what they actually do ('Open in Engine', 'Review & send') — or wire them to actually send with a confirm step.
One platform-wide convention for unknown values ('— Not measured yet' everywhere) replacing the current $0 / '—' / red-0 mix, so nothing unknown can be read aloud as a bad number.
A labeled Requests entry back on the rail, or at minimum an unread-count badge on the Cockpit's 'From <client>' rail card, so client replies can't be missed before a call.
noiseToCut: The same POV narrative renders four times on one Engine page (derived verdict headline, compact POV card, drawer editor, trust-spine preview) — I need it once, verbatim client-safe, plus the preview; the middle duplicates just make me wonder which copy actually gets sent.
Global Settings' wall of ~30 monospace SC-DOMAIN chips for the Google connection — zero call value, huge scroll cost on a surface I sometimes pass through mid-call.
The 'Record published work' data-entry form occupying the entire top half of Action Results — on a call I need to READ wins, not enter data; the form should collapse below the results.
Asset Manager's two permanent non-dismissible narrative banners ('Fixes the source, not the symptom' / 'From media fix to proof') — permanent copy that explains philosophy instead of state.
The dead 'Queued' board column on Content Pipeline that can never contain anything but forces horizontal scroll — a client asking 'what's queued?' sees an always-empty column.
resolvedFromPriorAsks: Verdict-first: delivered. The Cockpit opens with a one-line operator verdict and the whole surface fits one 1440x900 viewport; the Engine leads with a plain-language one-move POV. This was my top prior ask and it landed.
Plain-English POV: delivered — 'The point of view we send <client>' framing, no jargon in the hero, effort allocation in human terms (Win demand / Protect / Technical / Local).

Stale-artifact fear: substantially resolved — 'Generated Mar 12, 1:37 PM · Data as of Jul 16' is plainly dated, and the 'refresh available (evidence changed)' chip warns me BEFORE I read a stale narrative aloud, instead of letting a 4-month-old artifact ambush me mid-call.

Bonus I didn't ask for: the client trust-spine preview (a light-theme portal frame showing exactly what the client will see before send) directly answers 'am I about to send something embarrassing' — best new thing on the platform for my seat.

NOT resolved: 'one obvious send path'. Send now exists in four inconsistent forms — conditional on the Engine, cosmetic on the Cockpit, silent-failing in the SEO Editor, and genuinely improved on Competitors.

personas.seat: Seat 3 — Junior SEO / new hire, week one
personas.verdictLine: Honestly? The first hour is the best onboarding this platform has ever had — the Cockpit tells me what today's job is, every nav item explains itself on hover, the guides and the "how AI uses this" rail actually teach me the product, and when my lead sends me a link it drops me on the exact row they mean. But my three asks were learnability, one home per capability, and labels that say what things do — and the last two came back broken in ways the code audit confirms shipped: the same surface answers to "Insights Engine" in the sidebar, "Strategy" in the breadcrumb, and lives under a legacy group name in the palette; Architecture has two homes with different powers and AI Visibility has none; and worst of all for me, the words on client-facing buttons don't mean what they say — "Send" buttons that only navigate, a Site Audit drawer wearing a "14 pages" badge whose Accept touches one page, a CMS send that can fail without a single pixel of feedback. My whole job constraint is "don't break anything client-facing in week one," and this UI makes me guess. I can do my assigned tasks — fix metas is genuinely easier than legacy — but I'd be asking a senior "is this a bug or am I dumb?" five times a day, and that's the tax you'll actually pay for me.

topFindings.issue: I cannot trust the scope or the reality of client-facing actions: the Site Audit issue drawer shows a '14 pages' badge yet Accept/Send/Add-task act on only the FIRST page (I'd report a group fixed when 13 pages aren't); the SEO Editor has two differently-scoped CMS send paths where the drawer button reads item-scoped but sends the WHOLE selection; and the toolbar CMS 'Send to client' can fail with zero visible feedback — so I never know if the client got something, got everything, or got nothing.
topFindings.whereSeen: Code audit: site-audit LOST[blocker] per-page repair + CONFUSION[major] drawer scope; seo-editor REGRESSION[major] silent send + CONFUSION[major] dual send paths

topFindings.issue: Two taxonomies for one nav — my exact prior distrust trigger, shipped: sidebar says 'Insights Engine', breadcrumb says 'Strategy', document.title lags the route, and the ⌘K palette still groups by LEGACY zones ('Monitoring', 'Site Health') that don't match the sidebar zones I just learned. When a senior says 'check Strategy' I hit three names before I find it.
topFindings.whereSeen: Browser walk (Insights Engine breadcrumb drift, palette legacy groups, Cockpit title bug); code audit shell-nav CONFUSION[major] three-way drift

topFindings.issue: Global tabs silently adopt the FIRST workspace as active context — on /settings or /roadmap the breadcrumb reads 'Command Center → Expero 2023 → Settings' for account-global config, and the wrong workspace can leak into navigation and AdminChat. A week-one hire has no muscle memory to notice the client name is wrong; that's how I end up doing work on the wrong client.
topFindings.whereSeen: Browser walk (Settings/Roadmap breadcrumb context bug); code audit shell-nav REGRESSION[major] workspaces[0] adoption

topFindings.issue: The platform's own guidance dead-ends when I follow it: Competitors' 'Edit set' and BOTH setup CTAs land on Workspace Settings which has no competitor editor in any tab; Schema's guide step 1 says 'Generate the site plan first' but the site-plan panel doesn't exist pre-scan; Brand & AI's 'Generate personas from site' just opens a modal where the real button is buried behind 'Manage'; nothing anywhere links into Page Rewriter. Learning by exploration — the only way a new hire learns — gets punished.
topFindings.whereSeen: Code audit: competitors LOST[major] edit route, schema CONFUSION[major] fresh-workspace dead end, brand-ai CONFUSION[major] persona CTA, page-rewriter CONFUSION[major] no inbound links

topFindings.issue: My 'run an audit and work the fixes' task lost its work-through list: legacy's page-level inventory (every crawled page with its own score, traffic badge, expandable issue list) has no rebuilt home, and 'Accept all 785' is a one-click bulk action with no preview or undo — the legacy full old→new diff review is gone too. I either can't see the pages I'm fixing, or I'm offered a 785-item irreversible button I'm too scared to press.
topFindings.whereSeen: Code audit: site-audit LOST[major] page inventory; seo-editor LOST[major] bulk-preview diff; browser walk 'Accept all 785' band

topFindings.issue: False or silent outcomes make me report garbage upward: a rate-limited bulk PageSpeed run fires a SUCCESS toast and renders a fabricated red 0 average score (I'd escalate a fake emergency); Schema 'Add a page' failures are completely silent; the Links 'Add page' button does nothing at all.
topFindings.whereSeen: Code audit: performance REGRESSION[major] fabricated success, schema REGRESSION[major] silent add-page, links do-nothing button

topFindings.issue: The onboarding artifact aimed at exactly me is wrong: the 'Complete Cockpit setup — 3 of 4' modal told me to 'Run your first SEO audit' while a Mar 12 audit (score 78) exists on the same workspace — and legacy home simultaneously says 'No audit yet' while rebuilt shows 78. Day one, the checklist and the two UIs give me three different answers about whether an audit has ever run. A misfire-prone cousin: the workspace-wide 'Refresh national ranks' button sits inside a SINGLE keyword's drawer next to per-keyword controls — I'd fire a whole-workspace (paid) refresh thinking it was one keyword.
topFindings.whereSeen: Browser walk (stale onboarding step, Site Health 'No audit yet' discrepancy); code audit keywords CONFUSION[major] drawer-scoped global refresh

topFindings.issue: Count contradictions I can't self-diagnose: 2215 warnings in the chip row vs 4187 in the copy on the same Site Audit screen; 'On-page 0' in red under a 'solid shape' verdict; four Keywords lens tabs all showing the identical count (reads as a counting bug); '$0 Monthly value' where the Cockpit says '— Unavailable' for the same missing provider. Each one costs a 'is this broken or am I misreading?' interruption to a senior.
topFindings.whereSeen: Browser walk (Site Audit, Keyword Hub KPI band); code audit keywords CONFUSION[major] identical lens counts

distrustMoments: The Site Audit drawer was titled with the group check and a '14 pages' badge, offered 'Accept suggestion' and 'Send to client' with no page qualifier — and acts on one page. I would have told my lead the whole group was handled.

I clicked the Cockpit queue's 'Send' button braced to email a client — it just navigated me to another surface. Now I don't trust any button label until I've test-clicked it, and there's no sandbox workspace to test-click in.

Selected CMS rows, hit 'Send to client', nothing happened — no toast, no banner, no error. The failure message only renders inside a drawer I didn't have open. I genuinely could not answer 'did the client just get an email from us?'
⌘K palette groups surfaces under 'Monitoring' and 'Site Health' while the sidebar I'd just memorized says 'Search & Site Health' and 'Strategy & Content' — same destinations, two maps, week one.
The onboarding modal told me to run my first SEO audit on a workspace whose audit from Mar 12 was visible one click away — the feature built FOR new users was the first thing to lie to me.
Bulk PageSpeed returned a success toast and a red 0 average score; I nearly escalated a performance emergency that was actually a rate limit the legacy UI used to explain in plain words.
Competitors' own 'Edit set' button stranded me on a settings page with no competitor editor in any of its seven tabs — I assumed I lacked permissions and asked for an access grant I didn't need.

On /settings the breadcrumb said 'Command Center → Expero 2023 → Settings' for account-global config — I spent real minutes deciding whether I was about to change one client's setup or the whole agency's.

Keyword table showed rows at Rank #1 with 0 clicks and 'No page assigned', with opportunity scores repeating 33%/39% — on a real client I'd have no way to know if that's fixture residue or a data pipeline problem.
extensionsIWant: One name per surface, everywhere: sidebar label = breadcrumb = ⌘K palette group = document.title. Kill the palette's legacy taxonomy in the same pass.

Truth-in-labels for client-facing actions: every Send/Accept states its exact scope in the label or a confirm ('Send 1 page to client', 'Accept 785 suggestions — preview first'), navigation-only buttons never say 'Send', and a consistent visual marker (icon/color) means 'this action notifies the client' on every surface.

Surface every failure where I'm standing: CMS send errors as a toast/banner at the toolbar, Schema add-page errors visibly, restore the legacy empty-bulk PageSpeed guard with its plain-English rate-limit guidance instead of a fabricated 0.

Fix the dead-end CTAs the guides point at: give 'Edit set' a real competitor editor destination, give the Schema site plan a pre-scan home, make 'Generate personas from site' actually reach the generate button, and link the audit into Page Rewriter.
Make the onboarding checklist read real state (it should know a Mar 12 audit exists), and give new hires a sandbox/demo workspace where clicking Send can't touch a real client.
Give Requests / Action Results / Diagnostics / Roadmap labeled homes (or at least visible labels on the footer strip) — icon-only buttons are invisible to someone without legacy muscle memory.
Restore per-page browsing in Site Audit and single-field AI rewrite in the SEO Editor — my two actual assigned tasks (work audit fixes, fix metas) both lost their finest-grained tool.
Pick ONE unknown-value convention ('— Unavailable') and apply it everywhere $0/zero currently stands in for 'no data'.
noiseToCut: Global Settings' wall of ~30 monospace SC-DOMAIN chips — pure intimidation for a new hire, zero hierarchy; collapse to a count with a 'view all' expander.
The 'Record published work' form occupying the entire top half of Action Results — I will never enter data there in month one; it buries the results I was sent to read.
The permanently dead 'Queued' board column on Content Pipeline — I sat waiting to learn what goes in it; nothing ever can.
Asset Manager's two permanent non-dismissible narrative banners and the triple-stacked quota notices — the third repetition teaches nothing.
The Engine rendering the same POV narrative four times on one page — I read all four looking for the difference before realizing there wasn't one.

The double-collapsed 'Production tools' GroupBlock in SEO Editor — collapsing is fine for advanced extras, but it's hiding the pending-approvals (remind/retract) panel I actually need when a send is in flight.

Dead affordances that read as 'features I haven't learned yet': the do-nothing Links 'Add page' button and the ?detail= deep-link machinery with no receiver — to a newbie, every dead control is homework that never resolves.
resolvedFromPriorAsks: Learnability (partially delivered): the Cockpit verdict + grouped work queue orients my day in one viewport; nav tooltips/aria descriptions are rich and accurate; Content Pipeline and Schema ship actual Guides; Brand & AI's 'How this context is used' rail is the best explanation of AI grounding in the product.

Honest states teach me what's real: 'Measurement pending', 'proof pending', truthful unconfigured-provider and empty states mean I'm not chasing ghosts — a genuine improvement over legacy's red-0 'No audit yet' style.

URL-deep-linkable everything (?lens/?filter/?q/?page across Keywords, Links, SEO Editor, Assets, Search & Traffic): a senior can send me a link that lands on the exact row and filter they mean — the single biggest onboarding-handoff win in the rebuild.

'Fix metas' got a real one-home worksheet: the SEO Editor's unified Static/CMS/Manual table with missing-title/meta quick filters, inline editing, and live counters matches my assigned task better than legacy's stacked editors — though its count-vs-fix-scope mismatch still needs fixing.

Labels that say what things do (partially): sidebar item descriptions and plain-language status vocab (On your desk / With client / Published) are exactly what I asked for — it's the ACTION labels (Send/Accept/scope) where the ask remains unmet.

personas.verdictLine: Half of this rebuild was made for me — URL-persisted state on every surface, an inline meta worksheet instead of expand-a-row-500-times, bulk PageSpeed at Top 25, a table-first Keyword Hub — and then the other half quietly took away the things my hands actually do all day: the audit drawer reads as group-scoped but the code audit found it acts on ONE page of fourteen, my per-row keyword actions are gone behind a drawer round-trip, my link-repair loop lost every open-in-new-tab anchor, and my bulk meta tools are double-collapsed under "Production tools" with a CMS send that can fail without telling me. I will be faster on the good days and silently wrong on the bulk days, and silently wrong is worse than slow — fix the scope lies and the hidden bulk affordances before you take legacy away from me.

topFindings.issue: Site Audit issue drawer presents Accept suggestion / Send to client / Add task against a group badged '14 pages' but (per the code audit, blocker-rated) the actions are wired to only the FIRST affected page — my 785-fix bulk workflow silently applies ~1/N of the work while reporting success; the legacy per-page action rows and the per-page inventory (scores, traffic badges, expandable per-page issues) have no rebuilt home at all.
topFindings.whereSeen: Site Audit, issue detail drawer; walk notes show the '785 AI-fixable' bulk band and the code audit confirms first-page-only wiring (IssueDetailDrawer)

topFindings.issue: SEO Editor bulk-trust cluster: the missing-metadata banner counts CMS rows but 'Fix titles/descriptions' only drafts static pages (count and scope disagree); CMS 'Send to client' from the selected-rows toolbar fails silently (error renders only inside the drawer); and the legacy full pattern-preview diff (every page, old struck-through → new) is reduced to an InlineBanner — I'm asked to bulk-apply without seeing the diff.
topFindings.whereSeen: SEO Editor worksheet — missing-metadata band ('0 missing titles · 2 missing descriptions'), selected-rows toolbar, pattern apply flow

topFindings.issue: My bulk tools are hidden behind a collapsed-by-default 'Production tools' GroupBlock — the sent-to-client pending-approvals panel (remind/retract), the AI suggestions panel (itself a second collapsed block — double-collapse), and the static bulk/pattern tooling. Legacy had these one glance away; that's raw added clicks on my highest-frequency surface.
topFindings.whereSeen: SEO Editor, 'Production tools' GroupBlock (code audit CONFUSION finding)

topFindings.issue: Links surface dropped every open-in-new-tab affordance — legacy rendered clickable external anchors on the dead link itself and on redirect targets. Middle-click-to-verify IS the link-repair loop; without it every row is copy-paste-into-a-new-tab. Also the architecture orphan list truncates to 3 chips with a pointer to a tree that doesn't render orphans.
topFindings.whereSeen: Links workshop, Dead Links / Redirects / Architecture lenses (code audit, both LOST-major-undocumented)

topFindings.issue: Keyword Hub removed per-row quick actions (drawer round-trip per keyword now — on a 50-keyword triage that's 50 extra open/close cycles), and parked a workspace-wide 'Refresh national ranks' button INSIDE a single keyword's drawer next to per-keyword Pin/Refresh — a scope landmine begging me to fire a whole-workspace paid refresh thinking it's one keyword. Bonus confusion: four of five lens tabs show the identical count (512/512/512/512), which reads as a counting bug.
topFindings.whereSeen: Keyword Hub, keyword detail drawer 'Controls' section + lens tray

topFindings.issue: A rate-limited/failed bulk PageSpeed run fires a SUCCESS toast and renders a fabricated red 0 'average score' into the strategy snapshot — legacy explicitly caught the empty-bulk case with actionable rate-limit guidance. A power user burns a bulk run, gets told it worked, and stores garbage.
topFindings.whereSeen: Performance, Bulk PageSpeed scan (code audit REGRESSION-major)

topFindings.issue: Cross-surface hops I rely on are unreliable or misleading: the decay→'Draft brief' fixContext handoff silently drops on a cold chunk (works on warm — intermittent, the worst kind); Content Health decay/cannibalization rows all click through to the Intake lane, which has nothing to do with the clicked page; Competitors 'Edit set' and both setup CTAs dead-end on a Workspace Settings page with no competitor editor.
topFindings.whereSeen: Content Pipeline (Content Health rows, fixContext), Competitors 'Edit set'

topFindings.issue: Search & Traffic's Detail table renders up to 500 GSC rows uncapped directly in page flow — a literal scroll trap that buries the lower Search band; legacy kept the same data in a bounded scroll box. Cap, paginate, or virtualize it.
topFindings.whereSeen: Search & Traffic, Detail table below the trend chart (code audit REGRESSION-major)

topFindings.issue: Navigation muscle-memory tax: ⌘K palette group labels are legacy vocabulary ('Monitoring', 'Site Health') while the sidebar uses new zones ('Search & Site Health'), the same surface answers to 'Insights Engine' (sidebar) and 'Strategy' (breadcrumb/palette), the palette's first programmatic open was a no-op (toggle race), and Requests / Action Results / Diagnostics / Roadmap lost their labeled rail homes — icon-only footer strip or palette-only. For a keyboard-first operator the palette mostly saves this, but two taxonomies for one nav is friction I pay every hop.
topFindings.whereSeen: Rebuilt shell — ⌘K palette vs sidebar zones vs breadcrumb; sidebar footer icon strip (walk notes + shell-nav audit)

topFindings.issue: 'Accept all 785' is a one-click irreversible-looking bulk action with no preview or undo affordance on the button — given the drawer's scope problem on the same surface, I don't currently trust what 'all 785' will actually touch. Give me a dry-run diff and an undo window and it becomes my favorite button in the product.
topFindings.whereSeen: Site Audit bulk band (walk notes)

distrustMoments: The audit drawer showing a '14 pages' badge and offering 'Accept suggestion' with no page qualifier — and learning it hits one page. Once I know one bulk control lies about scope, I second-guess every bulk control on the platform, including 'Accept all 785'.

SEO Editor telling me '2 missing descriptions' and 'Fix descriptions' quietly skipping the CMS rows in that count — a count and its action disagreeing means I go re-verify in the table, which defeats the banner.

CMS 'Send to client' from the toolbar failing with no visible error unless I happen to have the drawer open — a silent failure on a client-facing send is the single fastest way to make me stop using the fast path and go back to doing it the slow, verifiable way.
Bulk PageSpeed toasting 'complete' and painting a red 0 average after a rate-limited run — a success signal on a failure teaches me to distrust the success signal.
Lens tray reading 'Rankings 512 · Opportunities 512 · Pages 512 · Lifecycle 512' — looks exactly like a counting bug even if it isn't; I paused and recounted.
A workspace-wide 'Refresh national ranks' sitting inside ONE keyword's drawer beside per-keyword buttons — I nearly can't tell what I'm about to spend money refreshing.
'$0 Monthly value' on Keyword Hub while Cockpit says '— Unavailable' for the same missing provider — two conventions for 'unknown' means I have to know which surface fabricates zeros.
extensionsIWant: Ship the SEO Editor keyboard review queue — it's already recorded as an 'explicit workflow exception' in the parity contract; for a keyboard-first operator on a 500-meta job it's the difference between a worksheet and a spreadsheet-with-a-mouse.
Make audit drawer actions honestly scoped: per-page action rows back (or an explicit 'Apply to all N pages' with a page list), plus a dry-run diff + undo window on 'Accept all 785'.

Restore per-row quick actions on the Keyword Hub table (or selection-level bulk actions beyond 'Select visible 50') so triage doesn't require a drawer round-trip per keyword; move the workspace-wide rank refresh out of the keyword drawer to surface level.
Open-in-new-tab anchors anywhere a URL renders — Links surface first (dead link, redirect target), then audit affected pages and keyword page assignments.
Restore the full pattern/bulk-preview diff (every affected page, old → new) before any bulk meta apply, and surface CMS send errors at the toolbar where the action was taken.

Unify the ⌘K palette taxonomy with the rebuilt sidebar zones and add ACTIONS to the palette (Run audit, Re-sync targets, Re-run scan, jump-to-lens) — the palette is my primary nav; make it my primary command surface too.
Cap/virtualize the Search & Traffic Detail table and give it its own scroll container.
Row-level keyboard nav (j/k + enter-to-open-drawer, esc-to-close with focus restore) on the big tables — Keywords, SEO Editor, Site Audit; the roving-tabindex machinery already exists in the DS.
Give Requests and Action Results labeled rail homes (or at minimum palette-parity labels) — icon-only footer buttons are a hunt the first ten times.
noiseToCut: The permanently dead 'Queued' board column on Content Pipeline — it can never contain anything and it forces a 920px min-width horizontal scroll. A dead column that costs me a scroll axis is the definition of noise.
The two permanent, non-dismissible narrative banners on Asset Manager ('Fixes the source, not the symptom.' / 'From media fix to proof') — read-once copy framing every daily visit.
The 'Record published work' form occupying the entire top half of Action Results — data entry above the results I came to read; collapse it to a button.
Cockpit right-rail explainer prose ('Technical fixes stay in the Cockpit — they move into the Insights Engine only when…') — onboarding copy squatting on permanent rail real estate.
The ~30-chip monospace GSC-domain wall in Global Settings — collapse to a searchable list.
Stacked/overlapping quota notices on Asset Manager (surface banner + bulk-result banner + lens banner for one quota event) — one truthful banner, please.

Dead machinery that signals drift and confuses anyone reading the URL: the Links ?detail= param with no consumer, the SEO Editor's unused tab state exports, Cockpit's parsed-but-never-consumed ?view= — either wire them or delete them.
The same POV narrative rendering four times on one Insights Engine page — once as the headline, once compact, once in the editor, once in the preview; two of those are enough.
resolvedFromPriorAsks: (New seat this panel — no prior asks on record. Judged against legacy friction instead:)
Legacy buried the keyword table below Local Presence and AI-visibility bands — rebuilt Keyword Hub is table-first with dense 10-col rows and filter/sort chips. Resolved.

Legacy had zero URL state on most surfaces — rebuilt persists ?lens/?filter/?sort/?search/?page nearly everywhere with validated fallbacks; my working state now survives reload and is shareable. Resolved, and it's the single best power-user change in the rebuild.
Legacy bulk PageSpeed was hard-coded to 3 pages and re-fired paid runs on strategy toggle — rebuilt offers Top 3/5/10/25 and loads saved snapshots instead. Resolved.
Legacy meta editing required expanding rows one at a time — rebuilt inline spreadsheet editing with live counters and a sticky selected-actions toolbar. Resolved.
Legacy fixed 200px sidebar — rebuilt collapsible icon rail with persisted preference gives my tables the width back. Resolved.

personas.verdictLine: The rebuild clearly heard my prior ask — provenance chips, dated artifacts, honest attribution, truthful "Awaiting data" badges are all real and I credit them — but an instrument earns trust by reconciling, and this one fails the cross-check in at least three places where a number is not merely inconsistent but false: a rate-limited PageSpeed run that toasts success and renders a fabricated red 0 average, an Outcomes Book that stamps "Rolling 90 days" over all-time, 28-day, and 30-day data, and a Keyword Hub that converts an unconfigured provider into a hard "$0 Monthly value" one click away from a Cockpit saying the same thing is "— Unavailable." One bad reconciliation and I stop trusting the whole instrument; I found several, so until the fabrication paths and the false window label are fixed I would not let a client-facing decision ride on these numbers — the fix is narrow and mostly server-side, but it is a stop-ship from my chair.

topFindings.issue: Fabricated data reported as success: a failed/rate-limited bulk PageSpeed run fires a 'bulk test complete' success toast and renders a red 0 'average score' from an empty result — the exact fabrication the parity contract claims cannot happen. Legacy had an explicit empty-result guard with rate-limit guidance; it was silently dropped.
topFindings.whereSeen: The code audit found it on the Performance surface bulk PageSpeed path (mutation onSuccess writes the empty SiteSpeedResult into the strategy snapshot cache).

topFindings.issue: The Outcomes Book — the money table — labels itself 'Rolling 90 days' (twice) while none of its data is a 90-day window: value/month and Wins are all-time last-100-wins, Clicks is 28-day GSC, win rate is 30-day. Three unlabeled windows under one untrue banner, plus the attention-triage status column legacy had is buried behind per-row expansion.
topFindings.whereSeen: Code audit, global-ops-work (OutcomesBookLens).

topFindings.issue: Unknown rendered as zero: Keyword Hub KPI band shows '$0 Monthly value' when DataForSEO is unconfigured. Verified in source: trafficValueFromPages (server/domains/keyword-command-center/read-projection.ts:28-35) returns null only when zero pages are mapped — with 772 pages and absent CPC it sums to a hard 0, so the UI's '—' branch (KeywordsSurface.tsx:347) can never fire. Meanwhile Cockpit renders the same unknown as '— Unavailable'. Two conventions for one unknown, one of them a lie.
topFindings.whereSeen: Browser walk (Keyword Hub KPI band vs Cockpit decision band); verified in server source.

topFindings.issue: Site Audit shows two irreconcilable warning totals side by side: chip row '2215 warnings' vs narrative '4187 more warnings and notices'. Verified: the copy's number is warnings + infos (SiteAuditSurface.tsx:346), but no notices/info chip exists anywhere on the surface, so the 1,972 gap cannot be reconciled from what's visible. Same surface also puts an 'On-page 0' red category score under a 'solid shape' verdict headline.
topFindings.whereSeen: Browser walk, Site Audit surface; computation verified in source.

topFindings.issue: Audit recency contradiction across the flag: legacy home tile says 'No audit yet' (red 0 badge) and the rebuilt Cockpit onboarding modal lists 'Run your first SEO audit' as pending, while the rebuilt Site Audit and work queue show the Mar 12 audit scoring 78 — same database, two answers. Adjacent, 'Overall health 74' and 'Score 78' appear near each other with nothing telling the operator they are different metrics.
topFindings.whereSeen: Browser walk: legacy home vs rebuilt Cockpit/Site Audit, same workspace, same day.

topFindings.issue: Anomaly counts do not survive the flag flip: legacy home shows 21 anomaly alerts (4 red / 16 amber) with narratives; the rebuilt Cockpit compresses this to '1 risk signal' and a Risk tab counting 0, and no labeled flag-ON home for the 21 exists. Counts that shrink 21→1 with no reconciliation read as data loss, whatever the classifier intended.
topFindings.whereSeen: Browser walk: legacy Anomaly Alerts strip vs rebuilt Cockpit work queue.

topFindings.issue: Roadmap Completion % denominator silently changed to done/(done+in_progress+pending), excluding deferred and closed, while the adjacent Total tile still shows the full count — a direct violation of the repo's own 'numerator and denominator must share a source' rule. An operator will back out wrong raw counts from the displayed percentage.
topFindings.whereSeen: Code audit, global-ops-business (RoadmapLens).

topFindings.issue: Data-source mislabel in Local Presence: the 'Reviews' mini card is subtitled 'Authenticated profile aggregate' but is fed by the PUBLIC scraped listings aggregate; two different review counts (scraped 128 vs synced 43) appear across modes with no reconciliation — legacy's explanatory sub-labels ('GBP aggregate' vs 'synced review subset') were dropped.
topFindings.whereSeen: Code audit, local-presence (useGbpReviews → /gbp-reviews wiring).

topFindings.issue: A cluster of count/scope mismatches where the displayed number and the thing it counts disagree: SEO Editor's missing-metadata banner counts CMS rows but its Fix actions only draft static pages; Content Pipeline's Published chip counts published MATRIX CELLS while the lens lists published posts; Keywords lens tray shows the identical total on 4 of 5 lenses (reads as a counting bug); Links lens badges mix semantics (total scanned pages vs broken-link count vs suggestion count) in one bar.
topFindings.whereSeen: Code audit (seo-editor, content-pipeline, keywords, links) + browser walk lens trays.

topFindings.issue: Global tabs silently adopt the FIRST workspace as context (/settings, /roadmap, /revenue show 'Command Center → <first-workspace> → …'), so numbers on global surfaces render under a wrong client's name and AdminChat can answer for the wrong workspace — a misattribution hazard, not just a cosmetic breadcrumb bug.
topFindings.whereSeen: Code audit shell-nav + browser walk (Settings/Roadmap breadcrumb reading 'Expero 2023' on account-global surfaces).

topFindings.issue: Metrics that can never be true dressed as pending: the Asset Manager audit health score renders 'awaiting server score' for a field the server never returns (permanently dead), and the Brand & AI completeness meter mathematically caps Voice & Messaging at 33% so a fully configured workspace reads 'Needs setup' forever.
topFindings.whereSeen: Code audit, asset-manager and brand-ai.

topFindings.issue: 'Backing moves live 99' beside '0 staged · 0 with client' is a three-way vocabulary an operator must decode — though to its credit the 99 reconciles exactly (12 new bets + 43 quick wins + 44 technical fixes), one of the few cross-checks that passes cleanly.
topFindings.whereSeen: Browser walk, Insights Engine metric frame; sum verified against the grouped moves list.

distrustMoments: Keyword Hub told me the whole portfolio earns '$0 Monthly value' while the Cockpit, one click away, called the same unknown '— Unavailable' — and the source shows the $0 is manufactured server-side from absent CPC data.
Site Audit's chip row said 2215 warnings while the sentence beside it said '4187 more warnings and notices', with no visible chip that lets me close the 1,972 gap.
The Outcomes Book banner said 'Rolling 90 days' over columns that are actually all-time, 28-day, and 30-day windows — on the table where dollar value per client is decided.
A rate-limited PageSpeed bulk run congratulated me with a success toast and then displayed a fabricated red 0 average score.

Legacy home said 'No audit yet' (red 0) and the Cockpit onboarding modal told me to run my first audit, while the rebuilt Site Audit showed the Mar 12 audit at 78 — same workspace, same day, two answers.
21 anomaly alerts flag-OFF became '1 risk signal / Risk (0)' flag-ON with no visible home for the other 20.
On /settings the breadcrumb read 'Command Center → Expero 2023 → Settings' for account-global configuration — global numbers wearing one client's name.
A 'Reviews' card labeled 'Authenticated profile aggregate' is actually scraped public data, sitting two clicks from a differently-counted authenticated set (128 vs 43) with no reconciliation.
Rank #1 rows with 0 clicks and 'No page assigned', and opportunity scores uniformly 33%/39% — nobody at the table would believe those #1s without an explanation of where they came from.
extensionsIWant: One platform-wide unknown-vs-zero convention, enforced at the SERVER: absent provider evidence must serialize as null (never 0), and every money/count tile must render '—' plus a why-chip ('provider not configured'). Fix trafficValueFromPages to return null when CPC evidence is absent, not only when zero pages are mapped.

A window label on every aggregate: each KPI, table, and card states its actual period (28d / 30d / all-time / as-of date) next to the number; ban decorative period banners like 'Rolling 90 days' that don't drive the query.

A reconciliation line wherever two counts of the same noun coexist on one screen or one click apart: audit warnings vs warnings+notices, scraped vs synced reviews, wins vs scored-in-30-days, anomaly alerts flag-OFF vs risk signals flag-ON. If the platform shows both, it should show the bridge.

Extend the existing pr-check 'rate display: numerator and denominator share a source' rule into a cross-surface metric-parity gate: the same metric key (health score, warnings, monthly value) rendered in two places must resolve from one shared selector, so 74/78/'No audit yet' divergences fail CI instead of shipping.
Fix the audit-recency read so the Cockpit onboarding checklist, the legacy home tile, and Site Audit agree on whether an audit exists — one truth function, three consumers.

Give anomaly alerts a labeled flag-ON home whose count reconciles against the classifier's input (e.g. 'Risk 1 — 20 more signals below threshold, view all'), so compression reads as triage rather than loss.
noiseToCut: The Asset Manager audit health score tile — the server never returns the field; delete the metric rather than dressing a corpse in 'awaiting server score' copy.
The Brand & AI completeness percentage until its math can actually reach 100% — a readiness meter that structurally maxes at 33% is anti-signal.
Both 'Rolling 90 days' banner instances on the Outcomes Book — until real windowing exists, no label beats a false one.

Mixed-semantics lens count badges (Links: total pages / broken count / suggestion count in one bar; Keywords: the same total repeated on four lenses) — show counts only where the number means the same thing across the row.
Page Rewriter's hardcoded '0 of 1 responses completed before the quota was hit' line — batch telemetry grafted onto a single-request chat, always zero, always noise.
resolvedFromPriorAsks: Honest provenance vocabulary shipped: 'Measurement pending', 'proof pending', '— Unavailable', and the 'Value proof is still being prepared' band are exactly the no-fabrication posture I asked for — where they're used.
Artifacts are plainly dated ('Generated Mar 12, 1:37 PM · 772 pages mapped · Data as of Jul 16') — a 4-month-old strategy can no longer masquerade as fresh.

Attribution honesty made it into the read paths: recent wins exclude not_acted_on and label externally_executed as 'Client-side · measured result' — the platform no longer claims credit it didn't earn.
The Outcomes Book now lists EVERY workspace with a truthful 'Awaiting data' badge instead of hiding no-data clients — unknown finally distinguished from absent, at least here.
The strategy warning banner honestly discloses generation without keyword-volume validation when DataForSEO is missing.
Backing-move group counts reconcile exactly (12+43+44=99) — the kind of cross-check I run, passing.

personas.seat: Seat 6 — The operator six months from now (maintenance voice)
personas.verdictLine: The rebuild's process discipline is the best I've inherited on this platform — 17 parity contracts, a deferred ledger, URL-persisted state everywhere, contract tests, an a11y ratchet — but the thing I was promised ("retire the flag someday") is structurally impossible as built: at least six rebuilt surfaces mount legacy components wholesale inside brittle nth-child CSS frames, so CLAUDE.md's own retirement rule (delete the OFF branch) would delete load-bearing parts of the ON branch; meanwhile the parity contracts I'd use to certify "safe to delete legacy" contain provably false claims, all 89 open deferred-ledger entries have null roadmap linkage with 49 of them hitting the same review date next month, and the code audit found duplicated implementations that have ALREADY drifted ten days after merge. This isn't one flag waiting on a pilot; it's two UIs, a third hybrid layer of legacy-inside-rebuilt, and an exception book with no exit ramps. Ship it — the surfaces are genuinely better — but if nobody writes the Phase Z retirement plan now, 2027-me maintains three UIs forever, and the platform's own CLAUDE.md predicted exactly this ("flag retirement is part of the build, not someday").

topFindings.issue: Flag retirement is structurally blocked: at least 6 rebuilt surfaces mount legacy components wholesale (OutcomeDashboard in OutcomeWorkspaceLens, WorkspaceSettings in WorkspaceSettingsLens, the entire legacy BrandHub context tab inside the Brand & AI mega-modal, ContentCalendar in ContentPipelineLenses, legacy SiteArchitecture in PageIntelligenceSurface, four Business panels in BusinessPanelFrame) styled via positional nth-child/:has() !important frames. Deleting the OFF branch — the documented retirement rule — deletes load-bearing pieces of the ON branch. No second-wave native-rebuild plan exists for these six.
topFindings.whereSeen: src/components/global-ops-rebuilt/{OutcomeWorkspaceLens,WorkspaceSettingsLens}.tsx, wave-b/business/BusinessPanelFrame.tsx, content-pipeline-rebuilt/ContentPipelineLenses.tsx, page-intelligence-rebuilt/PageIntelligenceSurface.tsx; code audit brand-ai/global-ops sections

topFindings.issue: The parity contracts — the only instrument a future maintainer can use to certify 'safe to delete legacy' — contain false certifications: site-audit claims issue-detail capabilities 'unchanged' while drawer actions silently act on only the FIRST affected page (a blocker); competitors certifies workspace-settings as the 'edit home' but no competitor editor exists in any of its seven tabs; performance claims the fabricated-zero 'cannot happen' and it happens. ~40 major LOST items across surfaces are UNDOCUMENTED in contracts. A contract that lies today is worthless as a retirement gate in 2027.
topFindings.whereSeen: severe-findings digest: site-audit, competitors, performance headlines vs docs/ui-rebuild/parity/coverage-audit.md 'owner-approved' rows

topFindings.issue: Exception debt has no exit ramp: the deferred ledger holds 89 open entries and 100% have roadmapItemId: null, with 49 sharing one review-by date (2026-08-18); the coverage audit carries ~30 owner-approved 'explicit exceptions' with no dated exit. verify:deferred-ledger passes green while linkage is universally null — the gate enforces schema and expiry, not that deferrals become work.
topFindings.whereSeen: data/ui-rebuild-deferred-ledger.json (89/89 open entries null roadmapItemId); coverage-audit.md exception language

topFindings.issue: Duplicated implementations are already drifting inside the rebuilt codebase itself: two annotation mutation stacks 'have already drifted' (Search & Traffic), two parallel five-step schema steppers, Architecture living in two homes with different capabilities (one of them the legacy component), and the rebuilt Content Pipeline re-implemented legacy's known fixContext race defect verbatim. Every bug now has two or three addresses while the flag lives.
topFindings.whereSeen: digest: search-traffic (annotation stacks), schema (SchemaWorkflowStrip vs WorkflowStepper), links vs page-intelligence (Architecture), content-pipeline (fixContext 'same defect')

topFindings.issue: Dead scaffolding shipped as parity ceremony across nearly every surface: a permanently-empty Queued board column forcing 920px horizontal scroll, a whole hidden fourth 'overview' lens with zero senders, dead ?detail= machinery (Links), dead tab machinery (SEO Editor hook), unused ?view= state (Cockpit), LocalPresenceSetup.tsx imported nowhere, SchemaQuickStats never used, a do-nothing 'Add page' button, and Cockpit 'Send'/'Propose' buttons that only navigate — blessed as 'explicit exceptions'. Each is a future archaeology session for whoever asks 'is this used?'
topFindings.whereSeen: digest OVERCOMPLICATED rows: content-pipeline, search-traffic, links, seo-editor, cockpit, local-presence, schema; coverage-audit Cockpit row ('unsupported prototype Send/Promote actions retained as explicit exceptions')

topFindings.issue: Surfaces are coupled to DS primitive INTERNALS via descendant-selector CSS surgery (KEYWORDS_HEADER_CLASS !important rewrites of PageHeader internals, EngineSurface SectionCard chains, deep child selectors on WorkStreamSelector, '[&>div>div:nth-child(2)]:hidden' on the calendar). Any DOM reorder inside a shared primitive silently breaks N surfaces and no gate — pr-check, typecheck, or test — catches it.
topFindings.whereSeen: digest: keywords, engine, cockpit, content-pipeline OVERCOMPLICATED rows; grep hits in keywords-rebuilt/KeywordsSurface.tsx, cockpit-rebuilt/CockpitSurface.tsx

topFindings.issue: Even flag-ON, the shell is not a clean two-state switch: '/' (Command Center) and /ws/:id/subscriptions never mount rebuilt chrome, so both shells run simultaneously in production, plus global tabs misattribute workspaces[0] as context. Retirement isn't 'flip and delete' — the dual-shell seam has its own unfinished scope.
topFindings.whereSeen: digest shell-nav: dual-shell seam (App.tsx:489 canMountRebuiltSurface false at '/'); browser walk cross-cutting notes

topFindings.issue: Three parallel nav label sources (NAV_REGISTRY, registry flagBehavior overrides, RebuiltSidebar-local spec.label) plus a hand-maintained 70-line SUB_TAB_LABELS_BY_PAGE breadcrumb duplicate produce the user-visible three-way vocab drift (sidebar 'Insights Engine' / breadcrumb 'Strategy' / palette legacy groups) — a maintenance triplication that will re-drift after every rename until collapsed to one source.
topFindings.whereSeen: digest shell-nav; browser walk: breadcrumb 'Strategy' vs sidebar 'Insights Engine', palette groups 'Monitoring'/'Site Health'

topFindings.issue: The flag's own lifecycle hygiene is already slipping: ui-rebuild-shell declares staleAuditCadence 'weekly' but lastReviewedAt is 2026-07-05 — 11 days stale at the time of this review. Small, but it's the canary for how the exception book will age.
topFindings.whereSeen: shared/types/feature-flags.ts:511-520

distrustMoments: Opening data/ui-rebuild-deferred-ledger.json and finding roadmapItemId: null on every single one of the 89 open entries — while the quality-gate checklist tells me verify:deferred-ledger checks 'roadmap links'. The gate is green and the linkage doesn't exist.

Reading the coverage audit's Cockpit row: 'unsupported prototype Send/Promote actions retained as explicit exceptions' — then the browser walk showing operators clicking a 'Send' button that sends nothing. A dead affordance was formally blessed instead of deleted.
The digest line 'two parallel annotation mutation stacks now exist and have already drifted' — drift within the rebuilt codebase itself, days after merge, on a surface certified owner-approved.

Site Audit's parity contract claiming issue-detail capabilities are 'unchanged' next to a blocker finding that accept/task/send/fix act on only the first of 14 affected pages. If I'd used that contract to retire legacy Site Audit, I'd have deleted the only working per-page repair path.

The rebuilt Content Pipeline reproducing legacy's known fixContext mount-time-sampling race — 'the same defect' — meaning defects are being copied across the flag boundary, so every fix now needs two commits for as long as both UIs live.

49 deferred-ledger entries all sharing review-by 2026-08-18: a review wall one month out that nobody will actually clear in one day, guaranteeing a blanket extension — the precise mechanism by which 'temporary' becomes permanent.
extensionsIWant: A written Phase Z flag-retirement plan, now, per CLAUDE.md's own rule: a per-surface table of which rebuilt surfaces still mount legacy components (the six wrapped ones), each with a roadmap item and a date, with native-rebuild of those six explicitly listed as blocking ui-rebuild-shell retirement.
Tighten verify:deferred-ledger: fail when an open entry older than N days has roadmapItemId: null, and redistribute the 49-entry 2026-08-18 review wall into staggered, owner-assigned dates.

A contract-veracity reconciliation pass: fold the ~40 UNDOCUMENTED LOST findings from the code audit into the parity contracts — each becomes either a signed exception or a fix ticket — so contracts can be trusted as the retirement gate they're supposed to be.

A mechanized pr-check rule banning structural descendant-selector overrides of DS primitives from surface code ('[&>div', ':nth-child(', ':has(' targeting primitive internals) — the same playbook as the other seven @ds-rebuilt gates; the current pattern is un-greppable-by-humans coupling.

A knip/ts-prune dead-export sweep wired into CI scoped to *-rebuilt directories, plus deletion of the already-enumerated dead files and machinery (LocalPresenceSetup.tsx, SchemaQuickStats, dead ?detail=/tab/?view= plumbing, the hidden overview lens or a visible affordance for it — pick one).

Collapse the three nav label sources into NAV_REGISTRY alone and derive SUB_TAB_LABELS_BY_PAGE from each surface's own tab definitions — one rename should change one file, and the visible three-way vocab drift disappears as a side effect.
noiseToCut: The permanently-empty 'Queued' board column kept for prototype-shape parity — it costs a quarter of a 920px-min board and a horizontal scrollbar to render nothing, forever.

The hidden fourth 'overview' lens in Search & Traffic: a whole maintained component + lens-conditional data matrices + URL canonicalization special cases with zero senders. Either give it a button or delete it; maintaining an unreachable feature is pure tax.
Cockpit queue 'Send'/'Propose' buttons that only navigate — dead prototype affordances kept as 'explicit exceptions'; label them as navigation ('Open in Engine') or remove them.
SUB_TAB_LABELS_BY_PAGE — a 70-line hand-maintained duplicate of every surface's tab vocabulary with contract coverage for exactly one page.
The second (hand-rolled) annotation mutation stack in AnnotationsLens — it has already drifted from the shared one; keep one.

Asset Manager's two permanent non-dismissible narrative banners ('Fixes the source, not the symptom', 'From media fix to proof') — locked marketing copy inside an operator tool that someone must now maintain forever.

Parity ceremony in Site Audit's DataTable plumbing: double-casting rows through 'as unknown as Record<string, unknown>[]' and passing the whole audit object plus six plucked fields into the same drawer — pick one interface.
resolvedFromPriorAsks: (New seat — no prior asks on record, but the rebuild pre-answered asks I would have made:) URL-persisted, validated deep-link state on essentially every rebuilt surface — shareable repro links are a maintainer's best debugging tool, and legacy had none.

The governance infrastructure EXISTS at all: 17 per-surface parity contracts, a schema-validated deferred ledger with expiry, contract tests for tab wiring and sidebar zones, an a11y floor with a ratchet. Most rebuilds ship with none of this; my complaints are about its follow-through, not its absence.
Honest error/stale/empty states replacing legacy's silent failures (ErrorState + retry, stale-data banners, truthful 'Awaiting data' badges) — fewer 3am 'why is it blank' tickets.
Live WS invalidation wired on rebuilt surfaces (e.g. Schema's four event registrations) instead of legacy's stale-until-reload reads.

personas.verdictLine: Per client, this is the best cockpit I've ever had — verdict-first, work classified into streams, honest about stale data — but my job is forty of these, and the rebuild stopped exactly one level below where I live: the cross-workspace Command Center at '/' is still the LEGACY shell (its 'Needs attention' strip is the only book-level triage in the product and it didn't get rebuilt), the Outcomes Book buried the attention-sort I'd run my morning on behind per-row expansion while stamping an untrue 'Rolling 90 days' on mixed windows, and global tabs silently impersonate my first workspace — which at forty clients isn't a papercut, it's how you read or send the wrong client's data. The raw material for a book-level cockpit already exists server-side per workspace; aggregate it and I'm sold. Until then: trust-but-not-compelled per client, and structurally underserved across clients.

topFindings.issue: Global tabs silently adopt the FIRST workspace as context: on /settings, /roadmap, /revenue the sidebar WorkspaceSelector shows workspaces[0] as active, the breadcrumb reads 'Command Center → <first-workspace> → Settings', and AdminChat binds to that wrong workspace. Legacy had an honest no-workspace-selected state. At 40 accounts, misattributed context is a wrong-client action waiting to happen.
topFindings.whereSeen: Browser walk (Settings + Roadmap breadcrumbs showing 'Expero 2023' on account-global surfaces); the code audit confirmed it's workspaces[0] substitution, a documented loss of the legacy disabled-state

topFindings.issue: The cross-workspace home is not rebuilt: '/' (Command Center / WorkspaceOverview) never mounts the rebuilt chrome, so flag-ON I bounce between two shells every time I switch clients — and the ONLY book-level 'which clients need me today' view (the legacy NeedsAttention strip, priority-sorted, capped at 8) lives in the shell that's being retired. There is no rebuilt all-clients view of risk or work.
topFindings.whereSeen: Code audit shell-nav (App.tsx canMountRebuiltSurface false at '/'); confirmed in source: WorkspaceOverview.tsx renders NeedsAttention cap=8; coverage-audit.md's 27-surface census simply doesn't include a Command Center

topFindings.issue: Outcomes Book — my one genuine book-level rollup — demoted attention triage: legacy sorted attention-needed workspaces to the top with a warning icon + attentionReason + Status column; rebuilt sorts by value/month and hides triage behind per-row expansion. Worse, the whole table is labeled 'Rolling 90 days' twice while the data is all-time value + 28-day GSC clicks + 30-day win rates — three unlabeled windows under one false banner.
topFindings.whereSeen: Code audit global-ops-work (LOST major undocumented + REGRESSION major)

topFindings.issue: Requests, Action Results, Diagnostics, and Roadmap have no labeled sidebar home flag-ON — they live in an icon-only footer strip or behind Cockpit in-surface links. Legacy listed Requests and Action Results as labeled rail items. 'Global tools hidden in a footer' is precisely the pattern that makes me stop trusting a nav.
topFindings.whereSeen: Browser walk a11y-tree verification of the rebuilt sidebar footer strip vs the legacy rail's labeled CONTENT/MONITORING items

topFindings.issue: Anomaly visibility collapsed on the operator's landing surface: legacy home showed a 21-alert strip (4 red / 16 amber) with per-alert cards and AI narrative; the rebuilt Cockpit on the same workspace, same day, reports '1 risk signal' and a Risk tab of 0. Whatever the new classification thresholds are, at scale I depend on the platform to surface which clients are on fire — silent under-reporting is worse than noise.
topFindings.whereSeen: Browser walk discrepancy: legacy Home anomaly strip (21) vs rebuilt Cockpit ('1 risk signal', work queue showing only the audit item)

topFindings.issue: Cross-shell number contradictions during the transition period I'll be living in: legacy Site Health tile says 'No audit yet' (red 0) while rebuilt Cockpit/Site Audit show the Mar 12 audit at 78; Keyword Hub shows '$0 Monthly value' where Cockpit says '— Unavailable' for the same unknown. When I compare clients side by side, the unknown-vs-zero convention has to be one convention.
topFindings.whereSeen: Browser walk: legacy Home vs rebuilt Cockpit/Site Audit on the same workspace; Keyword Hub KPI band vs Cockpit decision band

topFindings.issue: Cockpit queue-row buttons labeled 'Send' and 'Propose' only navigate to another surface — nothing is sent or proposed. A false affordance I'd hit dozens of times a day across clients teaches me to stop believing button labels.
topFindings.whereSeen: Code audit cockpit (CONFUSION major in their ranking; minor from my chair because I learn it once — but it erodes label trust)

topFindings.issue: OutcomesBookLens fetches the entire command-center aggregate (5 parallel endpoints including activity, anomalies, presence, AI time-saved — mostly unrendered) just to draw the book table. That's the surface I'd refresh most often, and it's the one that scales worst with workspace count.
topFindings.whereSeen: Code audit global-ops-work (OVERCOMPLICATED)

distrustMoments: Breadcrumb 'Command Center → Expero 2023 → Settings' on an account-global surface — if the shell can misattribute context on Settings, I have to assume it can misattribute it anywhere, including AdminChat
'Rolling 90 days' stamped twice on the Outcomes Book over all-time wins, 28-day clicks, and 30-day win rates — one dishonest period label and I re-verify every number on the page before a client call
Legacy home: 'No audit yet' / rebuilt: audit score 78 from Mar 12 — same workspace, same day, two shells disagreeing about whether work exists
21 anomaly alerts flag-OFF vs '1 risk signal' flag-ON with no explanation of where the other 20 went
'$0 Monthly value' (Keyword Hub) vs '— Unavailable' (Cockpit) for the same unconfigured provider — a hard zero where the platform elsewhere admits ignorance
'Backing moves live 99' next to '0 staged · 0 with client' — a three-way vocabulary (live/staged/with-client) I had to decode before trusting the count
'Send' and 'Propose' buttons that only navigate — labels that overpromise the action
extensionsIWant: Book-level Cockpit at '/': the server already classifies each workspace's work into opt/send/money/risk streams and builds a verdict (buildCockpitVerdict) — aggregate those into one cross-client triage table (client · verdict · risk count · to-send count · staleness) sorted by attention. This is THE surface for my seat and the parts already exist per-workspace.
Restore attention-first sorting + a visible attentionReason/status column in the Outcomes Book, and label each column with its actual time window instead of one banner.
Promote Requests and Action Results back to labeled nav items — a footer icon strip is not a home for surfaces where humans are waiting on replies.
A cross-workspace anomaly rollup (per-client anomaly counts on the Command Center) and a truthful account of what the Cockpit's risk classification includes vs the legacy 21-alert strip.
Cross-client batch operations, or at minimum a global jobs/queue view: run audits or strategy refreshes across selected workspaces without visiting 40 cockpits one at a time.
One platform-wide unknown-value convention ('— Unavailable' everywhere, never $0) so side-by-side client comparison is safe.
Workspace-neutral context on global tabs: an honest 'no client selected' state instead of workspaces[0] impersonation.
noiseToCut: Permanent non-dismissible narrative banners (Asset Manager's 'Fixes the source, not the symptom' + 'From media fix to proof', the Cockpit explainer prose) — I read the same paragraphs 40 times a day; make them dismissible or first-run-only.
The ~30-chip monospace SC-DOMAIN wall in Global Settings — collapse to a searchable count ('31 properties connected · manage').
The 'Record published work' form occupying the entire top half of Action Results — data entry above the readback the surface exists for; move it behind a button.
The dead 'Queued' board column in Content Pipeline that can never contain anything yet forces a 920px horizontal scroll.
The workspaces[0] substitution machinery on global tabs — replace with the honest empty state rather than maintaining code whose only output is a lie.

Duplicate/dead scaffolding the audit found riding along (unused ?view= state, dead deep-link params, LocalPresenceSetup.tsx imported nowhere) — at two-shells-behind-one-flag, every extra moving part is future drift.
resolvedFromPriorAsks: New seat this panel (no 2026-07-03 asks on record) — but crediting what the rebuild already delivers toward the implicit scale spec: the Outcomes Book is the product's FIRST every-workspace rollup with honest 'Awaiting data' badges, dollar value/month meters, and attribution honesty (not_acted_on excluded, client-side work labeled as theirs) — the right skeleton, wrong sort.

Per-client triage is now genuinely fast: the Cockpit verdict + stream-classified work queue means once I'm IN a workspace, orientation takes seconds — that's the unit cell a book-level view should be built from.

URL-persisted state on nearly every rebuilt surface (keywords, links, audit, editor, assets) means I can bookmark/share per-client deep links across my book — a real multi-account workflow win legacy never had.

complexityLanes.headline: The rebuild is genuinely decision-first at the hero level, but mid-level structure has accreted: ~30 visible top-level lens/mode options across 10 surfaces, 29 Drawer mounts, 11+ Segmented controls, and up to four stacked filter layers on one dataset (Keywords). The costliest ceremony is not widget count but (a) three parallel naming systems for the same destinations (sidebar overrides vs navRegistry vs palette groups), (b) two hierarchy inversions (outcomes leads with a data-entry form; global surfaces render under a workspace breadcrumb), and (c) six surfaces whose URL lens vocabulary exceeds their visible UI — including two dead vocabularies with no rendered switcher at all.

findings.finding: Keywords stacks four filter/sort layers over one dataset, and two of its five lenses are not distinct views. 'Rankings' vs 'Opportunities' differ only by column set; 'Pages'/'Clusters' are groupings of the same rows. The 'Opportunities' lens, the 'Striking distance' filter chip, and the 'Opportunity' sort chip are three ways to express the same intent. A code comment even acknowledges the sort-chip row is confusable with the filter-chip row.
findings.evidence: src/components/keywords-rebuilt/KeywordsTable.tsx:480 (`activeColumns = state.lens === 'opportunities' ? opportunityColumns : columns` — only difference between the two table lenses); KeywordsLenses.tsx:304-329 (pages/clusters call groupRowsByPage/groupRowsByCluster on the same rows); KeywordsSurface.tsx:374-420 (5-lens switcher + search + 'More filters' FormSelect + 7 FilterChips from useKeywordsSurfaceState.ts:22-40); KeywordsTable.tsx:77-83 (5 sort chips incl. 'Opportunity') and :485 (comment: leading 'Sort' label 'so this chip row is not mistaken for the status FilterChip row').

findings.finding: Six rebuilt surfaces carry URL lens vocabulary larger than their visible UI; two are fully dead. ENGINE_LENSES defines 6 lens ids (spine/changes/signals/pov/moves/operations) with NO rendered switcher — they exist only as ?lens= scroll anchors plus one internal setLens('moves') call. SEO Editor's SEO_EDITOR_SURFACE_TABS ('edit'/'research') is exported state (tab/setTab/tabOptions) that no component consumes — 'Research' survives only as drawer eyebrow copy. Also hidden-compat: search-traffic 'overview', site-audit's 3 compatibility subs, content-pipeline 8 URL tabs → 5 visible modes, local-presence 4 ids → 2 modes.
findings.evidence: src/components/engine-rebuilt/useEngineSurfaceState.ts:8-15 (6 lens ids) vs EngineSurface.tsx:496-499 (only use = scrollIntoView) and :452 (sole setLens call) — grep confirms no LensSwitcher renders ENGINE_LENSES; src/components/seo-editor-rebuilt/useSeoEditorSurfaceState.ts:9-12,171-179 (tabOptions built, never rendered; only trace is SeoEditorPagePanel.tsx:285 eyebrow 'Research · …'); useSearchTrafficSurfaceState.ts:18-21 ('overview' in LENS_VALUES but not in SEARCH_TRAFFIC_LENSES); site-audit-rebuilt/useSiteAuditSurfaceState.ts:5-16 (2 visible subs + 3 compat subs); content-pipeline-rebuilt/useContentPipelineSurfaceState.ts:5-14 vs ContentPipelineSurface.tsx:86-100 (modeForTab folds 8→5); local-presence-rebuilt/useLocalPresenceSurfaceState.ts:6-13 vs LocalPresenceSurface.tsx:205-209 (4→2 collapse).

findings.finding: Outcomes ('Action Results') inverts hierarchy and carries three names at once. The RecordPublishedWorkCard data-entry form renders ABOVE the TabBar that holds the surface's actual daily job (Top Wins/Scorecard/Playbooks/Actions/Learnings/Coverage), and the operator sees a double header stack with two different titles: the rebuilt lens h1 says 'Outcome Dashboard' while the wrapped legacy PageHeader says 'Outcomes'; the sidebar/nav label is 'Action Results'.
findings.evidence: src/components/admin/outcomes/OutcomeDashboard.tsx:40 (form) vs :42 (TabBar below it); src/components/global-ops-rebuilt/OutcomeWorkspaceLens.tsx:34 (h1 'Outcome Dashboard') wrapping OutcomeDashboard.tsx:36-39 (PageHeader 'Outcomes'); src/lib/navRegistry.tsx:120 (label 'Action Results'). Browser-walk notes confirm the form 'occupies the ENTIRE top half' on a real workspace.

findings.finding: Nav vocabulary drifts in three label pairs because the rebuilt sidebar hard-codes per-item label overrides that bypass the registry the breadcrumb and palette read. Sidebar says 'Insights Engine' / 'Keywords' / 'Asset Manager'; the registry (hence breadcrumb + command palette) says 'Strategy' / 'Keyword Hub' / 'Assets'. The user sees both names simultaneously (sidebar item highlighted 'Insights Engine' while the breadcrumb reads 'Strategy'), and in-surface cross-links use the third form (SEO Editor's button says 'Keyword Hub').
findings.evidence: src/components/layout/RebuiltSidebar.tsx:100-101,109,125 (GROUP_PRESENTATION label overrides) and :166 (`spec.label ?? resolveNavLabel(...)` — the drift vector) vs src/lib/navRegistry.tsx:134 ('Strategy', no flagBehavior), :136 ('Keyword Hub'), :130 ('Assets'); RebuiltBreadcrumb.tsx:111-115 resolves via registry; EngineSurface.tsx:536 PageHeader 'Insights Engine'; seo-editor-rebuilt/SeoEditorSurface.tsx:305-308 ('Keyword Hub' cross-link). Note 'home'→'Cockpit' is done correctly via flagBehavior (navRegistry.tsx:115), proving the right mechanism already exists.

findings.finding: Command palette and rebuilt sidebar present two different taxonomies for the same destinations: the palette groups by legacy NavGroupKey labels ('Monitoring', 'Site Health', 'Strategy') while the sidebar uses new zones ('STRATEGY & CONTENT', 'SEARCH & SITE HEALTH', 'CLIENT-FACING', 'ADMIN'). An operator who learns one map can't use it in the other entry point.
findings.evidence: src/components/CommandPalette.tsx:44-48 (PALETTE_GROUP_LABELS: 'Monitoring', 'Site Health', 'Strategy') vs src/components/layout/RebuiltSidebar.tsx:94-161 (GROUP_PRESENTATION zones). The sidebar file's own comment (:92-93) declares its grouping 'prototype presentation only'.

findings.finding: Global surfaces (Roadmap, Settings, Diagnostics, Revenue…) render under a workspace breadcrumb ('Command Center → Expero 2023 → Roadmap'). The breadcrumb pushes the workspace segment unconditionally, and App.tsx actively substitutes workspaces[0] as 'selected' for global tabs, so the misattribution is structural, not incidental — a trust landmine that implies account-level config belongs to one client.
findings.evidence: src/components/layout/RebuiltBreadcrumb.tsx:141-146 (`if (selected && workspaceLabel)` — no global-tab check) ; src/App.tsx:282 (`if (GLOBAL_TABS.has(tab)) return workspaces[0] ?? null`); src/routes.ts:36 (GLOBAL_TABS exists and is already consulted by adminPath and the sidebar's isNavItemDisabled at RebuiltSidebar.tsx:185, just not by the breadcrumb).

findings.finding: Insights Engine hides its primary workflow CTA in the default state: 'Send N staged' only renders when stagedCount > 0, so an operator arriving at the surface (or migrating from legacy, which always showed the green 'Send update' button) has no visible send affordance and no disabled-state guidance. Combined with the three-way stock vocabulary 'Backing moves live 99 / 0 staged / 0 with client', the send pipeline's mechanics are invisible until you already know them.
findings.evidence: src/components/engine-rebuilt/EngineSurface.tsx:381 (`canSendIssue = engine.stagedCount > 0`) and :407-419 (button conditionally mounted, not disabled); browser-walk notes: 'no visible Send on this surface… an operator switching from legacy may hunt for it' and '99 live vs 0 staged vs 0 with client is confusing at a glance'.

findings.finding: Site Audit concentrates the heaviest action ceremony in the rebuild: a 4-button bulk band with subtle distinctions ('Add error tasks' / 'Add visible tasks' / 'Add all tasks' / 'Accept all 785' — the last is one-click for 785 changes with no preview/undo affordance on the button), plus three parallel filtering mechanisms on one issue list (6 clickable category cards, a severity/traffic Segmented, a FilterChip row), and two disagreeing aggregations in view at once (chip row '2215 warnings' vs copy '4187 more warnings and notices'; 'On-page 0' red score under a 'solid shape' verdict).
findings.evidence: src/components/site-audit-rebuilt/SiteAuditSurface.tsx:913-925 (four bulk buttons), :862-869 (Segmented sort) + :853-857 (CategoryCards as filter toggles via activeCategories/onToggleCategory) + FilterChip usage in the same file; ~20 <Button> mounts in this one file (highest of any rebuilt surface). Contradictory aggregates and 'On-page 0' verdict clash observed in browser-walk notes (Site Audit section).

findings.finding: The 'Guide' concept ships with three different interaction patterns across rebuilt surfaces — a full lens (Schema: Generator | Workflow Guide), a drawer (Content Pipeline: Guide button → Drawer), and a collapsed disclosure (Site Audit: 'Audit Guide' inside the evidence block). Same concept, three ceremonies; Schema spends 50% of its lens slots on documentation.
findings.evidence: src/components/schema-rebuilt/useSchemaSurfaceState.ts:7-11 (guide as one of two lenses); content-pipeline-rebuilt/ContentPipelineSurface.tsx:417-420 + :559-570 (Guide button → Drawer); site-audit-rebuilt/SiteAuditSurface.tsx:629-640 (guide as Disclosure).

findings.finding: 'Content Health' means two different things in two places: a Content Pipeline top-level mode (decay board) and a Site Audit evidence disclosure (decay evidence). Same label, different views, different homes — an operator told to 'check Content Health' has two candidate destinations.
findings.evidence: src/components/content-pipeline-rebuilt/ContentPipelineSurface.tsx:92 (mode 'Content Health', count = decay.totalDecaying) vs src/components/site-audit-rebuilt/useSiteAuditSurfaceState.ts:12 + SiteAuditSurface.tsx:617 (Disclosure 'Content Health' rendering <ContentDecay>).

findings.finding: Unknown-vs-zero convention is inconsistent for money metrics: the Keywords KPI band prints '$0 Monthly value' when the provider is absent (trafficValue computes to numeric 0), while the Cockpit's organic-value tile honestly says '— Unavailable'. The rebuild's signature data-honesty is undercut where a hard $0 reads as 'this work is worthless'.
findings.evidence: src/components/keywords-rebuilt/KeywordsSurface.tsx:345-349 (renders '—' only when trafficValue is not a number; a computed 0 formats as $0); browser-walk notes: workspace with no DataForSEO shows '$0 Monthly value' on Keywords but '— Unavailable' on Cockpit for the same underlying gap.

findings.finding: Per-visit noise items: Global Settings renders every GSC property as an untruncated monospace chip wall (~30 chips on the walked account) with no summary/expand pattern; Requests presents 4 sub-tools (deliverables/signals/requests/actions) over a surface observed empty, and its vocabulary is a third taxonomy alongside the client Inbox's decisions/reviews/conversations. Counterweight worth stating: the rebuild is NOT uniformly ceremonial — Brand & AI, Competitors, Page Intelligence, and Page Rewriter carry zero lens switchers, and Local Presence's 4-value→2-mode collapse is a good internal template.
findings.evidence: src/components/global-ops-rebuilt/GlobalSettingsLens.tsx:149-156 (unbounded gscSites map to <code> chips); useGlobalOpsSurfaceState.ts:12 (REQUESTS_TABS = 4) with empty-state observed in browser-walk notes; lean surfaces: brand-ai-rebuilt (0 LensSwitcher/FilterChip, 2 modals), competitors-rebuilt (1 drawer), page-intelligence-rebuilt (1 Segmented sort); local-presence-rebuilt/LocalPresenceSurface.tsx:205-209.

recommendations.recommendation: Unify nav labels through the registry: delete the three GROUP_PRESENTATION label overrides in RebuiltSidebar.tsx (or express them as flagBehavior.labelWhenOn on the navRegistry entries, the mechanism 'home'→'Cockpit' already uses), and derive PALETTE_GROUP_LABELS from the sidebar zone taxonomy. Add a contract test asserting sidebar/breadcrumb/palette resolve identical labels per Page id.
recommendations.value: Kills the highest-frequency drift the operator sees on literally every screen (sidebar says 'Insights Engine' while the breadcrumb says 'Strategy'), and closes the override hatch so the next rename can't fork again.

recommendations.recommendation: Fix the global-surface breadcrumb: in RebuiltBreadcrumb.tsx skip the workspace segment when GLOBAL_TABS.has(tab) (the set already exists in src/routes.ts:36 and is consulted by the sidebar).
recommendations.value: Removes a trust landmine — account-level Settings/Roadmap/Revenue no longer appear to belong to whichever client happens to be first in the workspace list.

recommendations.recommendation: Outcomes: move RecordPublishedWorkCard below the TabBar or behind a 'Record published work' button that opens a drawer (Drawer primitive already exists), and pick ONE surface name ('Action Results' per navRegistry) for the lens h1, dropping the inner legacy PageHeader.
recommendations.value: Restores read-first hierarchy on a surface whose daily job is reviewing results, and eliminates the only triple-named surface in the app.

recommendations.recommendation: Keywords lens diet: fold 'Opportunities' into the Rankings table as a column preset (it already only swaps columns at KeywordsTable.tsx:480) and convert 'Pages'/'Clusters' into a 'Group by' control, leaving Rankings + Lifecycle as true lenses. Simultaneously de-duplicate the opportunity vocabulary (lens vs 'Striking distance' chip vs 'Opportunity' sort chip).
recommendations.value: 5 lenses → 2 with zero information loss; collapses the four stacked filter layers the operator must parse before reaching the one table, on the surface with the most per-visit traffic.

recommendations.recommendation: Engine: always render the Send button, disabled with helper text ('0 staged — stage moves below to send') instead of conditionally mounting it; consider surfacing the live/staged/with-client three-state as a small labeled stepper rather than three loose numbers.
recommendations.value: The primary workflow CTA becomes discoverable in the default (0-staged) state, matching the legacy affordance operators are migrating from.

recommendations.recommendation: Delete dead lens vocabulary at next touch of each file: remove SEO_EDITOR_SURFACE_TABS + tab/setTab/tabOptions from useSeoEditorSurfaceState.ts (unconsumed), and either render a visible jump-nav for ENGINE_LENSES or prune the ids to the ones actually deep-linked; retire the search-traffic 'overview' compat value and local-presence 'visibility'/'setup' URL ids once inbound links are re-pointed.
recommendations.value: Shrinks the URL contract surface that tests and future agents must honor, and prevents the dead 'Research' tab from being resurrected as UI by a future task that finds it in state.

recommendations.recommendation: Site Audit: collapse 'Add error tasks / Add visible tasks / Add all tasks' into a single split-button or Menu next to 'Accept all N', and give 'Accept all N' a confirm step showing what it will change; reconcile the two warning aggregations (chip row vs prose) to one number and gate the headline verdict on the worst category score so 'solid shape' can't sit above 'On-page 0'.
recommendations.value: Cuts the densest action row in the rebuild from ~9 adjacent controls to ~6, and removes the two places the surface visibly disagrees with itself.

recommendations.recommendation: Standardize the small conventions: 'Guide' is always a drawer (retire the Schema guide lens, keep the Site Audit disclosure or convert it too); unknown-vs-zero always renders '—' with a reason when the provider is unconfigured (fix the Keywords $0 by nulling trafficValue when no provider); rename one of the two 'Content Health' homes (e.g. Site Audit's disclosure → 'Decay evidence'); truncate the Settings GSC chip wall to ~6 + 'Show all N'.
recommendations.value: Four cheap consistency wins that each remove a recurring 'why is this different here?' moment; the unknown-vs-zero fix protects the rebuild's flagship data-honesty story.

complexityLanes.headline: The dual-UI carries ~27.0K LOC of legacy-only code and 39.1K LOC of rebuilt code behind a flag that has never been ON in production, with 89 open deferrals (0 scheduled), ~70 semantic drift findings already shipped between the two shells, 82 commits of parity fixes stranded on an unmerged branch, and at least one live production feature (AI Visibility) whose only home is the branch slated for deletion.

findings.finding: Two full admin UIs are carried simultaneously: the rebuilt tree is 168 files / 33,555 LOC across 17 src/components/*-rebuilt/ directories (201 files / 39,053 LOC counting all @ds-rebuilt files incl. DS primitives and shell chrome), mounting 27 surfaces via REBUILT_SURFACES; the legacy tree reachable only flag-OFF is 113 files / 26,976 LOC (104 component files / 26,256 LOC). On top of code: 38 rebuilt-only test files (15,481 LOC), 26 parity docs (4,453 lines in docs/ui-rebuild/parity/), and a 1,515-line deferred ledger.
findings.evidence: Import-closure analysis from App.tsx legacy mounts (src/App.tsx:402-484) vs src/components/layout/rebuiltSurfaces.ts:19-101 (27 entries). Legacy closure 586 files/102,698 LOC; rebuilt closure 655 files/112,515 LOC; overlap 473 files/75,722 LOC. Largest legacy-only files: KeywordStrategy.tsx (1,046), SeoAudit.tsx (842), KeywordHub.tsx (815), AssetBrowser.tsx (745), keyword-command-center/KeywordDetailDrawer.tsx (739).

findings.finding: Semantic drift between flag-ON and flag-OFF has already shipped at scale: the 20-surface code-grounded parity audit logged 30 LOST capabilities (27 UNDOCUMENTED, 1 blocker), 17 REGRESSION[major], 23 CONFUSION[major], and 40 OVERCOMPLICATED findings.
