The same operator sees different NUMBERS per shell: roadmap Completion % changed denominator (done/(done+in_progress+pending) vs legacy done/total, disagreeing with the adjacent Total tile), cockpit inflates 'briefs awaiting review' and files positive churn under Risk, Content Pipeline's Published chip counts matrix CELLS while the lens lists published POSTS, Outcomes Book stamps 'Rolling 90 days' on all-time + 28-day + 30-day windows, Performance renders a rate-limited bulk PageSpeed run as a success toast + fabricated red 0 average, and Local Presence labels the scraped public GBP aggregate 'Authenticated'.
findings.evidence: scratchpad/severe-findings-digest.md: grep counts LOST[=30 (blocker=1), REGRESSION[major]=17, CONFUSION[major]=23, OVERCOMPLICATED=40; specific items at digest lines 30-32 (performance fabricated 0), 90 (cockpit brief counts), 115 (Published chip = matrix cells), 194 (roadmap completion % denominator), 21 (local-presence 'Authenticated' mislabel), 217 ('Rolling 90 days'). Net verdicts: 14 surfaces 'mixed', 6 'better'.

findings.finding: A live production feature is marooned in the legacy branch: the AI Visibility (LLM-mention) KPI panel's ONLY mount is legacy KeywordHub — its feature flag was already retired globally-ON, so deleting the flag-OFF branch today deletes a shipped feature. It is one of the 27 undocumented losses no exception record covers.
findings.evidence: src/components/strategy/AiVisibilityPanel.tsx is imported only by src/components/KeywordHub.tsx:33 (mounted at KeywordHub.tsx:660); no keywords-rebuilt file references it. Digest line 101 confirms flag retired globally-ON (shared/types/feature-flags.ts:70-75 per audit).

findings.finding: 82 commits of post-parity closure work exist ONLY on origin/codex/ui-visual-parity and are not ancestors of this branch, including commit 69a686583 which fixes 11 of the exact defects the drift audit found (cockpit KPI refresh + source-chip counts, roadmap shipping-velocity metric, Published avg-position sign, seo-editor count labels). Meanwhile the parity docs ON this branch cite those commits (d611db84d, f46d4cfcd, 8f6c3ce83, AUD-D1..D7 closure hashes) as landed — the debt census itself documents commits the shipping lineage doesn't have. The parity branch is also ~136K deleted-lines behind HEAD (missing the whole MCP sprint), so rebase cost compounds daily.
findings.evidence: git merge-base --is-ancestor d611db84d HEAD → false; git rev-list --count HEAD..origin/codex/ui-visual-parity = 82; git diff HEAD origin/codex/ui-visual-parity --stat = 746 files, +7,610/-136,074 (src/components slice: 48 files, +654 unbanked). Docs citing the commits: docs/ui-rebuild/parity/parity-backlog.md:126, owner-decision-packet.md:429-442.

findings.finding: The exception/deferral debt has no dated exits anywhere: data/ui-rebuild-deferred-ledger.json holds 90 entries — 89 open, 1 done, ZERO 'scheduled', and every open entry has roadmapItemId=null (the verifier only requires roadmap linkage once status='scheduled', so nothing forces conversion). 49 of 89 open entries hit reviewBy=2026-08-18 simultaneously (a one-day review wall ~5 weeks out); the rest fall 2026-09-15/30 and 2026-10-31. On the decision side, 12 ODP decisions + 8 GO sub-decisions + ~15 named V-exceptions (ODP-001-V4..V6, ODP-BRAND-V5..V7, ODP-002-V6, ODP-003-V3/V5, Schema drawer, Links insert, Performance drawers, Keyword Hub trends, Local Presence geo-grid/GBP, Cockpit Send/Promote) are all condition-triggered ('revisit when X exists') with no dates at all.
findings.evidence: python census of data/ui-rebuild-deferred-ledger.json: status Counter({open:89, done:1}); roadmapItemId None for all 89; reviewBy Counter({2026-08-18:49, 2026-09-15:19, 2026-09-30:11, 2026-10-31:9, 2026-08-14:1}). scripts/verify-deferred-ledger.ts:57-59 gates roadmap linkage on status==='scheduled' only. Exceptions: docs/ui-rebuild/parity/owner-decision-packet.md:19-47 (ODP/GO tables), 104-113 (V4-V6), 125-135 (BRAND V5-V7), 446-453 ('Optional Later Decisions').

findings.finding: Retiring the legacy branch deletes far less than 'half the UI': 222 legacy component files remain load-bearing INSIDE the rebuilt closure (BrandHub.tsx 1,058 LOC wrapped whole in the Brand modal shell, PostEditor.tsx 1,273, WorkspaceSettings.tsx 388, SiteArchitecture.tsx 512 mounted by page-intelligence-rebuilt, ContentCalendar.tsx 671, the four Business panels, OutcomeDashboard tree, RequestManager.tsx 560). Several are held in place by brittle positional-CSS frames (BusinessPanelFrame/OutcomeWorkspaceFrame nth-child/:has() !important chains) that break on any legacy DOM reorder — a permanent coupling cost that flag retirement does not remove.
findings.evidence: Closure script: 222 non-@ds-rebuilt component files inside the rebuilt import closure. Digest lines 196 (BusinessPanelFrame selector chains), 219 (OutcomeWorkspaceFrame), 9 (legacy SiteArchitecture inside page-intelligence-rebuilt), 121 (calendar '[&>div>div:nth-child(2)]:hidden' structural hack), 177 (Brand mega-modal wrapping the entire legacy BrandHub context tab).

findings.finding: Even flag-ON, the legacy shell cannot be deleted: '/' (Command Center / WorkspaceOverview, where presence indicators live) and /ws/:id/subscriptions never mount rebuilt chrome — canMountRebuiltSurface is false at '/' and 'subscriptions' has no REBUILT_SURFACES entry — so legacy Sidebar/Breadcrumbs must survive any retirement until these two get rebuilt homes. The rebuilt shell also ships its own drift generators: global tabs substitute workspaces[0] as context (wrong-client navigation + wrong-workspace AdminChat), and RebuiltBreadcrumb carries SUB_TAB_LABELS_BY_PAGE, a ~70-line hand-maintained duplicate of every surface's tab vocabulary with contract coverage for only one page.
findings.evidence: src/App.tsx:489 (canMountRebuiltSurface = chromeWorkspace !== null || GLOBAL_TABS.has(tab)); App.tsx:282 (GLOBAL_TABS → workspaces[0] ?? null); rebuiltSurfaces.ts has no 'subscriptions' key; src/components/layout/RebuiltBreadcrumb.tsx:25 (SUB_TAB_LABELS_BY_PAGE); digest lines 204-209 (first-workspace misattribution, 3-way naming drift sidebar vs breadcrumb vs ⌘K).

findings.finding: Parallel machinery is already forking within the rebuilt era itself: two annotation mutation stacks (AnnotationsLens hand-rolled trio vs the legacy stack) 'have already drifted', two five-step schema stepper implementations coexist (SchemaWorkflowStrip vs shared WorkflowStepper), Architecture lives in two rebuilt-era homes with different capabilities (links ArchitectureLens vs page-intelligence's legacy SiteArchitecture), and dead code shipped inside rebuilt dirs (LocalPresenceSetup.tsx 78 lines imported nowhere, SchemaQuickStats, seo-editor's unused SEO_EDITOR_SURFACE_TABS tab machinery, cockpit's parsed-but-never-consumed ?view= state, links' dead ?detail= deep-link machinery).
findings.evidence: Digest lines 132 (annotation stacks 'already drifted'), 84 (two steppers), 9 (two Architecture homes), 23 (LocalPresenceSetup.tsx dead), 83 (SchemaQuickStats/formatSnapshotDate exported-never-used), 50 (dead tab machinery), 93 (?view= parsed never consumed), 10 (dead ?detail= machinery).

findings.finding: The flag itself is stale against its own lifecycle contract: 'ui-rebuild-shell' defaults to false, rolloutTarget is 'staging-validation' (production has never run flag-ON), staleAuditCadence is 'weekly' but lastReviewedAt is 2026-07-05 (11 days ago), and the removalCondition ('retire once the rebuilt shell ships unflagged') is blocked by the still-unexecuted authenticated live-provider smoke that the parity closure explicitly names as outstanding. Note the hook reads the GLOBAL flag map (useFeatureFlag), so a per-workspace override cannot pilot the shell — rollout is all-admins-or-nothing.
findings.evidence: shared/types/feature-flags.ts:129 ('ui-rebuild-shell': false), :511-523 (lifecycle meta); src/components/layout/RebuiltAppChrome.tsx:71-73 (useFeatureFlag global read); docs/ui-rebuild/parity/owner-decision-packet.md:444 ('Deterministic local evidence does not replace the still-unexecuted authenticated live-provider smoke').

recommendations.recommendation: Bank the stranded parity branch first: rebase/merge origin/codex/ui-visual-parity's ~7,610 unmerged lines — at minimum cherry-pick 69a686583 (the 11-defect fix) and the AUD-D* closure commits the docs already cite — onto staging lineage before the 136K-line gap grows further.
recommendations.value: Stops the single fastest-compounding debt: verified fixes for known shipped drift exist but aren't shipping, and the docs/code mismatch (docs citing non-ancestor commits) actively misleads any future agent auditing this history.

recommendations.recommendation: Declare a legacy-tree change freeze: no feature work on the 113 legacy-only files except security/data fixes; every admin feature/fix lands rebuilt-first. Enforce with a lightweight pr-check rule flagging diffs to legacy-only surface files without a paired rebuilt change or an inline hatch.
recommendations.value: Caps drift at its current ~70-finding level; every unfrozen week adds double-maintenance cost across 27 surfaces and widens the LOST census that retirement must burn down.

recommendations.recommendation: Run a port-or-except triage over the 30 LOST capabilities (27 undocumented, 1 blocker) before any prod flag-ON: priority order AiVisibilityPanel→keywords-rebuilt (live feature), site-audit per-page repair actions + page inventory (blocker), seo-editor CMS depth (extra SEO fields/slug/per-field AI rewrite), engine OrientZone visibility strip + content-gaps handoff, competitors edit-set dead-end route, local-presence GBP remediation links. Each gets ported, or an owner-approved dated exception in the parity contract.
recommendations.value: This is the actual retirement gate: today, deleting the OFF branch deletes shipped capability. Converting undocumented losses into ported code or dated exceptions is what makes 'delete the legacy branch' a safe mechanical act instead of a feature regression.

recommendations.recommendation: Close the dual-shell seam: give '/' (Command Center/WorkspaceOverview incl. presence) and 'subscriptions' rebuilt homes (or fold subscriptions into content-pipeline per its existing query alias), and fix the workspaces[0] global-tab misattribution while there.
recommendations.value: Until these two mount rebuilt chrome, the legacy Sidebar/Breadcrumbs/shell cannot be deleted even with the flag permanently ON — they are the hard structural blocker to the deletion payoff.

recommendations.recommendation: Defuse the 2026-08-18 ledger wall now: triage the 49 due entries (plus the other 40) into status='scheduled' with real roadmapItemIds or 'retired' — and tighten verify-deferred-ledger to cap consecutive reviewBy extensions or require scheduling after the first extension, since today 0 of 89 open entries link to any planned work.
recommendations.value: Converts a passive review-date treadmill into scheduled burn-down; without it the ledger's dated exits are dates for postponing, not exiting, and 49 items will be bulk-extended in one sitting five weeks from now.

recommendations.recommendation: Then execute the CLAUDE.md retirement template in order: staging flag-ON soak + the outstanding authenticated live-provider smoke → prod ON globally (per-workspace pilot is impossible — the hook reads the global map) → delete the OFF branch: remove renderContent() legacy mounts (App.tsx:402-487), legacy Sidebar/Breadcrumbs, and the 113 legacy-only files (~27.0K LOC); chase orphans with knip/ts-prune (expect the count to grow as shared hooks lose their last consumer); run the route-removal checklist for content-perf; remove the flag from FEATURE_FLAGS/CATALOG/GROUPS, add to RETIRED_FLAG_GROUPS in scripts/pr-check.ts, ship the migration deleting feature_flag_overrides + feature_flag_workspace_overrides rows; grep tests for the key (53 test files reference rebuilt; flag-transition tests will assert OFF behavior) and run the FULL suite; archive the 26 parity docs (4,453 lines) per docs/rules/rules-lifecycle.md, promoting any durable contracts into docs/rules/.
recommendations.value: Recovers ~27K LOC + the double-maintenance tax on 27 surfaces, retires 4.5K lines of point-in-time docs, and closes the flag's own lifecycle contract — but only pays out after recommendations 1-5; run early it ships the drift and deletes live features.

complexityLanes.headline: 116 extension opportunities across the 20 surface audits collapse into 9 recurring themes; the highest-leverage work is overwhelmingly SMALL — rendering evidence the surfaces already fetch, defusing count/label/freshness trust landmines, and finishing half-built cross-surface handoffs — while the three genuinely large items (keyword read models DEF-kw-001..003, the GO-004 book rollup, and the '/' Command Center rebuild) are already-ledgered deferrals with defined exit criteria, not new scope.

findings.finding: THEME — Half-built cross-surface handoff mesh: the rebuild's connective tissue (fixContext state + durable ?param deep links) exists but is asymmetric almost everywhere — receivers with no senders (page-rewriter ?pageUrl=, links ?detail=), senders with no receivers (Site Audit → performance fixContext dead in both eras), fragile receivers (content-pipeline reads location.state once and misses it under lazy-chunk loading), and unscoped handoffs (page-intelligence 'View traffic' lands on unfiltered analytics).
findings.evidence: Raised by 12 of 20 surfaces (~15 items): links, performance, seo-editor(outbound only), site-audit, competitors, cockpit, content-pipeline, engine, asset-manager, page-intelligence, brand-ai, ops-business, page-rewriter. Source: /private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-joshuahampson-CascadeProjects-asset-dashboard/6dfbaf80-85b3-4d1e-81ff-b64918521bf8/scratchpad/surface-audits.json (extensions arrays).

findings.finding: THEME — Fetched/computed-but-unrendered data: the rebuild repeats the 2026-06 SEO audit's 'fetched-and-discarded' pattern internally — data is already on the wire or computed in a helper but never displayed. Examples: cockpit moneyFrame (valueAtStake/recovered + dead provenanceBasis formatter), page-intelligence buildFixQueue returns 5 items and renders 1, seo-editor approvalBatches fetched but no per-item history, keywords drawer missing CTR/impressions/dates/reasons all present on the row, schema formatSnapshotDate/richEligible computed and dropped, competitors backlinks.referringDomains in every row, ops-work attention badge built but hidden, ops-settings GSC permissionLevel fetched and shown nowhere.
findings.evidence: Raised by 13 of 20 surfaces; nearly every item is frontend-only with zero new fetches (exceptions: asset-manager healthScore ~5-line server change; performance getSinglePageSpeed needs a GET route).

findings.finding: THEME — Count/label/freshness honesty gaps (trust landmines): displayed numbers whose denominator, window, or unit doesn't match what the surface shows. Worst cases are screenshot-to-client hazards: performance caches a 200-empty bulk PSI result as a fabricated red-zero average; ops-work labels all-time value 'Rolling 90 days'; ops-business shows 'Feature #N' and a completion % computed on a different denominator than the hero counts; brand-ai's Voice & Messaging score is permanently red because it ignores real calibration data; cockpit has a 'Send' button that doesn't send; competitors stamps cached data with a fresh 'Last scanned'.
findings.evidence: Raised by 12 of 20 surfaces (~14 items): links, performance, seo-editor, competitors, cockpit, keywords, content-pipeline, engine, ops-business, ops-work, brand-ai, local-presence, site-audit. Directly violates the repo's rate-display rule (CLAUDE.md 'numerator and denominator must share a source').

findings.finding: THEME — Silent failures on the highest-stakes mutations: client-facing sends and paid generation paths still swallow errors. seo-editor's CMS send path never surfaces cmsWorkflow.approvalError (silent failure on a client send); schema's 'Add a page' dead-clicks on Webflow API failure (generation.fetchPagesError unrendered); links has no sent-state latch so re-sends create duplicate client inbox items; site-audit claims 'copied' before clipboard resolution and discards the share URL.
findings.evidence: Raised by 4 surfaces on send/approval spines specifically (seo-editor, schema, links, site-audit); asset-manager and page-rewriter add adjacent error-state gaps. All small fixes.

findings.finding: THEME — Flag-ON coverage holes / dual-shell seam: two production capabilities go dark or split the shell the moment ui-rebuild-shell turns on — the AI-visibility panel (share-of-voice, mention trend, refresh) has no rebuilt home, and the '/' Command Center plus the 'subscriptions' alias still mount legacy chrome, flipping the operator between two shells and blocking legacy-shell retirement.
findings.evidence: keywords audit (AI-visibility, coverage-audit.md:55 / GO-008) and shell-nav audit (dual-shell seam). These are graduation blockers, not polish.

findings.finding: THEME — Deferred server-owned read models with ledger entries: the three LARGE items are all documented deferrals whose exit conditions the audits now re-confirm — keywords DEF-kw-001/002/003 (history-by-keyword sparklines, rollups/deltas, full-universe grouping; would fix the identical-count lens tray and page-scoped boards), ops-work GO-004 (book rollup endpoint replacing the permanent 'Book totals are not yet available' banner and the piggybacked per-workspace fetch), and the seo-editor keyboard review queue (owner-gated in its contract).
findings.evidence: keywords, ops-work, seo-editor audits; data/ui-rebuild-deferred-ledger.json entries DEF-kw-001..003 and GO-004 already scope them.

findings.finding: THEME — Broadcast/invalidation both-halves gaps: three violations of the mandatory data-flow rule — performance page-weight/PSI scans complete with no broadcastToWorkspace (neither era had it), keywords' STRATEGY_UPDATED handler doesn't invalidate keywordFeedback so the new client-feedback panel goes stale, and asset-manager's upload queue completion never invalidates webflowAssets so fresh uploads don't appear in Browse.
findings.evidence: performance, keywords, asset-manager audits; CLAUDE.md Data Flow Rules #1-2.

findings.finding: THEME — Dead controls and gate-state dead ends: every unconfigured/empty state should carry its remediation CTA and every rendered control must do something. Dead-enders: search-traffic's three unconfigured gates, local-presence's disconnected/reconnect and map-a-location states, competitors' setup states hiding the alerts feed. Dead controls: links' do-nothing 'Add page', site-audit's silently inert Traffic sort with an empty traffic map, cockpit's ?view= switcher plumbing with no implementation, local-presence's decorative status-census chips.
findings.evidence: Raised by 7 surfaces (search-traffic, local-presence, site-audit, competitors, links, cockpit, asset-manager error-vs-empty conflation). All small.

findings.finding: THEME — Scale ergonomics: several rebuilt list/board surfaces are excellent at 10 items and degrade at 50+ — search-traffic renders all 500 detail rows uncapped and unfilterable, content-pipeline's alphabetical four-column board has no search, schema's drawer-per-page model has no table filter on 50+ page sites, keywords taxes the highest-frequency triage flow with +1 drawer click per row, and site-audit's drawer narrows group actions to only the first page instance.
findings.evidence: Raised by 5 surfaces (search-traffic, content-pipeline, schema, keywords, site-audit); agencies with content subscriptions hit these limits quickly.

findings.finding: GAIN TO PROTECT #1 — URL-persisted, validated, deep-linkable state as the default: essentially every rebuilt surface persists lens/filter/search/sort/selection in the URL with validated fallbacks, legacy ?tab= alias handling, and explicit invalid-param warning banners (vs legacy's ephemeral useState and silent fallbacks). This is the rebuild's defining structural gain — new work must keep writing AND reading URL state (the two-halves contract).
findings.evidence: Cited as a gain by 15+ of 20 surfaces (links, local-presence, performance, seo-editor, site-audit, keywords, content-pipeline, search-traffic, engine, asset-manager, page-intelligence, brand-ai, ops-business, ops-work, page-rewriter).

findings.finding: GAIN TO PROTECT #2 — Honest data-state lifecycle: skeleton loading, typed ErrorState with retry, stale-data-kept-visible warning banners on refetch failure, and 'Data as of' freshness stamps, replacing legacy's silent console.error swallowing and view-destroying error screens. Failure recovery that preserves on-screen data is now a platform expectation.
findings.evidence: Cited by 14+ surfaces; canonical examples: performance stale-banner-with-retry, engine per-read retryable errors, ops-work error-banner-over-last-known-rows vs legacy's misleading empty state.

findings.finding: GAIN TO PROTECT #3 — Universal mutation feedback on the shared useToast, including truthful refresh (awaitSuccessfulRefetches) and canonical error extraction: legacy swallowed most mutation failures to console.error, including on client sends. Any new mutation without success/error surfacing is a regression.
findings.evidence: Cited by 10+ surfaces (site-audit, schema, asset-manager, links, local-presence, keywords, content-pipeline, cockpit, page-rewriter, ops-settings).

findings.finding: GAIN TO PROTECT #4 — Surface-owned WebSocket invalidation: rebuilt surfaces register their own useWorkspaceEvents handlers (engine 10 events, content-pipeline 8, schema 5, seo-editor 3, local-presence 5) so they stay live outside the legacy dashboard invalidator. Do not let new panels rely on the legacy shell's invalidation again.
findings.evidence: Cited by 7 surfaces; legacy SchemaSuggester had zero useWorkspaceEvents wiring.

findings.finding: GAIN TO PROTECT #5 — No-fabrication rendering + attribution honesty: '—/Establishing' instead of fabricated zeroes (cockpit), removal of the fabricated clicks*0.6/0.8/1.0 sparkline (content-pipeline), columns rendered only when data exists (competitors), Recent-wins excluding not_acted_on and labeling externally_executed honestly (ops-work), dash fallbacks on absent click evidence (links). This discipline is what the remaining honesty-gap theme is converging TOWARD — never trade it away for visual completeness.
findings.evidence: Cited by 6+ surfaces; ops-work applies the CLAUDE.md attribution-honesty seam where legacy applied no filter.

findings.finding: GAIN TO PROTECT #6 — Previously fetched-and-discarded server data now rendered: competitors' authority rank/Top-3 counts, local-presence's mapPackShareOfVoicePct, search-traffic's brandedDemand band, page-rewriter's rank/traffic/optimization evidence band, links' clicks/impressions/matchScore redirect evidence. These directly close the 2026-06 SEO-audit theme; future refactors must not drop these joins again.
findings.evidence: Cited by 5 surfaces as first-ever renders of long-returned server fields.

findings.finding: GAIN TO PROTECT #7 — Destructive-action, bulk-scope, race, and cost safety: ConfirmDialog with explicit counts replacing window.confirm/immediate-fire prunes (asset-manager, ops-settings), consistent bulk scope fixing legacy's silent selected∩filtered mismatch, RichText-only compress exclusion, send-note no longer silently dropped (site-audit), request-id/context-epoch race guards (page-rewriter), version-guarded approve-all (brand-ai), and paid-operation cost discipline (performance strategy-switch loads saved snapshots instead of re-firing paid PSI; 429 quota-lock UX in asset-manager/page-rewriter).
findings.evidence: Cited by 7 surfaces; several fixed silent legacy correctness bugs, not just UX.

findings.finding: GAIN TO PROTECT #8 — Accessibility + component-test floor: roving-tabindex radiogroups/tablists, skip-to-content, focus restoration from drawers, aria-current breadcrumbs, and — critically — real flag loading→loaded transition tests (cockpit, page-intelligence) that catch Rules-of-Hooks crashes mocked-hook tests cannot. The a11y ratchet already caught a real Meter no-name bug; keep the ratchet and the transition-test pattern on every new gated surface.
findings.evidence: Cited by 6 surfaces (cockpit, page-intelligence, shell-nav, ops-business, ops-work, keywords) with named test files.

recommendations.recommendation: R1 — 'Render what's already fetched' batch: one coordinated small-PR wave lighting up data already on the wire — cockpit moneyFrame on the Organic-value tile, keywords drawer evidence slices (CTR/impressions/dates/reasons/Strategy backlink), page-intelligence top-5 fix queue + score-trend chip, seo-editor per-item approval history from fetched approvalBatches, schema snapshot date + richEligible/stalePublished tiles, competitors referring-domains column, site-audit score delta + CWV tones, local-presence GBP competitor benchmark, ops-work attention-badge column + diagnostics traffic headline, ops-settings GSC permissionLevel chip.
recommendations.value: Highest value/effort ratio in the whole synthesis: raised by 13 surfaces, restores legacy evidentiary depth, needs essentially zero backend, and closes the fetched-and-discarded pattern inside the rebuild itself.

recommendations.recommendation: R2 — Trust-landmine hotfix sweep: performance 200-empty bulk guard (stop caching a fabricated red-zero average), brand-ai voice score wired to real calibration data (always-red fix), cockpit 'Send' relabel-or-wire, ops-work 'Rolling 90 days' truthful window labels, ops-business 'Feature #N' name resolution + completion-denominator reconciliation, competitors snapshot-date freshness (not fetchedAt), links/content-pipeline lens-count unit fixes, cockpit Risk-stream positive-signal filter.
recommendations.value: Raised by 12 surfaces; every item is a wrong claim an operator or client can screenshot. Small each, and directly enforces the repo's rate-display and no-fabrication rules.

recommendations.recommendation: R3 — Silent-failure surfacing on the send/approval spine: seo-editor cmsWorkflow.approvalError/approvalSent toasts at the toolbar send site, schema fetchPagesError banner+toast, links sent-state latch keyed on sourceId (prevents duplicate client inbox items), site-audit share-URL row + truthful 'copied' claim.
recommendations.value: Client sends are the highest-stakes mutations on the platform and currently fail silently or double-fire on 4 surfaces. All small; mostly re-wiring already-stateful values.

recommendations.recommendation: R4 — Handoff-mesh completion, phased: (a) make content-pipeline's fixContext receiver robust (useEffect/props, survives lazy-chunk mounting) — 6+ sender surfaces depend on it; (b) wire page-rewriter senders (Site Audit/Page Intelligence/Keyword rows → ?pageUrl=) into its validated, test-covered receiver; (c) consume the dead Site Audit → performance fixContext; (d) upgrade page-intelligence senders to the durable ?page= link and page-scope 'View traffic'; (e) asset-manager auto-run on ?tab=audit; (f) links 'Add page' → briefs prefill; (g) brand-ai ?focus= section targeting.
recommendations.value: The most-repeated theme (12 surfaces). Each completed loop turns a multi-step manual re-entry into one click; (a) alone fixes an intermittent operator-facing bug across 6+ senders.

recommendations.recommendation: R5 — Close the flag-ON coverage holes before shell graduation: give the AI-visibility panel a rebuilt home (dedicated surface per the prototype nav, or temporary Keywords mount), and rebuild the '/' Command Center + a 'subscriptions' receiver/redirect so a flag-ON session never flips to legacy chrome.
recommendations.value: A live, unconditional production feature (AI share-of-voice) goes invisible the moment ui-rebuild-shell turns on, and the dual-shell seam blocks retiring the legacy shell. These are graduation blockers, not enhancements (keywords + shell-nav audits).

recommendations.recommendation: R6 — Shell workspace-context correctness: remember the last-visited workspace (not workspaces[0]) for chromeWorkspace/AdminChat/palette context with an honest no-workspace state, plus a lightweight topbar workspace switcher and an always-rendered Requests affordance.
recommendations.value: Eliminates a wrong-client navigation and AI-context misattribution hazard for the multi-client agency operator — the core persona. Small effort, shell-wide payoff (shell-nav audit).

recommendations.recommendation: R7 — Gate/empty-state remediation CTA sweep: 'Open Workspace Settings' on search-traffic's three unconfigured gates, 'Open connection'/'Open mappings' on local-presence's dead-end states, disabled-with-tooltip Traffic sort on site-audit when the traffic map is empty, alerts feed mounted in competitors' setup states, distinct error-vs-empty state in asset-manager.
recommendations.value: 7 surfaces raised dead-end or silently-inert states; one-click paths to the fix are already standard EmptyState practice in this codebase. All small.

recommendations.recommendation: R8 — Broadcast both-halves completion: broadcastToWorkspace on performance page-weight/PSI scan completion with a useWorkspaceEvents receiver, keywordFeedback/keywordStrategy invalidation in keywords' STRATEGY_UPDATED handler (or reuse strategyMutationKeys), and webflowAssets invalidation on files:uploaded completion in asset-manager.
recommendations.value: Three concrete violations of the mandatory data-flow rule; fixes stale client-feedback panels, dead multi-admin freshness, and the broken upload→browse loop.

recommendations.recommendation: R9 — ConfirmDialog isPending prop + destructive/dirty guards: add loading/disabled to ui/ConfirmDialog and thread mutation isPending (ops-settings raised it; benefits every rebuilt surface), brand-ai workflow-modal dirty-close guard, local-presence ConfirmDialog on publishing awaiting_client responses, site-audit bulk Accept-all cancel wiring to the already-registered handler.
recommendations.value: One shared-primitive change closes the double-fire window platform-wide; the brand-ai guard closes the highest-stakes silent data-loss path the modal shell introduced; the local-presence confirm protects one-click publishes to Google.

recommendations.recommendation: R10 — Scale ergonomics for high-volume views: cap search-traffic's detail table (25 + expander) with an in-table text filter, add board search + a real Queued producer to content-pipeline, search/filter on schema's generated-pages table, per-row quick actions on the keywords DataTable, and per-instance actions in site-audit's issue drawer (hooks already take (page, issue)).
recommendations.value: 5 surfaces degrade sharply at 50+ items — exactly where paying agency workspaces live. The DataTable structure makes most of these trivial now.

recommendations.recommendation: R11 — Ship keywords' deferred read models (DEF-kw-001..003): batched history-by-keyword for inline sparklines + 7-day deltas, server rollups/deltas for avg-position KPIs, and full-set grouping so Pages/Clusters/Lifecycle become whole-universe views with per-lens distinct counts.
recommendations.value: Converts the surface's three biggest documented honesty caveats (page-scoped boards, no row trends, identical lens counts) into the lens IA the rebuild promises. Already ledgered with expiry — this is paying down committed debt.

recommendations.recommendation: R12 — Ship the GO-004 server-owned Outcomes Book rollup (value, clicks, wins, attention counts with the same attribution rules), replacing the permanent 'Book totals are not yet available' banner and the piggybacked per-workspace overview fetches; pair with the small R2 window-label fix in the interim.
recommendations.value: Closes a documented deferral's exit condition, restores the portfolio-level summary legacy had (now attribution-honest), and cuts the book's server cost dramatically (ops-work audit).

recommendations.recommendation: R13 — Restore open-in-new-tab affordances on Links: URL-cell + drawer 'Open link' anchors for dead links, redirect-target and chain final-destination anchors in Redirects; wire the already-built ?detail= param to the drawers in the same pass.
recommendations.value: The rebuilt Links surface has zero rendered anchors — removing the copy/paste round-trip from the single most common verification step in link repair; the links audit calls it the surface's biggest undocumented loss.

recommendations.recommendation: R14 — Nav vocabulary unification: labelWhenOn flagBehavior in NAV_REGISTRY for the shell flag, prototype names as ⌘K palette search aliases, rebuilt-zone palette group headers, and Competitors added to the palette while the flag is ON.
recommendations.value: One vocabulary across sidebar/breadcrumb/palette; 'Insights Engine' and 'Asset Manager' become searchable; deletes the drift-prone local label-override layer (shell-nav audit).

recommendations.recommendation: R15 — Duplicate-home consolidation per the delete-the-predecessor rule: retire the Page Intelligence tab's legacy SiteArchitecture mount in favor of the Links Architecture lens (they already disagree on orphans/depth-warnings), and give RevenueDashboard/SalesReport first-class embedded props (+React Query migration) so BusinessPanelFrame's nth-child CSS surgery can be deleted.
recommendations.value: Both duplicates are actively drifting or fragile today; consolidation prevents the next silent divergence and clears an acknowledged convention violation (hand-rolled fetch).

validations.claim: Open a URL in a new tab from anywhere on the surface. Legacy rendered clickable external anchors for the dead/redirecting link itself (LinkChecker.tsx:255-263, <a target=_blank> on link.url), the redirect target of a scanned page (RedirectManager.tsx:502-506), and a chain's final destination (RedirectManager.tsx:333-335). The rebuilt surface has zero rendered anchors — grep of links-rebuilt/ finds only the copied-HTML string template (InternalLinksLens.tsx:89). Dead-link drawer offers only Copy URL (DeadLinksLens.tsx:131-134); redirect target column is plain text (RedirectsLens.tsx:488-500); chain final destination is plain text (RedirectsLens.tsx:379-381). Verifying a broken link now takes copy -> new tab -> paste on every row.
validations.note: Verified all cites on origin/staging (5a2641b21; cited files identical in working tree). Legacy anchors exist at all three sites: LinkChecker.tsx:255-263 (<a href={link.url} target="_blank"> + ExternalLink icon per row), RedirectManager.tsx:333-335 (chain final destination anchor), RedirectManager.tsx:502-504 (redirect target column anchor). Rebuilt surface has zero rendered anchors: grep of src/components/links-rebuilt/ for <a|href=|window.open|target="_blank"|ExternalLink matches only the copied-HTML string template (InternalLinksLens.tsx:89) and a programmatic CSV-download helper (linksFormatters.ts:49-52, not a URL opener). DeadLinksLens.tsx:128-134 drawer footer offers only Mark reviewed/Copy URL/Review redirects/Re-check; drawer body shows the URL as plain text (~:158). RedirectsLens.tsx:380-381 final destination and :488-500 target column are plain spans; the lens's only clipboard action is bulk copy of accepted rules (:161-162), so redirect targets lack even per-row copy. No src/components/ui/ primitive renders target="_blank" (grep: zero hits); onRowClick handlers open drawers, not URLs. Flag-ON mount confirmed via rebuiltSurfaces.ts:41-42 (links → LinksSurface). Not an approved exception: docs/ui-rebuild/parity/links-contract.md:43,57's "copy/send exception" covers the internal-link Insert action, a different capability; carry-over lists (:83-85) never mention anchors/new-tab open, and parity-backlog.md has no links-anchor entry. Severity honest at major: cross-cutting friction on the surface's primary verify-a-link workflow (3 distinct loss sites), with only a partial workaround (Copy URL exists solely in the dead-link drawer).

validations.claim: Full architecture orphan-path list. Legacy listed every orphanPath in its own card (SiteArchitecture.tsx:377-392). Rebuilt shows only the first 3 as chips and claims "+N more in the URL tree" (ArchitectureLens.tsx:421-430) — but TreeNodeRow renders no orphan marker (ArchitectureLens.tsx:94-138) and the schema PriorityQueue dropped legacy's per-row "orphan" flag (legacy SiteArchitecture.tsx:470-472 vs rebuilt ArchitectureLens.tsx:273-318 columns: Page/Priority/Inbound/Link score). Orphans 4..N are unidentifiable anywhere in the rebuilt lens, and the banner actively misdirects.
validations.note: Verified all cited lines on origin/staging (git diff origin/staging empty for the cited files). Confirmed: legacy full list at src/components/SiteArchitecture.tsx:377-392; rebuilt slice(0,3)+banner at src/components/links-rebuilt/ArchitectureLens.tsx:415-431; no orphan marker in TreeNodeRow (94-138); PriorityQueue columns without orphan flag (273-318); no documented exception (docs/ui-rebuild/parity/links-contract.md:52,86 claims orphans preserved). Refuted the strong parts: (1) legacy TreeNode (SiteArchitecture.tsx:116-180) also had no orphan marker — parity, not regression; (2) the priority-queue 'orphan' flag is the link-orphan concept (server/internal-links.ts:254 inbound===0), distinct from architecture orphanPaths (server/site-architecture.ts:150-152), and survives as the Inbound column (numberOrDash renders 0, linksFormatters.ts:7-9), 'critical' priority (server/routes/site-architecture.ts:91), and InternalLinksLens orphan banner + full per-page inbound counts (InternalLinksLens.tsx:150-181, 200-210); (3) 'unidentifiable anywhere' fails because every architecture orphan is a Live child of a gap node (orphan condition :151 implies parent matches gap condition :173) and the rebuilt ArchitectureGaps panel renders all gaps untruncated with child counts and an Add page CTA (ArchitectureLens.tsx:236-255). Net: real but minor readout degradation (enumeration beyond 3 requires indirection through the complete gaps list/tree), not major functionality loss for a solo operator.
validations.correctedClaim: The rebuilt Architecture lens truncates the architecture orphan-path list to 3 example chips plus a count (ArchitectureLens.tsx:421-429) where legacy listed all paths (SiteArchitecture.tsx:377-392), and the links-contract's 'orphans preserved' line (links-contract.md:52,86) doesn't note the truncation. But this is a degraded readout, not lost functionality: the count is preserved; orphans 4..N remain locatable because every architecture orphan is by server construction a Live-badged child of a gap node (site-architecture.ts:151 vs :173) and the rebuilt gaps panel renders EVERY gap untruncated with child counts plus a new 'Add page' CTA (ArchitectureLens.tsx:240-252) — gaps being the actual remediation unit. The banner's '+N more in the URL tree' is literally accurate (the orphans are unmarked there, same as legacy's tree, which also had no orphan marker). The priority-queue point conflates concepts: legacy's per-row 'orphan' flag (SiteArchitecture.tsx:470-472) marked LINK-orphans (0 inbound), not architecture orphanPaths, and that signal survives as the sortable Inbound column rendering '0' (linksFormatters.ts:7-9), the 'critical' priority encoding (server/routes/site-architecture.ts:91), and the Internal Links lens's dedicated orphan banner + full per-page inbound counts (InternalLinksLens.tsx:150-181, 200-210).

validations.claim: GBP connection remediation navigation: legacy 'Open connection' (→ workspace-settings?tab=connections) and 'Open mappings' (→ brand?tab=business-footprint) buttons on the mapping status block. The rebuilt Reviews & replies mode shows 'Disconnected' / 'Reconnect needed' badges and 'Connect Google Business Profile first' empty states but offers NO navigation to actually connect or remap — grep confirms zero 'workspace-settings' references in the rebuilt dir.
validations.note: Verified HEAD == origin/staging (5a2641b21). Confirmed legacy buttons at src/components/google-business-profile/GbpMappingStatusBlock.tsx:34-49 (Open connection → workspace-settings?tab=connections; Open mappings → brand?tab=business-footprint&focus=locations-section), mounted at LocalPresencePage.tsx:182,218 behind gbp-auth-connection. Confirmed rebuilt reviews mode is status-only (LocalPresenceReviewsPipeline.tsx:162-192) with CTA-less empty states (:264-269, :510-517) and zero workspace-settings refs in local-presence-rebuilt/. REFUTING: grep found brand?tab=business-footprint&focus=locations-section navigation ALIVE in the rebuilt surface — LocalPresenceOverview.tsx:98 ('Location records' button, rendered in the default presence mode per LocalPresenceSurface.tsx:274-281; note LocalPresenceSetup.tsx:68 has the same link but that component is unmounted dead code). Destination verified: useBrandAiSurfaceState.ts:5-44 accepts tab=business-footprint (+legacy aliases business-profile/locations); BrandAiSurface mounts BusinessFootprintTab which mounts GbpLocationMappingPanel (settings/BusinessFootprintTab.tsx:10,132) — the real mapping editor. Connect capability verified intact in rebuilt shell: rebuiltSurfaces.ts:71 → global-ops-rebuilt WorkspaceSettingsLens.tsx:4,143 embeds legacy WorkspaceSettings whose ConnectionsTab.tsx:256-257 mounts GbpConnectionCard behind the same flag; CockpitSurface.tsx:83 routes settings → workspace-settings (navRegistry.tsx:103 excludes it from main nav by design). Parity docs: local-seo-contract.md:88 protects only 'connection/mapping status' (no button mention — claim accurate there), but :78 explicitly requires keeping the business-footprint&focus=locations-section handoff intact, which the rebuilt honors. No owner-approved exception found in parity-backlog.md/owner-decision-packet.md for the connection shortcut. Severity downgraded: feature is dark-launched (shared/types/feature-flags.ts:59 'gbp-auth-connection': false; :313-319 rolloutTarget 'staging-validation'), the mapping path survives on the same surface, and the connect UI is two clicks away in a solo-operator tool — a real but minor in-context-shortcut regression, not major lost functionality.
validations.correctedClaim: The rebuilt Reviews & replies mode drops the in-context 'Open connection' shortcut (dead-end 'Connect Google Business Profile first' empty states with no CTA), but the claim's 'NO navigation to actually connect or remap' is wrong on the remap half and overstated on the connect half: the surface's default Rank & profile mode has a live 'Location records' button (LocalPresenceOverview.tsx:95-102) targeting brand?tab=business-footprint&focus=locations-section — the exact legacy 'Open mappings' destination — which the rebuilt Brand & AI surface deep-links (useBrandAiSurfaceState validates 'business-footprint') to the actual GbpLocationMappingPanel editor; and the GBP connect UI itself (GbpConnectionCard in ConnectionsTab) remains mounted in the rebuilt shell at workspace-settings (WorkspaceSettingsLens.tsx:143 embeds legacy WorkspaceSettings), reachable via the cockpit (CockpitSurface.tsx:83). What is genuinely missing is a one-click connect/reconnect link from the reviews mode's disconnected states — a discoverability gap in a dark-launched feature (gbp-auth-connection defaults false, rolloutTarget staging-validation), not a lost capability.

validations.claim: GBP competitor review benchmark: legacy showed your listing vs the top competitor's rating/review count and the derived 'Review gap: N behind the leader' line, plus the Overview sentence 'the local leader has M reviews'. The server still returns top-5 competitors by review count, but the rebuilt surface reads only gbp.owned and completenessScore — competitor listing data is fetched and discarded (the map-pack share-of-voice table is a different dataset: SERP local-pack repeat competitors, which carry no rating/review counts).
validations.note: Every clause verified on origin/staging (5a2641b21). Legacy: GbpReviewsPanel.tsx:140-151 renders Your-listing vs Top-competitor ListingRows + 'Review gap: N behind the leader'; LocalPresencePage.tsx:118-119 renders the 'local leader has M reviews' Overview sentence. Server: local-seo.ts:264-268 still returns top-5 competitors by reviewCount on /gbp-reviews. Rebuilt: LocalPresenceSurface.tsx:70,80 fetches via useGbpReviews but the only payload reads in local-presence-rebuilt/ are gbp?.owned (LocalPresenceOverview.tsx:41, LocalPresenceVisibility.tsx:81) and gbp?.completenessScore (Overview.tsx:106-108) — competitors is genuinely fetched-and-discarded. Map-pack SoV table renders LocalSeoRepeatCompetitor (shared/types/local-seo.ts:537-553) which has NO rating/reviewCount fields — confirmed different dataset. No relocated home: GbpReviewsPanel's only other mount (LocalSeoVisibilityPanel.tsx:460) requires mode==='keywords', but rebuilt-shell mounts are mode='page' (PageIntelligenceSurface.tsx:273, early-returns at :378) and mode='strategy'/flag-OFF (KeywordStrategy.tsx:347); KeywordHub mounts neither. Docs: local-seo-contract.md carry-over (line 85, claim said 86 — trivial off-by-one) protects owned aggregate + completeness + map-pack SoV but is silent on the benchmark; the approved backend exceptions (line 52) don't cover it (data is live, not a backend gap); phase-a ticket local-presence.md:45 explicitly required BUILD 'own rating/review-count vs top competitor, review-gap sentence' and is still unchecked — the drop contradicts the surface's own ticket rather than being an approved exception. Severity: major is honest — local-gbp is default-OFF (feature-flags.ts:54) but its catalog label names 'review-gap vs local competitors' as headline functionality (feature-flags.ts:301) and its promotion condition presumes it; only partial mitigation is server-side review-gap recommendations, not a surface readout.

validations.claim: Authenticated-review triage readout: legacy showed 'Stored low rating' count (red triage signal), total review count, newest-review date, and a 'Recent review excerpts' list (5 most recent synced reviews with Replied/Unanswered badges). Rebuilt sync block shows only Stored / Unanswered / Average — replied reviews and low-rating triage are invisible; recent review content is only visible if a review is unanswered-and-eligible.
validations.note: Verified legacy 4-stat grid + excerpts at src/components/google-business-profile/GbpAuthenticatedReviewsPanel.tsx:104-109 (Average w/ totalReviewCount sub, Stored, Stored unanswered, Stored low rating w/ newestReviewAt sub) and :136-150 (recentReviews.slice(0,5) w/ hasReply Replied/Unanswered badge), mounted flag-gated at src/components/local-seo/LocalPresencePage.tsx:220-222. Verified rebuilt shows only Stored/Unanswered/Average at src/components/local-presence-rebuilt/LocalPresenceReviewsPipeline.tsx:245-249; repo-wide grep for lowRatingCount|totalReviewCount|newestReviewAt|recentReviews|hasReply outside legacy dirs returns zero hits, while shared/types/google-business-profile.ts:263-270 still carries the fields (fetched-and-discarded via the same useGbpAuthenticatedReviews hook). Checked docs/ui-rebuild/parity/local-seo-contract.md:88 — carry-over protects connection/mapping status, review sync, per-location health, copy-policy only; documented exceptions (:52,:58) are geo-grid/GBP-Performance, unrelated — so the loss is genuinely undocumented. Refutation attempts that partially landed: (1) shared/types/feature-flags.ts:64,326-334 shows gbp-auth-reviews is default-OFF, rolloutTarget staging-validation, awaiting Google API approval — the readout has never been production-live; (2) LocalPresenceReviewsPipeline.tsx:560-570 shows unanswered eligible reviews as cards with rating+excerpt+Unanswered badge, and :377,:527,:535 keep platform-replied (published) responses visible with excerpts, so 'replied reviews are invisible' is only true for externally-replied reviews; (3) LocalPresenceVisibility.tsx:258-264 still shows a review count on Rank & profile (scraped aggregate). Loss is real and undocumented but affects a dark-launched feature's secondary rollup stats while the actionable triage path is preserved — minor, not major.
validations.correctedClaim: The rebuilt Authenticated review sync block drops legacy's low-rating triage count, authenticated total review count, newest-review date, and the 5-item Recent review excerpts list; none of these appear anywhere else in the rebuilt UI and none are covered by the contract's carry-over list or documented exceptions. However, the impact is narrower than 'major': the feature is dark-launched behind the default-OFF gbp-auth-reviews flag (staging-validation, awaiting Google API approval) in BOTH legacy and rebuilt, so no production operator loses it today; unanswered low-rated reviews remain individually visible with per-review star rating and excerpt in the response-pipeline cards; platform-replied responses stay visible (with excerpts) in the Published desk, so 'replied reviews are invisible' holds only for reviews replied outside the platform; and a profile review count survives on Rank & profile (scraped-aggregate provenance). The real loss is the count-level rollup (stored low-rating count, authenticated total, newest date) and visibility of externally-replied synced reviews.

validations.claim: Data-source mislabel: the Rank & profile 'Reviews' mini card is subtitled 'Authenticated profile aggregate', but its data comes from useGbpReviews → /api/local-seo/:id/gbp-reviews, which is the PUBLIC scraped listings aggregate (DataForSEO business_listings, local-gbp flag) — explicitly not the authenticated GBP connection. Legacy honestly labeled the same data 'GBP aggregate'. An operator (or a client on a screenshare) is told scraped data is authenticated first-party data — a trust landmine by the platform's own attribution-honesty standard.
validations.note: Every cited fact verified on origin/staging (5a2641b21). Subtitle "Authenticated profile aggregate" is verbatim at src/components/local-presence-rebuilt/LocalPresenceVisibility.tsx:258, fed by gbp?.owned (line 81) from useGbpReviews (LocalPresenceSurface.tsx:70,285) -> GET /api/local-seo/:id/gbp-reviews (src/api/localSeo.ts:92) -> server/routes/local-seo.ts:252-264, whose comment says "Aggregates ONLY" and which reads getLatestOwnedListing() from business_listing_snapshots (server/business-listings-store.ts, migration 154) populated by server/local-gbp.ts via getConfiguredProvider() — the paid DataForSEO scrape (fixture tests/fixtures/dataforseo-business-listings.ts), not the GBP OAuth connection. A genuinely distinct authenticated path exists (useGbpAuthenticatedReviews in LocalPresenceReviewsPipeline.tsx), so "Authenticated" is a meaningful, contrasting term here. Refutation attempts failed and surfaced extra corroboration: the same rebuilt surface's own copy at LocalPresenceReviewsPipeline.tsx:130 calls this exact data "Aggregate GBP benchmark data" that appears even when the "Authenticated GBP connection is not available" — the subtitle contradicts its sibling mode. Legacy honest label confirmed at src/components/local-seo/LocalPresencePage.tsx:86 ('GBP aggregate'). Not an approved exception: docs/ui-rebuild/parity/local-seo-contract.md:85 itself calls it the "GBP aggregate card" and lists the authenticated connection separately at line 88; repo-wide grep of docs/ finds zero occurrences of "Authenticated profile aggregate". Severity major is honest: the mislabel can mask a broken/absent authenticated connection (card shows scraped data labeled Authenticated while the Reviews mode reports the connection unavailable), and stale scraped counts presented as first-party data on a client screenshare is exactly the trust landmine the platform's attribution-honesty rule targets. Fix is a one-line subtitle change, but impact classification stands.

validations.claim: Empty bulk-result guard with actionable rate-limit guidance. Legacy explicitly detected a 200 bulk response with pages.length === 0 and surfaced 'No pages could be tested. The Google PageSpeed API may be rate-limited. Add a GOOGLE_PSI_KEY env variable for higher limits.' plus a Try Again reset; it also rendered any persisted empty snapshot as an honest 'No results available' empty state. The rebuilt lens has no pages-length check anywhere: a 200-empty result (which runSiteSpeed returns when PSI is rate-limited or no baseUrl resolves, and which the route persists unconditionally) is rendered as a real result.
validations.note: Verified every facet against origin/staging (working tree matches — `git diff --stat origin/staging` on all cited files is empty). (1) Legacy guard confirmed verbatim: src/components/PageSpeedPanel.tsx:185 checks `pages?.length === 0` on a fresh 200 bulk response and sets the exact error 'No pages could be tested. The Google PageSpeed API may be rate-limited. Add a GOOGLE_PSI_KEY env variable for higher limits.'; :432 (`if (error || !data || data.pages.length === 0)`) renders the persisted-empty snapshot as EmptyState 'No results available' (:443) with Try Again (:446). (2) Rebuilt has no pages-length check: grep of src/components/performance-rebuilt/PageSpeedLens.tsx finds zero `pages.length` guards; the bulk block at :692-703 renders ScoreSummary (MetricRing averageScore=0 + all-'Unavailable' VitalsGrid, :272-288, :202) + DataTable for any truthy currentBulkResult, and :421 falls back to the persisted snapshot so a saved empty result renders identically after reload. (3) PSI-hint helper confirmed error-only: performanceFormatters.ts:121-123 is consumed only at PageSpeedLens.tsx:637 and :659, both gated on mutation error state (hardError :553); a 200-empty resolves as success (useAdminPerformance.ts:190 asSiteSpeedResult is a bare cast, :121) and even caches the empty result as the snapshot (:193-198). (4) Server 200-empty sources confirmed: server/pagespeed.ts:312-313 returns pages:[]/averageScore:0 on missing baseUrl; runPageSpeed returns null on any !res.ok incl. 429 (:52-58) so all-failed PSI yields pages:[] (:345,:365,:381-383); route persists and 200s unconditionally (server/routes/webflow-pagespeed.ts:40-42). Refutation attempts failed: no pages-length/rate-limit/GOOGLE_PSI handling exists anywhere else in performance-rebuilt/ or useAdminPerformance.ts (grep), and no parity doc approves this — the five DEF-performance-* ledger entries cover unrelated items, while docs/ui-rebuild/parity/performance-contract.md:91 ('No PageSpeed data or row-level fixes are fabricated') and :136 ('not permission to fabricate a result') actively contradict the current behavior, strengthening the claim. One minor nuance that does not change the verdict: DataTable's empty prop (PageSpeedLens.tsx:700) shows a generic 'No PageSpeed pages returned' EmptyState when rows are empty, so the operator gets a bare zero-rows cue — but with no rate-limit explanation, no GOOGLE_PSI_KEY remediation, no reset, beneath a fabricated red 0-average ring and after a success toast; the claim's evidence already cited this line range. Severity 'major' is honest for a solo operator: without a PSI key the free quota is tiny, so rate-limited empty runs are a realistic recurring state, and the rebuilt persists a misleading red-zero snapshot until a later successful run.

validations.claim: A failed/rate-limited bulk PageSpeed run is reported as SUCCESS and rendered as fabricated data: the mutation's onSuccess fires a '{Mobile|Desktop} PageSpeed bulk test complete' success toast, writes the empty SiteSpeedResult into the strategy snapshot cache, and the lens renders a red MetricRing score of 0 labelled '{Strategy} average' with a 'Lab test' badge and six 'Unavailable' vitals above an empty table. Because the server also persists the empty snapshot, this red-zero average survives reloads until a later run succeeds. Legacy treated the identical payload as an explicit error with the GOOGLE_PSI_KEY remediation hint. This is the FM-2 failure mode (external API error recorded as success) and directly contradicts the surface's own no-fabrication contract.
validations.note: Verified every link in the chain on origin/staging (files identical to HEAD). Server: server/pagespeed.ts:52-58 returns null per page on any non-OK PSI response (incl. 429) and runSiteSpeed returns {pages:[], averageScore:0, averageVitals:all-null} when all calls fail (:381-401) or baseUrl missing (:312-314); server/routes/webflow-pagespeed.ts:39-42 persists it (savePageSpeed, performance-store.ts:186 — no guard) and 200s. Client: useAdminPerformance.ts:121-123 bare-cast mapper, :192-198 onSuccess caches the empty result as the strategy snapshot; PageSpeedLens.tsx:445 fires the unconditional 'bulk test complete' success toast; :692-702 renders ScoreSummary for any truthy result with zero pages.length checks in the file (grep confirms). ScoreSummary (:263-291) shows MetricRing(0) — red per MetricRing.tsx:17-22 — labelled '{Strategy} average' (:694), amber 'Lab test' badge (:178-182, fieldDataAvailable undefined), and exactly six 'Unavailable' vitals (VITALS :101-112, VitalTile :195-202). PSI-key hint (performanceFormatters.ts:121-123) fires only on isError branches (:553,:637,:659). Snapshot fallback :421 makes the red-zero persist across reloads whenever Bulk is viewed. Legacy comparator accurate: PageSpeedPanel.tsx:185 (pages.length===0 → GOOGLE_PSI_KEY error) and :432 ('No results available'). Refutation attempts failed: no guard at any layer; performance-contract.md:91 ('No PageSpeed data...fabricated') and :136 strengthen the claim; parity-backlog item 19 is layout-only; deferred ledger DEF-performance-001/002 are unrelated. Minor nuances that do not weaken it: the empty DataTable shows a 'No PageSpeed pages returned' EmptyState (:700, claim already says 'above an empty table'), and mode defaults to 'single' on mount (:408) so the persisted red-zero appears on opening Bulk, not instantly. Textbook FM-2 (external API failure reported as success) violating the repo's own test conventions and generation-runtime-integrity contract; major severity is honest.

validations.claim: Editing ALL extra SEO fields on a CMS item. Legacy renders an input (with per-field AI rewrite) for every field in getExtraSeoFields(coll.seoFields) — e.g. OG title, search snippet, any additional SEO text field a collection defines. Rebuilt edits only the first title-like and first desc-like field (titleFieldSlug/descriptionFieldSlug from the resolver); other configured SEO fields are neither visible nor editable anywhere.
validations.note: Verified all cited lines. Legacy renders an editor + per-field AI rewrite for EVERY extra SEO field (src/components/cms-editor/CmsEditorCollections.tsx:285-346, extraSeoFields.map with aiRewrite(coll.collectionId, item.id, field.slug)), and the server deliberately detects OG title/OG description/open-graph fields (server/routes/webflow-cms.ts:118 SEO_FIELD_PATTERNS, :160-168), so >2 extra fields is a designed-for case. The rebuilt surface edits only the first title-like and first desc-like slug: resolver src/components/editor/seoWriteTargetResolver.ts:77-102 (via cmsEditorModel.ts:77-81 getTitleAndDescriptionFields), drawer SeoEditorPagePanel.tsx:187-190,465-489 (name + titleField + descField only), worksheet SeoEditorWorksheet.tsx:116-127,348-424 (titleFieldSlug/descriptionFieldSlug only). Capability did not move: grep of seo-editor-rebuilt/ + useSeoEditorRebuilt.ts finds no extra-field rendering, and rebuiltSurfaces.ts:53-55 + App.tsx:490-493 replace the legacy SeoEditorWrapper→CmsEditor entirely at flag-ON (no other CmsEditor mount). Not an approved exception: seo-editor-contract.md:111 requires "CMS item field edit" as a Carry-Over Home; the only documented exceptions are the keyboard review queue and H1/slug read-only (contract lines 80, 194); no parity doc mentions OG/extra fields — the Phase 0 inventory (docs/ui-rebuild/phase0/surfaces/seo-editor.md row D2) under-described legacy as "Name + detected SEO title/description fields" and marked it preserved, so the loss was never surfaced for decision. Mitigations that temper but don't refute: (a) loss only bites collections defining fields beyond one title + one desc; (b) hidden fields are not clobbered on save (buildInitialEdits seeds all slugs, useCmsEditorSaveWorkflow.ts:30-44 PATCHes full fieldData); (c) legacy bulk "All SEO fields" mode also only covered name/title/desc (useCmsEditorPublishBulkWorkflow.ts:119-138), so the loss is the inline per-field edit + per-field AI rewrite. For an SEO agency platform where OG metadata is part of the deliverable and the fields become silently invisible (no read-only display, no banner), major is honest.

validations.claim: Per-item CMS approval history. Legacy shows, inside each expanded CMS item, the latest approval batch (name, status tone, current→proposed value diff, timestamp) plus an expandable history of all prior approval items for that item (buildItemApprovalMap over cmsData.approvalBatches). Rebuilt never consumes approvalBatches; the drawer has no history, so an operator cannot see what was already sent/approved/rejected for an item before re-sending.
validations.note: Verified on origin/staging (working tree identical for all cited paths; git diff empty). Legacy confirmed: src/components/CmsEditor.tsx:40,94-96; src/components/cms-editor/cmsEditorModel.ts:104-118; src/components/cms-editor/CmsEditorCollections.tsx:412-467 (latest batch + diff + expandable history). Rebuilt confirmed absent: grep approvalBatches|buildItemApprovalMap|itemApprovalMap across src/components/seo-editor-rebuilt/ + src/hooks/admin/useSeoEditorRebuilt.ts = 0 hits; useSeoEditorRebuilt.ts:99-101 consumes only collections from cmsQuery; drawer CMS branch SeoEditorPagePanel.tsx:434-544 has no history. Refuting evidence found: SeoEditorSurface.tsx:447 mounts SeoEditorApprovalsPanel -> PendingApprovals (nameFilter='SEO', matches CMS batch names from useCmsEditorApprovalWorkflow.ts:74); server/approvals.ts:131-134 listBatches returns all statuses (no filter; serializer client-safe.ts:146-148 keeps status/items); PendingApprovals.tsx:89-91,117-119 shows approved/rejected/pending counts and :177-186 shows per-item StatusBadge + pageTitle + field when expanded — so item-level sent/approved/rejected status IS visible on the rebuilt surface, contradicting the claim's absolute final sentence. But no value diff: grep proposedValue across src/components (non-client) hits only payload-builders + legacy cms-editor, so proposed/client values of sent items render nowhere in the rebuilt admin. Note legacy mounted the same PendingApprovals too (CmsEditorShellPanels.tsx:149-150), so the panel is a partial substitute, not a moved home. Docs: seo-editor-contract.md:106-125 (Carry-Over Homes lists 'approval selection'+'PendingApprovals', per-item history unmentioned; not in Moved/Excluded/Deferred); zero hits for 'approval history'/'per-item' in parity-backlog.md, owner-decision-packet.md, rebuilt-surface-inventory.md. Flag-ON routing: App.tsx:436 (legacy SeoEditorWrapper) vs rebuiltSurfaces.ts:53-54 (SeoEditorSurface).
validations.correctedClaim: Per-item CMS approval history with value diffs is lost, but sent/approved/rejected STATUS per item is not. Legacy rendered, inside each expanded CMS item, the latest approval batch (name, status tone, currentValue -> proposedValue/clientValue diff, timestamp) plus expandable prior-item history (CmsEditorCollections.tsx:412-467 via buildItemApprovalMap). The rebuilt surface fetches approvalBatches (useCmsEditor.ts:70) but never renders them, and the drawer has no history section (SeoEditorPagePanel.tsx:434-544). However, the rebuilt surface's own SeoEditorApprovalsPanel (SeoEditorSurface.tsx:447 -> PendingApprovals, nameFilter='SEO' which matches 'CMS SEO Changes' batches) lists ALL batches regardless of status (server listBatches has no status filter) with per-batch approved/rejected/pending counts and, when expanded, per-item status + pageTitle + field. What is genuinely gone: (1) per-item indexing at the point of editing (operator must expand and scan every batch), and (2) any admin readback of the proposed/client-edited VALUES of already-sent items — mainly hurting rejection recall ('what title did the client reject?'). Not documented as an owner exception (seo-editor-contract.md Carry-Over Homes lists 'approval selection' and 'PendingApprovals' only). For a solo operator with a same-surface status substitute, this is a real parity gap worth backfilling but not a major workflow break.

validations.claim: Single-field AI rewrite. Legacy: static rows have 'AI' buttons for title-only and description-only (plus 'AI Generate Both'); CMS items have a per-field Wand2 AI rewrite on name and every extra SEO field. Rebuilt exposes only the paired 'Generate pair' (both) per row — staticWorkflow.aiRewrite(field) and cmsWorkflow.aiRewrite(single field) exist in the workflow state but have zero UI callsites for single fields.
validations.note: Verified legacy per-field buttons at src/components/editor/PageEditRow.tsx:281 ('title'), :330 ('description'), :222 ('both') and src/components/cms-editor/CmsEditorCollections.tsx:237 (name wand), :296 (per-extra-field wand). Verified rebuilt static callsites: only 'both' at src/components/seo-editor-rebuilt/SeoEditorPagePanel.tsx:364 and SeoEditorWorksheet.tsx:489; SeoEditorSurface.tsx:373/384 'Fix titles/descriptions' gate on missing-only (useSeoEditorBulkWorkflow.ts:208-214). REFUTED the CMS half: SeoEditorWorksheet.tsx:510-513 'Rewrite CMS' menu (Names/Titles/Descriptions) → useCmsEditorPublishBulkWorkflow.ts:130-137 calls single-field aiRewrite(collectionId,itemId,slug) per selected item (works with one row selected); variations applied per-field via CmsVariationList/applySingleVariation (SeoEditorPagePanel.tsx:492-502,133). Parity docs: seo-editor-contract.md:110-111 carries 'AI rewrite' generically; :119-125 lists no single-field exception; owner-decision-packet.md:173 ODP-002-V6 'per-field AI assists' is content-pipeline scoped, not SEO editor — so the static gap is undocumented, but it is a one-surface ergonomic gap with an existing workflow function (seoEditorSurfaceTypes.ts:56), not a major cross-surface functionality loss.
validations.correctedClaim: Static single-field AI rewrite (title-only / description-only on an existing value) has no UI path in the rebuilt SEO editor — only 'Generate pair' (both) at SeoEditorPagePanel.tsx:364 and bulk 'Rewrite static' (both) at SeoEditorWorksheet.tsx:489; 'Fix titles'/'Fix descriptions' only fill missing fields. However, the CMS half of the claim is wrong: per-field CMS rewrite moved to the selected-row toolbar's 'Rewrite CMS' menu (Names/Titles/Descriptions, SeoEditorWorksheet.tsx:510-513), which calls single-field cmsWorkflow.aiRewrite per item (useCmsEditorPublishBulkWorkflow.ts:130-137), works on a single selected row, and single-field variations apply in the panel via CmsVariationList/applySingleVariation. Remaining CMS narrowing is only that arbitrary extra SEO field slugs beyond the first title/desc match are no longer individually targetable. Net loss: static per-field rewrite ergonomics (workflow function already exists, trivial wire-up) — not a surface-wide loss of single-field AI rewrite.

validations.claim: Full pattern/bulk-preview diff review. Legacy 'Preview Changes' panel lists EVERY affected page with old value (struck through) → new value + length, scrollable, with Apply All and an explicit Cancel that clears the preview. Rebuilt shows an InlineBanner with only the first 3 changes compressed to 'old -> new' text and no cancel/clear control before 'Apply preview' writes to live Webflow pages.
validations.note: Every cited fact verified. Legacy src/components/editor/BulkOperations.tsx:163-209 renders a full "Preview Changes — N pages" panel: every preview item with page slug/title (line 198), struck-through old value (line 200), new value + char length (line 202), scrollable list (line 193), Apply All (169-177), and Cancel (178-186) wired to onClearPreview which clears preview+mode (src/components/SeoEditor.tsx:315). Rebuilt src/components/seo-editor-rebuilt/SeoEditorSurface.tsx:516-520 renders only an InlineBanner with bulkPreview.slice(0,3) compressed to "old -> new" text, no onDismiss, no page identity/lengths; "Apply preview" (503-513) calls applyPattern which POSTs /api/webflow/seo-pattern-apply/:siteId (useSeoEditorBulkWorkflow.ts:262-270), a live Webflow write via updatePageSeo (server/routes/webflow-seo-apply.ts:40,88). Refutation search: no cancel/clear for the preview anywhere in the rebuilt tree — only cancelAnalyze (Surface:470) and clear-filters (Worksheet:448); setBulkPreview/setBulkMode/cancelRewrite/applyBulkRewrite exist in seoEditorSurfaceTypes.ts:79-91 but are unwired in any rebuilt component; SeoSuggestionsPanel (Surface:437) covers only the AI-rewrite suggestion flow, not pattern preview. Not an approved exception: docs/ui-rebuild/parity/seo-editor-contract.md:113 lists "pattern preview/apply" as a Carry-Over Home; the only named workflow exception is the keyboard review queue (line 194). Severity major is fair for a solo operator: per-page preview shows title-truncation effects (legacy shows resulting value+length per page) before a bulk live Webflow write, and once previewed the only escape short of applying is navigating away to unmount state. Minor tempering context (doesn't change verdict): the pattern transform is deterministic user-typed append/prepend and pages come from the operator's own selection, so the first-3 sample partially represents the rest.

validations.claim: Missing-metadata banner counts include CMS rows but its 'Fix titles'/'Fix descriptions' actions only draft STATIC pages, so the displayed count and the action's scope disagree (legacy header badges counted writable static pages only). An operator reading '23 missing titles … Draft them in one pass' gets fixes for only the static subset with no explanation.
validations.note: Verified on origin/staging (5a2641b21; working tree matches for all cited files). Counts: SeoEditorSurface.tsx:149-160 — editableRows excludes only 'manual', so needs-title/needs-meta counts include CMS rows (CMS missing flags computed at :127-137); banner at :354-391 shows those counts with 'Draft them in one pass' and calls bulkWorkflow.handleBulkFix. Action scope: useSeoEditorRebuilt.ts:288-302 passes pages: staticPages to useSeoEditorBulkWorkflow; handleBulkFix (useSeoEditorBulkWorkflow.ts:208-247) calls filterPagesNeedingFix which excludes CMS again (seoEditorFilters.ts:71-78). Legacy comparator confirmed: SeoEditor.tsx:78+:222 fed writablePages (static-only, seoEditorFilters.ts:29-30) to both the count (useSeoEditorBulkWorkflow.ts:416-417 countMissingField, CMS-excluding) and the action, rendered in SeoEditorHeaderActions.tsx:50-59 — legacy count and action shared one scope. Refutation attempts failed: the 'Rewrite CMS' bulk path (SeoEditorWorksheet.tsx:501-514) requires manual selection and is mutually exclusive with the banner (banner renders only when selectedCount===0, SeoEditorSurface.tsx:354), so it does not close the gap; the transient 5s 'Started background fixes for N pages' message (useSeoEditorBulkWorkflow.ts:236, rendered SeoEditorWorksheet.tsx:454-456) reveals the true static N but doesn't explain the discrepancy, and in the all-CMS-missing case the feedback reads 'All pages already have SEO titles.' (:210-213) while the banner above still claims N missing — a direct self-contradiction. Parity docs: seo-editor-contract.md:113 makes the static-only bulk fix contractual and :26 promises the banner 'can draft title/meta fixes in one pass', but no doc (contract, parity-backlog.md, owner-decision-packet.md) records the CMS-inclusive count against the static-only action as an approved exception. Severity major is fair: a primary CTA whose displayed count overstates its scope violates the repo's own count/denominator-source rule, and a solo operator can reasonably believe CMS metadata was drafted when it was not.

validations.claim: CMS 'Send to client' from the selected-rows toolbar fails silently. useCmsEditorApprovalWorkflow reports failures via approvalError (validation 'No changes detected…' or network), but the rebuilt surface renders approvalError ONLY inside the CMS drawer's Collection target box — when sending from the toolbar with no drawer open, the button just stops spinning, the selection stays, and nothing tells the operator the batch was not sent. Legacy rendered an ErrorState next to the send button.
validations.note: Verified all four evidence cites on origin/staging (5a2641b21). (1) useCmsEditorApprovalWorkflow.ts:62-91 sets approvalError for validation ('No changes detected on selected items…') and network failures, auto-clears it after 5s (lines 49-51), clears selection only on success (line 81), and has no toast hookup. (2) Repo-wide grep confirms the ONLY rebuilt render site is SeoEditorPagePanel.tsx:525-529, inside the Drawer's cmsItem branch (line 434) 'Collection target' box; with no row open the panel returns <Drawer open={false}/> with no children (lines 276-278), so nothing renders. (3) The worksheet toolbar send (SeoEditorWorksheet.tsx:497-500) passes only loading={sendingApproval} — no error/success feedback anywhere in the file; the validation case is trivially reachable (checkbox-select rows at line 112 without edits → empty payload → silent early return). (4) Legacy CmsEditorShellPanels.tsx:134-140 renders an ErrorState in the same block directly below the Send button (lines 110-121). Refutation attempts failed: no toast wrapper exists (api/client.ts has none; useSeoEditorRebuilt.ts wires toast ONLY for the STATIC approval workflow at line 281 — src/components/editor/useSeoEditorApprovalWorkflow.ts:89,106,123 — making the CMS gap an asymmetric omission, which strengthens the claim); no DEF-seo-editor-001..007 ledger entry or seo-editor-contract.md exception covers approval-error feedback. Severity major is fair: silent failure of a client-facing send on the primary bulk path, with only the non-cleared selection as an implicit cue.

validations.claim: Per-page-instance repair actions on every affected page. Legacy rendered an action row per page-issue: Apply Now (accept AI suggestion), edit suggestion, Fix→ handoff, Send to Client, Add to Tasks — each targeting THAT page. The rebuilt drawer wires accept/task/flag/open-fix only to the FIRST page instance of a group (`firstPageInstance`); the other listed instances get only Hide / Hide pattern. For a group like 'Missing meta description (14 pages)' you can selectively fix page 1 only; pages 2–14 are reachable solely through indiscriminate bulk actions (Accept all / Add all tasks).
validations.note: Verified both sides. Legacy: SeoAudit.tsx:776-810 renders AuditIssueRow per page-issue; AuditIssueRow.tsx:96-147 (Apply Now + editable suggestion), :186-199 (Fix→ per page), :222-260/:300-336 (Send to Client / Add to Tasks / Suppress per page) — all confirmed. Rebuilt: SiteAuditSurface.tsx:415-419 firstPageInstance, :432-447 footer actions bound to firstPage, :461-515 suggestion edit + Accept + Send bound to firstPage, :521-551 instance rows Hide/Hide-pattern only, :555-558 ">12 instances included in batch actions" — all confirmed. Searched for the capability elsewhere: acceptSuggestion/flagForClient/createTaskFromIssue/openQuickFix consumed only by legacy SeoAudit, the rebuilt surface, and useSiteAuditRebuilt.ts (grep across src/) — no page-intelligence-rebuilt home. Weakening facts: batchCreateTasks('filtered') is filter-selective (useSiteAuditRebuilt.ts:403-411); hook returns suppression-filtered effectiveData (:280-282) so Hide-then-Accept-all is a technically possible (if abusive) selective-accept path; per-page manual title/meta repair exists in seo-editor-rebuilt/SeoEditorPagePanel.tsx:380-395. Docs: docs/ui-rebuild/parity/site-audit-contract.md (owner-approved 2026-07-10) covers lens demotion + visual composition only; line 50 claims issue-detail "capabilities are unchanged" and line 91 lists drawer carry-over as "affected pages and suppress/restore" — the per-page action reduction is NOT documented as an approved exception, so "undocumented" holds. Severity: degraded-with-workarounds, not impossible → major, not blocker.
validations.correctedClaim: The rebuilt issue drawer binds accept/edit-suggestion/task/send-to-client/open-fix to only the FIRST page instance of a group (SiteAuditSurface.tsx:415-419, 432-447, 461-515); remaining instances get only Hide/Hide-pattern (:542-551), and suggestions for instances 2+ cannot even be viewed before "Accept all" pushes them live. This is a real, undocumented parity loss — the parity contract even claims issue-detail capabilities are "unchanged" — but it is major, not blocker: single-page groups retain full parity, batch task creation is filter-selective ("Add visible tasks" respects severity/category/search, not purely indiscriminate), and the underlying title/meta repair remains available per-page in the rebuilt SEO Editor (SeoEditorPagePanel.tsx:380-395), albeit without audit context or AI suggestions. Only per-instance send-to-client and individual task creation for pages 2+ are fully unreachable.

validations.claim: Page-level inventory and per-page browsing: legacy listed every crawled page with its own score, 28-day traffic badge (clicks/views with impressions tooltip), edit-state status border + badge, noindex explanation banner, and an expandable per-page issue list. The rebuilt surface is issue-group-only; the drawer caps affected pages at 12 with 'N more affected pages are included in batch actions' and shows no per-page score anywhere.
validations.note: Verified legacy src/components/SeoAudit.tsx:723-817 (per-page score :761, traffic badge :753-757, StatusBadge :752, noindex banner :767-772) and rebuilt src/components/site-audit-rebuilt/SiteAuditSurface.tsx:521 (slice(0,12)), :555-558 (overflow), :529-536 (noindex + edit-state badges DO survive per instance), :563-572 (edit-state summary), :767-781+:931 (bulk actions cover all instances). No per-page audit score in rebuilt (hook computes it internally at useSiteAuditRebuilt.ts:119-129 for category aggregation only). Refuting evidence: docs/ui-rebuild/parity/site-audit-contract.md:5 (Status owner-approved 2026-07-10), :17-27 + :82-97 (issue-first interaction model; page inventory not a carry-over home), and prototype 'hmpsn studio Design System/mockup/audit.js':436-455 (issue-first, no page list, no per-page score, but renders ALL affected pages with per-page traffic + actions — the 12-cap and missing per-page traffic are the real residual parity gaps). Page Intelligence rebuilt (PageIntelligenceSurface.tsx:293-318) browses pages with a different metric (content optimizationScore), not a moved home. Legacy SeoAudit remains the production surface (App.tsx:435; ui-rebuild-shell defaults false), so nothing is lost in prod today.
validations.correctedClaim: The page-first inventory (per-page score, traffic badge, status border, noindex banner, expandable issue list) was deliberately replaced by an owner-approved issue-first model: docs/ui-rebuild/parity/site-audit-contract.md is owner-approved (2026-07-10), its interaction model and Carry-Over Homes intentionally omit a page inventory, and the prototype (mockup/audit.js) likewise has no page list or per-page audit score — so most of this is ratified redesign, not undocumented loss. Elements survive per affected page in the drawer: noindex badge, edit-state badge, edit-state summary, and bulk actions do cover pages beyond the 12-row cap.
