The genuine residual gap (vs both legacy AND the approved prototype) is narrower: the drawer caps visible affected pages at 12 with no per-page traffic badges or per-page actions beyond Hide/Hide-pattern (the prototype renders all affected pages with traffic and per-page fix/send/task actions), and there is no way to look up an arbitrary page's issues from the audit surface.

validations.claim: Working route from the surface to the competitor-set editor. Rebuilt 'Edit set' button and BOTH setup empty-state CTAs navigate to workspace-settings (src/components/competitors-rebuilt/CompetitorsSurface.tsx:191,212,231,245), but WorkspaceSettings has no competitor-domain editor and no SEO-provider config (src/components/WorkspaceSettings.tsx:74-76 SectionTab union = connections/features/flags/dashboard/publishing/export/llms-txt; zero competitor references anywhere in src/components/settings/). The actual editor (textarea + auto-discover) lives in the Strategy page settings panel (src/components/strategy/StrategySettings.tsx:114). Legacy had no button but its empty-state copy pointed to the correct home: 'Enter competitors in Strategy Settings → Competitor Domains' (src/components/strategy/StrategyCompetitiveTab.tsx:54). A new operator following the rebuilt setup flow lands on a page that cannot do what the CTA promised.
validations.note: Verified on origin/staging (78239b4). CONFIRMED facts: CompetitorsSurface.tsx:191,212,231,245 all navigate to adminPath(workspaceId,'workspace-settings'); WorkspaceSettings.tsx:74-83 SectionTab union has no competitor section; only competitor hit in src/components/settings/ is TargetGeoEditor.tsx doc comments; rebuilt destination global-ops-rebuilt/WorkspaceSettingsLens.tsx:4 wraps the same legacy WorkspaceSettings, so the dead-end exists under both shells; StrategySettings.tsx:114-141 holds the real editor; StrategyCompetitiveTab.tsx:54 legacy copy pointed to Strategy Settings. REFUTED framing: capability is not lost — rebuiltSurfaces.ts:66-68 mounts EngineSurface for seo-strategy, EngineSurface.tsx:1006 renders EngineOperations, and EngineOperations.tsx:105-113,204 passes competitors/setCompetitors/discoverCompetitors into StrategyConfigPanel → StrategySettings, so the editor is reachable in the rebuilt shell. Documentation check: competitors-contract.md:21,31,74,82 owner-approved the Workspace Settings routing (2026-07-10) and forbids inline editing; global-ops-contract.md:92 lists competitor-set editing in Workspace Settings as a required carry-over home that was never implemented (GO-006 defers final IA until moved capabilities have homes); not flagged in post-parity-functionality-wiring-ai-audit.md. validations.correctedClaim: Rebuilt Competitors 'Edit set' button and both setup CTAs route to Workspace Settings, which has no competitor-domain editor or SEO-provider config — a misdirecting CTA, not lost functionality. The competitor-set editor remains fully reachable in the rebuilt shell via Insights Engine (seo-strategy) → Operations → StrategyConfigPanel/StrategySettings (textarea + Auto-Discover), and in the legacy Strategy page flag-OFF. The routing itself is owner-approved (competitors-contract.md: 'Workspace Settings remains the edit home', approved 2026-07-10) but the contracted destination section (global-ops-contract.md:92 carry-over: 'Strategy inputs and competitor set editing in Workspace Settings') was never built — a documented-but-unfulfilled contract seam. Fix is to retarget the CTAs or land the contracted Workspace Settings section; for a solo operator who knows the editor's home, this is a minor navigation/copy defect.

validations.claim: Pre-scan homes for the Schema site plan (generate/roles/canonical entities/activate/send/retract), CMS field mapping, and business-profile callout. Legacy rendered all three in the not-started state so a fresh workspace could do plan-first setup before spending on generation; the rebuilt renders them only inside the pages.length > 0 results block, so on a fresh workspace they are unreachable until after a (paid) generation run — inverting the plan-first ordering the rebuilt's own guide instructs ('Generate the site plan first').
validations.note: Verified every cited line. Legacy SchemaSuggester.tsx:293-350 !started branch renders SchemaPlanPanel (:312), SchemaBusinessProfileCallout (:313), SchemaCmsFieldMappingPanel (:320) pre-scan. Rebuilt GeneratorLens.tsx:324-358 !started branch renders only SchemaGeneratorEmptySetup (pure "Generate all pages" CTA, SchemaSupportPanels.tsx:374-392) + page inventory; SchemaSitePlanBridge/SchemaBusinessProfilePanel/SchemaCmsMappingPanel mount only inside pages.length>0 (GeneratorLens.tsx:369, 526-541). started only flips true when a saved snapshot has results (useSchemaSuggesterGeneration.ts:60-63), so a fresh workspace cannot reach plan/CMS-mapping/BP panels without a paid generation run. Capability-moved search: repo-wide grep confirms SchemaPlanPanel's only rebuilt mount is SchemaSupportPanels.tsx:345 inside SchemaSitePlanBridge; SchemaSurface.tsx has only generator+guide lenses; no other rebuilt home. Guide contradiction real: WorkflowGuideLens.tsx:11 'Generate the site plan first', and :44-46 lists 'Active schema-plan role authority' + 'CMS field evidence' as generation inputs, so scan-before-plan degrades the first paid run. Not an approved exception: docs/ui-rebuild/parity/schema-contract.md:71-78 requires site plan (roles/canonical entities/activation/send/retraction) and CMS mapped-field publish to stay reachable; the only documented exception is the detail Drawer (:50-52); parity-backlog.md and owner-decision-packet.md contain no pre-scan exception. Severity major is fair for a solo operator: hits every new-client onboarding (the exact moment plan-first matters), violates the contract's carry-over requirement in that state, and contradicts the surface's own guide. Mitigations (single-page generation unlocks the panels; ui-rebuild-shell defaults false at shared/types/feature-flags.ts:129 so legacy is still the flag-off default) keep it below blocker but do not weaken the claim as stated.

validations.claim: List-level client-review/edit lifecycle status: legacy showed a per-row StatusBadge (live/in-review/approved/rejected/issue-detected/fix-proposed) on every collapsed page card plus the SchemaEditStatusSummary aggregate rollup. The rebuilt table has no edit-state column and the rollup is gone entirely — publishing.summary is never consumed; status is visible only one page at a time inside the drawer.
validations.note: Verified all cited evidence on origin/staging (schema files identical to working tree): legacy per-row badge at src/components/schema/SchemaPageCard.tsx:120 (editState from usePageEditStates, statuses live/in-review/approved/rejected/issue-detected/fix-proposed per src/hooks/usePageEditStates.ts:7-30); aggregate rollup at src/components/schema/SchemaResultsSummary.tsx:84-100 rendered by SchemaSuggester.tsx:556. Rebuilt SchemaPageTable.tsx:67-128 has only Page/Type/Validation/Publish columns (validation/publish badges per schemaFormatters.ts:82-107 are different vocabularies from edit lifecycle). useSchemaSuggesterPublishingWorkflow.ts:373-374 exports getState+summary; grep of src/components/schema-rebuilt/ shows only getState consumed (GeneratorLens.tsx:604 → SchemaPageDrawer.tsx:190 StatusBadge); publishing.summary and all lifecycle status strings have zero hits — rollup confirmed gone. Refutation search: no alternate home found in schema-rebuilt, layout/, or drawers beyond the single-page drawer. Docs: schema-contract.md:5,39,52 + parity-backlog.md item 17 owner-approve the Drawer exception (covers per-page status placement) but Carry-Over Homes (schema-contract.md:69-84) omit list-level edit status, no DEF-schema-* ledger entry covers it, and phase-a docs required carry (docs/ui-rebuild/phase-a/surfaces/schema.json Row 19 'restore dropped badges', Row 61 'edit-status breakdown stays client-side'; phase-a/tickets/schema.md:48 zero-drop StatusBadge). Mitigations: rebuilt retains PendingApprovals 'Client approval queue' (GeneratorLens.tsx:542-560; PendingApprovals.tsx:89-119 shows pending/approved/rejected per batch item). Facts accurate; severity overstated — visibility/scanability regression, not lost capability.
validations.correctedClaim: The rebuilt schema table drops the legacy per-row client-review lifecycle StatusBadge and the SchemaEditStatusSummary aggregate rollup (publishing.summary is exported but never consumed in schema-rebuilt), against phase-a zero-drop carry notes (schema.json Rows 19/61) and with no deferred-ledger entry — a real undocumented scanability regression. However, per-page lifecycle status remains reachable via the drawer (SchemaPageDrawer.tsx:190), which falls under the owner-approved Drawer exception (schema-contract.md, parity-backlog item 17, approved 2026-07-10), and the rebuilt Client approval queue panel (GeneratorLens.tsx:542-560 → PendingApprovals) still shows batch-level pending/approved/rejected counts. No action capability is lost; this is degraded at-a-glance visibility, minor for a solo operator.

validations.claim: 'Add a page' failures are completely silent. The shared generation hook catches the all-pages fetch error into fetchPagesError, but the rebuilt never renders it — no banner, no toast. If the Webflow pages read fails (expired token, API error), the operator clicks 'Add a page', sees a brief spinner, and nothing happens, with zero feedback. Legacy showed an inline error banner next to the button.
validations.note: Verified on origin/staging. (1) Hook: src/components/schema/useSchemaSuggesterGeneration.ts:166-181 — fetchPages() catches the /api/webflow/all-pages failure into setFetchPagesError (line 178) and never opens the picker on failure; the mount-time prefetch (lines 102-114) is deliberately silent with comment 'fetchPages surfaces errors on demand', so the hook contract expects the consumer to render this error. (2) Legacy renders it: src/components/SchemaSuggester.tsx:510-517 (InlineBanner next to Add Page button). (3) Rebuilt: git grep fetchPagesError in src/components/schema-rebuilt/ on origin/staging = zero hits; GeneratorLens.tsx:390-392 renders the button with only loading={generation.loadingPages} spinner, calls generation.fetchPages directly (no toast/banner wrapper); SchemaPagePickerDrawer opens only on generation.showPagePicker (GeneratorLens.tsx:578-579), which stays false on failure. The rebuilt handles all sibling errors (scanError→ErrorState 277-282, singlePageError→banner 503-510, sendToClientError 491-499, pageTypeErrors→toast 196), confirming this is a specific omission, not a design choice. (4) Not an owner-approved exception: docs/ui-rebuild/parity/schema-contract.md and parity-backlog.md contain no mention of fetchPagesError or Add-a-page error handling. Severity major is honest: expired Webflow token is a realistic recurring state, and the result is a completely silent dead-end (prefetch fails silently by design, on-demand click fails silently by omission) on a primary action, violating the platform's mandatory error-handling rule — though admin-facing and non-destructive, so not a blocker.

validations.claim: AI Visibility (LLM-mention) KPI panel — share-of-voice vs co-mentioned competitors, mention trend, source domains, and the 'Refresh AI visibility' trigger. The feature's flag was retired globally-ON (feature-flags.ts:70-75), so this is live production functionality whose ONLY mount is the legacy KeywordHub. With ui-rebuild-shell ON there is no home anywhere in the rebuilt shell (no ai-visibility Page exists; the prototype nav names 'AI Visibility' but nothing renders it).
validations.note: Every element checks out. (1) Only mount: KeywordHub.tsx:33,660 mounts AiVisibilityPanel (strategy/AiVisibilityPanel.tsx:88); useAiVisibility has no other consumers (hooks/admin/useAiVisibility.ts:15,32). (2) Live prod: feature-flags.ts:70-75 comment confirms flag retired globally-ON, UI now unconditional. (3) Shell-ON loss: rebuiltSurfaces.ts:20-22 replaces seo-keywords with KeywordsSurface (App.tsx:490-518), which imports nothing AI-visibility related; grep across all *-rebuilt dirs + layout/ finds zero refs (competitors-rebuilt/ShareOfVoice.tsx and local-presence-rebuilt/LocalPresenceVisibility.tsx are different features). No 'ai-visibility' in routes.ts Page union; RebuiltSidebar.tsx:120-125 Search & Site Health group omits it while the prototype nav names it (coverage-audit.md:55). (4) Not an approved exception: zero AI Visibility rows in parity-backlog.md, rebuilt-surface-inventory.md, shipping-manifest.md, registry-final-owner-review.md; keywords-contract.md Carry-Over Homes (89-105) omits it. Worse, pilot dod-walk keywords-dod-walk.md:52 claims 'Preserved elsewhere' via Brand & AI — but BrandAiSurface.tsx:840 explicitly punts it to Search & Site Health, where nothing renders it; the documented preservation is circular and false. Mitigating context (insufficient to downgrade): a full W3 build ticket exists (phase-a/tickets/ai-visibility.md, PR #1503) planning the consolidated Page with all panel capabilities as acceptance criteria, and GO-008 shows owner awareness the receiver is unbuilt — but its four W3 peers shipped (#1504-#1507), Waves 4-5 shipped past it (#1509-#1511), and it is the only ticketed surface never built with no tracking row in the parity recovery docs. For a solo-operator agency, a tier-gated paid-data KPI silently unreachable the moment the shell flag flips, while the docs claim it's preserved, is a fair 'major'.

validations.claim: Reliable cross-surface 'Draft brief' handoff (fixContext prefill). Legacy App.tsx captures location.state.fixContext into React state (App.tsx:246-256) and passes it as a prop to the lazy legacy ContentPipeline (App.tsx:446), so the prefill survives chunk loading and even arrives while already mounted (ContentPipeline.tsx:132-136 switches to Briefs). The rebuilt surface receives only workspaceId (rebuiltSurfaces.ts:17-19, App.tsx:518) and reads location.state exclusively in useState initializers (ContentPipelineSurface.tsx:126-127); App.tsx's same effect still clears router state on a setTimeout(0) replace-navigation. When the content-pipeline chunk is cold (first visit of a session), the state is wiped before the lazy surface mounts and the handoff silently no-ops — no blank brief opens, no keyword prefill. Senders affected: keywords-rebuilt/KeywordDrawer.tsx:190-199, competitors-rebuilt/KeywordGapsCard.tsx:133, strategy/ContentGaps.tsx:103/126, StrategyCompetitiveTab.tsx:77, ContentDecay.tsx:261-266, PageIntelligenceSurface.tsx:199. The component test (ContentPipelineSurface.test.tsx:1595) mounts the surface synchronously so it cannot catch the lazy-chunk race.
validations.note: Verified all cited code as described: App.tsx:246-256 (setTimeout(0) clearing effect), App.tsx:446 (legacy prop), App.tsx:490-518 (rebuilt mount, workspaceId only), rebuiltSurfaces.ts:17-19+50-52, ContentPipelineSurface.tsx:81-83+126-127 (useState-initializer-only reads), legacy ContentPipeline.tsx:127-136, all six senders, and the synchronous-mount test at tests/component/content-pipeline-rebuilt/ContentPipelineSurface.test.tsx:1596. Then refuted the race mechanism: (1) node_modules/react-router/dist/development/chunk-6CSD65Y2.mjs:6931 — RR 7.17 BrowserRouter/MemoryRouter wrap setState in React.startTransition by default (App.tsx:148 passes no opt-out); (2) all rebuilt tabs share one App.tsx branch (App.tsx:491-521), so the ErrorBoundary+Suspense boundary persists across rebuilt→rebuilt navigation — a suspended transition inside a revealed boundary delays the commit, so the clearing effect (post-commit) cannot fire before the surface's render-phase initializers; (3) built and ran a faithful vitest probe (persistent boundary, keyed deferred-lazy child, exact clearing-effect pattern, chunk resolved 50ms after navigation): output 'fallbackShown=false senderStillVisible=true' and 'captured:emergency dentist austin' — handoff survives cold; probe file deleted after run; (4) flag-ON senders are all hosted in rebuilt surfaces (keywords-rebuilt, competitors-rebuilt, site-audit-rebuilt→ContentDecay, page-intelligence-rebuilt, engine-rebuilt/EngineSurface.tsx:875→LostQueryRecoveryCard); strategy/ContentGaps + StrategyCompetitiveTab hosts (KeywordStrategy.tsx, CompetitorsPage.tsx) only render flag-OFF, where the legacy prop path works; legacy-only tabs (App.tsx:449-456: seo-briefs/content/calendar redirects, subscriptions, content-perf) host no content-pipeline fixContext senders. docs/ui-rebuild/parity/content-pipeline-contract.md:175 records the fix-context handoff as covered, not as an approved exception — irrelevant since the loss doesn't reproduce.
validations.correctedClaim: The rebuilt 'Draft brief' handoff works on cold chunks: React Router 7 wraps navigations in React.startTransition, so when the lazy ContentPipelineSurface suspends inside the already-mounted Suspense boundary (App.tsx:517, shared by all rebuilt tabs), React 19 delays the entire commit — including the location change that arms App.tsx's setTimeout(0) state-clearing effect — until the chunk resolves; the surface's useState initializers (ContentPipelineSurface.tsx:126-127) then capture the intact fixContext. Empirically reproduced: with a 50ms-cold chunk the receiver still captured the keyword. Residual minor hardening gaps only: (a) initializer-only reads would miss a fixContext arriving while the surface is already mounted, but no live sender navigates content-pipeline→content-pipeline with fixContext; (b) the lazyWithRetry stale-chunk hard-reload edge (history.state survives reload, boundary mounts fresh) would drop the prefill where the legacy App-state capture survived.

validations.claim: Visibility of approved (not yet delivered/published) posts. Legacy Posts tab was always one click away (ContentPipeline.tsx:46-48 TabBar) with an Approved status tile + filter, search, and sort (ContentManager.tsx:129-183). The rebuilt board excludes approved posts (ContentLifecycleBoard.tsx:130 'if (post.status === approved) continue'), the lens switcher has no Drafts/Posts entry (ContentPipelineSurface.tsx:88-94), and the Published lens shows only content-performance items (delivered/published requests), so an approved-but-unscheduled post appears nowhere in primary navigation. The ContentManager aggregate is reachable only via the ?tab=posts deep link or the Draft column's launcher card, which renders only when there are ZERO drafts (ContentLifecycleBoard.tsx:263-267). Calendar's schedule picker lists unscheduled drafts (ContentCalendar.tsx:113-116) but only inside a per-day popover.
validations.note: Verified every cited line on origin/staging (working tree confirmed identical via git diff). Claim facts all CHECK OUT: legacy Posts tab one click away (src/components/ContentPipeline.tsx:46), Approved tile + search/sort (src/components/ContentManager.tsx:130,154-183); rebuilt board skips approved posts (src/components/content-pipeline-rebuilt/ContentLifecycleBoard.tsx:130 `if (post.status === 'approved') continue`); lens switcher has only board/calendar/published/content-health/matrix (ContentPipelineSurface.tsx:88-94); Published lens is content-performance only, statuses published/delivered (PublishedContentLens.tsx:50-55); the Draft-column launcher to drafts renders only when grouped.draft.length===0 (ContentLifecycleBoard.tsx:263-267); calendar picker filters !plannedPublishAt && !publishedAt && status!=='generating' (so includes approved) but lives inside the selected-day popover (ContentCalendar.tsx:113-116,547-575); Intake also filters out approved requests (ContentPipelineIntake.tsx:40-48). COUNTER-EVIDENCE that downgrades the label: (1) ContentPipelineLenses.tsx:311-318 mounts the full legacy ContentManager for tab==='posts', and useContentPipelineSurfaceState.ts:5-14 keeps 'posts' a valid tab — nothing was deleted; (2) docs/ui-rebuild/parity/content-pipeline-contract.md:135 explicitly documents "the direct `?tab=posts` aggregate remains a compatibility receiver", and the queued/brief/draft/review board model with "Scheduled pieces move to Calendar / Published pieces move to Published" (contract lines 36-38) was owner-approved 2026-07-10; (3) more in-product senders than the claim admits: ContentCalendar.tsx:132 (request items → bare ?tab=posts lands on the aggregate), ContentWorkOrderLens.tsx:117, ContentPlanner.tsx:282, App.tsx:450 (/content redirect). SUPPORTING the residual finding: the surface's documented exceptions (rebuilt-surface-inventory.md:29 — queue/graduation/AI-assist/questionnaire/matrix-bulk only) never mention approved-post visibility, and the owner fixture had zero approved posts (contract line 80), so the gap was never exercised or explicitly ratified. Net: real, undocumented discoverability gap for a transient state; "functionality-lost / major" overstates it.
validations.correctedClaim: Approved-but-unscheduled posts lose primary-navigation visibility in the rebuilt Content Pipeline (board excludes them, no Drafts/Posts lens, no approved count anywhere in the chrome), but the capability is not lost: the full ContentManager aggregate — Approved status tile, filter, search, sort — is retained unchanged and is mounted by the rebuilt surface itself at ?tab=posts, which the owner-approved parity contract explicitly designates a preserved compatibility receiver. Beyond the two paths the claim lists, several in-product senders reach it (Calendar request-item click, strategy ContentWorkOrderLens, ContentPlanner matrix items, the /content legacy redirect), the Calendar schedule picker lists approved unscheduled posts by title, and approved+scheduled/published posts appear in Calendar/Published lenses. This is a discoverability regression for one transient post state (approve-then-close-without-publishing), not lost functionality — though the approved-state invisibility itself is not among the surface's documented exceptions and is worth a small fix (approved column or lens count).

validations.claim: Decay signal → per-page detail and prefilled refresh brief. Legacy pipeline's decay alert was a ClickableRow navigating to seo-audit?sub=content-decay (ContentPipeline.tsx:219-245), where ContentDecay offers per-page 'Draft refresh brief' that returns to the pipeline with the decaying pageSlug prefilled (ContentDecay.tsx:261-266). The rebuilt Content Health lens shows decay rows but clicking any row opens the Intake lane (ContentPipelineLenses.tsx:258 onRowClick → onOpenTab('intake')) — unrelated to the clicked page — and its 'Draft refresh brief' button opens a blank brief workspace with no page/keyword prefill (ContentPipelineLenses.tsx:268-271 → openBriefs, ContentPipelineSurface.tsx:323-332).
validations.note: Verified on origin/staging (78239b424). Cited facts confirmed: ContentPipelineLenses.tsx:258 `onRowClick={() => onOpenTab('intake')}`; lines 268-271 'Draft refresh brief' → onOpenTab('briefs') → openBriefs() (ContentPipelineSurface.tsx:325-329) which opens a blank brief; ContentPipelineIntake.tsx:60-90 deriveContentIntake composes only requests/suggestions/workOrders — no decay items, so the row click lands somewhere unrelated. Legacy flow confirmed: ContentPipeline.tsx decay ClickableRow → seo-audit?sub=content-decay; ContentDecay.tsx per-page 'Refresh brief' → navigate(content-pipeline, {state:{fixContext:{pageSlug,pageName}}}). REFUTING evidence the claim missed: (1) rebuilt site-audit-rebuilt/SiteAuditSurface.tsx:65 lazy-imports legacy ContentDecay and mounts it at line 625 in a Content Health disclosure (defaultOpen when ?sub=content-decay, line 619); useSiteAuditSurfaceState.ts:41-44 resolves the `sub` param; site-audit-contract.md:48,72 preserves ?sub=content-decay as compatibility receiver, surface `owner-approved` (registry-final-owner-review.md:23). (2) Rebuilt ContentPipelineSurface.tsx:82-83 + 126-127 reads fixContext from router state on mount (targetRoute==='content-pipeline'), opens the brief workspace, and passes briefFixContext at line 552 → ContentPipelineWorkspaces.tsx:355 `fixContext={briefFixContext}` into the real brief editor — so ContentDecay's prefilled handoff works end-to-end in the rebuilt shell. (3) content-pipeline-contract.md:75 documents the Content Health lens discrepancy ('per-row refresh action' in prototype; correction: 'use the existing Draft refresh brief path until a queue endpoint exists'); coverage-audit.md:101 marks Content Pipeline owner-approved with documented exceptions. Residual real defect: lens rows navigating to Intake (no decay items there) and no cross-link from the lens to seo-audit?sub=content-decay — a minor wiring/navigation gap for a solo operator, since the full per-page workflow remains reachable via Site Audit.
validations.correctedClaim: The per-page decay detail and prefilled refresh brief are NOT lost — the capability moved homes. The rebuilt SiteAuditSurface mounts the legacy ContentDecay component inside its Content Health disclosure with the legacy `seo-audit?sub=content-decay` deep link preserved (owner-approved), and ContentDecay's per-page 'Refresh brief' handoff still works because the rebuilt ContentPipelineSurface reads `location.state.fixContext` on mount and threads it into the brief workspace. What actually regressed is the pipeline-side convenience link: the rebuilt Content Health lens no longer links to the per-page detail — clicking a decay row opens the Intake lane, which contains no decay items (misleading row-click wiring), and the lens's single 'Draft refresh brief' button opens a blank brief; the latter is a documented interim decision in the parity contract ('use the existing Draft refresh brief path until a queue endpoint exists'). Worth fixing the row-click target/adding a cross-link, but it is a minor navigation gap, not lost functionality.

validations.claim: The Published lens chip count is a different metric than the lens contents: MODE_OPTIONS uses pipelineData.summary.published, which useContentPipeline computes as the number of published MATRIX CELLS (allCells.filter(status==='published').length), while the Published lens lists content-performance items (delivered/published requests + matrix pieces from the shared read model). A workspace with 3 published request-backed posts and no matrices shows 'Published 0' on the switcher while the lens contains 3 result cards; a matrix-heavy workspace shows an inflated chip over a near-empty readback list.
validations.note: Verified all three evidence citations on origin/staging (identical to working tree per git diff). Chip: ContentPipelineSurface.tsx:91 uses data?.summary?.published; the pipelineData memo (lines 136-148) overrides briefs/posts counts but not published. Metric: useContentPipeline.ts:70 computes published as allCells.filter(status==='published').length — matrix cells only; the aggregate-only variant (line 54) still fetches matrices. Lens: PublishedContentLens.tsx:312-315 lists useAdminContentPerformance items; server read model (server/domains/content/content-performance.ts:288) includes delivered+published requests plus (lines 367-369) only published matrix cells that have a targetKeyword and pass keyword dedup — so both divergence directions in the claim are real. Scenario 1 is exact: LensSwitcher.tsx:110 renders the count pill even for 0, so 3 request-backed published posts + no matrices shows 'Published 0' over a 3-card lens. Refutation attempts failed: (1) the mismatch is new to the rebuild — legacy ContentPipeline.tsx:200 used summary.published only as an internally-consistent '(X% published)' of cells and had no Published tab/chip; (2) no parity-doc exception — content-pipeline-contract.md:187 states Published 'uses the authoritative shared content-performance read model', which the chip contradicts; deferred-ledger Published entries (data/ui-rebuild-deferred-ledger.json:928-931, 1082-1083) cover other decisions; (3) no test pins the chip metric — the fixture in tests/component/content-pipeline-rebuilt/ContentPipelineSurface.test.tsx even carries the mismatch (published: 5 cells vs piecesPublished: 1) without asserting chip==lens. Severity major stands: the repo's mandatory count-honesty rule forbids mixed-source counts, and the chip is persistent across all pipeline modes, so an undercount hides live published work from the operator.

validations.claim: Cross-surface fixContext handoff is intermittent (works with a warm chunk, silently drops with a cold one) because the rebuilt surface samples router state only in mount-time useState initializers while App.tsx clears that state on a 0ms timer — the same defect described under functionalityLost, listed here because it is timing-dependent rather than a designed-out capability.
validations.note: Checked against origin/staging (working tree was on another branch, so all reads used git show). (1) Verified the cited mechanism exists: App.tsx:246-256 (setTimeout(0) → setFixContext + navigate replace state:{}), ContentPipelineSurface.tsx:81-83 readBriefFixContext + :126-127 mount-time useState initializers; grep confirms zero useEffect in the surface file, so state is never re-sampled after mount. (2) Verified mount topology: App.tsx:490-521 renders every rebuilt surface inside a single un-keyed ErrorBoundary+Suspense (App.tsx:517) with only the child keyed by tab:workspaceId; RebuiltAppChrome.tsx adds no Suspense of its own; BrowserRouter (App.tsx:148) with react-router-dom@7.17.0 (startTransition default). (3) Ran an empirical repro with the repo's installed react@19.2.4/react-dom@19.2.4/react-router-dom@7.17.0/jsdom, faithfully mirroring the clearing effect + lazy keyed child, chunk resolved 300ms after click: with the shared pre-existing boundary (the real structure) the log order was navigate-clicked → chunk-requested → target-render captured=KW → target-mounted → app-cleared-router-state; target captured the fixContext in both MemoryRouter and BrowserRouter. The drop only reproduced when the Suspense boundary was newly mounted during the transition (captured=null) — the legacy-shell→rebuilt case. (4) Sender census: flag-ON senders are all inside rebuilt surfaces — keywords-rebuilt/KeywordDrawer.tsx:191-199, competitors-rebuilt/KeywordGapsCard.tsx:133, page-intelligence-rebuilt/PageIntelligenceSurface.tsx:199, hooks/admin/useSiteAuditRebuilt.ts:375, ContentDecay.tsx:266 (mounted at site-audit-rebuilt/SiteAuditSurface.tsx:65), strategy/LostQueryRecoveryCard.tsx:24 (mounted at engine-rebuilt/EngineSurface.tsx:875). Legacy-only senders (KeywordHub.tsx:456, PageIntelligence.tsx:177, strategy/ContentGaps.tsx:103/126, StrategyCompetitiveTab.tsx:77) mount only flag-OFF, where the receiver is legacy ContentPipeline fed fixContext as a prop from App's captured state (App.tsx:446) — timing-immune. Page-union values absent from REBUILT_SURFACES (seo-briefs, content, calendar, subscriptions, content-perf) host no content-pipeline senders. (5) Existing test tests/component/content-pipeline-rebuilt/ContentPipelineSurface.test.tsx:1596-1622 proves the warm receiver only, so the audit's static reasoning was understandable, but the cold case it extrapolated does not manifest under RR7 transition semantics.
validations.correctedClaim: The cited code pattern is real (ContentPipelineSurface samples location.state only in mount-time useState initializers at src/components/content-pipeline-rebuilt/ContentPipelineSurface.tsx:126-127, and App.tsx:246-256 clears router state on a 0ms timer), but the claimed warm/cold intermittency does not occur: React Router 7 wraps navigate() in startTransition, and the rebuilt shell mounts every surface inside one persistent Suspense boundary (App.tsx:517-518), so a cold chunk suspends the entire navigation commit — App's clearing effect cannot run until the chunk has loaded and the receiver's initializer has already captured fixContext. Empirically verified with the repo's exact react@19.2.4 + react-router-dom@7.17.0: cold-chunk handoff succeeds (clear fires only after target mount). All flag-ON senders to content-pipeline live inside rebuilt surfaces (shared boundary), and flag-OFF navigations hit the legacy ContentPipeline receiver which is timing-immune via App's captured fixContext prop (App.tsx:446). What remains is only a latent fragility: a future sender rendered in the legacy shell branch (newly-mounted Suspense boundary) or any non-transition navigation would silently drop the handoff — that scenario does reproduce, but no reachable sender triggers it today.

validations.claim: Content Health rows are misleading controls: every decay and cannibalization row's click handler opens the Intake lane (requests/ideas/orders), which has no relationship to the clicked page or keyword. Legacy's equivalent click landed on the decay detail view.
validations.note: Verified on origin/staging. (1) Wiring confirmed exactly as cited: src/components/content-pipeline-rebuilt/ContentPipelineLenses.tsx:258 has onRowClick={() => onOpenTab('intake')} applied to ALL health rows (decay summary, per-page decay alerts, cannibalization warnings — rows built at lines 105-145), with no row context; onOpenTab → openIntake() → state.setTab('intake') (ContentPipelineSurface.tsx:334-337,355-364), and Intake is the requests/ideas/work-orders lane (deriveContentIntake in ContentPipelineIntake.tsx:60-80 composes only ContentTopicRequests + SuggestedBriefs + WorkOrders). DataTable rows with onRowClick are cursor-pointer/hover/keyboard-activatable (src/components/ui/DataTable.tsx:197-210), so 'misleading controls' is fair. The context-passing path exists (handleCreateBrief → setBriefFixContext, ContentPipelineSurface.tsx:371-374) but row clicks bypass it. (2) Legacy comparison partially holds: ContentPipeline.tsx:219-222 navigates the decay band to seo-audit?sub=content-decay — but it was ONE summary band, not per-row clicks, and legacy cannibalization (src/components/ui/CannibalizationAlert.tsx) has zero onClick handlers, so cannibalization rows have no legacy click to regress from. (3) Capability not lost: the decay detail view (ContentDecay) is mounted in rebuilt Site Audit's Content Health evidence Disclosure, deep-linkable via ?sub=content-decay (site-audit-rebuilt/SiteAuditSurface.tsx:616-627, useSiteAuditSurfaceState.ts:12), owner-approved per docs/ui-rebuild/parity/owner-decision-packet.md:233 — though nothing in content-pipeline-rebuilt links to it (grep: no seo-audit/content-decay refs). (4) Parity docs: content-pipeline-contract.md line 75 sanctions the 'Draft refresh brief' path for Content Health ('until a queue endpoint exists') but never documents/approves the row→intake routing; no decay/cannibalization entry in parity-backlog.md; tests cover only the Draft-refresh-brief flow, not row clicks — so the routing looks incidental, not owner-approved. Net: real misleading-affordance defect + lost decay-detail cross-link from this surface, but the detail capability survives elsewhere, the sanctioned action is on the same screen, and half the rows never had a legacy click — minor for a solo-operator platform, not major.
validations.correctedClaim: Rebuilt Content Health rows are misleading affordances: every decay and cannibalization row is rendered as an interactive control (cursor-pointer, hover, keyboard-activatable) whose click navigates away from Content Health to the Intake lane with no page/keyword context. However, this is only partially a regression: legacy offered a single decay summary band (not per-row clicks) that linked to seo-audit?sub=content-decay, legacy cannibalization entries were not clickable at all, the decay detail view survives owner-approved in the rebuilt Site Audit (?sub=content-decay), and the contract-sanctioned "Draft refresh brief" action sits directly below the table. The fix is a click-target correction (route decay rows to the Site Audit decay evidence or thread page context into the brief-fix path), not a lost capability.

validations.claim: The Detail table renders every returned GSC row (up to 500 queries / 500 pages) directly into page flow with no height cap, pagination, or virtualization — legacy contained the same data in a sidebar-height scroll box. On any workspace with real query volume this makes the Search report tens of thousands of pixels tall and buries the owner-approved lower band (Demand mix, Priority insights, Monitoring & insights) that the parity contract fought to keep 'mounted exactly once'. The DataTable sticky header also only sticks inside its own overflow-x container, so column context is lost while page-scrolling.
validations.note: Every element verified. (1) Uncapped render: SearchLens.tsx:263-266 maps full topQueries/topPages with no slice; :390-395 feeds them to DataTable in an uncapped ChartCard; grep found zero max-h/maxHeight/overflow-y in src/components/search-traffic-rebuilt/. (2) DataTable.tsx has no cap/pagination/virtualization props; container :122 is overflow-x-auto only, header :131 sticky top-0 — overflow-x:auto makes the table div its own (never-scrolling) scroll container, so the sticky header is dead during page scroll, exactly as claimed. (3) Server default 500+500 confirmed: server/search-console.ts:226-227, reached via routes/google.ts:328 → analytics-data.ts:25 passing {}. (4) Legacy containment confirmed: SearchDetail.tsx:249 (maxHeight from sidebar ref) + :279 (overflow-y-auto), with a working sticky header inside that scrolling box. (5) Lower band buried: contextBand (Demand mix/Priority insights) at SearchLens.tsx:398 and Monitoring Disclosure at :400 render after the table; the "mount exactly once" contract language is at docs/ui-rebuild/parity/analytics-hub-contract.md:50. (6) Undocumented: deferred ledger has only DEF-search-traffic-001 (insight graduation); contract/parity-backlog say nothing about table height. Stronger than claimed: phase0 ledger Q5 (docs/ui-rebuild/phase0/surfaces/search-traffic.md:43) explicitly catalogued "scrollable body height-matched to sidebar" and the phase-A ticket (tickets/search-traffic.md:47) silently dropped the containment. Owner approval of 2026-07-10 was against the local GSC fixture, which returns 5 query rows (server/providers/local-provider-fixtures.ts:120), so the 500-row state was never sighted. Refutation attempts failed: no other rebuilt home caps this table (Breakdowns drawer covers devices/countries/search types only), and the Keywords pilot (KeywordsTable.tsx:585-600) shows the intended pattern — consumers paginate/cap themselves — making SearchLens the outlier. Severity major is fair for a daily-driver admin report: nothing is unreachable, but the approved lower band is pushed ~20k px down on real workspaces and column context is lost while scrolling.

validations.claim: Series color collision in the cross-source Overview receiver: users uses the same token as clicks (both var(--blue)) and sessions the same as impressions (both var(--cyan)). The receiver's default active lines are clicks + users — two indistinguishable blue lines in the one view whose entire purpose is telling GSC and GA4 movement apart. Legacy kept users visually distinct (teal). The contract documents the retint away from teal but not that the replacement collides with the GSC palette.
validations.note: Verified all citations on origin/staging. Collision confirmed: searchTrafficUtils.ts:15/19 (clicks and users both 'var(--blue)') and :16/20 (impressions and sessions both 'var(--cyan)'); token values identical in both themes (src/tokens.css:152-159, 275-282). Default pair confirmed: OverviewLens.tsx:61 useToggleSet(['clicks','users']). Legacy distinctness confirmed: AnalyticsOverview.tsx:21-26 (users=teal, clicks=blue), default ['clicks','users'] at :107. Doc gap confirmed: analytics-hub-contract.md:103 documents the retint ('Retinted users/sessions series away from teal action color', rationale at :33) but not the collision. However, severity is overweighted: the Overview lens is a hidden compatibility receiver — SEARCH_TRAFFIC_LENSES (useSearchTrafficSurfaceState.ts:6-10) offers only search/traffic/annotations; 'overview' is accepted (:18-21) but unselectable, repo-wide grep found zero '?lens=overview' senders in src/, and the receiver shows an explicit compatibility banner (SearchTrafficSurface.tsx:104-107). Visible lenses have no collision (SearchLens.tsx:173 defaults clicks+impressions = blue+cyan; TrafficLens.tsx:67 defaults users+sessions = blue+cyan). Tooltips and labeled toggle tiles still disambiguate. Real regression, accurate citations, but major→minor for a deep-link-only legacy view on a solo-operator platform.
validations.correctedClaim: In the hidden ?lens=overview compatibility receiver (not reachable from any in-app control or sender — legacy bookmarks only), the default active lines clicks + users both render as var(--blue) and impressions/sessions share var(--cyan), making co-rendered GSC and GA4 lines indistinguishable by color; legacy kept users teal. The visible default reports (Search performance, Site traffic) have internally distinct palettes and are unaffected, and series remain disambiguable via labeled tooltips and toggle tiles, so this is a minor legibility regression confined to a deep-link-only view, plus a small doc gap (the contract records the retint away from teal but not the resulting collision).

validations.claim: OrientZone strategy-momentum readout: CTR-weighted visibility score ring + deterministic verdict + 4-stat delta strip (clicks / impressions / ranked keywords / avg position vs previous strategy generation). Rebuilt keeps only a static 'Average position' tile with no deltas, no visibility score, no clicks/impressions trend.
validations.note: Verified legacy: KeywordStrategy.tsx:509-511 (orientEl) rendered in all three branches (:783 the-issue disclosure, :873 command-center, :912 flag-OFF); OrientZone.tsx:19-24 deterministic verdict, :47 visibilityScore, :66-73 delta strip. Verified rebuilt: EngineSurface.tsx:697-707 static avg-position tile only; no MetricRing/CompactStatBar/TrendBadge in engine-rebuilt/; useEngineRebuilt.ts:129 reads only strategyUx.localSync (orient fetched-and-discarded); grep confirms no rebuilt surface uses OrientZone/visibilityScore; ClientTrustSpinePreview (EngineSurface.tsx:206-320) also lacks it. Verified docs: OrientZone absent from engine-contract.md Carry-Over Homes (:106-121) and parity-backlog.md — BUT engine-contract.md:30 explicitly specifies the owner-approved replacement value frame ("Average position or equivalent strategy momentum"), and parity-backlog.md P1 item 2 records Joshua approving ODP-001-V1..V6 on 2026-07-10 after multiple visual review rounds. Verdict survives via CommandCenterVerdict (EngineSurface.tsx:630-660); StrategyDiff (:617-624) covers keyword-level what-changed vs previous generation; clicks/impressions prior-period trends exist in search-traffic-rebuilt (OverviewLens.tsx:32, AnnotationsLens.tsx:54-55); visibility score still live on client surfaces (StrategyClientOrientHeader.tsx:44, NarratedStatusHeadline.tsx:47). Loss is real but is a documented owner-approved prototype composition with a minor doc-completeness gap, so severity major → minor.
validations.correctedClaim: The OrientZone readout (CTR-weighted visibility score ring, deterministic verdict, 4-stat delta strip vs previous generation) is absent from the rebuilt Engine, but its replacement is an owner-approved documented composition, not an unratified loss: engine-contract.md:26-30 (Required Interaction Model, status owner-approved, ODP-001-V1..V6 approved 2026-07-10) specifies the value frame as "Pipeline value at stake / Recovered so far / Backing moves live / Average position or equivalent strategy momentum," which EngineSurface implements exactly. The verdict role survives via CommandCenterVerdict (PoV-sourced), vs-previous-generation change is partially covered by StrategyDiff in the spine, clicks/impressions trends exist in the rebuilt Analytics Hub, and the visibility score still renders on client surfaces. Residual gap: the visibility score + generation-keyed metric deltas exist nowhere in the rebuilt admin, OrientZone is not named in Carry-Over Homes or Moved/Excluded/Deferred, and useEngineRebuilt fetches strategyUx.orient but discards it — a small re-add wire and a one-line parity-doc addendum, not a major undocumented loss.

validations.claim: Admin content-gaps evidence list with pre-seeded execution handoffs: intent chips, SERP-feature/AI-Overview badges, and the 'generate content' / 'create brief' CTAs that carry the full computed gap context into content-pipeline. In the rebuilt shell no admin surface renders strategy.contentGaps as a list; the gap-to-pre-seeded-brief workflow is gone (gaps still flow into recommendations server-side via finalization.ts:72 content_gap→topic_cluster and appear per-keyword in the KCC drawer, so mitigation is partial).
validations.note: Verified legacy: ContentGaps.tsx:99-143 CTAs carry full gap context (rationale, competitorProof, volume, intent, questionKeywords, serpFeatures, autoGenerate) into content-pipeline fixContext; mounted at KeywordStrategy.tsx:467/798/942-951. Verified rebuilt: no ContentGaps import in engine-rebuilt/content-pipeline-rebuilt/keywords-rebuilt (grep), and the D8 redirect target for legacy ?tab=content (content-pipeline?tab=content-health, ContentPipelineLenses.tsx:147-240) renders decay+cannibalization only. REFUTING evidence the claim missed: (a) gaps render per-gap as Engine backing moves with rationale in the evidence drawer — server/domains/recommendations/strategy-producers.ts:96-130 builds one rec per gap via buildRecommendationStory(topic, targetKeyword, rationale, intent), rules.ts:781-790 strategyInsight includes the rationale, EngineMoveDrawer.tsx:74 renders rec.insight; (b) a per-gap brief handoff survives — server row-lifecycle.ts:294-303 adds a 'Generate brief' next action for content_gap-role KCC rows (read-model.ts:190 materializes gaps as rows), and keywords-rebuilt/KeywordDrawer.tsx:190-200 navigates to content-pipeline with fixContext {primaryKeyword, pageType} — thin but present; (c) intent chips (KeywordsTable.tsx:291 IntentTag) and SERP/AI-Overview badges (KeywordDrawer.tsx:379-395, shared serpBadges) survive per keyword. Docs: grep of docs/ui-rebuild/parity/ confirms zero gap-evidence records (claim correct there), but docs/ui-rebuild/phase-a/surfaces/engine.json capabilityClassification "F1 Content tab folds" (resolution ui-only, note "Delegated Pipeline/Editor; folds must keep per-item CTAs incl. DecayingPages send-to-client") documents the delegation as an owner-ratified phase-A decision — the ContentGaps fold failing to land in the delegated home is an unmet acceptance criterion, not an undocumented removal. Genuinely lost: competitorProof/questionKeywords display, full pre-seed, autoGenerate, gap Keep. Severity for a solo operator: minor — the workflow survives in two rebuilt homes with degraded context, legacy is byte-identical flag-OFF, and the actionable fix is a parity-backlog entry plus enriching the KeywordDrawer generate_brief fixContext.
validations.correctedClaim: The dedicated Content Gaps evidence card and its FULL-context pre-seeded brief handoff are gone from the rebuilt shell, but the gap-to-brief workflow itself survives in thinner form: (1) each strategy content gap becomes a backing-move recommendation rendered in the rebuilt Engine with topic/keyword/rationale/intent evidence in the move drawer (strategy-producers.ts:96-130, EngineMoveDrawer.tsx:74); (2) content-gap keywords appear as rows in the rebuilt Keywords surface with intent chips (KeywordsTable.tsx:291) and SERP-feature/AI-Overview badges in the drawer (KeywordDrawer.tsx:379-395), and content_gap-role rows get a server-provided 'Generate brief' handoff to content-pipeline (row-lifecycle.ts:294-303) — pre-seeding only primaryKeyword+pageType (KeywordDrawer.tsx:190-200). What is actually lost: rationale/competitorProof/volume/intent/questionKeywords/serpFeatures in the brief fixContext, autoGenerate draft-now, competitorProof+questionKeywords display anywhere in the rebuilt shell, and the gap Keep affordance. The Content-tab delegation was an owner-ratified phase-A decision (engine.json "F1 Content tab folds": "Delegated Pipeline/Editor; folds must keep per-item CTAs"), so this is an unmet acceptance criterion of a documented move — untracked in parity-backlog.md — not a wholesale undocumented capability loss. Legacy remains reachable flag-OFF.

validations.claim: Upload and processing queue for workspaces WITHOUT a linked Webflow site. Legacy rendered the Upload sub-tab (DropZones + processing queue) unconditionally — only Browse and Audit were siteId-gated — and the server upload route is pure folder-based optimization with no Webflow dependency. The rebuilt surface early-returns a 'Link a Webflow site' EmptyState for the ENTIRE surface, making UI-driven upload/optimization impossible for unlinked workspaces (only the global clipboard-paste path survives).
validations.note: Every cited fact verified against origin/staging (78239b4). Legacy: src/components/MediaTab.tsx:51-66 renders the Upload sub-tab (DropZone asset + meta, ProcessingQueue) with no siteId gate, while Browse/Audit are gated at :69-92. Server: server/routes/misc.ts:36-52 and :55-71 (meta) are pure folder-based moveUploadedFiles+triggerOptimize; server/processor.ts:210-236 shows the Webflow push is conditional on ws.webflowSiteId, so the optimize+alt-text pipeline is fully functional for unlinked workspaces — legacy upload was useful there, not a dead-end. Rebuilt: AssetManagerSurface.tsx:777-788 early-returns the whole surface on !siteId (siteId from workspace.webflowSiteId at :203); the rebuilt UploadLens (UploadLens.tsx:24-35,61; Drawer at AssetManagerSurface.tsx:1082-1089, toolbar button :939-941) exists but sits entirely behind the gate. Searched for the capability elsewhere: UploadLens is the only rebuilt DropZone mount (grep across asset-manager-rebuilt/, other *-rebuilt dirs, layout/); only the global clipboard-paste handler survives (src/App.tsx:307-344 — single-image, asset-type only, no meta path, no bulk), exactly as the claim acknowledges. Parity docs: grep of docs/ui-rebuild/parity/ for siteId/unlinked/'Link a Webflow' returns zero hits — not an owner-approved exception; media-contract.md:45 in fact requires 'upload queue' to 'remain reachable', which the unlinked state violates. Mitigation noted but non-refuting: rebuilt surface mounts only when ui-rebuild-shell is ON (rebuiltSurfaces.ts:32-34; default false, staging-validation rollout, feature-flags.ts:129,511-517), so legacy still serves flag-OFF production. Severity 'major' is honest: an entire UI workflow (bulk drag-drop upload, meta-file upload, queue visibility) is lost for the unlinked-workspace class, meta uploads have no workaround at all, and the loss is both undocumented and contrary to the surface's own parity contract.

validations.claim: Audit issue rows keep a stale asset id after compression. Compress replaces the Webflow asset and deletes the old one server-side; legacy remapped the row to newAssetId (and updated fileSize) so follow-up actions kept working. Rebuilt only strips the oversized/unoptimized-png issue types and ignores the returned newAssetId, so on a multi-issue asset (e.g. oversized + missing-alt) the surviving row's Generate alt / Delete actions POST/DELETE against a deleted asset id and fail.
validations.note: Verified all three legs. Rebuilt: src/components/asset-manager-rebuilt/AuditLens.tsx:235-254 — handleCompress types newAssetId/newSize in the response but discards both, calling only removeIssueType(assetId, ['oversized','unoptimized-png']) (line 244); removeIssueType (188-208) never remaps ids, and audit state is local useState so nothing else can heal the row. Legacy: src/components/AssetAudit.tsx:154-166 remaps { assetId: newAssetId, fileSize: newSize }; legacy bulk compress even auto-re-scanned (line 124) while rebuilt bulk (309-326) only shows a re-scan hint banner. Server: server/domains/webflow-assets/image-optimization.ts:309 deletes the old asset (always in this flow — rebuilt sends no cmsUsages, so cmsRepairsSkipped=false) and returns newAssetId at :319; the route (server/routes/webflow-alt-text.ts:318) returns it verbatim, so the frontend does receive it. Failure scenario holds and is if anything slightly worse than stated: Delete (AuditLens.tsx:259) DELETEs the dead id and fails with an error toast, but Generate alt fails SILENTLY — the route's updateAsset(oldId) write-back fails and returns 200 {altText, updated:false, writeError} (webflow-alt-text.ts:119-123), and the rebuilt UI checks only data.altText (AuditLens.tsx:218), so it strips the missing-alt badge and reports success while nothing was written to Webflow. Searched all of asset-manager-rebuilt for any other newAssetId handling: none. Parity docs (media-contract.md:92 lists compress/generate-alt/delete-unused as preserved capabilities; grepped parity-backlog.md, owner-decision-packet.md, rebuilt-surface-inventory.md for newAssetId/stale-id) document no owner-approved exception. Major is fair for a solo operator: it breaks the surface's core compress-then-fix-remaining-issues loop with one silent-false-success mode, though a manual re-scan recovers, so not a blocker.

validations.claim: Ranked fix queue (top 5 pages by impressions x optimization gap, each showing rank, impressions, score/100, and impact, with the 'ranked by traffic x optimization gap' rationale label; clicking any item opens that page)
validations.note: Verified legacy: pageIntelligenceData.ts:119-136 (buildFixQueue, impact=impressions×(100−score)/100, slice(0,5)); PageIntelligencePagesHeader.tsx:142-175 (full list: rank :156, impressions :158-161, score badge :163-169, impact :170, rationale label :147, clickable :153). Verified rebuilt: PageIntelligenceSurface.tsx:282-288 renders only fixQueue[0] as a 'Fix first' chip with +impact (:283-286); grep across src/ shows buildFixQueue consumed nowhere else in rebuilt UI; sort options (:42-47) can't reproduce impact ranking — 'priority' uses opportunityScore (pageIntelligenceDisplay.ts:42-51, position-band formula), confirming the claim's technical accuracy. Refutation via docs partially succeeded: docs/ui-rebuild/parity/page-intelligence-contract.md:23 describes the Joshua-approved composition as a 'fix-first page rail' (singular) and :30 requires only 'fix priority... reachable exactly once'; docs/ui-rebuild/phase-a/surfaces/site-audit.json P9 documents the collapse as resolution 'ui-only' ('Collapses into per-page priority chip; confirm at build that a triage list isn't needed' — confirmation never recorded); parity-backlog.md:79 + owner-decision-packet.md:405,421 show the ODP-012 B bundle including this composition was owner-approved 2026-07-11, though they loosely claim 'fix queue' preserved. Partial preservation (top item + per-row scores at PageIntelligenceSurface.tsx:318 + full detail pane via legacy sections) plus contract-consistent intent downgrade this from major functionality-lost to a minor, mostly-documented design reduction with one unclosed confirmation caveat. Flag-off still renders the legacy full queue (contract :43).
validations.correctedClaim: The rebuilt Page Intelligence collapses the legacy 5-item ranked fix queue to a single 'Fix first' chip: only the top-impact page with its +impact number survives (click-to-open preserved); items 2-5, per-item impressions, per-item score/100, rank numbers, and the 'ranked by traffic × optimization gap' label are gone and not reproducible via the rebuilt sort options (Priority uses a different position-band opportunityScore formula). However, this is a largely intentional, documented design reduction rather than an undocumented functionality loss: the owner-approved contract (page-intelligence-contract.md:23) specifies a 'fix-first page rail' and requires only that 'fix priority remain reachable exactly once' (:30), and the phase-A audit (phase-a/surfaces/site-audit.json P9) recorded the collapse as resolution 'ui-only' — though its 'confirm at build that a triage list isn't needed' caveat was never explicitly closed, and parity-backlog.md:79 loosely claims the 'fix queue' is preserved. Per-page scores remain visible on every rail row and in the detail pane, so triage remains workable; the loss is the at-a-glance top-5 session-planning list — a minor workflow regression for a solo operator, not a major capability loss.

validations.claim: Optimization score trend indicator (delta badge with up/down arrow and 'changed from X to Y' tooltip, driven by optimizationScoreHistory via summarizeScoreTrend)
validations.note: Verified every citation on origin/staging. Legacy: PageIntelligencePageRow.tsx:86 computes scoreTrend via summarizeScoreTrend(strategy?.optimizationScoreHistory); :173-181 render the delta badge + exact tooltip; definition at pageIntelligenceData.ts:24-37. Rebuilt: grep for trend/delta/sparkline/history in src/components/page-intelligence-rebuilt/ = zero hits; rail row shows only current score (PageIntelligenceSurface.tsx:316-320); detail pane shows static 'Score X/100' (PageIntelligenceDetailPane.tsx:99). Moved-home check: the rebuilt pane's PageIntelligencePersistedAnalysisSummary (DetailPane.tsx:8,:131) reads optimizationScoreHistory only for the last-run timestamp (PersistedAnalysisSummary.tsx:16-17), not a trend; PageRow is imported only by legacy PageIntelligencePageList.tsx:67; no other src/ consumer of PageOptimizationScoreSnapshot/ScoreTrendSummary exists. Docs: docs/ui-rebuild/parity/page-intelligence-contract.md capability list omits the trend; no exception recorded (owner-decision-packet.md:453,462 trend deferrals are Keyword Hub, a different surface) — 'undocumented' holds. Severity downgrade: data intact server-side (server/page-keywords.ts still writes optimizationScoreHistory), flag-OFF legacy path contractually retained, current score + fix-first impact still visible, and platform-level outcome tracking covers 'did it work'; display-only, cheaply restorable → minor (boundary case).
validations.correctedClaim: Real, undocumented flag-ON loss: the optimization-score trend delta badge (summarizeScoreTrend over optimizationScoreHistory, with 'changed from X to Y' tooltip) is rendered only by legacy PageIntelligencePageRow and has no equivalent anywhere in the rebuilt surface; however it is a display-only regression — the history data is still persisted and returned by the API, the current score and fix-queue impact remain visible, flag-OFF retains the legacy renderer, and restore is a small component addition — so minor, not major, for a solo-operator platform.

validations.claim: 'Unvalidated' keyword badge (strategy.validated === false, tooltip 'Keyword not validated in DataForSEO')
validations.note: Verified against origin/staging (78239b42). Legacy badge exists exactly as claimed: src/components/page-intelligence/PageIntelligencePageRow.tsx:157-161 renders 'Unvalidated' with title 'Keyword not validated in DataForSEO' when strategy?.validated === false, adjacent to the KeywordMetricCell volume/KD it qualifies. Rebuilt surface: grep for 'validat' returns zero hits across src/components/page-intelligence-rebuilt/ (PageIntelligenceSurface.tsx, PageIntelligenceDetailPane.tsx, pageIntelligenceRouting.ts) AND the shared sections the DetailPane composes (PageIntelligenceStrategySection.tsx, PageIntelligenceAnalysisSection.tsx, PageIntelligencePersistedAnalysisSummary.tsx). The rebuilt rail row (Surface.tsx:296-321) shows only title/path/keyword/score, and StrategySection.tsx:113-129 presents volume/KD%/CPC with no validation cue — exactly as the evidence states. Relocation search: the only other admin-side cue is the coarse whole-strategy warning in src/components/strategy/StrategyStalenessNudges.tsx:18-24, which fires only when NO pageMap keyword has volume>0 and thus misses the routine mixed case (server/keyword-strategy-synthesis/provider-validation.ts:85-98 logs 'N validated, M unvalidated' per run; page-assignment.ts:120,173 sets validated:false on AI-fallback keywords). The client-facing PageKeywordMapContent.tsx:246 badge survives but is not the admin workbench. Docs check: no mention of validated/unvalidated in docs/ui-rebuild/parity/page-intelligence-contract.md, parity-backlog.md, coverage-audit.md, or owner-decision-packet.md — not an owner-approved exception, and contract point 5 requires provider-metric evidence to remain reachable. Severity holds at major: the rebuilt admin decision surface now presents provider-unvalidated (potentially AI-fabricated) volume/KD/CPC indistinguishably from validated data, a silent data-honesty regression on the operator's primary page-prioritization surface.

validations.claim: The cockpit's headline completeness metric can never be satisfied: the Voice & Messaging group counts at most 1 configured input (only hasText(brandVoice)) against a total of 3, so a workspace with a fully saved brand voice shows 33% with a red 'Needs setup' badge forever, directly contradicting its own item rows (the Brand voice row shows emerald 'Ready' at the same time). The surface-wide counter consequently caps at 9/11 'inputs configured' — 11/11 is unreachable in any workspace state. The parity contract's V1 claim of a 'truthful 5/11' fixture masked this because the fixture never approached the ceiling.
validations.note: Verified against origin/staging (78239b424). Every mechanical element of the claim checks out. (1) src/components/brand-ai-rebuilt/BrandAiSurface.tsx:255 — `voiceConfigured = countComplete([hasText(ws?.brandVoice)])` is a single-boolean array, so it is structurally capped at 1; buildBrandContextSummary (line 252) is the only computation site (useBrandAiSurfaceState.ts contains no alternate path). (2) Lines 291-292 set the Voice & Messaging group to `configured: voiceConfigured, total: 3`, so the group percent maxes at Math.round(1/3*100)=33 (percentage(), line 248-250). (3) contextGroupStatus (lines 493-497) requires >=40 for 'part' and >=80 for 'set', so 33% → 'empty'; statusLabel('empty')='Needs setup' (line 471) and statusTone('empty')='red' (line 477), rendered on the cockpit group card at lines 553-554/578 — a fully saved brand voice is permanently red 'Needs setup'. (4) Simultaneously the 'Brand voice & style' item row inside the same group has status 'set' (line 303) which renders the emerald ready check via ContextStatusIcon (lines 480-490) — the self-contradiction is real (trivial wording nuance: the item row shows an emerald 'set' check icon; the literal 'Ready' text label only appears on group badges). (5) Surface headline (lines 441-442, rendered lines 536-540 'inputs configured'): max reachable = voice 1 + knowledge 3 + audience 1 + facts 4 = 9 of total 11; 11/11 is unreachable in any workspace state — I verified all knowledge/audience/facts booleans (lines 256-283) are independently satisfiable, so 9 is the exact ceiling. (6) The behavior is even test-pinned: tests/component/brand-ai-rebuilt/BrandAiSurface.test.tsx:299-302 asserts the Voice meter is red #f87171, 'Needs setup' appears, and the count is '7/11' — with a fixture whose brandVoice IS set (line 143), confirming the cockpit misreports a configured voice. The test pins the buggy output; it does not legitimize it. (7) Not an owner-approved exception: docs/ui-rebuild/parity/brand-ai-contract.md's cockpit row records only V1 'truthful 5/11 inputs configured' on the live fixture and color bands emerald>=80/amber>=40/red<40; the V5-V7 approved exceptions cover data rollups/topbar/rail typography, not a voice-count cap; parity-backlog.md has no entry for it. The 5/11 fixture never approached the 9/11 ceiling, so the unsatisfiability was masked exactly as claimed. (8) Legacy src/components/brand/BrandOverviewTab.tsx (~lines 119-123) showed qualitative notes ('Brand voice saved') and a 'Configured' badge, never a percent — accurate. Severity: major is fair — the cockpit is the surface's headline calibration metric and the rebuild's own contract repeatedly demands 'truthful' readouts; a structurally unsatisfiable KPI that contradicts its own item rows misleads the operator on every workspace, though it blocks no workflow and loses no data.

validations.claim: Unsaved brand-voice, knowledge-base, and persona edits are silently discarded when the Context editors modal closes: the DS Modal traps Escape at document level 'regardless of focus target' (so Esc while typing in the RichTextEditor closes the modal) and also closes on backdrop click, and the workflow modal's onClose unconditionally returns to overview with no dirty-state guard. Legacy carried the same local-draft state but on a full page, where accidental dismissal was far less likely than Esc/stray-click on a modal.
validations.note: Verified every link in the chain. (1) Modal.tsx:92-101 closes on Escape via a document-level keydown listener; line 129's own comment says it "intentionally traps Escape + Tab regardless of focus target"; no Escape handling exists in RichTextEditor.tsx or BrandHub.tsx, so Esc while typing in the editor bubbles to document and closes the modal. (2) Modal.tsx:178-191 closes on backdrop mousedown+click. (3) BrandAiSurface.tsx:1135 onClose is an unconditional setTab('overview') with no dirty guard, and line 1005 unmounts the modal body — which at line 920-922 is the legacy BrandHub (activeTab="context") whose local drafts (brandVoice/brandVoiceEditorHtml at BrandHub.tsx:281-282, kbDraft/kbEditorHtml at 310-311, localPersonas/personaDraft at 320-323) are destroyed on unmount; the 'Unsaved changes' indicator at BrandHub.tsx:728-729 confirms dirty state exists but is unused on close. No autosave/beforeunload/confirm anywhere in either component. (4) Capability did not move: the only close path in brand-ai-rebuilt/ is the unguarded one. (5) Not an approved exception: docs/ui-rebuild/parity/brand-ai-contract.md:40,50 approves mounting legacy panels inside modals, and :97 says closing should return to overview "without dropping workspace state"; owner-decision-packet.md ODP-BRAND-V1..V7 (:123-131) covers modal width/interiors only — no decision accepts unsaved-edit discard. (6) Legacy comparison holds: App.tsx:224,490 shows legacy renders BrandHub as a full page where Esc/stray-click cannot dismiss it. Major is a fair rating for silent loss of typed brand-voice/KB/persona content from a single common keystroke in a core workflow on a rebuilt surface; not a blocker since persisted data is unaffected.

validations.claim: The right-rail 'Also on this client' card tells operators 'Page strategy and content planning stay with the strategy and content pipeline surfaces' — but Page Strategy (site blueprints + the entire copy pipeline: BlueprintDetail, CopyReviewPanel, BatchGenerationPanel, CopyExportPanel, CopyIntelligenceManager) mounts ONLY inside this surface's Context editors modal, in both flag states. An operator following the signpost to the content-pipeline surface finds nothing; meanwhile no overview row names Page Strategy, so the carried capability is both hidden and actively misdirected.
validations.note: Verified on origin/staging. (1) Rail copy exact-match at src/components/brand-ai-rebuilt/BrandAiSurface.tsx:840 inside the 'Also on this client' card (833-842): 'Page strategy and content planning stay with the strategy and content pipeline surfaces.' (2) Actual mount confirmed: BrandAiSurface.tsx:920-923 renders BrandHub chromeless activeTab='context' inside the modal titled 'Context editors' (BrandAiSurface.tsx:193); BrandHub.tsx:587 gates renderedTab==='context' and the Page Strategy SectionCard with BlueprintDetail/PageStrategyTab/BlueprintVersionHistory sits at BrandHub.tsx:1003-1026. (3) Refutation search failed: repo-wide grep shows PageStrategyTab/BlueprintDetail/CopyReviewPanel/BatchGenerationPanel/CopyExportPanel/CopyIntelligenceManager referenced only by BrandHub.tsx + src/components/brand/*; zero blueprint/page-strategy references in src/components/content-pipeline-rebuilt/, ContentPipeline.tsx, or src/components/strategy/ (strategy's pageMap is the unrelated keyword page-map). No rebuilt overview row names Page Strategy — enumerated all rows (Brand voice & style, Voice calibration, Brandscript, Knowledge base, Discovery, Industry & goals, personas, Business footprint, Locations, E-E-A-T, Identity & awards); the only 'page strategy' string in brand-ai-rebuilt/ is the misdirecting rail line itself. (4) Docs strengthen the claim: brand-ai-contract.md:110 and :115 codify ratified owner decision C-6 (CROSS_SURFACE_CONTRACTS.md:41-45,86; owner-decisions.json:6 acceptedAsPrefilled) keeping Page Strategy/Copy Pipeline ON Brand & AI — the rail copy is a carried-over prototype pointer (brand.js:414) that Phase 0 itself flagged as a hard-stop hazard (docs/ui-rebuild/phase0/surfaces/brand-ai.md:91), and it contradicts C-6; the only approved rail exception is stylistic (13.5px/700 title, brand-ai-corrective-plan.md:77), not this content. One immaterial imprecision: in flag-OFF the subsystem mounts in legacy BrandHub's Context TAB (App.tsx:453), not a modal — but the misdirecting rail only exists flag-ON, and in both states the capability lives solely on Brand & AI. Severity major is honest for a solo-operator platform: an entire copy-production subsystem has zero named entry point on the rebuilt overview and its only signpost points to two surfaces where it does not exist.

validations.claim: Roadmap feature-name resolution: legacy joined the features API (featureId -> feature title) so filter options and row chips showed human feature names; rebuilt never fetches features and renders literal 'Feature #N' in both the filter dropdown and row badges, which is unresolvable anywhere in the UI (the Feature Library does not display ids).
validations.note: Verified every cited line on origin/staging: legacy join at src/components/Roadmap.tsx:38-49 (featuresApi.get + featureMap), name-labelled filter at src/components/RoadmapFilterBar.tsx:73-81, FeatureChip renders at RoadmapBacklogView.tsx:100,148-150 and RoadmapSprintView.tsx:74,105; rebuilt literal label at src/components/global-ops-rebuilt/wave-a/roadmapModel.ts:81, filter values from labels at RoadmapLens.tsx:114-123, badges at wave-a/RoadmapItemRow.tsx:92,113. Confirmed no features API usage anywhere in global-ops-rebuilt/ (grep), FeatureLibrary.tsx:31-53 FeatureCard shows no ids (f.id only a React key at line 191), and no documented exception covers it (global-ops-contract.md:108-115 GO-001..GO-008; phase0 surfaces/global-ops.md:80 marks the chip 'preserved'). REFUTING FACT on impact: data/roadmap.json on staging has 0/251 items with featureId, roadmap.archive.json has 0, and `git log -S featureId` on both files returns nothing — so neither legacy chips nor rebuilt 'Feature #N' labels have ever rendered on real data, and the rebuilt filter is disabled when featureValues is empty (RoadmapLens.tsx:298). Code-level regression real and undocumented; practical severity for a solo operator is minor.
validations.correctedClaim: The rebuilt roadmap does drop the legacy featureId→title join (roadmapModel.ts:81 substitutes 'Feature #N'; no features fetch exists in global-ops-rebuilt/; the Feature Library never displays ids; not covered by GO-001..GO-008 and phase0 inventory marked the chip 'preserved') — but the loss is latent, not user-visible: zero roadmap items in data/roadmap.json (251 items), the archive, or all git history have ever carried a featureId, so legacy never rendered a FeatureChip, the rebuilt 'Feature #N' label never renders, and the rebuilt feature filter is simply disabled (RoadmapLens.tsx:298). It is a dormant code-path parity regression worth a small fix, not a major functional loss.

validations.claim: Completion % denominator silently changed and now disagrees with the adjacent 'Total items' tile: legacy computed done/total; rebuilt computes done/(done+in_progress+pending), excluding deferred and closed, while the tile row still displays the full total. An operator inferring raw counts from the percentage gets wrong numbers. This violates the repo's rate-display rule (numerator/denominator must share a displayed source) and the surface's own build-ticket criterion 'Completion rate denominator must be the same item count displayed next to it'.
validations.correctedClaim: Accurate as stated, with one contextual addendum: the denominator change was not accidental rebuild drift but a deliberate, post-owner-approval change in PR #1520 (roadmap value audit, 2026-07-12) intended to keep deferred/closed items from inflating shipment metrics — however the completion-tile denominator swap itself is undocumented, unlabeled in the UI, untested, uncovered by any GO-* exception, leaves legacy (done/total) and rebuilt (done/executableTotal) disagreeing in the same PR, and violates both the ticket criterion at global-ops.md:95 and the repo's rate-display rule exactly as claimed.
validations.firstHandCheck: First-hand re-verified: RoadmapLens.tsx:103 done/executableTotal vs legacy Roadmap.tsx:142 done/total; adjacent Total tile uses full count.

validations.claim: Global tabs misattribute context to the FIRST workspace: on /settings, /roadmap, /revenue etc. the rebuilt sidebar's WorkspaceSelector displays workspaces[0] as the active workspace, the breadcrumb reads 'Command Center / <first-workspace> / Settings' for a GLOBAL page, clicking any workspace nav item silently navigates INTO workspaces[0] (an operator who was working in workspace B lands in workspace A's Keywords), and AdminChat mounts bound to workspaces[0] — legacy mounted no AdminChat and disabled workspace nav on global tabs. In a multi-client agency tool this is a wrong-client hazard.
validations.note: Verified every cited mechanic on origin/staging (78239b424). CONFIRMED in code: src/App.tsx:280-284 chromeWorkspace falls back to workspaces[0] on GLOBAL_TABS (routes.ts:36); App.tsx:497 passes it as selected to RebuiltAppChrome; RebuiltAppChrome.tsx:186-213 threads it to RebuiltSidebar + RebuiltBreadcrumb; RebuiltSidebar.tsx:367-379 WorkspaceSelector displays workspaces[0] as active; RebuiltSidebar.tsx:184-187+267-278 nav items enabled and navigate to adminPath(workspaces[0].id, item.id); RebuiltBreadcrumb.tsx:141-148 pushes the workspace crumb for any non-null selected; App.tsx:526-539 CommandPalette + AdminChat get chromeWorkspace (AdminChat bound to workspaces[0].id). Legacy contrast confirmed: legacy App.tsx:610-623 passes selected(null) to palette and gates AdminChat on selected; legacy Sidebar.tsx disables workspace items when !selected. BUT the claim's "regression" framing is weakened by docs/ui-rebuild/parity/global-ops-contract.md (status owner-approved, 2026-07-10): the first-workspace chrome context is an explicit documented judgment call ("shell mount fix, not an IA change") that fixed a real bug where /settings, /roadmap, /revenue, /outcomes-overview fell through to legacy panels; the behavior is pinned by tests/component/App.test.tsx:626 ("mounts rebuilt global Settings with the first workspace as chrome context"). ui-rebuild-shell defaults false (shared/types/feature-flags.ts:129, rolloutTarget staging-validation), so no production exposure. Residual value: the contract does not enumerate the breadcrumb/nav-click/AdminChat consequences, so the wrong-client-hazard observation stands as a follow-up item — severity minor for a flag-gated, solo-operator shell where the selector, breadcrumb, and page data all visibly show which workspace is active.
validations.correctedClaim: On global tabs the rebuilt shell deliberately uses workspaces[0] as chrome context — an owner-approved, documented, test-pinned decision (docs/ui-rebuild/parity/global-ops-contract.md: "Let global rebuilt routes use the first available workspace as chrome context... This is a shell mount fix, not an IA change", approved 2026-07-10; pinned by tests/component/App.test.tsx:626), made to fix global routes falling through to legacy entirely. However, the contract approves the mechanism without enumerating three operator-visible consequences that diverge from legacy: the breadcrumb names the first workspace on global pages (RebuiltBreadcrumb.tsx:141-148), workspace nav clicks land in workspaces[0]'s tab instead of being disabled (RebuiltSidebar.tsx:184-187, 267-278), and AdminChat newly mounts bound to workspaces[0] (App.tsx:531-539). These merit a follow-up UX hardening pass (e.g. remember the last-visited workspace, or suppress the workspace crumb/chat on global pages), but this is a documented tradeoff on a default-OFF staging flag for a solo operator, not an unnoticed major regression.
validations.firstHandCheck: First-hand browser check on bare /settings: breadcrumb read 'Command Center → cascade-debug-1783977240399 → Settings' (a junk debug workspace = workspaces[0]) with that workspace active in the sidebar selector.

validations.claim: Outcomes Book at-a-glance attention triage: legacy sorted attention-needed workspaces to the top, showed an inline warning triangle + attentionReason under the workspace name, and had a dedicated Status column (Review/On track). Rebuilt sorts by value/month and only reveals the attention badge and reason after expanding each row.
validations.note: Verified every cited line on both sides. Legacy: src/components/admin/outcomes/OutcomesOverview.tsx:206-212 (attention-first sort), :112-120 (inline triangle + attentionReason), :177-183 (Review/On track Status column) — all accurate. Rebuilt: src/components/global-ops-rebuilt/OutcomesBookLens.tsx:25-29 (value-desc sort only), OutcomesBookTable.tsx:91 (collapsed columns Workspace/Value/Clicks/Wins/Trend/Open, no attention), :236 + :254-262 (attention badge/reason expanded-only), :47 header promise — all accurate. Refutation search: grep attentionNeeded|attentionReason across src/ hits ONLY these two files, so the triage did not move elsewhere in the rebuilt UI. Weakening facts: (1) server/routes/outcomes.ts:229-237 shows attentionNeeded = pendingCount>10 OR trend==='declining', and the collapsed rebuilt row still shows Trend with amber 'Declining' (OutcomesBookTable.tsx:37-48,196-199), so one of two attention triggers survives at-a-glance; (2) the one-step-deeper disclosure is explicit design (subtitle at OutcomesBookTable.tsx:74) and the contract Must-Preserve only requires 'outcome coverage/attention fields' (docs/ui-rebuild/parity/global-ops-contract.md:99), which is satisfied; (3) the whole Global Ops route family incl. Outcomes Book expanded-evidence captures was owner-approved 2026-07-10 (ODP-009, owner-decision-packet.md; global-ops-contract.md:5,135); (4) legacy remains mounted flag-off (src/App.tsx:412) while rebuilt mounts only under ui-rebuild-shell (src/App.tsx:224,490; rebuiltSurfaces.ts:89-90). The demotion is real and not itemized in GO-001..GO-008, but 'functionality-lost / major' overstates a deliberate, owner-approved progressive-disclosure change where the info is one click away and half the trigger set stays visible; honest severity for a solo operator is minor, with the header-copy overpromise as the legitimate residue.
validations.correctedClaim: Outcomes Book attention triage is demoted, not lost: the attention badge and reason are preserved but require per-row expansion (OutcomesBookTable.tsx:236, 254-262), and attention-first sorting is replaced by value/month ranking (OutcomesBookLens.tsx:25-29). Mitigations: attentionNeeded has only two server triggers (server/routes/outcomes.ts:229-237 — pendingCount>10 OR declining trend), and the declining-trend trigger remains visible at a glance in the collapsed row's amber Trend column (OutcomesBookTable.tsx:196-199); the one-step-deeper disclosure is deliberate and labeled in the table subtitle (OutcomesBookTable.tsx:74); the surface is owner-approved with expanded-evidence captures reviewed (ODP-009, 2026-07-10, global-ops-contract.md:99,135). Residue: the lens header copy overpromises at-a-glance attention triage (OutcomesBookLens.tsx:47) relative to what the collapsed table shows, and the specific demotion is not itemized as a GO-* exception.

validations.claim: The rebuilt Outcomes Book labels the whole table 'Rolling 90 days' (twice), but none of the displayed data is a 90-day window: 'Value delivered / mo' and 'Wins' come from an ALL-TIME last-100-wins rollup with no date filter, 'Clicks' is a 28-day GSC window, and win rate / scored counts in the expansion are 30-day. Legacy made no period claim (and correctly labelled its one windowed column 'Scored (30d)'). An operator or client-facing report built from this screen would misstate the measurement period.
validations.note: Verified on origin/staging. Both "Rolling 90 days" labels exist: src/components/global-ops-rebuilt/OutcomesBookLens.tsx:43 and src/components/global-ops-rebuilt/wave-c/outcomes/OutcomesBookTable.tsx:77. None of the displayed data is a 90-day window: (1) Value delivered/mo + Wins come from getWorkspaceOutcomeValueRollup (server/routes/workspaces.ts:339 → server/outcome-tracking.ts:1018) whose SQL (getWinsWithValueByWorkspace, outcome-tracking.ts:98-114) has no date predicate, only LIMIT 100 — all-time; the windowed variant at :115 is unused on this path. (2) Clicks is a 28-day GSC read (server/routes/workspaces.ts:133, getSearchOverview(...,28,...)). (3) Expansion "Scored in 30 days" is 30-day (scoredLast30d, server/routes/outcomes.ts:249), and "Win rate" is actually ALL-TIME (totalWins/totalScored, outcomes.ts:216) merely display-gated on scoredLast30d>0 — the claim's one imprecise detail, and it makes the mismatch worse, not better. (4) Trend is an all-time split-half (outcomes.ts:210-224). Legacy comparator confirmed: src/components/admin/outcomes/OutcomesOverview.tsx:268 labels its one windowed column "Scored (30d)" and makes no global period claim, so this is a genuine labeling regression. No owner-approved exception found: docs/ui-rebuild/parity/global-ops-contract.md (GO-004, owner approval 2026-07-10) covers server-owned rollups and documented GO-* exceptions but never mentions the "Rolling 90 days" label; the string appears nowhere else in src/. The copy likely came from the Phase-0 prototype's "90-day scorecard" concept (docs/ui-rebuild/phase0/checkpoints/), which was never backed by 90-day data. Severity major is fair: the false period claim appears twice on a proof/outcomes surface used to build client-facing reports, on a platform whose own contracts treat measurement honesty as first-class — though the fix is a trivial two-string change.
